-- them).  Measuring that length by walking the list was O(N) over an O(N) list once
-- per group — O(N²), and the single hottest symbol in the typecheck stage on a large
-- file.  This counter mirrors the list's length exactly; the ONLY mutators of the
-- list are `resetState` (clears both), `pushPendingObl` (extends both), and
-- inferDefaultMethodBody's error rollback (restores both from a snapshot).

-- The one and only way to add an obligation: keeps the length counter in step.
pushPendingObl : (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Unit
pushPendingObl obl =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.pendingImplObligations (obl::perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value)
  setRef
    perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN
    (perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value + 1)

-- PLAN.md #11: one entry per source integer literal (ENumLit):
-- (litVar, fref, n, dref).  [litVar] is the literal's fresh Num-obligated type var;
-- [fref] is the node's own Option Float cell; [n] the int value; [dref] is the
-- node's `fromInt`-route cell (filled by resolveSites — the literal is modelled as a
-- `fromInt n` method occurrence).  After defaulting + grounding, the post-HM pass
-- setNumlitFloats decides the runtime rep (mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's
-- set_numlit_floats): litVar ground Float ⇒ fref Some, dref cleared ⇒ ELit (LFloat f);
-- litVar ground Int ⇒ both cleared ⇒ ELit (LInt n); litVar still polymorphic `Num a`
-- (the soundness case — `1` in `inc x = x + 1`) ⇒ dref KEPT (RDictFwd onto the
-- enclosing Num dict) ⇒ dictPass emits `EApp (EMethodAt "fromInt" …) (ELit (LInt n))`.

-- Chunk A (error framing): the union-find root ids of every type var that took
-- part in a unification failure (`typeMismatch`).  Consulted ONLY by
-- `checkUndeterminedObligation` to SUPPRESS a secondary `ambiguous instance`
-- cascade on a var the primary mismatch already explained — never read on the
-- inference path, so a well-typed program (no mismatch) never poisons anything
-- and behaves identically.

-- Chunk D (Num mis-framing): (litVar, loc) for every numeric literal whose var
-- carried a Num obligation — captured at inferNumLit/inferNumLitBare record time
-- while the var is still unbound.  Lets checkOneImplObligation reframe a purely
-- literal Num obligation forced to a concrete non-Num type (`Type mismatch: Int vs
-- T`) while an OPERATOR-sourced one (`x + 1`) keeps `No impl of Num for T`.  Storing
-- the VAR (not just the loc) lets the operator-taint pass below share a root through
-- `normalize`, so `1 + x` and `x + 1` are both recognised as operator-sourced
-- regardless of unify direction.  Error-path only; reset per run.

-- PERF: the literals that are NOT YET operator-tainted.  `markNumlitOpTaint` scans
-- THIS instead of the full `numlitVarLocs` (which is never trimmed).  Sound because
-- tainting is idempotent: it appends a loc to `numlitOpLocs`, whose only reader
-- (`numlitReframeLoc`) does a membership test — so re-tainting an already-tainted
-- literal cannot change any outcome, and a tainted literal may leave the worklist.
-- Scanning EVERY literal ever recorded on EVERY arithmetic operand was O(N²), and
-- after the allocation fix (see `rootIdOf`) it was still the typecheck stage's
-- dominant remaining cost on a large file.
--
-- `numlitVarLocs` itself keeps every literal — `numlitReframeLoc` and
-- `tagLitLocsWithRoot` both still need the complete list.

-- Chunk D: locs of literals whose var was ALSO an operand of an arithmetic op
-- (recordIfaceObligation).  Such a literal keeps the Num message — the operator is the
-- real culprit — so the reframe skips it (it reframes `litLocs \ opLocs`).

-- Chunk D: (literalLoc, contextKind) recorded when inferIf / inferListLit / consOp
-- is about to force a still-unbound literal var against a concrete sibling type —
-- lets the reframe pick site-specific wording (if-branches / list elements / cons
-- head-vs-element) over the bare `Type mismatch: Int vs T`.

-- LSP hover: every local binding (let-bound name, lambda param, function-clause
-- param, match-arm binder) flows through inferPat's PVar/PAs arms.  Capture
-- (name, mono var) here so hover can render a *local* variable's inferred type:
-- `checkProgramSchemes` only returns top-level + global schemes, so without this
-- a hover over e.g. a `let input = …` name finds nothing and returns null.  The
-- mono var is union-find-solved by the time inference ends; checkProgramSeeded
-- then generalizes each into `localSchemesOut`, which the LSP reads as a hover
-- fallback.  This side-channel does NOT enter the returned env, so completion and
-- inlay-hint output (which consume the env directly) are unaffected.

-- Hover side-channel for the SEED (runtime.mdk externs: putStrLn, readLine, …).
-- checkProgramSeeded keeps the seed in scope for inference but excludes it from
-- the returned env (see its comment), so externs aren't otherwise hoverable.

-- Getters for the LSP (cross-module).  The raw Refs are nullary value globals
-- (@mdk_g_), which the import-aware mangling map doesn't carry, so expose them
-- through functions — the form every other tools→typecheck import already uses.
export currentLocalSchemes : Unit -> List (String, Scheme)
currentLocalSchemes _ = perRun.value.localSchemesOut.value

export currentSeedSchemes : Unit -> List (String, Scheme)
currentSeedSchemes _ = perRun.value.seedSchemesOut.value

recordLocalBind : String -> Mono -> Unit
recordLocalBind name v =
  setRef
    perRun.value.localBindRefs
    ((name, v)::perRun.value.localBindRefs.value)

-- dict-passing state (the typed pipeline's `=>`-constraint layer):
--   activeDictVars   : constraint tyvar id → the dict parameter name a method
--                      occurrence at that variable's type reads (RDict).
--   funConstraintsRef: constrained fn name → its constraints' quantified tyvar
--                      ids (one per `=>` constraint, in slot order) — a call site
--                      maps each to its fresh instantiation var to pick a route.
--   pendingDictApps  : a constrained-fn occurrence's routes ref + the instantiated
--                      constraint monos, resolved to RKey/RDict after inference.

-- WS-1b: the program decls (interfaces) the supers-expansion reads.  Set by each
-- driver (elaborateDict / the module path) BEFORE inference; read by inferDictAtFound
-- to expand a call site's constraint slots with their transitive supers.  Read-only
-- during inference, so it needs no reset-lifecycle care.

-- WS-1b: the USER-module interface names (NOT core/prelude).  Super-expansion is
-- gated to user interfaces only: a prelude super chain (Ord requires Eq) is already
-- serviced by arg-tag dispatch and is NOT dict-passed on the eval path, so adding an
-- Eq slot to `clamp : Ord a =>` would desync its def arity from its arg-tag call sites
-- (the prior-attempt clamp regression).  User interfaces (Sub requires Sup) ARE
-- dict-passed and need the super slot.  Set by elaborateModules.

-- #11 soundness (native obligation hole): the INTERFACE names of a constrained
-- fn's `=>` constraints, slot-parallel to funConstraintsRef's ids.  funConstraintsRef
-- keeps only the tyvar ids (enough to pick a dict route); but to record a real impl
-- obligation when a constrained fn is INSTANTIATED at a concrete type (`g = f "hello"`
-- where `f : Num a => a -> a`), the call site needs the iface ("Num") so it can check
-- `Num String` against the impl table.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's fun_constraints
-- carrying the iface name into check_constraint_obligations.  Populated at the SAME
-- three sites as funConstraintsRef (registerMember / registerInferredFor /
-- seedDictAritiesFromSigs), with the SAME cross-module accumulate+reseed lifecycle.

-- a constrained-fn CALL site's instantiated constraint obligations: (iface, the
-- call's instantiated constraint mono).  Resolved after inference (when the mono is
-- ground) by checkCallObligations — a CONCRETE-head mono with no matching impl is an
-- ERROR; a still-polymorphic head is DEFERRED (left to a further-out caller).  This
-- is the compiler analog of the oracle's constraint_obligations + check_constraint_
-- obligations: pendingDictApps routes the dict, this list checks the impl exists.

-- PERF: `pendingCallObligations`' length in O(1) — see `pendingImplObligationsN`.
-- processSCC brackets each top-level group with a length-before / length-after pair
-- (three reads per group), and this list is never trimmed, so walking it to measure
-- its length was O(N) per group — O(N²) over a large file.  Mutated ONLY by
-- `resetState` and `pushCallObl`.

-- One obligation per PREDICATE: (iface, argument VECTOR, loc).  A 1-param
-- interface pushes a singleton vector; `Ix a i` pushes [a, i] JOINTLY, so the
-- checker can ask "is there an impl of Ix for THIS pair" rather than two
-- unanswerable questions about `a` and `i` separately (#607).
pushCallObl : (String, List Mono, Option Loc) -> Unit
pushCallObl obl =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.pendingCallObligations (obl::perRun.value.pendingCallObligations.value)
  setRef
    perRun.value.pendingCallObligationsN
    (perRun.value.pendingCallObligationsN.value + 1)

-- #11 soundness (native obligation hole, ALWAYS-ON, independent of the dict-
-- elaboration machinery): per top-level binding, the deferred impl obligations it
-- generalizes over — (iface, the quantified-var id the obligation discriminates on).
-- Populated UNCONDITIONALLY at generalization in processSCC/processTopGroups (unlike
-- funConstraintsRef, which the check path leaves empty because its inferred-constraint
-- registration is gated on dictEligibleSetRef).  At a USE site (inferVar) the binding's
-- entry is freshened through the call's instantiation subst and checked against the
-- impl table — the compiler analog of lib/typecheck.ml's inferred_constraints carried
-- on a scheme and re-checked at every instantiation.

-- E6 (module emit path only): funConstraintsRef is wiped by resetState at the start
-- of EACH module's typecheck, but a cross-module `=>`-constrained CALL site (entry
-- calling an imported `clampU`) must still find the callee's generalized constraint
-- ids so inferDictAt records a real pendingDictApp (→ the call gets its dict ARG).
-- This ref ACCUMULATES every module's registered fn-constraints across the graph and
-- is NOT cleared by resetState; checkModuleFullImpl re-seeds funConstraintsRef from
-- it after the per-module reset.  The DEFINING module's entry (real
-- ids, registered first) wins the lookupAssocSL2 first-match.
-- WS-2/D2: module-attributed mirror of crossModuleFunConstraintsRef.  Keyed by
-- (owning module id, fn name) so the FINAL dict-pass (scopeArities) can keep only
-- the inferred-arity entry whose DEFINING module is in the dict-passed module's
-- scope.  Without this, two distinct top-level fns sharing a bare name but with
-- different `=>`-constraint arity (e.g. `wrap : Tag a => …` in module A vs
-- `wrap : (P a, Q a) => …` in module B) both land in the bare cross-module table;
-- scopeArities keeps both (both pass the bare scopeNames membership), and
-- dictArityOf's first-match returns the WRONG module's arity → a call site in A's
-- importer is sized to B's 2 dict params (over-application) → run-path crash while
-- the emit path's arg-stamp recovers (the §1 D2 repro).  Populated per-module in
-- checkModuleFullImpl (each fn DEFINED in this module's `prog` is attributed to mid);
-- consumed by scopeAritiesQual.  The bare crossModuleFunConstraintsRef is KEPT — the
-- per-module reseed at checkModuleFullImpl:7546 needs the bare form for the current
-- module's E6 cross-module call-site discovery.
-- cross-module accumulator for funConstraintIfacesRef, mirroring
-- crossModuleFunConstraintsRef exactly (accumulate across the graph, NOT cleared by
-- resetState, re-seeded after the per-module reset).
-- WS-2/D2 (ifaces analog of crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef): module-attributed
-- mirror of crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesRef, keyed by (DEFINER module id, fn name),
-- slot-parallel to crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef.  inferDictAtFound reads the
-- callee's dict ids AND their ifaces from the SAME qualified (definer,name) source so
-- the two stay aligned across a bare-name cross-module collision (a foreign same-named
-- fn of different `=>`-arity must not contribute its ifaces).  Populated/reset in
-- lockstep with the ids qual ref (checkModuleFullImpl / elaborateModules).
-- WS-2/D2: the current module's imported VALUE name → its source module id (the dotted
-- id its `import` form names == the loader mid == the qual tables' DEFINER key for a
-- DIRECT import).  Lets inferDictAtFound resolve a cross-module constrained callee's
-- dict arity by MODULE IDENTITY (the (definer,name)-keyed qual tables) instead of the
-- bare-name first-match over the jointly-seeded funConstraintsRef — which picks the
-- MOST-RECENTLY-PREPENDED same-named entry, i.e. a FOREIGN module's `wrap` when the
-- importer merely imports a different `wrap`.  That bare-name collision is an
-- observable run-crash today (`applied non-function`) on `medaka run` of a direct
-- cross-module import of two same-named, different-`=>`-arity constrained fns, while
-- `check` accepts and `build` is saved by universal mangling.  Set per-module in
-- checkModuleFullImpl from `prog`'s DUse decls; only EXPLICIT imports
-- (UseName/UseGroup/UseAlias) are mapped — a wildcard `import mod.*` can't enumerate
-- names here, and a re-exported fn's import source ≠ its definer, so both fall back to
-- the bare table (correct, byte-identical; the qual path is the fix for the common
-- direct-import case).  Reset by resetState (per module).

-- WS-1c: the third field is the slot-parallel interface-name list (one per mono),
-- so resolveDictApps can resolve an UNDETERMINED top-level constraint (a constraint
-- tyvar pinned by neither arg nor result) by counting that interface's impls: sole
-- impl → a concrete RKey to it; ≥2 → an AmbiguousImpl error.  Recorded from the same
-- `expanded` (iface, id) pairing inferDictAtFound already builds for recordCallObligations.

-- PERF: `pendingDictApps`' length in O(1) — same O(N²)-per-group length scan as
-- `pendingCallObligationsN`.  Mutated ONLY by `resetState` and `pushDictApp`.

pushDictApp : (Ref (List Route), List Mono, List String) -> Unit
pushDictApp app =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.pendingDictApps (app::perRun.value.pendingDictApps.value)
  setRef perRun.value.pendingDictAppsN (perRun.value.pendingDictAppsN.value + 1)

-- method-level-constraint dict-passing (the reference's res_method_dicts, Phase
-- 69.x-e).  An interface method whose signature carries a `=>` constraint over a
-- tyvar that is NOT the interface param (canonically `foldMap : Monoid m => …`,
-- where `Monoid m` constrains the result monoid, not the container `t`):
--   methodConstraintsRef : method name → one tyvar id per such constraint, in slot
--                          order.  Captured while BUILDING the env method schemes
--                          (ifaceMethodSchemes) so the ids are the SAME quantified
--                          ids a call-site instantiation maps through its subst.
--   pendingMethodDicts   : a method occurrence's method-dicts ref to fill + the
--                          instantiated constraint monos (mirrors pendingDictApps),
--                          routed by resolveMethodDicts after inference.

-- Per method name, the POSITIONS (indices into the scheme's ordered quantified id
-- list) of its constraint slots.  Positions are allocation-INDEPENDENT: the env
-- scheme and the registry scheme are separate `sigToSchemeTvs mty` instantiations
-- with DIFFERENT fresh ids, but the SAME var order, so a slot's index is stable.
-- recordMethodDicts indexes THIS occurrence's ordered subst at these positions to
-- get the exact live constraint monos — sidestepping the cross-sweep/parametric-
-- sibling id-overlap that defeats countInSubst's overlap heuristic (a stale id can
-- land in the live range mapping to the container `t` instead of the applicative
-- `m`; the position never confuses the two).  One entry per method (dedup);
-- cleared in resetState.  Absent ⇒ fall back to alignedMethodConstraintIds.

-- Cross-module analog of methodConstraintsRef, mirroring crossModuleFunConstraintsRef
-- exactly.  resetState wipes methodConstraintsRef at each module start, then
-- checkModuleFullImpl re-registers via `ifaceMethodSchemes prog` over the CURRENT
-- module's decls only — so a method declared in an imported module (canonically
-- core's `foldMap : Monoid m =>` on `Foldable`) is absent during a later USER
-- module's pass, and its dict never gets recorded.  This ref ACCUMULATES every
-- module's registered method-constraints across the graph and is NOT cleared by
-- resetState; checkModuleFullImpl re-seeds methodConstraintsRef from it after the
-- per-module reset+registration (MERGE, the per-module entry wins first-match).
-- WS-2/D2 (method-level analog of crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef): module-attributed
-- mirror of crossModuleMethodConstraintsRef, keyed by (owning interface's module id,
-- method name).  Without it, two modules declaring distinct interfaces that share a
-- bare METHOD name but with different method-level `=>`-constraint arity (e.g.
-- `shape : Num e => …` in module A vs `shape : (Num e, Num f) => …` in module B) both
-- land in the bare methodConstraintsRef; methodDictArityOf's first-match returns the
-- WRONG module's arity → an impl/default method in one module is sized to the other's
-- dict-param count → a dict is mis-bound into a value parameter (`unknown op '+'` /
-- garbage / run≠build — the method-level twin of the §1 D2 fn repro, which `check`
-- accepts).  Populated per-module in checkModuleFullImpl (each method whose INTERFACE
-- is declared in this module's `prog`); consumed by scopeMethodArities in the final
-- per-scope dict-pass.  The bare crossModuleMethodConstraintsRef is KEPT — the
-- per-module reseed at checkModuleFullImpl needs the bare form for the current module's
-- cross-module call-site discovery (recordMethodDicts), exactly like the fn side.
-- #158 PR2: the 22 per-run cross-module accumulators declared above (the six
-- crossModule* dict-arity mirrors of #143/#144, plus the #154 universe*/obUniv* universe
-- accumulators) are bundled into ONE typed record so their survive/clear lifecycle is a
-- TYPE FACT, not prose.  `freshCrossRun` constructs exactly the field-Refs that
-- `resetCrossModuleState` installs, so "what survives resetState" == "what CrossRun holds"
-- == "what a per-run reset clears" — enforced by the constructor: a new accumulator cannot
-- be added to the survive-set without appearing in freshCrossRun (and thus the reset).
-- This is a record OF Refs (fields stay `Ref …`), NOT a record of values: the grow helpers
-- (appendUniverseAccums / appendDataUniverse / growImplUniverse) and the Module-arm copies
-- mutate the field-Refs in place, so no whole-record copy is allocated per grow/push.
data CrossRun = CrossRun {
    universeIfaceMethodsRef : Ref (OrdMap Unit),
    universeFunNamesRef : Ref (OrdMap Unit),
    universeKeyBucketsRef : Ref (OrdMap (List KeyEntry)),  -- = KeyBuckets, inlined: that alias is defined below this record, so it wouldn't expand in a field-type projection
    universeIfaceRequiredRef : Ref (OrdMap (List String)),
    universeMethodIfaceParamsRef : Ref (OrdMap (String, List String, Ty)),
    universeRegisteredIfacesRef : Ref (OrdMap Unit),
    universeMethodDispatchIdxRef : Ref (List (String, Int)),
    universeRecords : Ref (List (String, RecordInfo)),
    universeRecordByName : Ref (OrdMap RecordInfo),
    universeFieldOwners : Ref (OrdMap (List String)),
    universeDataParamKinds : Ref (List (String, List Kind)),
    universeAliasTable : Ref (List (String, (List String, Ty))),
    universeDataEnv : Ref TcEnv,
    universeDataDecls : Ref (List Decl),  -- #674: prior modules' PUBLIC data DECLS (universeDataEnv is a lossy bare-name ctor env) — see appendDataUniverse
    obUnivConcreteRef : Ref (OrdMap (List (List Ty, List Require))),
    obUnivHeadlessRef : Ref (OrdMap (List (List Ty, List Require))),
    obUnivIfaceTagsRef : Ref (OrdMap (OrdMap Unit)),
    crossModuleFunConstraintsRef : Ref (List (String, List Int)),
    crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef : Ref (List ((String, String), List Int)),
    crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesRef : Ref (List (String, List String)),
    crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesQualRef : Ref (List ((String, String), List String)),
    crossModuleMethodConstraintsRef : Ref (List (String, List Int)),
    crossModuleMethodConstraintsQualRef : Ref (List ((String, String), List Int)),
    coreSchemeObligationsRef : Ref (OrdMap (List (String, List Int))),  -- #673: core's OWN schemeObligationsRef, snapshotted after the core pass (see declaredCrossModuleObls)
  }

-- Construct all 22 field-Refs fresh (each reproduces its former top-level ref's initial).
-- ⚠️ `env` is the initial TcEnv (always `initialEnv`) and is a PARAMETER rather than a
-- direct `initialEnv` reference on purpose — do NOT "simplify" it back to a `Unit` arg.
-- `crossRun` below is a nullary top-level binding, i.e. a wasm value GLOBAL eagerly
-- initialized in `$__init`, and the WasmGC backend orders those globals with a topo sort
-- whose edges come from `eagerVars` (backend/emit_support.mdk) — a DIRECT free-var scan of
-- the binding's body that does NOT follow calls into a callee's body.  So a global read
-- that hides inside this function is INVISIBLE to that sort: `crossRun = Ref (freshCrossRun
-- ())` yielded the single edge `freshCrossRun` (a function, not a value bind ⇒ no edge at
-- all), `crossRun` was emitted before `initialEnv`, and `$__init` read a `ref.null` →
-- "dereferencing a null pointer" at instantiate, which killed the whole playground (#543).
-- Taking the env as an ARG puts `initialEnv` back in `crossRun`'s own body, where
-- `eagerVars` can see it and order the two globals correctly.
--
-- ⚠️ NATIVE HAS THE SAME BUG — it is merely not EXPLOITED.  An earlier draft of this
-- comment claimed "native is unaffected (it does not eagerly init these)"; that was FALSE
-- and is corrected here with receipts, because it is exactly the kind of unproven claim
-- that steers a later general fix wrong.  `llvm_emit.mdk`'s `orderedValBinds` is fed by
-- `bindFreeVars` = the SAME shared `eagerVars`, with the same does-not-follow-calls blind
-- spot, and its own doc comment says an unordered eager forward ref "captures the
-- still-zero global cell".  Emitting the PRE-FIX source with the native emitter proves the
-- inversion was live there too: in the init prologue `crossRun` stored at IR line 1693
-- (calling `freshCrossRun` at 1687) while `initialEnv` stored at 1768 — 75 lines later —
-- and `@mdk_g_types_typecheck__initialEnv = global i64 0` with a `load` of it inside
-- `freshCrossRun`.  Native really did read a ZERO.
-- It never BIT because the zero is overwritten before anything dereferences it: the only
-- readers of `universeDataEnv` sit on the `Module` arm (`registerAllData
-- crossRun.value.universeDataEnv.value`), and every multi-module entry calls
-- `resetCrossModuleState ()` FIRST (`checkModulesPreamble`, `elaborateModules`), which
-- replaces the whole poisoned bundle once `initialEnv` is properly set.  That is luck, not
-- design — native's failure mode is a SILENT zero where wasm traps loudly, which is the
-- only reason #543 was noticed at all.  The general fix (an emitter-side eager-reachability
-- closure that follows calls) is #553 and MUST cover both backends.
freshCrossRun : TcEnv -> CrossRun
freshCrossRun env = CrossRun {
  universeIfaceMethodsRef = Ref omEmpty,
  universeFunNamesRef = Ref omEmpty,
  universeKeyBucketsRef = Ref omEmpty,
  universeIfaceRequiredRef = Ref omEmpty,
  universeMethodIfaceParamsRef = Ref omEmpty,
  universeRegisteredIfacesRef = Ref omEmpty,
  universeMethodDispatchIdxRef = Ref [],
  universeRecords = Ref [],
  universeRecordByName = Ref omEmpty,
  universeFieldOwners = Ref omEmpty,
  universeDataParamKinds = Ref [],
  universeAliasTable = Ref [],
  universeDataEnv = Ref env,
  universeDataDecls = Ref [],
  obUnivConcreteRef = Ref omEmpty,
  obUnivHeadlessRef = Ref omEmpty,
  obUnivIfaceTagsRef = Ref omEmpty,
  crossModuleFunConstraintsRef = Ref [],
  crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef = Ref [],
  crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesRef = Ref [],
  crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesQualRef = Ref [],
  crossModuleMethodConstraintsRef = Ref [],
  crossModuleMethodConstraintsQualRef = Ref [],
  coreSchemeObligationsRef = Ref omEmpty,
}

-- The single module-level cell holding the current run's accumulator bundle.
crossRun : Ref CrossRun
crossRun = Ref (freshCrossRun initialEnv)

-- ── the ONE lifecycle owner for the 22 cross-module accumulators (#143/#144, #154) ──
-- These 22 deliberately SURVIVE the per-module `resetState` (so dict arities/ifaces and
-- the universe/data/impl accumulators cross module boundaries within one graph — see the
-- declaration comments above), but they must NOT survive a whole multi-module RUN: a
-- long-lived process (LSP, playground, snapshot runner) drives the analyze/elaborate path
-- repeatedly, and without a per-run reset run K+1 seeds `funConstraintsRef` /
-- `funConstraintIfacesRef` (and the universe accumulators) from run K's residue
-- (checkModuleFullImpl reads them for the FIRST module before any fresh snapshot lands),
-- while the `*Qual` mirrors are prepend-appended and never truncated → unbounded growth.
-- Give them exactly ONE reset owner, called at the top of EVERY multi-module entry
-- (`checkModules`, `checkModulesDiags`, `checkModulesEntryFull`, `elaborateModules`), so
-- the next entry added cannot silently miss one — and so all are cleared symmetrically.
-- Replacing `crossRun` with a fresh bundle IS the reset: freshCrossRun re-mints every
-- field, so the set that resets == the set in CrossRun, enforced by the constructor.
-- Scoped to exactly these 22; do NOT fold into `resetState` (they must survive THAT).
resetCrossModuleState : Unit -> Unit
resetCrossModuleState _ = setRef crossRun (freshCrossRun initialEnv)

-- self/mutually-recursive constrained-fn call sites.  At such a site the callee's
-- own constraints are not yet registered (its letrec group is still inferring),
-- so funConstraintsRef can't map its routes inline.  Defer them: RecDictApp holds
-- (routes ref to fill, callee name, the ENCLOSING fn's name, the live occurrence
-- mono).  realizeRecDictApps fills the routes after inference, forwarding the
-- enclosing fn's OWN dict parameter (RDict) for each constraint — the dict already
-- in scope in its body — rather than rebuilding one (which it cannot, the type is
-- still the constraint variable).  Mirrors the reference's recursive_promoted_usages
-- / realize_recursive_dict_apps (Phase 115/136).
public export data RecDictApp = RecDictApp (Ref (List Route)) String String Mono

-- the top-level fn whose body is currently being inferred ("" = none); captured
-- into a deferred RecDictApp so its recursive calls forward the right dict param.

-- G4: True while inferring an impl method BODY (set around inferImplMethod).  A
-- comparison/equality OPERATOR site records this flag so resolveBinopSite consults
-- `activeDictVarOf` (→ route the impl's threaded element dict, RDict) ONLY for sites
-- genuinely inside an impl body.  A TOP-LEVEL `<=`/`==` must NOT — its operand could
-- normalize to an unsolved tyvar whose id collides with a stale activeDictVars entry
-- left by a previously-inferred impl, spuriously routing it RDict (which flipped the
-- repl's String-keyed `:browse` sort).  activeDictVars is global + uncleared, so the
-- flag is the scope gate the id-lookup alone cannot provide.

-- INFERRED (unsignatured) `=>`-constraint promotion (the reference's two-pass
-- Elaborate / Phase 83/84/115).  An unsignatured fn whose body uses a
-- return-position method — or calls a constrained fn — at one of its OWN
-- generalized tyvars carries a constraint only AFTER inference.  We discover this
-- by recording, per occurrence, the enclosing fn (currentFn) and the live mono,
-- then (post-generalization) intersecting with the fn's quantified ids.
--   methodSiteFns : (enclosing fn, a return-position method site's result mono)
--   dictAppFns    : (enclosing fn, a constrained-fn call's instantiated constraint
--                   monos) — propagates a constraint to an unsignatured CALLER
--   promotedRef   : fns discovered THIS pass to carry an inferred constraint; read
--                   by elaborateDict to grow the dict-passed set on the next pass
--   dictEligibleRef: the USER fn names eligible for promotion (the prelude's
--                   constrained fns stay arg-tag/RKey — out of scope).  Set by
--                   elaborateDict; NOT cleared by resetState (constant per run).
--
-- PERF: both are INDEXED BY ENCLOSING FN rather than kept as flat (fn, mono) assoc
-- lists.  Their sole reader, `inferredConstraintIds`, selects the entries of ONE fn,
-- and it runs once per member of every top-level group — so scanning a flat list that
-- accumulates a site for every constrained occurrence in the whole file was O(N²).
-- Each key's list is built by consing, exactly like the flat lists were, so a fn's
-- sites keep the same most-recent-first order the old scan produced.

-- cons [x] onto the entry for [fn] (absent ⇒ a fresh single-element entry)
consSiteFn : String -> a -> OrdMap (List a) -> OrdMap (List a)
consSiteFn fn x idx = omInsert fn (x :: sitesFor fn idx) idx

-- the sites recorded for [fn], most-recent-first
sitesFor : String -> OrdMap (List a) -> List a
sitesFor fn idx = fromOption [] (omLookup fn idx)

-- #194: cross-module accumulator for the FIRST (bare-dict) elaboration sweep's
-- direct promotions.  resetState clears promotedRef per module, so elabHarvestWorker
-- unions each module's promotedRef.value into here right after that module's
-- elabModuleStamp (before the next module's resetState wipes it).  elaborateModules
-- reads it to decide the empty-case short-circuit vs the discard-and-fallback.  Set
-- to [] by elaborateModules before the sweep; NOT touched by resetState.

-- O(log n) membership index of dictEligibleRef.  #415 item 3: DERIVED from the list by
-- `setDictEligible`, the single writer of both — not mirrored at each set site, so the
-- two cannot drift.  maybeInferConstraint tests it per SCC member per discovery pass —
-- an O(members × eligible) scan over the whole program when it was a list.

-- Whether checkProgramSeeded ALSO infers each parametric impl's return-position
-- method bodies (instance-`requires` dict-passing, Phase 83/84 single-level).  ON
-- only for the elaborate / dict path (elaborateDict sets it); OFF for the
-- `=== TYPES ===` golden path (checkProgram) and the multi-module path
-- (elaborateModules) so their scheme output stays byte-identical.  Deliberately
-- NOT reset by resetState — the entrypoint that owns the path sets it.

-- ── D0.5 arg-position dispatch MEASUREMENT (probe-only) ─────────────────────
-- Classify every ARGUMENT-position interface-method occurrence (the bare-EVar
-- arg-tag population D0 counted, upper bound 1065) by the inferred type of its
-- DISCRIMINATING argument: concrete-primitive / concrete-ADT / type-variable.
-- Mirrors recordSite/resolveSites (which stamp RETURN-position sites) but for
-- arg-position sites it MEASURES, never stamps.  An arg-position method is exactly
-- one that reached inferVar (return-position + method-constrained methods were
-- already rewritten to EMethodAt/EDictAt by prePassDict, so they never arrive there).

-- method name → 0-based index of its first argument mentioning the interface
-- typaram (its dispatch position).  Set by the probe before elaboration; absence
-- of a name means "not an arg-position interface method" → not measured.

-- ── D3a arg-position dispatch STAMPING (Native backend, ALL elaboration paths) ──
-- The action analog of the D0.5 measurement: instead of classifying each
-- ARGUMENT-position interface-method occurrence, STAMP a static `RKey <head>` for
-- every one whose discriminating-argument type resolves to a concrete head (the
-- 903 concrete sites = 25 primitive + 878 ADT).  Type-variable occurrences (the
-- 110 D3b sites) stay RNone → the emitter falls back to runtime arg-tag dispatch.
-- Stamping is UNCONDITIONAL: emit, eval, and the `=== TYPES ===` / Core-IR golden +
-- ceval-oracle drivers all elaborate identically — one single elaboration mode
-- (#157).  Measured byte-identical across the 204/204 fixtures, so the unification
-- is representation-neutral, not a behaviour change.

-- the route refs awaiting an arg-position stamp + their discriminating-argument
-- mono (captured during inference, resolved by resolveArgStamps while the tyvar
-- cells are still bound).  Reset per pass by resetState, like pendingSites.
-- D3b-1: also carries the method name + the EMethodAt impl-dicts ref, so a
-- concrete site whose head names a parametric `requires` impl can thread the
-- element dict (resolveArgStamp → argImplDictRoutesFor), exactly as the
-- return-position pendingSites carry implRef for resolveSite.
-- #156 S3a: entries are `PendingEntry`s of kind `SKArg`.

-- the dispatch-index registry: each interface method → index of its first arg
-- mentioning the interface typaram.  Methods with NO such arg (return-position)
-- contribute nothing.  Computed structurally from interface decls.
export argDispatchIndices : List Decl -> List (String, Int)
argDispatchIndices prog = flatMap argDispatchOfDecl prog

argDispatchOfDecl : Decl -> List (String, Int)
argDispatchOfDecl (DAttrib _ d) = argDispatchOfDecl d
argDispatchOfDecl (DInterface { typarams, methods, ... }) =
  flatMap (argDispatchOfMethod typarams) methods
argDispatchOfDecl _ = []

argDispatchOfMethod : List String -> IfaceMethod -> List (String, Int)
argDispatchOfMethod typarams (IfaceMethod mname mty _) = match firstDispatchIdx (dispatchTyparams typarams) 0 (methodArgs mty)
  Some i => [(mname, i)]
  None => []

-- The DISCRIMINATING (receiver) typaram(s) of an interface — its FIRST typaram.
-- A multi-param interface (`FromEntries c e`) dispatches on its head/receiver
-- param `c`; the trailing `e` is a phantom element param that may appear in
-- argument types (`List e`) WITHOUT making the method arg-position dispatched.
-- Classifying by ALL typarams mis-routes `fromEntries : List e -> c` to its `List`
-- argument (an arg-tag site that has no `List` impl) instead of return-position
-- dispatch on `c`.  Single-param interfaces are unaffected (`take 1` is identity).
dispatchTyparams : List String -> List String
dispatchTyparams [] = []
dispatchTyparams (p::_) = [p]

firstDispatchIdx : List String -> Int -> List Ty -> Option Int
firstDispatchIdx _ _ [] = None
firstDispatchIdx typarams i (ty::rest)
  | argMentions typarams ty = Some i
  | otherwise = firstDispatchIdx typarams (i + 1) rest

nthArgMono : Int -> Mono -> Option Mono
nthArgMono 0 m = match normalize m
  TFun a _ _ => Some a
  _ => None
nthArgMono n m = match normalize m
  TFun _ _ b => nthArgMono (n - 1) b
  _ => None

-- D3a: queue an arg-position occurrence's route ref for stamping, keyed on the
-- inferred type of its discriminating argument (the dispatch-position arg mono).
-- The cell is read later (resolveArgStamps), once inference has solved it.  D3b-1:
-- also carry [name] + the impl-dicts ref so a concrete-head site can thread the
-- element dict for a parametric `requires` impl.
recordArgStamp : String -> Ref Route -> Ref (List Route) -> Int -> Mono -> Unit
recordArgStamp name tagRef implRef idx mono
  | toggles.value.suppressArgStamp.value = ()
  | otherwise = match nthArgMono idx mono
    Some am => setRef perRun.value.pendingArgStamps (PendingEntry name tagRef am perRun.value.currentFn.value (SKArg implRef mono) :: perRun.value.pendingArgStamps.value)
    None => ()

-- Cause-A prerequisite: an ARG-position method occurrence's discriminating-arg
-- mono, keyed by the enclosing fn (currentFn), pushed into methodSiteFns — the
-- arg-position analogue of recordSite's return-position push.  inferredConstraintIds
-- reads methodSiteFns to discover the enclosing fn's INFERRED constraint, so this
-- lets `f s = display s` promote `f` to a dict-passed fn (it had no signature, so
-- the constraint is inferred, not declared).  Idx-th arg mono = the receiver whose
-- (constraint) tyvar must be discovered.
recordArgSiteFn : String -> Int -> Mono -> Unit
recordArgSiteFn _ idx mono = match nthArgMono idx mono
  Some am => setRef perRun.value.methodSiteFns (consSiteFn perRun.value.currentFn.value am perRun.value.methodSiteFns.value)
  None => ()

-- D3a/D3b-1: stamp each queued arg-position route from its now-resolved
-- discriminating arg mono.  D3b-1 first: a mono pinned to an impl's `requires`
-- element tyvar (registered in activeDictVars by registerImplRequires) → the
-- element dict param RDict $dict_<method>_<slot> — the ARGUMENT-position analogue
-- of resolveSite's return-position RDict.  Else (D3a): a concrete head
-- (Int/String/Float/… or any ADT tycon) → a static `RKey tag …`, and when that
-- head names a parametric impl WITH a requires, fill the impl-dicts ref so the
-- call threads the element dict (concrete element → RKey Int; a recursive element
-- of the SAME parametric type → RDict forwarding the in-scope dict param).  A
-- still-unsolved free type variable → left RNone (the emitter arg-tag-dispatches).
resolveArgStamps : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> List PendingEntry -> Unit
resolveArgStamps _ _ [] = ()
resolveArgStamps implTable keyTable ((PendingEntry name tagRef am encl kind)::rest) =
  let _ = match kind
    SKArg implRef fullMono =>
      resolveArgStamp implTable keyTable name tagRef implRef am fullMono encl
    _ => ()
  resolveArgStamps implTable keyTable rest

-- #156 S3c: a thin adapter over `entail` (EKArg kind).  `entail` returns
-- `(primaryRoute, elementRoutes)`; stamp them into (tagRef, implRef).  Reproduces the
-- pre-S3c arm-by-arm body: assum → `(RDict dname, [])`, inst → `(RKey key [],
-- argImplDictRoutesForEncl …)`, fallback → `(RNone, [])`.
-- TYPECHECK-AUDIT C7 (arg-position): when ≥2 impls of this method share head tycon [tag]
-- but differ in type ARGS (e.g. `MyPair Int Bool` vs `MyPair Bool Int`), bare-head `RKey
-- tag` narrows to the FIRST impl (eval's hasTag matches the head `t`).  `keyForSite`
-- (inside entailInst) upgrades to the canonical full-type impl key so eval's hasTag
-- matches the right impl's `k`.  No collision → bare head, unchanged.
-- ⚠️ tagRef is NOT always RNone-seeded: rewriteArgScoped (~7438) seeds a standalone-shadow
-- arg site to `RLocal <sym> []`, and resolveRLocalSites (later) leaves an importer-shadow
-- site UNTOUCHED — so the pre-S3c None arm's `()` was load-bearing: it PRESERVED that
-- RLocal seed.  For EKArg `entail` yields RNone ONLY from its fallback (assum→RDict,
-- inst→RKey), so guarding the stamp on RNone reproduces that do-nothing None arm exactly
-- and never clobbers an RLocal seed.  implRef is []-seeded everywhere (every EMethodAt
-- construction — 7313/7355/7356/7438/7441/7443 — uses `(Ref [])`, and only this resolver
-- ever writes an arg-site's implRef), so []-over-[] needs no guard (matches resolveSite).
resolveArgStamp : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> Ref Route -> Ref (List Route) -> Mono -> Mono -> String -> Unit
resolveArgStamp implTable keyTable name tagRef implRef am fullMono encl =
  let (route, routes) = entail implTable name am encl (EKArg keyTable fullMono)
  let _ = match route
    RNone => ()
    _ => setRef tagRef route
  setRef implRef routes

-- Native Gap C (parametric default-method dict-synthesis, 2026-06-12): a BY-NAME
-- relational method (`lt`/`gt`/…) at a parametric Ord head (tuple/`List a`/`Box a`)
-- has NO own impl, so its element `requires` dicts must be keyed off the inner
-- `compare` (whose impl carries them).  emitDefaultDefine eta-prepends matching
-- dict params; emitDefaultRKey forwards these words.  Concrete head / a method
-- with its own impl ⇒ same key as before (empty for non-parametric), unchanged.

-- ── structured typecheck diagnostic (TcDiag) ───────────────────────────────
-- #159: ONE structured record replaces the old flat (code, msg, Option Loc)
-- triple PLUS its parallel message-keyed side channels — `typeErrorHelpFix` for
-- errors, and `matchWarningLocs` / `matchWarningHelp` for the non-exhaustive-match
-- warnings.  Every accumulated diagnostic (errors AND match warnings) now carries
-- its code, severity, span, message, and optional help/fix INLINE, so nothing
-- downstream has to zip parallel lists or look help up by message text.  The
-- observable output is unchanged: `driver/diagnostics.mdk` maps a TcDiag to its
-- `Diag` and thence to the `--json` shape (DIAGNOSTIC-CODES-DESIGN.md), byte for
-- byte as before.  `severity` is an Int (1 = error, 2 = warning — the same codes
-- `--json` emits) because the real `Severity` type lives in `driver/diagnostics.mdk`,
-- which imports THIS module, so importing it back would cycle.  `fix` is a plain
-- (Loc span, replacement) pair, converted to a real `Fix` on the diagnostics side.
public export data TcDiag =
  | TcDiag String Int (Option Loc) String (Option String) (Option (Loc, String))

export tcCode : TcDiag -> String
tcCode (TcDiag c _ _ _ _ _) = c

export tcSeverity : TcDiag -> Int
tcSeverity (TcDiag _ s _ _ _ _) = s

export tcLoc : TcDiag -> Option Loc
tcLoc (TcDiag _ _ l _ _ _) = l

export tcMsg : TcDiag -> String
tcMsg (TcDiag _ _ _ m _ _) = m

export tcHelp : TcDiag -> Option String
tcHelp (TcDiag _ _ _ _ h _) = h

export tcFix : TcDiag -> Option (Loc, String)
tcFix (TcDiag _ _ _ _ _ f) = f

-- ── type-error accumulator ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Unification failures record diagnostics here instead of panicking, so
-- ill-typed programs emit matching `TYPE ERROR: …` lines.  occursCheckFailed
-- is a per-bindVar flag: occursAdjustUnbound sets it so bindVar can skip the
-- actual link (preventing a cyclic union-find entry).
-- B.10.2b: each accumulated error carries the `currentLoc` span in effect at the
-- push site (its `Option Loc`), mirroring lib/typecheck.ml binding `!current_loc`
-- into `Type_error (e, !current_loc)` at the RAISE site rather than reading the
-- global last-entered loc afterwards.  `pushTypeError` captures `currentLoc.value`
-- so locs don't drift as inference continues past the error.  severity = 1.

-- G1 (SOUNDNESS): a STICKY mirror of typeErrors that resetState does NOT clear.
-- The multi-module elaborate path (elaborateModules → checkModuleFullImpl) calls
-- resetState at the start of EACH module, wiping `typeErrors`, so after the whole
-- graph only the LAST module's errors survive there.  build/run must abort on a
-- type error in ANY module, so `pushTypeError` also records into this accumulator,
-- which only `resetTypeErrorsSticky` clears (the driver, once, before a run).  Read
-- via `hadTypeErrors` to gate the CLI exit code BEFORE emit/eval.
typeErrorsSticky : Ref Bool
typeErrorsSticky = Ref False

-- Clear the sticky accumulator.  Call once at the start of a build/run pipeline.
export resetTypeErrorsSticky : Unit -> Unit
resetTypeErrorsSticky _ = setRef typeErrorsSticky False

-- True iff any type error was pushed since the last resetTypeErrorsSticky.
export hadTypeErrors : Unit -> Bool
hadTypeErrors _ = typeErrorsSticky.value

-- Push one type-error message, snapshotting the current ELoc span (B.10.2b).
pushTypeError : String -> String -> Unit
pushTypeError code msg =
  let _ = setRef typeErrorsSticky True
  setRef
    perRun.value.typeErrors
    (TcDiag code 1 currentLoc.value msg None None :: perRun.value.typeErrors.value)

-- Push one type-error message attributed to [loc] rather than the live
-- `currentLoc` (#414).  Same non-deduping semantics as `pushTypeError` — the
-- deduping `pushTypeErrorOnceAt` is for obligation errors, which two checkers can
-- each detect; a coherence conflict is pushed exactly once per run.  `None` falls
-- back to `currentLoc`, i.e. the old behaviour.
pushTypeErrorAt : String -> Option Loc -> String -> Unit
pushTypeErrorAt code loc msg =
  let _ = setRef typeErrorsSticky True
  setRef
    perRun.value.typeErrors
    (TcDiag code 1 (orElseLoc loc currentLoc.value) msg None None :: perRun.value.typeErrors.value)

-- #11 soundness: push an obligation error only if the SAME message isn't already
-- accumulated.  A single missing impl can be detected by both checkImplObligations
-- (the original deferred obligation, once it grounds) and checkCallObligations (the
-- call-site instantiation) — the oracle raises ONE error per missing impl, so dedup
-- by message to keep native == oracle (one TYPE ERROR line).
pushTypeErrorOnce : String -> String -> Unit
pushTypeErrorOnce code msg =
  if anyList (e => tcMsg e == msg) perRun.value.typeErrors.value then
    ()
  else
    pushTypeError code msg

-- B.10.2c: push a deduped type error attributing it to the supplied loc rather
-- than the live `currentLoc` (which is stale during the post-HM obligation
-- check — inference has long since left the offending ELoc).  The loc was
-- snapshotted into the obligation tuple at the record site.  `None` falls back
-- to the live `currentLoc` (the old behaviour) so an obligation that never
-- captured a span is unchanged.
pushTypeErrorOnceAt : String -> Option Loc -> String -> Unit
pushTypeErrorOnceAt code loc msg =
  if anyList (e => tcMsg e == msg) perRun.value.typeErrors.value then ()
  else
    let _ = setRef typeErrorsSticky True
    setRef
      perRun.value.typeErrors
      (TcDiag code 1 (orElseLoc loc currentLoc.value) msg None None :: perRun.value.typeErrors.value)

-- Push a deduped type error (same dedup rule as pushTypeErrorOnceAt) that also
-- carries a human `help` string and an optional machine-applicable `fix`
-- (ERROR-QUALITY dim F).  #159: help/fix now ride INSIDE the TcDiag — no
-- message-keyed side channel.  `Fix`/`Diag` live in driver/diagnostics.mdk (which
-- imports this module), so the fix is carried as a plain (Loc, String)
-- span+replacement pair, converted to a real `Fix` on the diagnostics.mdk side.
pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt : String -> Option Loc -> String -> String -> Option (Loc, String) -> Unit
pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt code loc msg help fix =
  if anyList (e => tcMsg e == msg) perRun.value.typeErrors.value then ()
  else
    let _ = setRef typeErrorsSticky True
    setRef
      perRun.value.typeErrors
      (TcDiag code 1 (orElseLoc loc currentLoc.value) msg (Some help) fix :: perRun.value.typeErrors.value)

-- the first `Some` loc, else the second (so a captured record-site span wins,
-- and a `None` capture still degrades to the live currentLoc).
orElseLoc : Option Loc -> Option Loc -> Option Loc
orElseLoc (Some l) _ = Some l
orElseLoc None fallback = fallback

-- Source-location attribution (Phase B.10.2): the innermost ELoc span the
-- `infer` walk has entered.  Set by infer's transparent ELoc arm before
-- recursing (mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's `current_loc` ref, set in its ELoc
-- arm; cf. `fail e = raise (Type_error (e, !current_loc))`).  Surfaced through
-- Diag / the LSP via `pushTypeError`.  `None` until the first ELoc is entered
-- (top-level decls aren't ELoc-wrapped).
currentLoc : Ref (Option Loc)
currentLoc = Ref None

currentDoOrigin : Ref (Option Loc)
currentDoOrigin = Ref None

-- C8b Gap 2 (diagnostic-quality parity): set to `Some methodName` around the
-- OUTER unify of an interface default body against its declared return type, so a
-- leaf TypeMismatch is reported as the oracle's specialized single line
-- `Method '<name>': expected type <e> but got <a>` (lib/typecheck.ml's
-- MethodTypeMismatch catch, :3904-3907) instead of a bare `Type mismatch: …`.
currentMethodMismatch : Ref (Option String)
currentMethodMismatch = Ref None

-- ASYNC-DESIGN Stage 2 (D5): the entry program's inferred `main` scheme, stashed
-- by elabWorker on the EVAL path so the native `run` driver can detect a
-- `main : Async _` and route it through `runAsync` (perform its row) instead of
-- forcing it to an inert Async value.  Last module wins (the entry is last in
-- dependency order), matching bin/main.ml's `final_schemes` lookup.  `None` until
-- a module binding `main` is elaborated; reset at the top of elaborateModules so a
-- prior run can't leak.  Type-level only → no emitted IR changes → fixpoint-safe.

-- Head type-constructor of a (possibly applied) mono: walk the TApp left spine to
-- the constructor.  `Async e Unit` → Some "Async".
monoHeadCon : Mono -> Option String
monoHeadCon m = match normalize m
  TCon n => Some n
  TApp a _ => monoHeadCon a
  _ => None

-- Stage 2 query: did the last-elaborated `main` infer to an `Async`-headed type?
export mainTypeIsAsync : Unit -> Bool
mainTypeIsAsync _ = match driverState.value.mainSchemeRef.value
  Some (Forall _ _ t) => monoHeadCon t == Some "Async"
  None => False

-- Stage 3 query: did the last-elaborated `main` infer to a bare Unit return type
-- (with effects stripped at each arrow, mono resolves to TCon "Unit")?  Used by
-- the LLVM emitter to suppress the auto-print of Unit-returning mains even when no
-- explicit `main : <IO> Unit` annotation is present — e.g. `main = applyEff f 42`
-- where `applyEff : (a -> <IO> b) -> a -> <IO> b` and `f : Int -> <IO> Unit`.
export mainTypeIsUnit : Unit -> Bool
mainTypeIsUnit _ = match driverState.value.mainSchemeRef.value
  Some (Forall _ _ t) => match normalize t
    TCon "Unit" => True
    _ => False
  None => False

-- Stage 4 query: did the last-elaborated `main` infer to a bare Float return type?
-- Used by the LLVM/WasmGC emitters to render a bare Float value main correctly
-- even when the structural `mainKind`/`refMainKind` can't see through a polymorphic
-- HOF's return type (e.g. `main = fold (acc x => acc + x) 0.0 [1.0, 2.0, 3.0]`).
export mainTypeIsFloat : Unit -> Bool
mainTypeIsFloat _ = match driverState.value.mainSchemeRef.value
  Some (Forall _ _ t) => match normalize t
    TCon "Float" => True
    _ => False
  None => False

-- ── non-exhaustive-match warning accumulator (the type-aware `check_match`) ──
-- `inferMatch` pushes a warning here for every `match` whose non-guarded arms
-- don't cover the scrutinee's type.  `matchOracle` is the ctor oracle the matrix
-- recursion consults; the `checkMatchToLines` driver sets it from the program's
-- data decls (so it sees the same user + builtin ctors check_match does) before
-- inference runs.  Neither is touched by `resetState` — the driver seeds them and
-- reads `matchWarnings` after the typecheck pass; runs that ignore match warnings
-- (the schemes/selfproc paths) simply never read it.
-- #159: element type is now `TcDiag` (severity 2), carrying the span (formerly the
-- parallel `matchWarningLocs`) and the actionable-fix help (formerly the message-
-- keyed `matchWarningHelp`) INLINE — no parallel lists, no lookup-by-message.  The
-- `TcDiag.msg` holds the FULL "Warning: …"-prefixed string exactly as the old
-- `List String` did, so the text-rendering paths (checkMatchToLines,
-- checkToLinesWithRuntime, checkModulesEntryReport) stay byte-identical; the
-- `W-*` code and the prefix strip happen on the driver/diagnostics.mdk side.

-- True iff the last typecheck pass pushed any match warning.  Read by the snapshot
-- runner (`tools/snapshot.mdk`) to decide whether a rendered `# TYPES` section carries
-- diagnostic prose — a `W-*` warning is graded by `compiler/ERROR-QUALITY.md` exactly
-- like an error is, so a `# TYPES` section holding one is NOT `--bless`-able.  Keyed on
-- the accumulator rather than sniffing the rendered text: the renderer must not have to
-- guess which of its own lines are diagnostics.
export hadMatchWarnings : Unit -> Bool
hadMatchWarnings _ = match driverState.value.matchWarnings.value
  [] => False
  _ => True

-- #158 PR3: the ~58 per-RUN accumulators/counters that `resetState` clears are bundled
-- into ONE typed record so their reset lifecycle is a TYPE FACT, not a hand-maintained
-- block.  `freshPerRun` constructs exactly the field-Refs `resetState` installs, so "what
-- resetState clears" == "what PerRun holds" — enforced by the constructor: a new per-module
-- accumulator cannot be added without appearing in freshPerRun (and thus the reset).  This
-- is a record OF Refs (fields stay `Ref …`), NOT a record of values: the hot accumulators
-- (pendingSites, typeErrors, pendingCallObligations, activeDictVars, …) are pushed with
-- `setRef x (v :: x.value)`, so the field must be a Ref for that to stay one Ref-write — a
-- record of values would rebuild the whole 58-field record on every push.
-- (#158 PR4 extracted shadowHeadCtxRef OUT of here into `Toggles`; its resetState clearing is
-- preserved by freshToggles being minted in the same resetState — see resetState below.)
data PerRun =
  | PerRun {
      tyvarCounter : Ref Int,
      effvarCounter : Ref Int,
      curEffect : Ref EffRow,
      recordsRef : Ref (List (String, RecordInfo)),
      recordByNameRef : Ref (OrdMap RecordInfo),
      fieldOwnersRef : Ref (OrdMap (List String)),
      dataParamKindsRef : Ref (List (String, List Kind)),
      aliasTableRef : Ref (List (String, (List String, Ty))),
      pendingSites : Ref (List PendingEntry),
      pendingRLocalSites : Ref (List PendingEntry),
      shadowStandaloneSchemesRef : Ref (List (String, Scheme)),
      definerShadowNamesRef : Ref (List String),
      definerShadowSigsRef : Ref (List (String, Ty)),
      shadowKeyTableRef : Ref (OrdMap (List KeyEntry)),
      pendingBinopSites : Ref (List PendingEntry),
      pendingUnopSites : Ref (List PendingEntry),
      pendingArithSites : Ref (List (Ref Route, Mono)),
      methodIfaceParamsRef : Ref (OrdMap (String, List String, Ty)),
      registeredIfacesRef : Ref (OrdMap Unit),
      pendingImplObligations : Ref (List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc)),
      pendingImplObligationsN : Ref Int,
      numlitRefs : Ref (List (Mono, Ref (Option Float), Int, Ref Route)),
      poisonedVars : Ref (List Int),
      tupleCallCandidates : Ref (List (Mono, String, Option (Loc, String))),
      numlitVarLocs : Ref (List (Mono, Loc)),
      numlitUntainted : Ref (List (Mono, Loc)),
      numlitOpLocs : Ref (List Loc),
      numlitCtxTags : Ref (List (Loc, String)),
      localBindRefs : Ref (List (String, Mono)),
      localSchemesOut : Ref (List (String, Scheme)),
      seedSchemesOut : Ref (List (String, Scheme)),
      pendingArgStamps : Ref (List PendingEntry),
      activeDictVars : Ref (List (Int, String)),
      activeDictPreds : Ref (List (String, Mono, String)),
      funConstraintsRef : Ref (List (String, List Int)),
      funConstraintIfacesRef : Ref (List (String, List String)),
      currentImportDefinersRef : Ref (List (String, String)),
      currentImportOriginsRef : Ref (List (String, String)),
      pendingDictApps : Ref (List (Ref (List Route), List Mono, List String)),
      pendingDictAppsN : Ref Int,
      pendingCallObligations : Ref (List (String, List Mono, Option Loc)),
      pendingCallObligationsN : Ref Int,
      schemeObligationsRef : Ref (List (String, List (String, List Int))),
      methodConstraintsRef : Ref (List (String, List Int)),
      methodConstraintPositionsRef : Ref (List (String, List Int)),
      pendingMethodDicts : Ref (List (Ref (List Route), List Mono)),
      pendingRecDictApps : Ref (List RecDictApp),
      currentFn : Ref String,
      currentImplBody : Ref Bool,
      methodSiteFns : Ref (OrdMap (List Mono)),
      dictAppFns : Ref (OrdMap (List (List Mono))),
      promotedRef : Ref (List String),
      methodShadowNamesRef : Ref (List String),
      flatShadowScopingRef : Ref Bool,
      flatCoreFnNamesRef : Ref (OrdMap Unit),
      flatUserShadowNamesRef : Ref (List String),
      groupConstraintMonosRef : Ref (List (String, Mono)),
      typeErrors : Ref (List TcDiag),
      occursCheckFailed : Ref Bool,
      currentLevel : Ref Int,
    }

-- Construct all 58 field-Refs fresh (each reproduces its former top-level ref's initial).
freshPerRun : Unit -> PerRun
freshPerRun _ = PerRun {
  tyvarCounter = Ref 0,
  effvarCounter = Ref 0,
  curEffect = Ref (EffRow [] None),
  recordsRef = Ref [],
  recordByNameRef = Ref omEmpty,
  fieldOwnersRef = Ref omEmpty,
  dataParamKindsRef = Ref [],
  aliasTableRef = Ref [],
  pendingSites = Ref [],
  pendingRLocalSites = Ref [],
  shadowStandaloneSchemesRef = Ref [],
  definerShadowNamesRef = Ref [],
  definerShadowSigsRef = Ref [],
  shadowKeyTableRef = Ref omEmpty,
  pendingBinopSites = Ref [],
  pendingUnopSites = Ref [],
  pendingArithSites = Ref [],
  methodIfaceParamsRef = Ref omEmpty,
  registeredIfacesRef = Ref omEmpty,
  pendingImplObligations = Ref [],
  pendingImplObligationsN = Ref 0,
  numlitRefs = Ref [],
  poisonedVars = Ref [],
  tupleCallCandidates = Ref [],
  numlitVarLocs = Ref [],
  numlitUntainted = Ref [],
  numlitOpLocs = Ref [],
  numlitCtxTags = Ref [],
  localBindRefs = Ref [],
  localSchemesOut = Ref [],
  seedSchemesOut = Ref [],
  pendingArgStamps = Ref [],
  activeDictVars = Ref [],
  activeDictPreds = Ref [],
  funConstraintsRef = Ref [],
  funConstraintIfacesRef = Ref [],
  currentImportDefinersRef = Ref [],
  currentImportOriginsRef = Ref [],
  pendingDictApps = Ref [],
  pendingDictAppsN = Ref 0,
  pendingCallObligations = Ref [],
  pendingCallObligationsN = Ref 0,
  schemeObligationsRef = Ref [],
  methodConstraintsRef = Ref [],
  methodConstraintPositionsRef = Ref [],
  pendingMethodDicts = Ref [],
  pendingRecDictApps = Ref [],
  currentFn = Ref "",
  currentImplBody = Ref False,
  methodSiteFns = Ref omEmpty,
  dictAppFns = Ref omEmpty,
  promotedRef = Ref [],
  methodShadowNamesRef = Ref [],
  flatShadowScopingRef = Ref False,
  flatCoreFnNamesRef = Ref omEmpty,
  flatUserShadowNamesRef = Ref [],
  groupConstraintMonosRef = Ref [],
  typeErrors = Ref [],
  occursCheckFailed = Ref False,
  currentLevel = Ref 0,
}

-- The single module-level cell holding the current run's per-module accumulator bundle.
perRun : Ref PerRun
perRun = Ref (freshPerRun ())

-- Replacing `perRun` with a fresh bundle IS the per-module reset: freshPerRun re-mints
-- every field, so the set that resets == the set in PerRun, enforced by the constructor.
-- #80/#201: resetState does NOT (re-)seed effect domains.  effectDomains is now owned by
-- populateEffectDomains, called ONCE per compile over the WHOLE import graph
-- (checkProgramSeededSplit for the flat path; checkModulesPreamble / elaborateModules driver
-- preambles for the multi-module paths).  A per-module re-seed here would wipe module A's
-- declared effect domains before module B is checked — a module using an effect declared in A
-- would then see only builtins.  effectDomains has exactly ONE writer (populateEffectDomains →
-- seedEffectDomains + registerEffectDomain), so it persists untouched across the per-module
-- resets — and it is NOT a PerRun field, so freshPerRun cannot disturb it.
-- #158 PR4: mint a fresh Toggles in the SAME reset that mints freshPerRun, so
-- shadowHeadCtxRef (formerly a PerRun field) is still cleared per-module at exactly this
-- point.  The other two toggles are always False at a module boundary (their save/restore
-- brackets never straddle one), so re-minting them here is a no-op — byte-identical.
resetState : Unit -> Unit
resetState _ =
  let _ = setRef perRun (freshPerRun ())
  setRef toggles (freshToggles ())

-- ── small helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
lookupAssocI : Int -> List (Int, b) -> Option b
lookupAssocI _ [] = None
lookupAssocI k ((k2, v)::rest)
  | k == k2 = Some v
  | otherwise = lookupAssocI k rest

containsI : Int -> List Int -> Bool
containsI _ [] = False
containsI x (y::ys) = x == y || containsI x ys

-- `support/util`'s `anyList` takes a PURE predicate, so it cannot carry the
-- path-compressing (hence `<Mut>`) mono predicates below — `monoIsFunction`,
-- `numUnimplementableHead`, … all normalize.  Same short-circuiting `||` fold, one
-- effect row wider.  (Medaka has no effect-polymorphic HOFs, so this must be a
-- second definition rather than a reuse of `anyList`.)
anyListM : (a -> Bool) -> List a -> Bool
anyListM _ [] = False
anyListM p (x::xs) = p x || anyListM p xs

-- ── union-find core ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- PERF (issue #115): this is a union-find FIND, and it PATH-COMPRESSES.  Without
-- compression it was the compiler's worst performance defect: `unifyVars` links the
-- current root to the *fresh* var it meets (`bindVar c1 (TVar c2)`), so inferring a
-- single large declaration — an N-arm `match` whose arms all unify against one
-- result var, an N-element list literal — builds a Link chain of length N.  Every
-- later `normalize`/`rootIdOf` of any var in that class then walked O(N) links, and
-- the post-inference walkers (`walkDispatch`, `setNumlitFloatsGo`, `monoUnboundIds`)
-- plus the Chunk-D numlit scans do that once per literal ⇒ O(N²) link steps, on a
-- non-allocating hot loop that no allocation-graded gate could see.  Compression
-- makes it amortized near-O(1) and leaves the ROOT IDENTITY untouched — this is a
-- representation change only, so no program's inferred type moves.
--
-- Returns the ARGUMENT ITSELF (never a rebuilt `TVar`) when already at a root, so
-- the overwhelmingly common case allocates nothing — the old multi-clause form's
-- `Unbound _ _ => TVar cell` rebuilt a cell on every call.  That is why this matches
-- on the whole parameter instead of destructuring it in the head: a multi-clause
-- definition cannot name the value it matched, so it cannot give it back unchanged.
export normalize : Mono -> Mono
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-match-on-param
normalize m = match m
  TVar cell => match cell.value
    Link t => normalizeLink cell t
    Unbound _ _ => m
  _ => m

-- [cell] is a `Link` to [t]: resolve [t] to its root, re-point [cell] STRAIGHT at
-- that root, and return it.  The write happens only when the chain is longer than
-- one hop — a direct link is already compressed, so re-writing it would be pure cost.
-- Matches on the whole [t] for the same no-rebuild reason as `normalize` above.
normalizeLink : Ref Tyvar -> Mono -> Mono
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-match-on-param
normalizeLink cell t = match t
  TVar c2 => match c2.value
    Unbound _ _ => t
    Link t2 =>
      let r = normalizeLink c2 t2
      let _ = setRef cell (Link r)
      r
  _ => t

tyvarId : Ref Tyvar -> Int
tyvarId cell = match cell.value
  Unbound id _ => id
  Link _ => 0 - 1

tyvarLevel : Ref Tyvar -> Int
tyvarLevel cell = match cell.value
  Unbound _ lvl => lvl
  Link _ => 0

-- occurs-check + lower the levels of vars in [t] to <= [level] (Phase: Rémy levels)
occursAdjust : Int -> Int -> Mono -> Unit
occursAdjust id level t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => occursAdjustVar id level cell
  TCon _ => ()
  TApp a b =>
    let _ = occursAdjust id level a
    occursAdjust id level b
  TFun a r b =>
    let _ = occursAdjust id level a
    let _ = lowerRowLevels level r
    occursAdjust id level b
  TEff r => lowerRowLevels level r

occursAdjustVar : Int -> Int -> Ref Tyvar -> Unit
occursAdjustVar id level cell = match cell.value
  Unbound id2 lvl2 => occursAdjustUnbound id level cell id2 lvl2
  Link t => occursAdjust id level t

occursAdjustUnbound : Int -> Int -> Ref Tyvar -> Int -> Int -> Unit
occursAdjustUnbound id level cell id2 lvl2
  | id2 == id =
    let _ = setRef perRun.value.occursCheckFailed True
    pushTypeError
      "T-INFINITE-TYPE"
      ("Cannot construct infinite type involving " ++ ppMono (TVar cell))
  | lvl2 > level = setRef cell (Unbound id2 level)
  | otherwise = ()

unify : Mono -> Mono -> Unit
unify a b = unifyN (normalize a) (normalize b)

unifyN : Mono -> Mono -> Unit
unifyN (TVar c1) (TVar c2) = unifyVars c1 c2
unifyN (TVar c) t = bindVar c t
unifyN t (TVar c) = bindVar c t
unifyN (TCon a) (TCon b) = if a == b then () else typeMismatch (TCon a) (TCon b)
-- A tuple TYPE is now a `__tupleN__`-headed `TApp` spine; when it clashes with a
-- differing-arity tuple or with a concrete non-tuple head, report the FULL types
-- (which render `(a, b)` via ppGo) instead of letting the generic `TApp~TApp`
-- recursion bottom out on a partial sub-spine — preserving the pre-refactor
-- tuple mismatch wording.  Guard is False when the other side is a TVar-headed
-- spine (so `p a b` unifying with a tuple still flows through the generic arm).
unifyN t1 t2
  | tupleUnifyClash t1 t2 = typeMismatch t1 t2
unifyN (TApp a1 b1) (TApp a2 b2) =
  let _ = unify a1 a2
  unify b1 b2
unifyN (TFun a1 r1 b1) (TFun a2 r2 b2) =
  let _ = unify a1 a2
  let _ = unifyRow r1 r2
  unify b1 b2
unifyN (TEff r1) (TEff r2) = unifyRow r1 r2
unifyN a b = typeMismatch a b

unifyVars : Ref Tyvar -> Ref Tyvar -> Unit
unifyVars c1 c2 = if tyvarId c1 == tyvarId c2 then () else bindVar c1 (TVar c2)

bindVar : Ref Tyvar -> Mono -> Unit
bindVar cell t =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.occursCheckFailed False
  let _ = occursAdjust (tyvarId cell) (tyvarLevel cell) t
  if perRun.value.occursCheckFailed.value then () else setRef cell (Link t)

-- Chunk A: record the union-find roots of every free var on either side of a
-- unification failure so a later obligation check can suppress a secondary
-- `ambiguous instance` cascade on the same (already-errored) var.
poisonMismatchVars : Mono -> Mono -> Unit
poisonMismatchVars a b =
  setRef
    perRun.value.poisonedVars
    (monoUnboundIds a ++ monoUnboundIds b ++ perRun.value.poisonedVars.value)

typeMismatch : Mono -> Mono -> Unit
typeMismatch a b =
  let _ = poisonMismatchVars a b
  typeMismatchReport a b

typeMismatchReport : Mono -> Mono -> Unit
typeMismatchReport a b = match currentMethodMismatch.value
  Some mname => pushTypeError "T-METHOD-MISMATCH" "Method '\{mname}': expected type \{ppMono a} but got \{ppMono b}"
  None => match currentDoOrigin.value
    Some doLoc => typeMismatchInDo a b doLoc
    None => match firstTupleCallHint [a, b]
      -- P0-16: one side is a tuple-call residual (e.g. `z : Int; z = add(1, 2)`
      -- → `Type mismatch: Int vs (a, b) -> (a, b)`) — append the call-syntax hint.
      Some (help, fix) =>
        pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt "T-TYPE-MISMATCH" currentLoc.value "Type mismatch: \{ppMono a} vs \{ppMono b} — \{help}" help fix
      None => pushTypeError "T-TYPE-MISMATCH" "Type mismatch: \{ppMono a} vs \{ppMono b}"

-- a mismatch inside a do-lowered chain: if it is the monad-shape (`m b`) vs
-- non-monad obligation, retarget to the tailored "do requires a monad" error;
-- otherwise fall back to the plain mismatch (the loc stays the do-block's).
typeMismatchInDo : Mono -> Mono -> Loc -> Unit
typeMismatchInDo a b doLoc
  | monoIsApp (normalize a) != monoIsApp (normalize b) =
    recordDoMonadError doLoc
  | otherwise =
    pushTypeError "T-TYPE-MISMATCH" "Type mismatch: \{ppMono a} vs \{ppMono b}"

recordDoMonadError : Loc -> Unit
recordDoMonadError doLoc =
  let _ = setRef currentDoOrigin None
  let _ = setRef typeErrorsSticky True
  setRef
    perRun.value.typeErrors
    (TcDiag "T-DO-NOT-MONAD" 1 (Some doLoc) doRequiresMonadMsg None None :: perRun.value.typeErrors.value)

monoIsApp : Mono -> Bool
monoIsApp (TApp _ _) = True
monoIsApp _ = False

doRequiresMonadMsg : String
doRequiresMonadMsg = "`do` requires a Thenable impl (e.g. `Option`/`Result`). For IO sequencing use a bare indented block, not `do`"

-- SYMMETRIC complement of doRequiresMonadMsg: a `<-` bind reached a bare
-- (non-`do`) block.  `<-` is monadic-`do`-only; a bare indented block is IO
-- sequencing and does not allow it (language-design.md; "IO is not a monad").
bindOutsideDoMsg : String
bindOutsideDoMsg = "`<-` bind is only valid inside a `do` block. For IO sequencing use a bare indented block without `<-`"

-- ── generalization & instantiation ────────────────────────────────────────
generalize : Mono -> Scheme
generalize t =
  Forall
    (dedupI (freeGenVars perRun.value.currentLevel.value t))
    (dedupI (freeEffvars t))
    t

-- Generalize each captured local binding's (now-solved) mono into a Scheme for
-- the LSP hover fallback (see localBindRefs).  Runs at the end of inference when
-- currentLevel is back at the top, so a still-free local var quantifies cleanly
-- to `a`; a ground var (e.g. a `let` bound to a String) renders concretely.
mapLocalSchemes : List (String, Mono) -> List (String, Scheme)
mapLocalSchemes [] = []
mapLocalSchemes ((n, m)::rest) = (n, generalize m) :: mapLocalSchemes rest

-- ── value restriction (T1) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- A binding RHS is *non-expansive* (a syntactic value) iff it cannot allocate a
-- mutable cell: literals, variables and lambdas are values; annotations are
-- transparent; tuples/lists are values iff their elements are.  Constructor
-- applications of non-expansive arguments are non-expansive (SML value
-- restriction relaxation, Phase 66 extension) EXCEPT `Ref`, which is the sole
-- uppercase constructor that builds a mutable cell (`extern Ref : a -> Ref a` in
-- stdlib/runtime.mdk).  Generalizing `Ref x` would be unsound (polymorphic
-- reference: write at one type, read at another).  ANY future uppercase
-- mutable-cell extern MUST be added to the exclusion in `isCtorAppHead` below
-- — audit with `grep '^extern [A-Z]' stdlib/runtime.mdk` to find candidates.
-- Record creates are non-expansive iff all field expressions are.
-- Note: this ignores the `mut` flag (like the oracle), so `let mut x = []`
-- generalizes — the separate `mut`-gen hole is a both-sides follow-up (T1b).
isNonexpansive : Expr -> Bool
isNonexpansive (ELoc _ e) = isNonexpansive e
isNonexpansive (EDoOrigin _ e) = isNonexpansive e
isNonexpansive (ELit _) = True
isNonexpansive (EVar _) = True
isNonexpansive (ELam _ _) = True
isNonexpansive (EAnnot e _) = isNonexpansive e
isNonexpansive (EHeadAnnot e _) = isNonexpansive e
isNonexpansive (ETuple es) = allList isNonexpansive es
isNonexpansive (EListLit es) = allList isNonexpansive es
-- Constructor application: non-expansive iff the spine head is an
-- uppercase-initial EVar (a data/record constructor) that is NOT `Ref`, and
-- every spine argument is itself non-expansive.  Partial ctor applications
-- (e.g. `MkTwo []`) are also non-expansive — they are closures, not mutable
-- cells.  `isCtorAppHead` strips ELoc/EDoOrigin wrappers before testing.
isNonexpansive (EApp f x) = isCtorAppHead (EApp f x) && isNonexpansive x
-- Record creation: non-expansive iff every field expression is non-expansive.
isNonexpansive (ERecordCreate _ fs) = allList
  (fa => match fa
    FieldAssign _ e => isNonexpansive e)
  fs
isNonexpansive _ = False

-- Walk the left spine of an application to its head and test whether the
-- head is a data/record constructor (uppercase-initial EVar) that is safe to
-- generalize.  `Ref` is excluded because it is the only uppercase extern that
-- builds a mutable cell — see the comment on `isNonexpansive` above.
isCtorAppHead : Expr -> Bool
isCtorAppHead (ELoc _ e) = isCtorAppHead e
isCtorAppHead (EDoOrigin _ e) = isCtorAppHead e
isCtorAppHead (EApp f _) = isCtorAppHead f
isCtorAppHead (EVar name) = name != "Ref" && ctorHeadIsUpper name
isCtorAppHead _ = False

-- True iff the first character of a name is an ASCII uppercase letter,
-- i.e. the name looks like a data constructor or type name.
ctorHeadIsUpper : String -> Bool
ctorHeadIsUpper name =
  let cs = stringToChars name
  arrayLength cs > 0 && isUpper (arrayGetUnsafe 0 cs)

-- Lower every unbound var in [t] deeper than the current level down to the
-- current level, so a value-restricted (non-generalized) binding's vars can't be
-- picked up by an enclosing let's generalize.  Mirrors the lowering `unify`
-- performs via occursAdjust in the non-PVar pattern path (oracle
-- `lib/typecheck.ml:555-565`).
lowerToCurrent : Mono -> Unit
lowerToCurrent t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => match cell.value
    Unbound id lvl =>
      if lvl > perRun.value.currentLevel.value then
        setRef cell (Unbound id perRun.value.currentLevel.value)
      else
        ()
    Link t2 => lowerToCurrent t2
  TCon _ => ()
  TApp a b =>
    let _ = lowerToCurrent a
    lowerToCurrent b
  TFun a r b =>
    let _ = lowerToCurrent a
    let _ = lowerRowLevels perRun.value.currentLevel.value r
    lowerToCurrent b
  TEff r =>
    let _ = lowerRowLevels perRun.value.currentLevel.value r
    ()

-- Generalize a value RHS; value-restrict (monomorphize, lowering free vars)
-- otherwise.  monoScheme leaves the mono ungeneralized.  Oracle
-- `lib/typecheck.ml:569-571`.
genRestricted : Bool -> Mono -> Scheme
genRestricted isValue t
  | isValue = generalize t
  | otherwise =
    let _ = lowerToCurrent t
    monoScheme t

freeGenVars : Int -> Mono -> List Int
freeGenVars lvl t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => if tyvarLevel cell > lvl then [tyvarId cell] else []
  TCon _ => []
  TApp a b => freeGenVars lvl a ++ freeGenVars lvl b
  TFun a _ b => freeGenVars lvl a ++ freeGenVars lvl b
  TEff _ => []

dedupI : List Int -> List Int
dedupI xs = dedupIGo xs []

dedupIGo : List Int -> List Int -> List Int
dedupIGo [] _ = []
dedupIGo (x::xs) seen
  | containsI x seen = dedupIGo xs seen
  | otherwise = x :: dedupIGo xs (x::seen)

instantiate : Scheme -> Mono
instantiate (Forall ids evars t) =
  substMonoP True (freshSubst ids) (freshEffSubst evars) t

freshSubst : List Int -> List (Int, Mono)
freshSubst [] = []
freshSubst (id::rest) = (id, freshVar ()) :: freshSubst rest

freshEffSubst : List Int -> List (Int, Ref Effvar)
freshEffSubst [] = []
freshEffSubst (id::rest) = (id, freshEffvar ()) :: freshEffSubst rest

substMono : List (Int, Mono) -> List (Int, Ref Effvar) -> Mono -> Mono
substMono subst esub t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => fromOption (TVar cell) (lookupAssocI (tyvarId cell) subst)
  TCon n => TCon n
  TApp a b => TApp (substMono subst esub a) (substMono subst esub b)
  TFun a eff b =>
    TFun (substMono subst esub a) (substRow esub eff) (substMono subst esub b)
  TEff r => TEff (substRow esub r)

-- Phase 146 variance-aware re-open: when instantiating a generalized scheme,
-- re-open a CLOSED row carrying concrete labels (`EffRow labels None`) at a
-- COVARIANT (positive) position — an arrow the value itself produces.  This turns
-- a concrete effectful value (`putStrLn : String -> <IO> Unit`) meeting a pure
-- closed bound into the open-row subset check, rejecting the laundering escape.
-- Contravariant rows (a parameter / ctor field the scheme accepts) stay closed so
-- a legitimately pure argument in an effect-allowing slot still subsumes.  Pure
-- (label-less) rows are never re-opened — nothing to leak.
reopenRow : Bool -> EffRow -> EffRow
reopenRow pos r = reopenRowN pos (effrowNorm r)

reopenRowN : Bool -> EffRow -> EffRow
reopenRowN True (EffRow (l::ls) None) = EffRow (l::ls) (Some (freshEffvar ()))
reopenRowN _ r = r

-- positional twin of substMono used by scheme instantiation: flips variance on
-- the arrow domain and re-opens covariant closed-with-labels rows (start pos=True)
substMonoP : Bool -> List (Int, Mono) -> List (Int, Ref Effvar) -> Mono -> Mono
substMonoP pos subst esub t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => fromOption (TVar cell) (lookupAssocI (tyvarId cell) subst)
  TCon n => TCon n
  TApp a b => TApp (substMonoP pos subst esub a) (substMonoP pos subst esub b)
  TFun a eff b => TFun (substMonoP (not pos) subst esub a) (reopenRow pos (substRow esub eff)) (substMonoP pos subst esub b)
  TEff r => TEff (substRow esub r)

-- ── rendering (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml pp_mono byte-for-byte) ─────────────
export ppScheme : Scheme -> String
ppScheme (Forall _ _ t) = ppMono t

-- Render a scheme WITH its constraint context (`Num a => a -> a -> a`).  The
-- constraint tyvar ids are threaded through the SAME ctx/cnt that renders the
-- body, so a constraint's letter matches the body's (id → letter shared).  The
-- body is rendered FIRST so ctx already carries the body's letter assignments
-- when the constraints reuse it via nameOf.  Constraints are rendered+sorted+
-- deduped for a stable display order.  Empty list ⇒ plain ppScheme (no `=>`).
-- Single-vs-multiple form mirrors ppConstrDoc in tools/doc.mdk (`Num a =>` /
-- `(Num a, Ord b) =>`).
export ppSchemeCon : List (String, List Int) -> Scheme -> String
ppSchemeCon [] s = ppScheme s
ppSchemeCon cons (Forall _ _ t) =
  let ctx = Ref []
  let cnt = Ref 0
  let body = ppGo ctx cnt 0 t
  let rendered = sortUniqS (renderConstraintCtx ctx cnt cons)
  let ctxStr = match rendered
    [c] => c
    _ => "(" ++ joinWith ", " rendered ++ ")"
  "\{ctxStr} => \{body}"

-- a predicate renders with its WHOLE argument vector — `Ix a i =>`, not the
-- `(Ix a, Ix i) =>` two-1-ary-facts rendering the shattered representation produced
-- before #607.
renderConstraintCtx : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> List (String, List Int) -> List String
renderConstraintCtx _ _ [] = []
renderConstraintCtx ctx cnt ((iface, ids)::rest) =
  let s = joinWith " " (iface :: map (id => nameOf ctx cnt id) ids)
  s :: renderConstraintCtx ctx cnt rest

-- Render a binding's scheme WITH its inferred/declared constraint context, looked
-- up by NAME from schemeObligationsRef (the always-on obligation side-channel the
-- most-recent typecheck left populated).  A name with no recorded obligations
-- renders as plain ppScheme.  Used by schemeLines (`check --types`) and the LSP
-- hover/completion/inlay renderers so all display surfaces show `Num a => …`.
export ppSchemeNamed : String -> Scheme -> String
ppSchemeNamed n s =
  ppSchemeCon
    (fromOption [] (lookupAssocS2 n perRun.value.schemeObligationsRef.value))
    s

ppMono : Mono -> String
ppMono t =
  let ctx = Ref []
  let cnt = Ref 0
  ppGo ctx cnt 0 t

letters : String
letters = "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"

nameOf : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> Int -> String
nameOf ctx cnt id = match lookupAssocI id ctx.value
  Some s => s
  None => assignName ctx cnt id

assignName : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> Int -> String
assignName ctx cnt id =
  let n = cnt.value
  let _ = setRef cnt (n + 1)
  let s = if n < 26 then stringSlice n (n + 1) letters else "t" ++ intToString n
  let _ = setRef ctx ((id, s)::ctx.value)
  s

ppGo : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> Int -> Mono -> String
ppGo ctx cnt prec t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => ppVar ctx cnt cell
  TCon n => ppConName n
  TApp a b => ppApp ctx cnt prec a b
  TFun a eff b => ppFun ctx cnt prec a eff b
  TEff r => ppEffArg ctx cnt r

-- Render a row sitting in type-argument position (the `e` of `Async e a`).  Bare
-- open tail → the row var's name (reads `Async e a`); labels → `<IO>`/`<IO | e>`;
-- closed empty → `<>`.  Effvar ids are offset into a private letter namespace so
-- they never collide with tyvar letters.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml pp_mono TEff.
ppEffArg : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> EffRow -> String
ppEffArg ctx cnt r = match effrowNorm r
  EffRow labels tail =>
    let lbl = match labels
      [] => ""
      ls => renderAtoms ls
    let tailS = match tail
      Some cell => match cell.value
        EUnbound id _ => nameOf ctx cnt (id + 1000000)
        ELink _ => ""
      None => ""
    ppEffArgFmt lbl tailS

ppEffArgFmt : String -> String -> String
ppEffArgFmt l t
  | l == "" && t == "" = "<>"
  | l == "" = t
  | t == "" = "<" ++ l ++ ">"
  | otherwise = "<\{l} | \{t}>"

ppVar : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> Ref Tyvar -> String
ppVar ctx cnt cell = match cell.value
  Unbound id _ => nameOf ctx cnt id
  Link _ => "_"

ppApp : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> Int -> Mono -> Mono -> String
ppApp ctx cnt prec a b = match tupleSpine (TApp a b)
