-- receiver-directed (Phase 72), like inferFieldAccess: infer base first, then
-- pick the owning record by the base's head tycon, falling back to the first
-- update field's owners.
inferRecordUpdateField : TcEnv -> Expr -> List FieldAssign -> String -> Ref String -> Mono
inferRecordUpdateField env base fields fn r =
  let bt = normalize (infer env base)
  match resolveFieldRecord bt fn
    None => freshVar ()
    Some (rname, ri) =>
      -- stamp the RESOLVED receiver record onto the node (bug #38): both emitters
      -- read it to pick the record by name, instead of guessing from the first
      -- update label (which picks the wrong record — and so the wrong slot — when
      -- two records share a field name at different indices).
      let _ = setRef r rname
      inferRecordUpdatePicked rname bt env fields ri

inferRecordUpdatePicked : String -> Mono -> TcEnv -> List FieldAssign -> RecordInfo -> Mono
inferRecordUpdatePicked rname bt env fields ri =
  let ir = instantiateRecord ri
  let _ = unify bt (fst ir)
  let _ = unifyFieldAssigns env rname (snd ir) fields
  fst ir

inferRecordUpdateWith : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> List FieldAssign -> RecordInfo -> Mono
inferRecordUpdateWith env rname base fields ri =
  let ir = instantiateRecord ri
  let _ = unify (infer env base) (fst ir)
  let _ = unifyFieldAssigns env rname (snd ir) fields
  fst ir

-- operators infer by SHAPE (the typeclass constraint they carry — Num/Eq/… — is
-- tracked at the value level but never rendered in a scheme, so it's irrelevant
-- to the === TYPES === output; numeric literals are Int, not polymorphic)
inferBinopE : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Ref Route -> Mono
inferBinopE env op l r dref =
  let lt = infer env l
  let rt = infer env r
  -- Type-error span (Bite 2, fork 1 = WHOLE BINOP): after inferring both
  -- operands, the live `currentLoc` is the LAST operand atom (ELoc wraps
  -- leaves, not the binop level).  Point mismatches at the whole `l <op> r`
  -- span (l's start .. r's end) so both the Num-obligation errors and the
  -- comparison/Eq/Ord `typeMismatch` binop errors squiggle the operator
  -- expression rather than a lone operand.
  let _ = match binopSpanLoc l r
    Some sp => setRef currentLoc (Some sp)
    None => ()
  let _ = recordBinopSite op dref lt
  let _ = recordArithSite op dref lt
  inferBinop op lt rt

-- Synthesize the whole-binop span from its operand Exprs: file + start of the
-- left operand, end of the right operand.  Falls back to whichever single
-- operand loc is available (None only when neither operand is ELoc-wrapped).
binopSpanLoc : Expr -> Expr -> Option Loc
binopSpanLoc l r = match (exprLoc l, exprLoc r)
  (Some (Loc f sl sc _ _), Some (Loc _ _ _ el ec)) => Some (Loc f sl sc el ec)
  (Some ll, None) => Some ll
  (None, Some rr) => Some rr
  (None, None) => None
-- Phase 151 / Gap G: record a binop site for the comparison/equality operators
-- so resolveBinopSites can stamp [dref] once the operand type grounds.  The
-- operand mono is [lt] (unified with [rt] by eqCompareOp/ordCompareOp below).

-- Map a comparison/equality operator to its Eq/Ord method and stash a pending
-- binop site; non-comparison operators record nothing.  G4: snapshot
-- currentImplBody so resolveBinopSite gates the RDict element-dict route to genuine
-- in-impl-body sites (a top-level operand may collide with stale activeDictVars ids).
recordBinopSite : String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> Unit
recordBinopSite op dref m = match binopMethod op
  Some method => setRef perRun.value.pendingBinopSites (PendingEntry method dref m perRun.value.currentFn.value (SKOp perRun.value.currentImplBody.value) :: perRun.value.pendingBinopSites.value)
  None => ()

-- SHARED-FLOAT-RESIDUAL §3(C): stash an arithmetic binop's operand mono + route
-- ref for the deferred scalar-type stamp.  Non-arithmetic operators record
-- nothing (their route is handled by recordBinopSite or left RNone).  The operand
-- mono is [lt] (unified with [rt] by inferBinop).
isArithBinop : String -> Bool
-- Intentional cross-file duplicate of the same helper in wasm_emit.mdk; not consolidating (tiny helper / divergent-by-design backend pair).
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-duplicate-body
isArithBinop op = contains op ["+", "-", "*", "/", "%"]

recordArithSite : String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> Unit
recordArithSite op dref m
  | isArithBinop op = setRef perRun.value.pendingArithSites ((dref, m)::perRun.value.pendingArithSites.value)
  | otherwise = ()

-- Roadmap #18a: `++` is the surface operator for `Semigroup`'s `append`, so it
-- joins the comparison operators on the recordBinopSite/resolveBinopSites seam —
-- a ground non-builtin operand (a user `impl Semigroup MyRope`) gets its route
-- stamped and rewriteBinopExpr turns the EBinOp into an `EMethodAt "append"`
-- application.  The builtin String/List appends never route (binopBuiltinHead) —
-- see the recursion note there.
binopMethod : String -> Option String
binopMethod "==" = Some "eq"
binopMethod "!=" = Some "eq"
binopMethod "<" = Some "lt"
binopMethod ">" = Some "gt"
binopMethod "<=" = Some "lte"
binopMethod ">=" = Some "gte"
binopMethod "++" = Some "append"
binopMethod _ = None

-- Roadmap #18c: `-` (unary minus) is the surface operator for `Num`'s `negate`,
-- joining `++`/comparisons on the record*Site/resolve*Sites/rewrite*Expr seam.
-- `!` never routes — Bool negation is not a typeclass method.
unopMethod : String -> Option String
unopMethod "-" = Some "negate"
unopMethod _ = None

-- The EUnOp analogue of recordBinopSite: stash a pending unop site for `-`
-- (nothing for `!`).  Same currentImplBody/currentFn snapshot, same reason.
recordUnopSite : String -> Ref Route -> Mono -> Unit
recordUnopSite op dref m = match unopMethod op
  Some method => setRef perRun.value.pendingUnopSites (PendingEntry method dref m perRun.value.currentFn.value (SKOp perRun.value.currentImplBody.value) :: perRun.value.pendingUnopSites.value)
  None => ()

-- The EUnOp analogue of inferBinopE: infer the single operand, record its
-- pending unop site (for `-`), then dispatch to inferUnop for the actual
-- obligation/unification work (negateOp for `-`, Bool-unify for `!`).
inferUnopE : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Ref Route -> Mono
inferUnopE env op e dref =
  let t = infer env e
  let _ = recordUnopSite op dref t
  inferUnop op t

inferBinop : String -> Mono -> Mono -> Mono
inferBinop "+" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "-" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "*" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "/" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "%" lt rt = numArithOp lt rt
inferBinop "++" lt rt = appendOp lt rt
inferBinop "==" lt rt = eqCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop "!=" lt rt = eqCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop "<" lt rt = ordCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop ">" lt rt = ordCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop "<=" lt rt = ordCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop ">=" lt rt = ordCompareOp lt rt
inferBinop "&&" lt rt = boolOp lt rt
inferBinop "||" lt rt = boolOp lt rt
inferBinop "::" lt rt = consOp lt rt
inferBinop "|>" lt rt = pipeOp lt rt
inferBinop ">>" lt rt = composeOp lt rt False
inferBinop "<<" lt rt = composeOp lt rt True
inferBinop op _ _ = panic ("typecheck: unsupported operator " ++ op)

-- Roadmap #18a: `++` (append) carries a `Semigroup` obligation on its (unified)
-- operand type — a verbatim mirror of `eqCompareOp`/`recordIfaceObligation` for `==`,
-- and the reason `"x" ++ Box 1` is now a located `No impl of Semigroup for Box`
-- reject at CHECK time rather than an eval-time `E-PANIC`.  Reuses the same
-- pendingImplObligations / checkImplObligations path: a synthetic Semigroup
-- obligation with a bare iface-param declared type (`TyVar "a"`, typaram ["a"]) so
-- dispatchMonosOf recovers exactly the operand mono.  `Monoid a requires Semigroup
-- a`, so a declared `Monoid a =>` constraint superclass-closes to satisfy it (this
-- is what keeps core.mdk's `foldMap f = fold (acc x => acc ++ f x) empty` typing).
-- A still-polymorphic operand defers to the caller, exactly like Num/Eq/Ord.
--
-- Gated on the `Semigroup` interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded), via
-- ifaceRegistered: the bare-HM no-prelude oracle (tc_probe /
-- typecheck_main.mdk) has no `Semigroup` class, so recording the obligation only
-- when it exists keeps those drivers byte-identical while `medaka check` rejects.
appendOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
appendOp lt rt =
  let _ = unify lt rt
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Semigroup" lt
  lt

-- #146: unified operator-obligation recorder.  The arithmetic (`+ - * / %`, unary
-- `-`), comparison (`== != < > <= >=`) and append (`++`) operators each carry an
-- interface obligation on their (unified) operand type via pushPendingObl — a
-- synthetic obligation with a bare iface-param declared type (`TyVar "a"`, typaram
-- ["a"]) so dispatchMonosOf recovers exactly the operand mono.  Gated on that
-- interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded): the bare-HM no-prelude oracle
-- (tc_probe / typecheck_main.mdk) registers none of Num/Eq/Ord/Semigroup, so
-- recording only when the interface exists keeps those drivers byte-identical while
-- full check_main (prelude) rejects, matching `medaka check`.  Callers:
-- numArithOp/negateOp ("Num"), eqCompareOp ("Eq"), ordCompareOp ("Ord"), appendOp
-- ("Semigroup").
recordIfaceObligation : String -> Mono -> Unit
recordIfaceObligation iface lt
  | ifaceRegistered iface =
    pushPendingObl (iface, ["a"], TyVar "a", lt, currentLoc.value)
  | otherwise = ()

-- True iff `iface` is registered (has ≥1 method registered from the DInterface
-- decls — present once core.mdk is loaded).  #147: an O(log n) membership test on
-- the cached registeredIfacesRef set, replacing the old full linear scan of the
-- method table per operator/numeric-literal.
ifaceRegistered : String -> Bool
ifaceRegistered iface = omHasKey iface perRun.value.registeredIfacesRef.value

-- G2: arithmetic `+ - * / %` carry a `Num` obligation on their (unified) operand
-- type — mirrors the oracle's `No impl of Num for <T>` reject (lib/typecheck.ml).
-- `++` (append) is NOT Num — it carries a `Semigroup` obligation instead (appendOp
-- above).  We reuse the
-- existing pendingImplObligations / checkImplObligations path: a synthetic Num
-- obligation with a bare iface-param declared type (`TyVar "a"`, typaram ["a"]) so
-- dispatchMonosOf recovers exactly the operand mono.  When it grounds to a concrete
-- non-Num head (String/Bool) with no `impl Num`, the post-inference check rejects;
-- while still a polymorphic var (a `Num a =>` site) allConcreteHeads defers it to
-- the caller — exactly the oracle's behaviour.
--
-- Gated on the `Num` interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded): the bare-HM,
-- no-prelude oracle (tc_probe / typecheck_main.mdk) imposes NO class constraint on
-- `+`, so `1 + 2 : Int` and `"a" - "b" : String` both type there.  Recording the
-- obligation only when Num exists keeps the no-prelude drivers byte-identical to
-- that oracle while the full check_main (prelude) path rejects, matching `medaka
-- check`.
numArithOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
numArithOp lt rt =
  -- Chunk D: taint any literal operand BEFORE the unify grounds it — so a literal
  -- fed to an arithmetic op (`1 + "x"`, `x + 1`, `1 + x`) keeps `No impl of Num for
  -- T` (the `+` is the culprit) rather than being reframed as a bare mismatch.
  let _ = markNumlitOpTaint lt
  let _ = markNumlitOpTaint rt
  let _ = unify lt rt
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Num" lt
  lt

-- PLAN.md #11 soundness arm: model an integer literal as a `fromInt n` method
-- occurrence so its route cell [dref] gets stamped (RKey ground / RDictFwd poly) by
-- resolveSites — exactly like core.mdk's `fromInt 0` in `sum`.  Direct mirror of
-- lib/typecheck.ml's ENumLit infer arm.  Gated on the prelude (`Num` registered AND
-- `fromInt` bound): with no prelude (bare-HM oracle) fall back to a plain fresh
-- Num-obligated var, leaving [dref] RNone (⇒ a static Int literal at rewrite time).
inferNumLit : TcEnv -> Int -> Ref (Option Float) -> Ref Route -> Mono
inferNumLit env n fref dref
  | ifaceRegistered "Num" = match lookupVar env "fromInt"
    None => inferNumLitBare n fref
    Some _ =>
      -- inferMethodAt records the Num impl obligation + a pendingSite on [dref].
      let fromIntTy = inferMethodAt env "fromInt" dref (Ref []) (Ref [])
      let a = freshVar ()
      let _ = unify fromIntTy (TFun (TCon "Int") (openRow ()) a)
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.numlitRefs ((a, fref, n, dref) :: perRun.value.numlitRefs.value)
      let _ = recordNumlitLoc a  -- Chunk D: literal provenance for Num mis-framing
      a
  | otherwise = inferNumLitBare n fref

-- no-prelude fallback: a fresh var, no route (dref untouched ⇒ stays RNone ⇒ Int).
-- PLAN.md #11 (locked §0.2): a numeric literal is ALWAYS a `fromInt n` occurrence,
-- so it carries a `Num` obligation on its var unconditionally — exactly the OCaml
-- oracle, whose ENumLit infer arm `infer env (EVar "fromInt")` records the Num
-- usage even no-prelude (`initial_env` seeds `fromInt`/`Num`).  Recording it here
-- (vs. the prelude-gated `recordIfaceObligation`) lets value-level defaulting ground
-- an AMBIGUOUS literal var to Int (`nums = [1,2,3] : List Int`) so the no-prelude
-- `typecheck_main` matches the prelude-aware oracle golden.  The matching reject is
-- SUPPRESSED no-prelude in checkOneImplObligation (no-prelude imposes no Num impl
-- constraint — see its `ifaceRegistered` guard), so a literal still types Int
-- without a spurious `No impl of Num for Int`.
inferNumLitBare : Int -> Ref (Option Float) -> Mono
inferNumLitBare n fref =
  let a = freshVar ()
  let _ = pushPendingObl ("Num", ["a"], TyVar "a", a, currentLoc.value)
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.numlitRefs ((a, fref, n, Ref RNone)::perRun.value.numlitRefs.value)
  let _ = recordNumlitLoc a  -- Chunk D: literal provenance for Num mis-framing
  a

-- ── Chunk D: Num mis-framing provenance (error-path only) ──────────────────
-- Record a literal's (var, loc) so a later Num-obligation reframe can tell a bare
-- literal from an arithmetic-operator operand.  [currentLoc] is the ENumLit span.
recordNumlitLoc : Mono -> Unit
recordNumlitLoc a = match currentLoc.value
  Some l =>
    let _ = setRef perRun.value.numlitVarLocs ((a, l)::perRun.value.numlitVarLocs.value)
    setRef
      perRun.value.numlitUntainted
      ((a, l)::perRun.value.numlitUntainted.value)
  None => ()

-- Called from recordIfaceObligation (arithmetic `+`/`-`/`*`/`/`/`%`): any recorded
-- literal whose var shares a union-find root with [m] was an operand of an op, so
-- mark its loc — the reframe then keeps `No impl of Num for T` for it.  Runs during
-- inference (vars still unbound), so `normalize` reveals the shared root regardless
-- of which operand became the root at unify time.
markNumlitOpTaint : Mono -> Unit
markNumlitOpTaint m =
  let id = rootIdOf m
  if id < 0 || not (anyRootMatch id perRun.value.numlitUntainted.value) then
    ()
  else
    setRef perRun.value.numlitUntainted (taintAndDrop id perRun.value.numlitUntainted.value)

-- True iff SOME worklist literal shares the union-find root [id].  Short-circuits via
-- `||` and allocates nothing, so the common case (this operand taints no literal)
-- leaves the worklist alone — rebuilding it unconditionally would put back an O(N)
-- allocation per operand, which is the very thing `rootIdOf` removed.
anyRootMatch : Int -> List (Mono, Loc) -> Bool
anyRootMatch _ [] = False
anyRootMatch id ((v, _)::rest) = sameRootId id v || anyRootMatch id rest

-- Taint every worklist literal sharing the root [id] (record its loc as
-- operator-tainted) and DROP it from the worklist; keep the others.
taintAndDrop : Int -> List (Mono, Loc) -> List (Mono, Loc)
taintAndDrop _ [] = []
taintAndDrop id ((v, l)::rest)
  | sameRootId id v =
    let _ = setRef perRun.value.numlitOpLocs (l::perRun.value.numlitOpLocs.value)
    taintAndDrop id rest
  | otherwise = (v, l) :: taintAndDrop id rest

-- The union-find ROOT id of [v]; -1 if it does not normalize to a type variable.
-- NON-ALLOCATING (it reads the root's id rather than rebuilding a `TVar` for it) and
-- PATH-COMPRESSING (it goes through `normalizeLink`, so a walked chain is flattened).
-- PERF: `sameRootId` is applied to EVERY recorded literal on EVERY arithmetic /
-- comparison operand (markNumlitOpTaint and tagLitLocsWithRoot each scan the whole,
-- never-trimmed `numlitVarLocs` list), so an uncompressed chain made this the single
-- hottest symbol in the compiler — 57% of a large-declaration typecheck (issue #115).
-- Tyvar ids come from a counter that starts at 0 (`newTyvarId`), so -1 can never
-- collide with a real id.
rootIdOf : Mono -> Int
rootIdOf (TVar cell) = match cell.value
  Unbound id _ => id
  Link t => rootVarId (normalizeLink cell t)
rootIdOf _ = 0 - 1

-- The id of an ALREADY-NORMALIZED mono: its root tyvar's id, or -1 if it is not a
-- type variable at all.  (`normalizeLink` never returns a `Link`, so that arm is
-- unreachable; it folds into the not-a-tyvar answer.)
rootVarId : Mono -> Int
rootVarId (TVar cell) = match cell.value
  Unbound id _ => id
  Link _ => 0 - 1
rootVarId _ = 0 - 1

-- True iff [v] normalizes to the unbound var [id] (same union-find root).
sameRootId : Int -> Mono -> Bool
sameRootId id v = rootIdOf v == id

-- Chunk D: if one of [a]/[b] is a still-unbound literal var and the other is
-- concrete-headed, tag that literal's loc with [kind] so the reframe can use
-- site-specific wording.  Pure state capture — never alters unification.
noteNumlitCtx : Mono -> Mono -> String -> Unit
noteNumlitCtx a b kind =
  let _ = tagNumlitCtxIf a b kind
  tagNumlitCtxIf b a kind

-- Tag EVERY literal whose var shares [v]'s root (not just the first) when [other]
-- has a concrete head — so a heterogeneous list forcing several literals to one
-- type tags them all with the same [kind], letting their reframed messages dedup to
-- a single "list elements have different types" line.
tagNumlitCtxIf : Mono -> Mono -> String -> Unit
tagNumlitCtxIf v other kind
  | isSome (monoHeadCon other) = tagAllLitLocs v kind
  | otherwise = ()

tagAllLitLocs : Mono -> String -> Unit
tagAllLitLocs v kind = match normalize v
  TVar cell =>
    tagLitLocsWithRoot (tyvarId cell) kind perRun.value.numlitVarLocs.value
  _ => ()

tagLitLocsWithRoot : Int -> String -> List (Mono, Loc) -> Unit
tagLitLocsWithRoot _ _ [] = ()
tagLitLocsWithRoot id kind ((mv, l)::rest) =
  let _ = if sameRootId id mv then
    setRef perRun.value.numlitCtxTags ((l, kind)::perRun.value.numlitCtxTags.value)
  else
    ()
  tagLitLocsWithRoot id kind rest

-- Stage-1 Eq dispatch (EQ-DISPATCH-DESIGN.md §2.1): `==`/`!=` carry an `Eq`
-- obligation on their (unified) operand type — a verbatim mirror of
-- `recordIfaceObligation` for `+`.  We reuse the existing pendingImplObligations /
-- checkImplObligations path: a synthetic Eq obligation with a bare iface-param
-- declared type (`TyVar "a"`, typaram ["a"]) so dispatchMonosOf recovers exactly
-- the operand mono.  When it grounds to a concrete non-Eq head (a function, an
-- un-derived ADT) with no `impl Eq`, the post-inference check rejects; while still
-- a polymorphic var (an `Eq a =>` site) allConcreteHeads defers it to the caller
-- — exactly the Num behaviour.  `<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=` carry the sibling `Ord`
-- obligation via `ordCompareOp` below.  `!=` shares this seam and lowers to
-- `not (eq …)` at the dict layer (binopMethodApp), so it does not double-report.
--
-- Gated on the `Eq` interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded), mirroring
-- `ifaceRegistered`: the bare-HM no-prelude oracle (tc_probe / typecheck_main)
-- has no `Eq` class, so recording the obligation only when Eq exists keeps those
-- drivers byte-identical while full check_main (prelude) rejects.
eqCompareOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
eqCompareOp lt rt =
  let _ = unify lt rt
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Eq" lt
  TCon "Bool"

-- Stage-1 Ord dispatch (EQ-DISPATCH-DESIGN.md §"sibling Ord follow-up"): the four
-- ordering comparisons `<`/`>`/`<=`/`>=` carry an `Ord` obligation on their
-- (unified) operand type — a verbatim mirror of `eqCompareOp` for `==`.  Reuses the
-- same pendingImplObligations / checkImplObligations path.  `Ord` supersets `Eq`
-- (`interface Ord a requires Eq a`), so a declared `Ord a =>` constraint already
-- superclass-closes to satisfy BOTH the `<`→Ord and a sibling `==`→Eq obligation;
-- an `Eq a =>` declaration does NOT close upward, so a bare `<` under `Eq a =>` is
-- correctly rejected as needing `Ord a`.  When the operand grounds to a concrete
-- non-Ord head (a function, an un-derived ADT) with no `impl Ord`, the
-- post-inference check rejects; while still a polymorphic var it defers to the
-- caller — exactly the Eq/Num behaviour.  NO change to eval/emit: `<` still lowers
-- to `EMethodAt "lt"` at the dict layer, so runtime/emit dispatch is unchanged.
--
-- Gated on the `Ord` interface being REGISTERED (prelude loaded), mirroring
-- `ifaceRegistered`.
ordCompareOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
ordCompareOp lt rt =
  let _ = unify lt rt
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Ord" lt
  TCon "Bool"

boolOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
boolOp lt rt =
  let _ = unify lt (TCon "Bool")
  let _ = unify rt (TCon "Bool")
  TCon "Bool"

consOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
consOp lt rt =
  let listTy = TApp (TCon "List") lt
  let _ = noteNumlitCtx lt rt "cons"  -- Chunk D: tag cons head-vs-list Num mismatch
  let _ = unify rt listTy
  listTy

pipeOp : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
pipeOp lt rt =
  let res = freshVar ()
  let eff = openRow ()
  let _ = unify rt (TFun lt eff res)
  let _ = performEffect perRun.value.curEffect eff
  res

-- g >> h : (a->b) -> (b->c) -> (a->c)  [flipped=False]
-- g << h : (a->b) -> (c->a) -> (c->b)  [flipped=True]
composeOp : Mono -> Mono -> Bool -> Mono
composeOp gt ht False =
  let a = freshVar ()
  let b = freshVar ()
  let c = freshVar ()
  let _ = unify gt (TFun a (openRow ()) b)
  let _ = unify ht (TFun b (openRow ()) c)
  TFun a (openRow ()) c
composeOp gt ht True =
  let a = freshVar ()
  let b = freshVar ()
  let c = freshVar ()
  let _ = unify gt (TFun a (openRow ()) b)
  let _ = unify ht (TFun c (openRow ()) a)
  TFun c (openRow ()) b

inferUnop : String -> Mono -> Mono
inferUnop "-" t = negateOp t
inferUnop "!" t =
  let _ = unify t (TCon "Bool")
  TCon "Bool"
inferUnop op _ = panic ("typecheck: unsupported unary op " ++ op)

-- Roadmap #18c: unary minus `-x` carries a `Num` obligation on its operand type —
-- a verbatim mirror of `numArithOp`/`recordIfaceObligation` for binary `+`/`-`/…,
-- and the reason `-"x"` is now a located `No impl of Num for String` reject at
-- CHECK time rather than an eval-time `panic "unary minus on non-number"`.
-- markNumlitOpTaint keeps the same Chunk-D literal-provenance error framing as
-- the binary arithmetic operators (`-"x"` blames the operator, not a bare
-- literal-vs-String mismatch).
negateOp : Mono -> Mono
negateOp t =
  let _ = markNumlitOpTaint t
  let _ = recordIfaceObligation "Num" t
  t

-- backtick infix `x `f` y` is just f applied to x then y
inferInfix : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferInfix env op l r =
  let ft = inferVar env op
  let lt = infer env l
  let rt = infer env r
  -- reconstruct the equivalent app spine so a hole-bearing extern fills from
  -- its FIRST arg (l) — mirror of the EApp path (v2 Stage 2b).
  let inner = EApp (EVar op) l
  inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp inner r) (inferApp (envAlphaLets env) inner ft lt) rt

inferVar : TcEnv -> String -> Mono
inferVar env x = match maybeStandaloneValueMono x
  -- P0-19 d4b (S4): bare value-position definer shadow → the standalone scheme.
  Some m => m
  None => inferVarPlain env x

inferVarPlain : TcEnv -> String -> Mono
inferVarPlain env x = match lookupVar env x
  Some s =>
    let mono = instantiateVarTracked x s
    recordImplObligation x mono
  None => unboundVarFresh x

-- #11 soundness (native obligation hole, plain check path): a constrained binding used
-- as a plain EVar (`g = f "hello"`) instantiates its DEFERRED obligations at the call.
-- schemeObligationsRef (always-on, unlike funConstraintsRef) records each binding's
-- (iface, quantified-var-id); here we instantiate TRACKED so each obligation var maps
-- to this call's fresh mono, recorded as a call obligation checked after inference.
-- A binding with no recorded obligations takes the plain instantiate path (identical
-- mono).  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml re-checking inferred_constraints at every use.
instantiateVarTracked : String -> Scheme -> Mono
instantiateVarTracked x s = match lookupAssocS2 x perRun.value.schemeObligationsRef.value
  Some obs =>
    let inst = instantiateTracked s
    let _ = recordSchemeCallObligations obs (snd inst)
    fst inst
  -- #673: schemeObligationsRef is a PER-MODULE ref (reset per module), so a CROSS-MODULE
  -- constrained binding used as a plain EVar has NO entry here.  For a PRELUDE binding
  -- (`println : Display a => …`, the #673 repro) the plain-`instantiate` fallback silently
  -- DROPPED its call obligation, so `check` false-greened on `println (Foo 1)` / a 6-tuple
  -- with no Display while `run`/`build` (dict elaboration is a separate net) rejected.
  None => match declaredCrossModuleObls x
    Some obs =>
      let inst = instantiateTracked s
      let _ = recordSchemeCallObligations obs (snd inst)
      fst inst
    None => instantiate s

-- #673: the declared call obligations of a CROSS-MODULE constrained binding [x] used as a
-- plain EVar, in schemeObligations shape (`(iface, quantified-var-id VECTOR)` list) so the
-- caller records them exactly like the same-module `Some obs` branch.  TWO source-EXACT
-- lookups, NEVER a name-only whole-table scan (both correctness- and perf-unsound, #673 rework):
--
--   (1) qualConstraintFor x — a USER import resolves through currentImportDefinersRef to the
--       ACTUAL owning module's (src, name) qual entry, so a same-named import from a DIFFERENT
--       module (foo's constrained `bar` vs baz's unconstrained `bar`, where `x` resolves to
--       baz) can never be misattributed.  It also gates on the cheap currentImportDefinersRef
--       membership, so only a genuine import pays a scan — not every unconstrained/local var.
--   (2) coreSchemeObligationsRef — core's OWN captured schemeObligationsRef (snapshotted right
--       after the core pass, before the first module's resetState).  Prelude names are
--       deliberately excluded from currentImportDefinersRef (importDefinersOf skips `core`),
--       so qualConstraintFor cannot see them; this is the source-EXACT store for exactly that
--       set (`println`/`print`), an O(log n) OrdMap lookup with ZERO wrong-module surface.
declaredCrossModuleObls : String -> Option (List (String, List Int))
declaredCrossModuleObls x = match qualConstraintFor x
  Some (ids, ifaces) => Some (shatterVecObls ifaces ids)
  None => omLookup x crossRun.value.coreSchemeObligationsRef.value

-- pair each iface with its own single scheme-var id (the "shattered per-tyvar dict slots"
-- shape funConstraintsRef stores — see recordCallObligations), skipping unnamed ("") slots.
shatterVecObls : List String -> List Int -> List (String, List Int)
shatterVecObls [] _ = []
shatterVecObls _ [] = []
shatterVecObls (iface::ifs) (id::ids)
  | iface == "" = shatterVecObls ifs ids
  | otherwise = (iface, [id]) :: shatterVecObls ifs ids

-- map each (iface, scheme-var-id VECTOR) through the instantiation subst to this
-- call's monos, pushing ONE joint obligation per predicate onto
-- pendingCallObligations.  If ANY id is absent from the subst (shouldn't happen for a
-- quantified var) the whole predicate is skipped — a partial vector would be a
-- different predicate (#607).
recordSchemeCallObligations : List (String, List Int) -> List (Int, Mono) -> Unit
recordSchemeCallObligations [] _ = ()
recordSchemeCallObligations ((iface, ids)::rest) subst = match substMonos ids subst
  Some monos =>
    let _ = pushCallObl (iface, monos, currentLoc.value)
    recordSchemeCallObligations rest subst
  None => recordSchemeCallObligations rest subst

-- the monos [ids] map to under [subst], in order; None if any id is unmapped.
substMonos : List Int -> List (Int, Mono) -> Option (List Mono)
substMonos [] _ = Some []
substMonos (id::rest) subst = match lookupAssocI id subst
  Some mono => map (mono :: _) (substMonos rest subst)
  None => None

-- String-keyed lookup over schemeObligationsRef.
lookupAssocS2 : String -> List (String, List (String, List Int)) -> Option (List (String, List Int))
lookupAssocS2 _ [] = None
lookupAssocS2 k ((k2, v)::rest)
  | k == k2 = Some v
  | otherwise = lookupAssocS2 k rest

-- Error-path B4: if [x] is a registered interface method, record this occurrence's
-- (post-instantiation) mono so checkImplObligations can verify a matching impl
-- exists once the dispatch type is resolved.  Returns the mono unchanged.  A
-- non-method name records nothing (the common case).  The marked path records the
-- same via inferMethodAt; the bare typecheck_main path records here.
recordImplObligation : String -> Mono -> Mono
recordImplObligation x mono
  -- A standalone shadow (a top-level funDef that reuses an interface-method name)
  -- resolves in-scope to that standalone, not the method — the scope-blind marker
  -- still rewrites its occurrences to EMethodAt, but a NO-IMPL receiver legitimately
  -- falls back to the standalone (resolveRLocalSite stamps RLocal), so there is no
  -- impl obligation.  Recording one surfaces a spurious `No impl of <Iface> for <T>`
  -- under checkImplObligations (the build path skips that check, so it only bites
  -- `check`).  Skip for EVERY standalone shadow — definer (`definerShadowNamesRef`)
  -- AND importer (an imported standalone; `standaloneValuesRef` holds both).  P0-18:
  -- this closes the `check`-side of the importer-shadow-on-a-no-impl-receiver residual
  -- (`size 3` where `size` is imported) which was REJECTED while `run` correctly
  -- returned the standalone.  Sound: the name genuinely has a standalone fallback, so
  -- a no-impl receiver is not an error — the route decides impl-vs-standalone per
  -- receiver; a receiver WITH an impl still dispatches (and that impl exists).
  -- Check BOTH refs: the single-file check path (checkProgram) sets only
  -- `definerShadowNamesRef`, while the multi-module path sets `standaloneValuesRef`
  -- (importer ∪ definer) — so either alone would miss one path's shadows.
  | contains x perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef.value || contains x driverState.value.standaloneValuesRef.value =
    mono
  | otherwise = match omLookup x perRun.value.methodIfaceParamsRef.value
    None => mono
    Some (iface, typarams, mty) =>
      let _ = pushPendingObl (iface, typarams, mty, mono, currentLoc.value)
      mono

-- Expected-type propagation for an application whose argument is a lambda.
-- Inferring `andThen e (stream => Ok [JArray stream])` naively infers the lambda
-- BODY first with `stream : freshVar`, so a downstream conflicting use inside the
-- body (`JArray stream` wants `Array Json`) pins `stream` to the WRONG type before
-- the function's declared param type (`List Json`) is ever reconciled — the later
-- reconciliation then fails at the application with no good site, and the hover
-- side-channel records the wrong `stream` type.  Fix (bidirectional "checking"
-- mode, the do-bind/higher-order case): infer `f` first, peel its parameter type,
-- and unify it onto the lambda's parameter BEFORE inferring the body.  Then the
-- body sees `stream : List Json` and the type error localizes to the `JArray`
-- USE, and recordLocalBind captures the right hover type.  Purely additive: the
-- final `unify ft (TFun xt …)` in inferApp imposes the same equality regardless,
-- so the solution is unchanged — we only make it available earlier.
-- ── Batch B #5: swapped-argument detection (2-arg transposition) ───────────
-- A 2-arg application whose in-order arg/param types clash but whose
-- TRANSPOSITION type-checks is almost always a caller who wrote the arguments
-- in the wrong order (`greet 3 "Alice"` for `greet : String -> Int -> …`).
-- Detect that shape UPSTREAM in inferAppExpr — where the callee name + BOTH
-- args are still in scope — and emit ONE actionable diagnostic naming the
-- swapped call, instead of letting the two per-arg `inferApp` mismatches leak
-- (`Int vs String` + `Int literal vs String`) with no hint that a swap fixes it.
--
-- SOUNDNESS (a wrong "swap" suggestion is worse than the status quo): fire ONLY
-- when the transposition GENUINELY type-checks.  Two guards make the structural
-- test exact — never a wildcard-var approximation:
--   (1) both callee params must be fully CONCRETE (no free tyvars).  A
--       polymorphic param accepts any argument, so a "swap" past it is
--       meaningless AND its fit can't be decided structurally.
--   (2) both arguments must be LITERALS.  A literal's type is known exactly
--       from its syntax (WITHOUT re-inference side effects), so `argLitFits`
--       decides the transposition's type-fit structurally.  A numeric literal
--       (`ENumLit`/`LInt`) fits any `Int`/`Float` param (it is `Num`-poly); a
--       float literal fits only `Float`; other literals fit their exact type.
-- Under both guards, `(not in-order) && swapped` holds only for a real
-- transposition, so the suggested swap always type-checks.  We further require
-- the two params to DIFFER (same-type params ⇒ order is irrelevant, no swap).
-- Anything outside this scope (>2 args, non-literal args, polymorphic params,
-- same-type params) falls through to the normal per-arg path unchanged.

unlocExpr : Expr -> Expr
unlocExpr (ELoc _ e) = unlocExpr e
unlocExpr (EDoOrigin _ e) = unlocExpr e
unlocExpr e = e

exprLoc : Expr -> Option Loc
exprLoc (ELoc l _) = Some l
exprLoc (EDoOrigin l _) = Some l
exprLoc _ = None

-- The span of an application's outermost argument (Bite 3): peel any ELoc
-- wrapper on the `EApp` head, then read the argument Expr's loc.  Non-EApp
-- shapes (never passed as `appExpr`) return None.
appArgLoc : Expr -> Option Loc
appArgLoc (ELoc _ e) = appArgLoc e
appArgLoc (EApp _ x) = exprLoc x
appArgLoc _ = None

-- True iff [m] has NO unbound tyvar anywhere (a fully concrete type).
monoConcrete : Mono -> Bool
monoConcrete m = match normalize m
  TVar _ => False
  TCon _ => True
  TApp a b => monoConcrete a && monoConcrete b
  TFun a _ b => monoConcrete a && monoConcrete b
  TEff _ => True

-- structural equality of two concrete monos (both guaranteed var-free here)
monoSameShape : Mono -> Mono -> Bool
monoSameShape a b = match normalize a
  TCon x => match normalize b
    TCon y => x == y
    _ => False
  TApp a1 b1 => match normalize b
    TApp a2 b2 => monoSameShape a1 a2 && monoSameShape b1 b2
    _ => False
  TFun a1 _ b1 => match normalize b
    TFun a2 _ b2 => monoSameShape a1 a2 && monoSameShape b1 b2
    _ => False
  _ => False

monoIsNumeric : Mono -> Bool
monoIsNumeric m = match normalize m
  TCon "Int" => True
  TCon "Float" => True
  _ => False

monoIsFloat : Mono -> Bool
monoIsFloat m = match normalize m
  TCon "Float" => True
  _ => False

isLitArg : Expr -> Bool
isLitArg e = match unlocExpr e
  ENumLit _ _ _ _ => True
  ELit _ => True
  _ => False

-- does the LITERAL argument [e] fit the CONCRETE param type [pt]?
argLitFits : Expr -> Mono -> Bool
argLitFits e pt = match unlocExpr e
  ENumLit _ _ _ _ => monoIsNumeric pt
  ELit (LInt _) => monoIsNumeric pt
  ELit (LFloat _) => monoIsFloat pt
  ELit l => monoSameShape (litType l) pt
  _ => False

-- render a literal argument back to its source text (for the swapped-call hint)
renderLitArg : Expr -> Option String
renderLitArg e = match unlocExpr e
  ENumLit n _ _ _ => Some (intToString n)
  ELit (LInt n) => Some (intToString n)
  ELit (LFloat f) => Some (floatToString f)
  ELit (LString s) => Some ("\"" ++ s ++ "\"")
  ELit (LChar c) => Some ("'" ++ c ++ "'")
  ELit (LBool b) => Some (if b then "True" else "False")
  ELit LUnit => Some "()"
  _ => None

-- the two param types of a function of arity EXACTLY 2 (result NOT an arrow).
-- Requiring exact arity 2 keeps the detector off a >2-arg call's inner 2-arg
-- SUB-spine: currying makes `g 3 "Alice"` a real inferAppExpr node inside
-- `g 3 "Alice" True`, and firing there would suggest `g "Alice" 3`, silently
-- dropping the third argument.  A 3-param `g` has a `Bool -> String` result
-- after two params, so it bails here; only a genuinely 2-param callee matches.
funParams2 : Mono -> Option (Mono, Mono)
funParams2 m = match normalize m
  TFun p1 _ rest => match normalize rest
    TFun p2 _ result => match normalize result
      TFun _ _ _ => None
      _ => Some (p1, p2)
    _ => None
  _ => None

-- Machine fix: replace the span from arg1's start to arg2's end with the
-- swapped source text.  Requires both args to carry real source spans.
swapArgFix : Expr -> Expr -> String -> Option (Loc, String)
swapArgFix a1 a2 repl = match (exprLoc a1, exprLoc a2)
  (Some (Loc f sl sc _ _), Some (Loc _ _ _ el ec)) =>
    Some (Loc f sl sc el ec, repl)
  _ => None

-- If [f] is exactly `<callee> arg1` (a 2-arg call's function part — the callee
-- applied to just its first argument, NOT itself a nested application), resolve
-- the callee to its NAME and its two concrete param types.  `None` for any
-- other shape (nested spine, unbound callee, non-arity-2 type).
twoArgCallee : TcEnv -> Expr -> Option (String, Expr, Mono, Mono)
twoArgCallee env f = match unlocExpr f
  EApp g a1 => match unlocExpr g
    EApp _ _ => None                           -- 3+ args: out of scope
    gHead =>
      do
        name <- appSpineName gHead
        scheme <- lookupVar env name
        (p1, p2) <- funParams2 (instantiate scheme)
        Some (name, a1, p1, p2)
  _ => None

-- Detect a 2-argument transposition on `f x` (`f` = callee applied to arg1, `x`
-- = arg2).  Returns `Some resultVar` (poisoned, so no cascade) when it fires and
-- has emitted the diagnostic; `None` to fall through to the normal path.
detectSwappedArgs2 : TcEnv -> Expr -> Expr -> Option Mono
detectSwappedArgs2 env f a2
  | not (isLitArg a2) = None
  | otherwise = match twoArgCallee env f
    None => None
    Some (name, a1, p1, p2) =>
      if not (isLitArg a1) then
        None
      else
        trySwapReport name a1 a2 p1 p2

trySwapReport : String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono -> Mono -> Option Mono
trySwapReport name a1 a2 p1 p2
  | not (monoConcrete p1 && monoConcrete p2) = None
  | monoSameShape p1 p2 = None                 -- same-type params: order is moot
  | otherwise =
    let inOrder = argLitFits a1 p1 && argLitFits a2 p2
    let swapped = argLitFits a2 p1 && argLitFits a1 p2
    if (not inOrder) && swapped then emitSwapDiag name a1 a2 else None

emitSwapDiag : String -> Expr -> Expr -> Option Mono
emitSwapDiag name a1 a2 = match (renderLitArg a1, renderLitArg a2)
  (Some s1, Some s2) =>
    let swappedCall = "\{name} \{s2} \{s1}"
    let msg = "Arguments to '\{name}' are in the wrong order. Try '\{swappedCall}'"
    let help = "swap the arguments: \{swappedCall}"
    let fix = swapArgFix a1 a2 "\{s2} \{s1}"
    let _ = pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt "T-TYPE-MISMATCH" (orElseLoc (exprLoc a1) currentLoc.value) msg help fix
    let v = freshVar ()
    let _ = poisonMismatchVars v v
    Some v
  _ => None

inferAppExpr : TcEnv -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferAppExpr env f x = match lamUnderLoc x
    Some (loc, pats, body) =>
      let ft = infer env f
      let _ = setLocIfSome loc
      -- ft is `(lamType) -> result`; its FIRST domain is the lambda's expected
      -- type.  Peel that, then peel the lambda type itself for its per-param types.
      let lamExpected = funDomain (normalize ft)
      let xt = inferLamChecked env (expectedParamTypes lamExpected (listLen pats)) pats body
      inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) ft xt
    None => match shadowStandaloneHead env f
      Some (mname, tagRefs) => inferShadowApp env mname tagRefs f x
      None => match definerShadowArgHead f
       Some (dname, dtagRef, dimplRef) => inferDefinerShadowApp env dname dtagRef dimplRef f x
       None => match definerShadowVarHead f
        Some dname => inferDefinerShadowVarApp env dname f x
        None => match detectSwappedArgs2 env f x
         Some swapResult => swapResult
         None =>
          let ft = infer env f
          let xt = infer env x
          -- P0-16: detect `f(1, 2)` on the PRISTINE ft (before inferApp unifies
          -- its domain with the tuple), then record the residual result so a
          -- downstream no-impl / mismatch can surface the tailored hint.
          let pend = tupleCallShapeHint f x ft
          let res = inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) ft xt
          let _ = match pend
            Some (help, fix) => setRef perRun.value.tupleCallCandidates ((res, help, fix)::perRun.value.tupleCallCandidates.value)
            None => ()
          res

-- @Impl: if [x] is an `@Name` disambiguation hint (a bare `EVar "@Name"`, possibly
-- under an ELoc wrapper), return the name with the leading `@` stripped.  Mirrors
-- the two oracle arms `EApp(f, EVar h)` and `EApp(f, ELoc(_, EVar h))` with
-- `h.[0]='@'`.  Anything else → None → the ordinary application path.

-- Bug C (mirror lib/typecheck.ml:2293-2329): peel a single-argument application's
-- head to a standalone-shadow interface-method reference (`toList m` where `toList`
-- is BOTH a Foldable method AND an imported `Map` standalone).  Handles BOTH the
-- MARKED form (`EMethodAt`, the eval/build path — carries a route ref to stamp RLocal)
-- and the UNMARKED form (`EVar`, the `check` path — no route ref; the empty list).
-- Only fires for a SINGLE-param interface method whose name is a registered importer
-- shadow with a stashed standalone scheme — exactly the oracle's guard.  Everything
-- else (no shadow, multi-param iface, non-method head) returns None → the ordinary path.
shadowStandaloneHead : TcEnv -> Expr -> Option (String, List (Ref Route))
shadowStandaloneHead env (ELoc _ f) = shadowStandaloneHead env f
shadowStandaloneHead env (EMethodAt name tagRef _ _)
  | isShadowStandalone name = Some (name, [tagRef])
  | otherwise = None
shadowStandaloneHead env (EVar name)
  | isShadowStandalone name = Some (name, [])
  | otherwise = None
shadowStandaloneHead _ _ = None

-- a name that is BOTH a registered importer shadow (standaloneValuesRef), has a
-- stashed standalone scheme, and names a single-TYPARAM interface's method.
--
-- ⚠️ IMPORTER side ⇒ singleTyparamIfaceMethod, NOT ifaceMethodName (#54).  Fork 1 keeps
-- the per-receiver rule here, and that rule keys the impl query on the receiver's head
-- tycon standing at the interface's ONE typaram.  A multi-typaram interface has no such
-- correspondence to key on -- `FromEntries c e`'s `fromEntries : List e -> c` does not
-- even take its first typaram as an argument -- so the importer arms stay scoped to the
-- single-typaram case.  The DEFINER arms do not: under the S2 inversion they never query
-- the impl universe at all, so they are typaram-agnostic (ifaceMethodName).
isShadowStandalone : String -> Bool
isShadowStandalone name = contains name driverState.value.standaloneValuesRef.value
  && isSome (lookupAssoc name perRun.value.shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value)
  && singleTyparamIfaceMethod name

-- True iff [name]'s interface (in methodIfaceParamsRef) takes exactly ONE type
-- parameter -- the receiver-directed case the per-receiver IMPORTER rule (Fork 1) is
-- scoped to.
--
-- ⚠️ RENAMED from `singleParamIfaceMethod` (#54).  The old name said "single-PARAM
-- method" while the body counts interface TYPE PARAMS; it stated the opposite of what it
-- does and sent one agent down a wrong hypothesis (issue #54; SHADOW-SEMANTICS.md S8).
-- A two-PARAM method of a one-typaram interface (`comb : a -> a -> Int`, cell d7) passes
-- this predicate, and always did.
singleTyparamIfaceMethod : String -> Bool
singleTyparamIfaceMethod name = match omLookup name perRun.value.methodIfaceParamsRef.value
  Some (_, typarams, _) => listLen typarams == 1
  None => False

-- True iff [name] is a method of SOME visible interface, whatever its typaram arity --
-- the DEFINER-side gate (#54, closing S-3 / SHADOW-SEMANTICS.md row 26).
--
-- Under the S2 inversion a definer shadow denotes its module's own standalone
-- unconditionally: the impl universe is never consulted, so there is no receiver-to-
-- typaram correspondence to require, and gating the definer entry points on the typaram
-- COUNT only excused `interface Ix a i` from the machinery.  That excusal was the S-3
-- bug: d11 typed+emitted per-receiver (the OLD, pre-inversion semantics) while eval, which
-- has no route stamp to follow, resolved the bare name lexically to the standalone and
-- E-PANICked `unknown op '*'` on a `Box` -- an S7 path-agreement violation.
ifaceMethodName : String -> Bool
ifaceMethodName name =
  isSome (omLookup name perRun.value.methodIfaceParamsRef.value)

-- Bug C: type `toList m` for a no-impl receiver against the imported STANDALONE
-- scheme (giving the concrete result element type, e.g. `List (String, Int)`) and
-- stamp the route RLocal (when marked); if the receiver DOES have an impl (or its head
-- is not yet concrete) fall through to ordinary dispatch (typed against the method
-- scheme the env rebound the name to).  Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml:2308-2329.
--
-- ✅ S1-RESIDUAL-B CLOSED (2026-07-14).  This function used to have NO groundShadowReceiver
-- call, unlike its definer peer inferDefinerShadowApp (which P0-20 gave one).  An UNGROUNDED
-- receiver — a bare `Num` LITERAL is the everyday case, it is `Num a => a`, not `Int` — so
-- never reached the standalone arm below (headTyconMono is None) and fell to the
-- ordinary-app arm, which types against the METHOD scheme the env rebound the name to.
--
--   -- lib/prov.mdk:  export isEmpty : Int -> Bool
--   import lib.prov.{isEmpty}
--   main = println (debug (isEmpty 0))     -- Type mismatch: Int literal vs Int Int
--
-- The `Int Int` in that message is the tell: the PRELUDE's `Foldable.isEmpty : t a -> Bool`
-- is higher-kinded, so unifying `t a` with the literal's tyvar solved `t := Int, a := Int`.
-- The user's imported `isEmpty : Int -> Bool` was never consulted.  (Filed as "under-applies
-- on run" for a CONSTRAINED standalone; the root is broader — it breaks an UNCONSTRAINED one
-- at `check` too, whenever the shadowed method is higher-kinded.)
--
-- Fix, exactly as filed: ground an ungrounded receiver to the IMPORTED standalone's declared
-- domain BEFORE asking the S2 impl question, so typecheck and the post-inference route
-- resolver ask it about the SAME head.  That symmetry is the whole P0-20 lesson — a decision
-- derived twice, at two different times, over a receiver that changed in between.
--
-- ⚠️ NOT affected by the S2 inversion: an IMPORTER shadow keeps the per-receiver rule
-- (Fork 1).  Grounding only decides WHICH head the rule is applied to; a grounded head with
-- a live impl still dispatches (`isEmpty [1, 2]` → `Foldable.isEmpty`, fixture i4).
inferShadowApp : TcEnv -> String -> List (Ref Route) -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferShadowApp env mname tagRefs f x =
  -- capture the application's loc BEFORE inferring the argument (which moves currentLoc),
  -- so a domain mismatch localizes to the call site, not the argument.
  let dloc = currentLoc.value
  let tx = infer env x
  -- The dict-var carve-out (S5/Fork 2) applies to importer shadows too: a receiver that is
  -- the enclosing fn's `=>`-bound constraint var DISPATCHES through that dict, so it must
  -- NOT be ground to the standalone's domain (that would monomorphise the written signature).
  -- groundShadowReceiver is also inert on an already-grounded receiver, so i1/i3/i4 (all
  -- concrete receivers) are byte-identical.
  let _ = groundShadowReceiver (importerShadowDomain mname) tx (definerReceiverIsDictVar mname tx) dloc
  match headTyconMono tx
    Some head => if implExistsForHead perRun.value.shadowKeyTableRef.value mname head then inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) (inferShadowAppHead env f) tx
    else match lookupAssoc mname perRun.value.shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value
      Some sscheme =>
        let smono = instantiate sscheme
        let r = freshVar ()
        let eff = openRow ()
        let _ = unify smono (TFun tx eff r)
        let _ = performEffect perRun.value.curEffect eff
        let _ = stampRoutes tagRefs
        -- S-1 (Fork D — the importer half): an IMPORTED constrained standalone shadowing
        -- a method breaks identically to a local one (its def is dict-passed in its own
        -- module; this call must supply the dicts).  Compute them off the head type we
        -- just unified, and defer the ROUTE stamp to resolveRLocalSites — stampRoutes
        -- above ran mid-inference, when the receiver is not yet solved, so it could only
        -- write `RLocal sym []`.  forceLocal=True: this arm has already committed the
        -- site's TYPE to the standalone, so its route is not open to re-derivation.
        let _ = recordImporterShadowDicts env mname tagRefs tx
        r
      None => inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) (inferShadowAppHead env f) tx
    None => inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) (inferShadowAppHead env f) tx

-- #411: infer an IMPORTER-shadow application's head with shadowHeadCtxRef flagged, so
-- maybeStandaloneValueMono's new importer arm does not pin an APPLIED occurrence to the
-- standalone scheme (which would kill per-receiver dispatch — Fork 1 / i4).  The definer
-- path has always bracketed its head this way (inferDefinerShadowApp); this is the peer
-- the importer path was missing, and the reason it could be missing before is that
-- nothing pinned importer value positions at all — which IS the #411 bug.
-- Save/restore, not set-False: a nested shadow head inside [f] must not clobber an outer
-- True on its way out (see inferDefinerShadowApp's bracket).
inferShadowAppHead : TcEnv -> Expr -> Mono
inferShadowAppHead env f =
  let saved = toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef.value
  let _ = setRef toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef True
  let ft = infer env f
  let _ = setRef toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef saved
  ft

-- S1-RESIDUAL-B: the IMPORTED standalone's declared domain — the first arrow domain of the
-- scheme stashed in shadowStandaloneSchemesRef (the concrete-head pick made per module by
-- checkModuleFullImpl).  The importer peer of shadowDomainFor's mangled arm, and it must NOT
-- be `lookupVar env mname`: env1 rebound the bare name to the interface METHOD, which is the
-- very scheme whose higher-kinded domain caused the bug.
--
-- None (⇒ grounding is a no-op) when the name has no stashed standalone scheme, or when that
-- standalone's own first parameter is itself polymorphic (`f : a -> Bool`) — unifying a bare
-- tyvar domain with an ungrounded receiver leaves it ungrounded anyway, so the caller's
-- headTyconMono still says None and the old ordinary-app arm runs, byte-identically.
importerShadowDomain : String -> Option Mono
importerShadowDomain mname = match lookupAssoc mname perRun.value.shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value
  None => None
  Some s => match normalize (instantiate s)
    TFun a _ _ => Some a
    _ => None

-- S-1 (importer half): re-record each of the importer shadow's route refs as an
-- RLocalSite carrying the imported standalone's own constraint dicts, so
-- resolveRLocalSites re-stamps the ref post-inference with the dicts filled in.
-- forceLocal=True — inferShadowApp's standalone arm already committed the site's type.
-- Inert (no dicts, same RLocal stamp) for an unconstrained imported standalone.
recordImporterShadowDicts : TcEnv -> String -> List (Ref Route) -> Mono -> Unit
recordImporterShadowDicts _ _ [] _ = ()
recordImporterShadowDicts env mname (r::rest) tx =
  -- the IMPORTED standalone's scheme, NOT `lookupVar env mname` (which env1 rebound to
  -- the interface method) — see shadowStandaloneDictsWith.
  let dicts = shadowStandaloneDictsWith
    (lookupAssoc mname perRun.value.shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value)
    mname
    (routeLocalSym r.value)
    tx
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.pendingRLocalSites
    (PendingEntry mname r tx "" (SKRLocal (routeLocalSym r.value) True (fst dicts) (snd dicts))
      ::perRun.value.pendingRLocalSites.value)
  recordImporterShadowDicts env mname rest tx

-- P0-18: peel a single-argument application's head to a DEFINER-shadow interface-
-- method occurrence (the user's OWN top-level fn reuses a single-param interface
-- method name, e.g. this module's `size : Int -> Int` shadowing `Sizeable`'s
-- `size`).  Unlike importer shadows (shadowStandaloneHead / inferShadowApp), the
-- user's definition SHADOWS the method in scope, so the occurrence is typed against
-- the standalone scheme whose declared domain (`Int`) is NOT the actual receiver.
-- Returning the route ref lets inferDefinerShadowApp record the REAL argument mono
-- for per-receiver routing (`Box` has a `Sizeable` impl → method; `Int` → standalone).
-- P0-18 (importer residual): on the EMIT path a definer shadow's bare name is NOT
-- in definerShadowNamesRef when the standalone is IMPORTED from another module (that
-- ref is keyed on module-local funDefs).  But the mark pass ALREADY seeded such an
-- occurrence's route with the mangled standalone symbol (`RLocal <sym>`), so a
-- non-empty route symbol is an equally-good "this is a shadow" signal that works
-- cross-module.  Firing here routes the occurrence through inferDefinerShadowApp,
-- which types it against the standalone scheme and records the per-receiver mono —
-- so an importer shadow on a live-impl receiver dispatches to the impl, and one on a
-- no-impl receiver falls back to the standalone (RLocal <sym>), exactly like a
-- definer shadow.  On the un-mangled run/check path the route is RNone (sym ""), so
-- this branch is inert there and the ordinary definerShadowNames branch governs.
definerShadowArgHead : Expr -> Option (String, Ref Route, Ref (List Route))
definerShadowArgHead (ELoc _ f) = definerShadowArgHead f
-- ⚠️ #54: the two disjuncts carry DIFFERENT gates, because they serve different shadow
-- KINDS — this predicate is a Fork-1 boundary, not an arity test.  `isDefinerShadow` is
-- typaram-agnostic; `importerShadowOnEmitPath` is scoped to single-typaram interfaces (see
-- its header).  Spelling the definer disjunct as `isDefinerShadow` — rather than inlining
-- its conjuncts here — is deliberate: that function is ALSO what resolveRLocalSite routes
-- on, and "the typing entry point and the route stamper must apply the same gate" is the
-- P0-20 invariant.  Sharing the symbol makes them impossible to drift.
definerShadowArgHead (EMethodAt name tagRef implRef _)
  | isDefinerShadow name || importerShadowOnEmitPath name tagRef =
    Some (name, tagRef, implRef)
  | otherwise = None
definerShadowArgHead _ = None

-- #54: the SECOND disjunct of definerShadowArgHead — the IMPORTER-on-emit signal, kept
-- separate because it carries a DIFFERENT gate from the definer one.  The mark pass seeds
-- an importer shadow's route with the provider's mangled standalone symbol, so a non-empty
-- route symbol says "this is a shadow" cross-module, where definerShadowNamesRef (keyed on
-- module-local funDefs) cannot.  It stays scoped to singleTyparamIfaceMethod because what
-- it feeds is Fork 1's per-receiver rule; the definer disjunct (isDefinerShadow) does not,
-- because the S2 inversion never queries the impl universe.  On the un-mangled run/check
-- path the route is RNone (sym ""), so this is inert there.
importerShadowOnEmitPath : String -> Ref Route -> Bool
importerShadowOnEmitPath name tagRef = singleTyparamIfaceMethod name
  && routeLocalSym tagRef.value != ""

-- P0-18: type `size (Box 3)` exactly as the ordinary path (against the standalone
-- scheme the env rebound `size` to, preserving program types), but record the
-- ACTUAL argument mono (`Box`) as this site's receiver so resolveRLocalSite routes
-- per-receiver: a receiver head WITH an impl of the shadowed interface dispatches to
-- the method (route left untouched → eval arg-tag / emit RKey), one WITHOUT an impl
-- stays the standalone (RLocal).  suppressRLocalRecord stops recordRLocalSite from
-- ALSO pushing the standalone's declared domain (which would mis-route `Box`→`Int`).
inferDefinerShadowApp : TcEnv -> String -> Ref Route -> Ref (List Route) -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferDefinerShadowApp env name tagRef implRef f x =
  let sym = routeLocalSym tagRef.value
  -- P0-19: capture the application's location BEFORE inferring the argument (which
  -- moves currentLoc), so a standalone-domain mismatch reports at the call site.
  let dloc = currentLoc.value
  -- P0-19 d8 (S2/S6): infer the argument FIRST so the head scheme is chosen PER
  -- RECEIVER.  A receiver with a VISIBLE impl of the shadowed interface (impl universe
  -- is GLOBAL — shadowKeyTableRef spans local ∪ imported ∪ prelude) DISPATCHES → type
  -- the head against the METHOD scheme; else it is the STANDALONE.  Without this the
  -- mangled emit path (sym != "") ALWAYS typed against the standalone scheme
  -- (`Int -> Int`), so a definer shadow whose impl is IMPORTED (`size (Box 3)`, d8)
  -- rejected `Int vs Box` at build/run instead of dispatching to the imported impl.
  let xt = infer env x
  -- P0-19 (constrained-receiver arm): an ungrounded receiver that is a dict-bound
  -- constraint var dispatches through that dict — it must neither be typed against the
  -- standalone scheme nor stamped RLocal (which would send eval/emit to the standalone
  -- and, e.g., apply `sz n = n + 1` to a Bool).
  let isDictVar = definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt
  -- P0-20: ground an ungrounded receiver to the standalone's declared domain BEFORE the
  -- dispatch decision, so typecheck asks the impl question about the SAME head the
  -- post-inference route resolver will.  See groundShadowReceiver.
  let _ = groundShadowReceiver (shadowDomainFor env name sym) xt isDictVar dloc
  let dispatches = definerReceiverDispatches name xt
  -- #158 PR4: SAVE/RESTORE the three toggles around the head computation (restore to the
  -- saved value, NOT unconditional False) so a nested definer-shadow head re-entering this
  -- bracket inside `ft` cannot clobber an outer True on its way out.
  let savedRLocal = toggles.value.suppressRLocalRecord.value
  let savedArgStamp = toggles.value.suppressArgStamp.value
  let savedShadowHead = toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef.value
  let _ = setRef toggles.value.suppressRLocalRecord True
  let _ = setRef toggles.value.suppressArgStamp True
  -- P0-19 d4b: this is an application head (marked EMethodAt on run/build) → keep the
  -- method scheme for per-receiver dispatch; suppress the value-position standalone pin.
  let _ = setRef toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef True
  -- P0-18: for the STANDALONE arm, type the head against the STANDALONE scheme.  On the
  -- un-mangled run/check path `infer env f` already resolves the bare name to the
  -- standalone (which shadows the method in scope).  On the EMIT path the bare name
  -- resolves to the interface METHOD scheme instead (the standalone was renamed to
  -- `<mid>__name`), whose result loses the concrete element type — e.g. Map's `toList`
  -- would type `List a` not `List (k, v)`, so a downstream `debug (toList m)` resolves
  -- the WRONG element dict and SIGSEGVs.  When we carry the mangled symbol, instantiate
  -- its (standalone) scheme directly so the impl-body types exactly as the run path
  -- does.  For the DISPATCH arm, type against the METHOD scheme (`definerShadowHeadType
  -- env ""`) so a live-impl receiver unifies and the result comes from the method.
  -- DISPATCH arm: the interface METHOD scheme, fetched EXPLICITLY from
  -- methodIfaceParamsRef (which spans imported interfaces) — NOT `infer env f`, because
  -- on the multi-module path the env rebinds the bare method name to the LOCAL
  -- standalone (`Int -> Int`), so inferMethodAt's lookupVar would hand back `Int -> Int`
  -- and reject a live-impl receiver (`Box`).  STANDALONE arm: the standalone scheme
  -- (mangled sym on the emit path preserves the concrete result type).
  let ft = if dispatches then shadowVarHeadMethodScheme env name f else definerShadowHeadType env sym f
  let _ = setRef toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef savedShadowHead
  let _ = setRef toggles.value.suppressRLocalRecord savedRLocal
  let _ = setRef toggles.value.suppressArgStamp savedArgStamp
  -- S-1: on the STANDALONE arm ONLY, compute that standalone's own `=>`-constraint
  -- dicts (and record its call obligations).  The DISPATCH arm typed `ft` against the
  -- interface METHOD scheme, whose dicts are the method's, not the standalone's — asking
  -- there would be meaningless.  A dict-var receiver dispatches through its dict and is
  -- likewise not the standalone.  ([], []) for an unconstrained standalone.
  let dicts = if dispatches || isDictVar then ([], [])
    else shadowStandaloneDicts env name sym xt
  -- Route by the ACTUAL argument type on BOTH the eval path (pendingRLocalSites,
  -- read by resolveRLocalSites) and the emit path (pendingArgStamps, read by
  -- resolveArgStamps — only recorded when this method arg-dispatches, i.e. on the
  -- build path).  xt grounds to the real receiver post-inference; the two default
  -- pushes (recordRLocalSite / recordArgStamp) that would carry the standalone's
  -- declared domain are suppressed above.
  -- ...EXCEPT for a dict-bound constraint-var receiver: resolveRLocalSite's ungrounded
  -- arm stamps RLocal unconditionally, which would route a genuine dict dispatch to the
  -- shadowing standalone.  Not recording the site leaves the route for ordinary dispatch
  -- (RNone → eval arg-tag / emit RKey via resolveArgStamps).
  -- P0-20: the route FOLLOWS THE ARM (`not dispatches` ⇒ forceLocal).  `ft` above has
  -- already committed this site's TYPE, so re-deriving its route from a receiver mono
  -- that may ground LATER (Num defaulting on an unsignatured standalone) is exactly how
  -- `check` and the binary came to disagree.  A dispatching receiver still re-derives
  -- (its head is already ground; RKey comes from resolveArgStamps on the emit path).
  let _ = if isDictVar then ()
    else setRef perRun.value.pendingRLocalSites
      (PendingEntry name tagRef xt "" (SKRLocal (routeLocalSym tagRef.value) (not dispatches) (fst dicts) (snd dicts))
        ::perRun.value.pendingRLocalSites.value)
  let _ = match argDispatchOf name
    -- #609: the SKArg full mono is the DISPATCH arm's method scheme (`ft`) — the
    -- only arm whose route this stamp decides.  On the STANDALONE arm `ft` is the
    -- standalone's own scheme, which is not an interface-method occurrence at all;
    -- pass the receiver mono `xt` so ifaceParamMonos fails its shape match and the
    -- selector falls back to the singleton goal, exactly as before #609.
    Some _ => setRef perRun.value.pendingArgStamps (PendingEntry name tagRef xt perRun.value.currentFn.value (SKArg implRef (if dispatches then ft else xt))::perRun.value.pendingArgStamps.value)
    None => ()
  -- P0-19: on the UN-MANGLED path (sym == "") `ft` is the permissive METHOD scheme
  -- (`a -> Int`), so `inferApp ft xt` would accept ANY receiver.  When this
  -- occurrence resolves to the STANDALONE — a grounded receiver head with no visible
  -- impl of the shadowed interface, or an ungrounded receiver (S5) — impose the
  -- standalone's DECLARED domain on the argument, so a domain mismatch (`size "hi"`,
  -- or a wrapper generalized over the shadow receiver then called at a live-impl type)
  -- becomes a located reject instead of build-time garbage.  A grounded receiver WITH
  -- an impl still dispatches (no enforcement) — that arm is untouched.  On the mangled
  -- emit path (sym != "") `ft` is already the standalone scheme (definerShadowHeadType),
  -- which enforces the domain itself, so this stays inert there.
  let _ = if sym == "" then enforceStandaloneDomain env name xt dloc else ()
  inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) ft xt

-- S1/S2 (THE INVERSION, 2026-07-14): is [name] a DEFINER shadow — a top-level fn defined in
-- THIS module that reuses a single-param interface method's name?  This is the exact
-- predicate that decides whether the inversion applies (Fork 1: definer-only).
--
-- ⚠️ It is NOT the same question as "did we reach inferDefinerShadowApp".  That entry point
-- (definerShadowArgHead) ALSO fires for an IMPORTER shadow on the mangled emit path, where
-- the mark pass seeded the route with the provider's mangled symbol (`routeLocalSym != ""`)
-- — see its header.  Inverting on that signal would invert importer shadows too, which
-- Fork 1 forbids: it breaks the everyday `import map` pattern (`isEmpty [1,2]` must still
-- reach `Foldable.isEmpty`).  definerShadowNamesRef holds bare names on EVERY path — the
-- emit path recovers a mangled local funDef through the mangle map (buildDefinerShadows) —
-- and never holds an imported standalone, so it is the reliable definer/importer test.
--
-- The ifaceMethodName conjunct mirrors definerShadowVarHead / definerShadowArgHead's
-- definer disjunct verbatim, so every entry point agrees on which occurrences the
-- machinery owns.  ✅ S-3 / row 26 CLOSED (#54): a multi-TYPARAM interface
-- (`interface Ix a i`) is now IN scope.  The gate used to be singleParamIfaceMethod (a
-- typaram COUNT test), which excused `Ix a i` from every typing entry point and left its
-- occurrences to ordinary dispatch — check/build kept the pre-inversion per-receiver
-- answer while eval resolved the bare name lexically and E-PANICked (SHADOW-SEMANTICS.md
-- S8; the S7 violation d11 pinned).
isDefinerShadow : String -> Bool
isDefinerShadow name = ifaceMethodName name
  && contains name perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef.value

-- S2 (THE INVERSION, 2026-07-14; SHADOW-INVERSION-DESIGN.md): True iff the shadow [name]
-- applied to a receiver of type [xt] should DISPATCH to the interface method rather than
-- call the standalone.
--
-- ⚠️ FOR A DEFINER SHADOW THE IMPL UNIVERSE IS NO LONGER CONSULTED.  It used to be: a
-- receiver whose head tycon had a visible impl of the shadowed interface dispatched, and the
-- standalone was only a FALLBACK for a no-impl head.  That rule silently ERASED the user's
-- function —
--
--   eq : List Int -> List Int -> Bool
--   eq a b = True
--   main = println (debug (eq [1] [2]))   -- printed False: `impl Eq List` won
--
-- — and `check --types` did not even report the user's signature.  Since the prelude declares
-- ~49 method names over every common type (`map`, `filter`, `length`, `compare`, `eq`, `min`,
-- `empty`, `toList`, `display`, …), that made the most natural names in the language into
-- landmines.  A top-level name written in a module is now THAT MODULE'S NAME: a definer
-- shadow is the standalone, unconditionally, and the argument must type against the
-- standalone's declared domain (a mismatch is a located reject — S2/S3/S6).
--
-- The ONE carve-out (S5, Fork 2): a receiver that is a dict-bound `=>` constraint var of the
-- enclosing function still DISPATCHES.  Writing `Sz a => …` is an explicit, written-down
-- request to resolve through the interface, and there is currently no other way to name the
-- method inside a shadowing module (SHADOW-SEMANTICS.md §1.1).  A grounded head never
-- normalizes to a TVar, so it correctly answers False for every concrete receiver.
--
-- An IMPORTER shadow reaching here (via definerShadowArgHead's mangled-sym arm) keeps the
-- OLD per-receiver rule — Fork 1.
definerReceiverDispatches : String -> Mono -> Bool
definerReceiverDispatches name xt
  | isDefinerShadow name = definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt
  | otherwise = match headTyconMono xt
    Some head =>
      implExistsForHead perRun.value.shadowKeyTableRef.value name head
    None => definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt

-- P0-19 (constrained-receiver arm): True iff an UNGROUNDED definer-shadow receiver is
-- one of the ENCLOSING group's `=>`-constraint variables for the SHADOWED interface (or
-- a superinterface of it).  Such an occurrence dispatches through that dict — it is a
-- METHOD call, not the standalone — so it must be typed against the method scheme and
-- must NOT have the standalone's declared domain imposed on it.
--
-- Without this, ANY constrained function that calls the method on its own dict-bound
-- var, in a program where some top-level fn shadows that method's name, is silently
-- monomorphised to the shadow's domain.  That includes the PRELUDE's own bodies: on the
-- flat single-file path `prog` is core ++ user, so a user `eq : Int -> Int -> Int`
-- made core's `neq : Eq a => a -> a -> Bool` (`neq x y = not (eq x y)`) infer `eq x y :
-- Int` and reject with `Type mismatch: Bool vs Int` at the top of the user's file.
definerReceiverIsDictVar : String -> Mono -> Bool
definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt = match normalize xt
  TVar cell => match omLookup name perRun.value.methodIfaceParamsRef.value
    Some (mIface, _, _) => dictVarSatisfiesIface mIface (tyvarId cell) perRun.value.groupConstraintMonosRef.value
    None => False
  _ => False

-- does any of the group's constraint vars BOTH normalize to tyvar [id] AND carry an
-- interface whose (self + transitive supers) closure includes [mIface]?
dictVarSatisfiesIface : String -> Int -> List (String, Mono) -> Bool
dictVarSatisfiesIface _ _ [] = False
dictVarSatisfiesIface mIface id ((cIface, cm)::rest)
  | monoIsTyvarId cm id && contains mIface (ifaceSelfAndSupers cIface) = True
  | otherwise = dictVarSatisfiesIface mIface id rest

monoIsTyvarId : Mono -> Int -> Bool
monoIsTyvarId m id = match normalize m
  TVar cell => tyvarId cell == id
  _ => False

-- [iface] plus its transitive `requires` supers (a `Ord a =>` constraint also supplies
-- the `Eq` dict).  Reads superDeclsRef, the program's interface decls; NOT gated on
-- userIfaceNamesRef (unlike superSlotsOf, whose gate is about dict SLOTS, not about
-- which interfaces a constraint satisfies) — a PRELUDE super chain satisfies the
-- superinterface just as much, it is simply serviced by arg-tag dispatch.
ifaceSelfAndSupers : String -> List String
ifaceSelfAndSupers iface = supersFix [iface] [iface]

supersFix : List String -> List String -> List String
supersFix acc [] = acc
supersFix acc frontier =
  let next = removeAllS acc (dedup (flatMap directSupers frontier))
  supersFix (acc ++ next) next

directSupers : String -> List String
directSupers iface = match ifaceSupersOf driverState.value.superDeclsRef.value iface
  None => []
  Some (_, supers) => map superIfaceName supers

superIfaceName : Super -> String
superIfaceName (Super n _) = n

-- P0-19: impose a definer-shadow standalone's declared domain on the actual argument
-- [xt] WHEN the occurrence resolves to the standalone (RLocal).  No declared signature ⇒
-- nothing to enforce (permissive fallback, unchanged).  [dloc] re-points currentLoc at the
-- application so a mismatch localizes to the call, not the argument.
--
-- S2 (THE INVERSION): a definer shadow now ALWAYS resolves to the standalone, so its domain
-- is ALWAYS enforced.  This used to skip enforcement whenever the receiver's grounded head
-- had a visible impl of the shadowed interface — that head dispatched, so no domain applied.
-- That skip is precisely what let `eq [1] [2]` sail through `check` against the PRELUDE's
-- `Eq.eq` while the user's `eq : List Int -> List Int -> Bool` was discarded.  The only
-- surviving exemption is the Fork-2 dict-var carve-out: imposing a concrete domain on the
-- enclosing group's `=>`-constraint var would monomorphise its written signature.
enforceStandaloneDomain : TcEnv -> String -> Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
enforceStandaloneDomain _ name xt dloc = match standaloneShadowDomain name
  None => ()
  Some dom =>
    if definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt then
      ()
    else
      unifyStandaloneDomain dom xt dloc

-- P0-20 (SILENT WRONG ANSWER, `eq 1 2` → the binary printed 0): typecheck's
-- dispatch-vs-standalone decision and the POST-inference route resolver
-- (resolveRLocalSite) must ask the S2 impl question about the SAME receiver head.
--
-- They did not.  A definer-shadow receiver that is not yet grounded AT INFERENCE TIME —
-- a NUMERIC LITERAL is the everyday case, it is `Num a => a`, not `Int` — took the
-- ungrounded arm (S5 ⇒ standalone) and was typed against the standalone's scheme.  That
-- typing then UNIFIES the standalone's declared domain into the receiver, so by the time
-- resolveRLocalSite runs the receiver IS grounded — to `Int` — and `Int` has `impl Eq
-- Int`, so the route resolver left the site to DISPATCH.  Result: `check` typed
-- `eq 1 2 : Int` (the user's `eq : Int -> Int -> Int`) while eval/emit called the
-- interface method and produced a `Bool` — `run` panicked (`intToString: not an Int`)
-- and `build` SILENTLY PRINTED 0.  Only literal receivers were affected: `f k = eq k 1`
-- (k : Int, already grounded) dispatched consistently on all three paths.
--
-- Fix: perform S5's monomorphisation FIRST (it is the same unification the standalone
-- arm would have done, only earlier), then decide.  With the receiver grounded to `Int`,
-- S2 sees the impl and DISPATCHES on every path — check == run == build.  A grounded
-- receiver, a dict-bound one, and an unsignatured standalone (no domain to ground to)
-- are all untouched; so is a standalone whose domain head has NO impl of the shadowed
-- interface (map's `toList : Map k v -> …` — there is deliberately no `impl Foldable
-- Map`), which is every definer shadow in the tree.
--
-- [dom] MUST come from shadowDomainFor, not standaloneShadowDomain: on the MANGLED emit
-- path the standalone was renamed `<mid>__eq`, so the bare-name sig table is empty there
-- and grounding would be silently inert — which is not a no-op, it flips the emit path to
-- the STANDALONE arm while check/run dispatch.  (Observed: `build` printed 3, `run`
-- printed False.)  Same bug, other direction.
groundShadowReceiver : Option Mono -> Mono -> Bool -> Option Loc -> Unit
groundShadowReceiver dom xt isDictVar dloc
  | isDictVar = ()
  | isSome (headTyconMono xt) = ()
  | otherwise = match dom
    None => ()
    Some d => unifyStandaloneDomain d xt dloc

-- P0-20: the definer-shadow standalone's declared domain, resolved on EITHER path.
-- Un-mangled run/check (sym == ""): the module's own sig table, keyed by the bare name.
-- Mangled emit (sym != ""): private_mangle renamed the standalone to `<mid>__name`, so
-- definerShadowSigsRef has no `name` entry — take the domain off the env binding for the
-- mangled symbol, which is the very scheme definerShadowHeadType types this site against.
shadowDomainFor : TcEnv -> String -> String -> Option Mono
shadowDomainFor _ name "" = standaloneShadowDomain name
shadowDomainFor env _ sym = match lookupVar env sym
  None => None
  Some s => match normalize (instantiate s)
    TFun a _ _ => Some a
    _ => None

-- unify the standalone domain against the argument at the call's location.
unifyStandaloneDomain : Mono -> Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
unifyStandaloneDomain dom xt dloc =
  let _ = setRef currentLoc dloc
  unify dom xt

-- a definer-shadow standalone's declared parameter type, freshly instantiated (so a
-- polymorphic standalone domain does not leak vars across sites).  None when there is
-- no top-level signature, or the signature is not a function type.
standaloneShadowDomain : String -> Option Mono
standaloneShadowDomain name = match lookupAssoc name perRun.value.definerShadowSigsRef.value
  None => None
  Some ty => match normalize (instantiate (sigToScheme ty))
    TFun a _ _ => Some a
    _ => None

-- P0-19 d4b (S4): a definer-shadow occurrence in bare VALUE position (NOT the head of
-- its own application — shadowHeadCtxRef is False) denotes the STANDALONE.  Returns the
-- standalone's declared scheme freshly instantiated so a HOF sees `Int -> Int`, not the
-- permissive method scheme `a -> Int` — making `map size [Box …]` a located reject
-- (Box vs Int) while `map size [1,2,3]` still accepts.  Only fires for a single-param
-- interface-method name that is a module-local standalone WITH a declared signature;
-- everything else (app head, non-shadow, unsignatured standalone) returns None → the
-- ordinary env lookup / method scheme, unchanged.
-- #411 (row 21, S4): the IMPORTER half of the same cell.  S4 is not keyed on where the
-- standalone lives — a value-position shadow denotes the standalone whether this module
-- DEFINED it or IMPORTED it — so the definer arm below is followed by the importer arm,
-- reading the imported standalone's scheme out of shadowStandaloneSchemesRef (the same
-- scheme inferShadowApp's standalone arm uses, so `check` and the applied path cannot
-- drift).  That ref is populated ONLY on the Module path (checkModuleFullImpl), so the
-- single-file Flat path finds it empty → None → byte-identical.
--
-- ⚠️ FORK 1 IS NOT TOUCHED.  This arm is gated on shadowHeadCtxRef = False, i.e. bare
-- VALUE position only; every APPLIED importer occurrence goes through inferShadowApp,
-- which now flags its head (inferShadowAppHead) exactly as the definer path always did.
-- So per-receiver importer dispatch (i4's `isEmpty [1, 2]` → `Foldable.isEmpty`) is
-- unchanged — rows 15-20/22 do not move.
--
-- #54: the definer arm is gated on ifaceMethodName (typaram-agnostic) and the importer arm
-- on singleTyparamIfaceMethod (Fork 1's per-receiver scope) — the guards are now per-arm,
-- where they used to share one leading singleParamIfaceMethod test.
maybeStandaloneValueMono : String -> Option Mono
maybeStandaloneValueMono name
  | toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef.value = None
  | ifaceMethodName name && contains name perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef.value = standaloneShadowScheme name
  | not (singleTyparamIfaceMethod name) = None
  | otherwise = importerStandaloneValueScheme name

-- #410/#669 (S4, EMIT path only): the sym-recovering peer of maybeStandaloneValueMono,
-- for the marked (EMethodAt) value-position occurrence whose bare name maybeStandaloneValueMono
-- can no longer see because the mangler renamed the definer standalone's funDef to
-- `<mid>__name`.  The signal is PER-OCCURRENCE, not graph-wide: rewriteArgScoped (:7937)
-- seeds the tagRef route with `RLocal <mangled>` for EXACTLY the value-position standalone
-- shadows (the `sm` mangled-shadow map), while an ordinary return/arg-position method value
-- gets `RNone` — so a non-empty routeLocalSym is the mark pass's own "this occurrence is a
-- standalone shadow" verdict, and keying on it (not on the whole-graph `standaloneValuesRef`)
-- is what keeps this from pinning the compiler's genuine method-values and breaking dispatch.
-- The scheme is recovered by `standaloneSchemeFor`: an IMPORTER shadow via
-- shadowStandaloneSchemesRef, a DEFINER shadow via the env under that mangled sym — the same
-- accessor recordRLocalSite already trusts to compute this site's RLocal dicts.  Value-position
-- ONLY (shadowHeadCtxRef False, mirroring maybeStandaloneValueMono's first guard); an
-- application head is bracketed True by inferShadowAppHead / inferDefinerShadowApp and keeps
-- ordinary per-receiver dispatch.  None (⇒ caller keeps the method scheme) when the route
-- carries no mangled sym, or when the standalone scheme is unavailable.
-- ⚠️ #724: the gate is PER SHADOW KIND (emitValueShadowGate), mirroring the check-path
-- maybeStandaloneValueMono's per-arm split exactly — NOT one blanket singleTyparamIfaceMethod.
-- The classifier is isDefinerShadow (`contains name definerShadowNamesRef`, the SAME test the
-- check path uses), NOT shadowStandaloneSchemesRef membership: that ref is standaloneSchemeFor's
-- scheme-SOURCE selector and an importer occurrence is not reliably in it at this pin, so keying
