-- on it mis-grounds i10's multi-typaram importer app head to a garbage-pointer build.
--   • DEFINER arm (isDefinerShadow): typaram-agnostic, mirroring the check-path definer arm
--     (which grounds a MULTI-typaram definer value position — #724's exact skew: check grounded,
--     emit declined, so `map size [1,2,3]` stamped a null Display element dict and SIGSEGVd).
--     Safe at multi-typaram width because a DEFINER app head is ALWAYS bracketed: it is caught
--     by definerShadowArgHead's isDefinerShadow disjunct (typaram-agnostic) →
--     inferDefinerShadowApp, which sets shadowHeadCtxRef True and types the head via
--     shadowVarHeadMethodScheme / definerShadowHeadType — NEVER through inferMethodAt.  So the
--     only definer occurrence reaching here with shadowHeadCtxRef False is a genuine value
--     position, which is precisely what must ground (d22z is the app-head control).
--   • otherwise (importer / non-shadow): singleTyparamIfaceMethod is REQUIRED, not incidental.
--     At MULTI-typaram width the importer app-head path (`get (Box 3) 1`, interface `Ix a i`,
--     i10) uses ORDINARY dispatch, so an app head can reach inferMethodAt with shadowHeadCtxRef
--     False AND a mangled route sym — indistinguishable from a value position by those two
--     signals alone.  The check-path importer arm is singleTyparamIfaceMethod-gated (Fork 1)
--     for exactly this reason; keeping the guard here leaves i10's per-receiver dispatch
--     (4 then 3) intact.  Sz/Sizeable are single-typaram, so #410/#669 still pin.
--
-- #410/#669 DIRECTION-2 (deliberately NOT taken): the alternative floor was to leave the type
-- alone and instead make a container element route that resolves to RNone from a genuinely-free
-- var a LOUD `T-AMBIGUOUS-INSTANCE` rather than a silent null dict.  Rejected: an empty
-- container's RNone element is BENIGN (never dereferenced) — `main = println []` is a VALID
-- program that stamps exactly that RNone element — so a fail-loud rule would reject valid code,
-- and the `Undetermined`/KeepNone design (:9899) forbids element-position errors for this reason.
-- Grounding the shadow occurrence's TYPE here (direction 1) closes the actual SIGSEGV vector at
-- its source without that hazard, so the loud-floor was not needed.
maybeStandaloneValueMonoEmit : TcEnv -> String -> Ref Route -> Option Mono
maybeStandaloneValueMonoEmit env name tagRef
  | toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef.value = None
  | routeLocalSym tagRef.value == "" = None
  | not (emitValueShadowGate name) = None
  | otherwise =
    map instantiate (standaloneSchemeFor env name (routeLocalSym tagRef.value))

-- #724: the per-shadow-kind gate for the emit-path value-position pin, mirroring the
-- check-path maybeStandaloneValueMono's per-arm split (definer arm typaram-agnostic; importer
-- arm singleTyparam-scoped, Fork 1).  The definer/importer classifier is isDefinerShadow —
-- `contains name definerShadowNamesRef`, which holds BARE names on EVERY path (the emit path
-- recovers the mangled local funDef through the mangle map, buildDefinerShadows) and NEVER
-- holds an imported standalone, so it is the reliable test (its header, :5717).  ⚠️ It is NOT
-- shadowStandaloneSchemesRef membership: that ref is standaloneSchemeFor's SCHEME-SOURCE
-- selector, not a definer/importer classifier — an importer occurrence is not reliably in it
-- at this pin, so keying on it mis-grounded i10's multi-typaram importer APP head to a
-- garbage-pointer build (the P0-20 shape).
--   • DEFINER shadow (isDefinerShadow): admit typaram-agnostically.  Safe at multi-typaram
--     width because a definer app head is ALWAYS bracketed by inferDefinerShadowApp
--     (definerShadowArgHead's isDefinerShadow disjunct fires typaram-agnostically), so it
--     reaches maybeStandaloneValueMonoEmit with shadowHeadCtxRef True and is declined by that
--     function's first guard — the ONLY definer occurrence reaching the pin with
--     shadowHeadCtxRef False is a genuine value position, which is what must ground.
--   • otherwise (importer or non-shadow): keep singleTyparamIfaceMethod — a MULTI-typaram
--     importer app head (`get (Box 3) 1`, i10) uses ORDINARY dispatch and is NOT bracketed,
--     so it too can reach the pin with shadowHeadCtxRef False and a mangled route sym; the
--     singleTyparam gate is what keeps it (and its per-receiver dispatch, 4 then 3) intact,
--     exactly as the check-path importer arm does.
emitValueShadowGate : String -> Bool
emitValueShadowGate name
  | isDefinerShadow name = True
  | otherwise = singleTyparamIfaceMethod name

-- #411: is [name] an occurrence whose TYPE maybeStandaloneValueMono pins to the
-- standalone?  ⭐ THE ROUTE MUST FOLLOW THE ARM (the P0-20 lesson): a site typed against
-- the standalone must ROUTE to the standalone, or type and route disagree — which is this
-- arc's recurring bug class.  So recordRLocalSite stamps such a site forceLocal, skipping
-- resolveRLocalSite's per-receiver query entirely.  Under S4 there is no receiver to
-- dispatch on, so the query was never meaningful for a value position in the first place.
--
-- For a DEFINER shadow this is behavior-preserving both ways: resolveRLocalSite's grounded
-- arm already forced RLocal (`not (isDefinerShadow name)` is False ⇒ stampRLocalOrFallback
-- False), and its ungrounded arm already stamped RLocal for definerShadowNamesRef members.
-- For an IMPORTER shadow it is the #411 fix: the grounded arm used to let a live impl steal
-- the value (row 21b), and the ungrounded arm left the route UNSTAMPED (row 21a) — which is
-- why eval arg-tag-dispatched into the Box impl and the emitter, having no standalone
-- fallback, panicked `no impl of method 'size' for type 'Int'`.
--
-- Mirrors maybeStandaloneValueMono's guards exactly; keep the two in lockstep.
standaloneValuePinned : String -> Bool
standaloneValuePinned name
  | toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef.value = False
  | ifaceMethodName name && contains name perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef.value = isSome (standaloneShadowScheme name)
  | not (singleTyparamIfaceMethod name) = False
  | otherwise = isSome (importerStandaloneValueScheme name)

-- #411: the IMPORTED standalone's full scheme, freshly instantiated (`Int -> Int`), for a
-- bare value-position occurrence.  Peer of standaloneShadowScheme's definer arm; None when
-- the name is not an importer shadow of this module, leaving the ordinary env lookup (the
-- permissive method scheme) exactly as before.
importerStandaloneValueScheme : String -> Option Mono
importerStandaloneValueScheme name =
  map
    instantiate
    (lookupAssoc name perRun.value.shadowStandaloneSchemesRef.value)

-- P0-19: a definer-shadow standalone's full declared scheme (`Int -> Int`), freshly
-- instantiated.  None when the standalone has no top-level signature (nothing to pin —
-- the permissive fallback stays).  Used both for value-position pinning (d4b) and to
-- type a standalone-fallback application against the standalone's result type (d8).
standaloneShadowScheme : String -> Option Mono
standaloneShadowScheme name = map
  (ty => instantiate (sigToScheme ty))
  (lookupAssoc name perRun.value.definerShadowSigsRef.value)

-- P0-19: peel an application head to a definer-shadow interface-method occurrence on
-- the UN-MARKED path (the `check` pipeline runs no marker, so the head is a bare
-- `EVar`, not the `EMethodAt` that definerShadowArgHead matches on run/build).  Same
-- guard as definerShadowArgHead's DEFINER disjunct: an interface method (of an interface
-- of ANY typaram arity, #54) whose name is a module-local standalone
-- (definerShadowNamesRef).
definerShadowVarHead : Expr -> Option String
definerShadowVarHead (ELoc _ f) = definerShadowVarHead f
definerShadowVarHead (EVar name)
  | ifaceMethodName name && contains name perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef.value = Some name
  | otherwise = None
definerShadowVarHead _ = None

-- S2 (THE INVERSION): type an un-marked (check-path) definer-shadow application against
-- the module's OWN standalone, unconditionally.  Peer of inferDefinerShadowApp (the marked
-- run/build path); the two must agree cell-for-cell or `check` and the binary diverge (S7),
-- which is the bug class this whole arc keeps producing.
--
-- The receiver's impl universe is no longer consulted (see definerReceiverDispatches).  The
-- sole dispatch arm left is the Fork-2 carve-out: a dict-bound `=>` constraint var, which is
-- an explicit written request to resolve through the interface.  Everything else — grounded
-- live-impl head included — types against the LOCAL standalone scheme, so a domain mismatch
-- (`size (Box 3)` against `size : Int -> Int`) is a located reject at the call, not a silent
-- dispatch that erases the user's function.
inferDefinerShadowVarApp : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferDefinerShadowVarApp env name f x =
  let dloc = currentLoc.value
  let xt = infer env x
  let isDictVar = definerReceiverIsDictVar name xt
  -- P0-20: ground an ungrounded receiver to the standalone's declared domain BEFORE the
  -- dispatch decision (see groundShadowReceiver).  Still load-bearing under the inversion:
  -- it is what gives a bare `Num` literal receiver (`eq 1 2`) the standalone's domain
  -- rather than leaving it open for Num-defaulting to ground LATER, behind typecheck's back.
  let _ = groundShadowReceiver (standaloneShadowDomain name) xt isDictVar dloc
  if isDictVar then
    inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) (shadowVarHeadMethodScheme env name f) xt
  else
    inferDefinerStandaloneVarApp env name f x xt dloc

-- P0-19 (d8 dispatch arm): the interface METHOD scheme for [name], from
-- methodIfaceParamsRef (which spans imported interfaces) so per-receiver dispatch works
-- even when the multi-module env has rebound the bare name to the local standalone.
-- Falls back to `infer env f` (flagged as an app head so maybeStandaloneValueMono does
-- not pin it) when the method type is unavailable.
shadowVarHeadMethodScheme : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Mono
shadowVarHeadMethodScheme env name f = match omLookup name perRun.value.methodIfaceParamsRef.value
  Some (_, _, ty) => instantiate (sigToScheme ty)
  None =>
    -- #158 PR4: save/restore, not set-False (see inferDefinerShadowApp's bracket).
    let saved = toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef.value
    let _ = setRef toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef True
    let ft = infer env f
    let _ = setRef toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef saved
    ft

-- P0-19 (standalone arm): type the application against the LOCAL standalone scheme so
-- the result is the standalone's (S2/S5); a domain mismatch (`size "hi"`) or a wrapper
-- generalized over the shadow receiver then called at a foreign type localizes to the
-- call [dloc].  No declared signature ⇒ fall back to the permissive method-scheme head
-- + enforceStandaloneDomain (the pre-P0-19 permissive behavior, unchanged).
inferDefinerStandaloneVarApp : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono -> Option Loc -> Mono
inferDefinerStandaloneVarApp env name f x xt dloc = match standaloneShadowScheme name
  Some smono =>
    let r = freshVar ()
    let eff = openRow ()
    let _ = setRef currentLoc dloc
    let _ = unify smono (TFun xt eff r)
    let _ = performEffect perRun.value.curEffect eff
    -- S-1 (REJECT direction).  standaloneShadowScheme instantiates the DECLARED SIG via
    -- sigToScheme, which DROPS the `=>` constraints entirely — so `Num String` was never
    -- asked and `check` ACCEPTED `size "hi"` against `size : Num a => a -> a`.  Re-read
    -- the constraints off the signature and record them as call obligations at the
    -- receiver, which makes it a located reject.  (This arm has a signature by
    -- construction; the no-signature arm below goes through `infer env f` → inferVarPlain
    -- → instantiateVarTracked, the ordinary obligation path.)
    let _ = recordStandaloneSigObligations name "" xt
    r
  None =>
    -- #158 PR4: save/restore, not set-False (see inferDefinerShadowApp's bracket).
    let saved = toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef.value
    let _ = setRef toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef True
    let ft = infer env f
    let _ = setRef toggles.value.shadowHeadCtxRef saved
    let _ = enforceStandaloneDomain env name xt dloc
    inferApp (envAlphaLets env) (EApp f x) ft xt

-- P0-18: the type of a definer-shadow application HEAD.  With a mangled symbol (the
-- emit path), instantiate the standalone's own scheme (bound under `<mid>__name`) so
-- the concrete result type survives; else infer the head node (the un-mangled path,
-- where the bare name already resolves to the standalone).
definerShadowHeadType : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Mono
definerShadowHeadType env "" f = infer env f
definerShadowHeadType env sym f = match lookupVar env sym
  Some s => instantiate s
  None => infer env f

-- stamp every route ref (one for the marked EMethodAt form, none for the unmarked
-- check-path EVar form) RLocal.  S-1: the standalone's own constraint dicts are NOT
-- resolvable here (inference has not solved the receiver yet) — the caller ALSO pushes
-- an RLocalSite so resolveRLocalSites re-stamps the same ref with the filled dicts
-- post-inference.  An unconstrained standalone never gets a second stamp that differs.
stampRoutes : List (Ref Route) -> Unit
stampRoutes [] = ()
stampRoutes (r::rest) =
  let _ = setRef r (RLocal (routeLocalSym r.value) [])
  stampRoutes rest

-- ── S-1: the shadowing standalone's OWN constraint dicts ──────────────────────
-- A `=>`-constrained top-level fn whose name collides with a visible interface method
-- (`eq : Num a => a -> a` vs the prelude's `Eq.eq`) sits in BOTH the shadow name set
-- and the dict name set.  The marking prePass is a first-match guard chain whose shadow
-- arm is tested FIRST, so the occurrence becomes `EMethodAt`, never `EDictAt` — and no
-- dict is ever computed for it.  But `dictPassDecl` (keyed on the same dict name set)
-- still prepends the dict PARAM to the definition.  Def arity 2, call arity 1 ⇒ the
-- call silently UNDER-APPLIES and the first real argument lands in the dict slot.
--
-- So: when a shadow occurrence resolves to the STANDALONE arm, compute that
-- standalone's constraint slots here — the shadowed interface decides WHICH function,
-- the standalone's own constraints decide WHICH DICTS (clause S9).
--
-- [xt] is the RECEIVER's mono.  We take a tracked instantiation of the standalone's own
-- scheme and unify it with `xt -> <fresh>`, which pins the standalone's constraint
-- variable to the receiver exactly as inferApp will pin the site's real head — so the
-- subst maps each constraint id to whatever that constraint variable becomes at THIS
-- call.  The fresh result var is a throwaway: it does NOT re-type the site (the caller's
-- own `ft`/`inferApp` still owns the site's type), it only ties the dict to the receiver.
--
-- ⚠️ Unify against the RECEIVER, not against the caller's head type `ft`.  On the
-- UN-MANGLED path `ft` is the permissive interface-METHOD scheme, not the standalone's
-- (the two doc-comments in inferDefinerShadowApp contradict each other on this; `:5050`
-- is the correct one).  Unifying the standalone's `a -> a` against the method's
-- `a -> a -> Bool` is an OCCURS-CHECK failure — "Cannot construct infinite type" on the
-- perfectly legal `eq : Num a => a -> a`.  The receiver is common to both.
--
-- Returns the slot-ordered (monos, ifaces) that routesOfMonosTop turns into Routes, and
-- records the call's impl obligations so `size "hi"` (⇒ `Num String`, which has no
-- impl) becomes a LOCATED REJECT at `check` instead of build-time garbage — the second,
-- independent hole in this same arm.  ([] , []) for an unconstrained standalone, which
-- SHORT-CIRCUITS BEFORE THE UNIFY, so an unconstrained shadow (all 5 of the compiler's
-- own) does not even perturb the substitution — the fixpoint provably cannot move.
shadowStandaloneDicts : TcEnv -> String -> String -> Mono -> (List Mono, List String)
shadowStandaloneDicts env name sym xt =
  -- P0-20's sym-awareness lesson, mirrored: on the mangled emit path the standalone is
  -- bound under `<mid>__name`, so a BARE-name lookup here would be silently inert and
  -- `build` would emit the un-dicted call while run/check dicted correctly — the same
  -- bug, mirrored.  shadowDomainFor exists for exactly this reason.
  shadowStandaloneDictsWith (lookupVar env (if sym == "" then name else sym)) name sym xt

-- as shadowStandaloneDicts, but with the standalone's scheme supplied explicitly.
-- The IMPORTER shadow needs this: in the importing module `env1` deliberately rebinds the
-- bare name to the polymorphic INTERFACE-METHOD scheme (that rebinding is the entire
-- reason shadowStandaloneSchemesRef exists), so `lookupVar env name` hands back the
-- METHOD — whose quantified ids are not the ones funConstraintsRef recorded for the
-- imported standalone, so every dict slot silently resolves to nothing.  That is why the
-- importer shadow was correct on `build`/`wasm` (which reach the standalone through the
-- MANGLED symbol) and still under-applied on `run`.
shadowStandaloneDictsWith : Option Scheme -> String -> String -> Mono -> (List Mono, List String)
shadowStandaloneDictsWith mscheme name sym xt =
  let _ = recordStandaloneSigObligations name sym xt
  shadowStandaloneDictSlotsAt mscheme (if sym == "" then name else sym) xt

-- the dict slots ONLY — no call obligations.
-- recordRLocalSite needs this: it fires for EVERY shadow method occurrence, at a point
-- where the standalone-vs-dispatch choice is NOT yet made (resolveRLocalSite decides it
-- post-inference from the grounded receiver).  Recording an obligation here would fire on
-- the DISPATCH arm too and spuriously reject `size (Box 3)` with `Num Box`.  When the
-- receiver does turn out to have an impl, stampRLocalOrFallback simply discards these
-- slots and the route dispatches as before.
shadowStandaloneDictSlotsAt : Option Scheme -> String -> Mono -> (List Mono, List String)
shadowStandaloneDictSlotsAt mscheme key xt =
  let slots = declaredConstraintSlots key
  -- REJECT direction — from the SIGNATURE, NOT from schemeObligationsRef.
  -- ⚠️ For a SIGNATURED binding the two obligation tables live in DIFFERENT ID SPACES:
  -- funConstraintsRef's ids (and the env scheme's quantified ids) come from
  -- sigToSchemeTvs over the declared signature, while schemeObligationsRef's come from
  -- GENERALIZATION.  Mapping the latter's ids through the former's substitution silently
  -- finds nothing — which is why the dict ROUTES resolved correctly (they use
  -- funConstraintsRef) while the obligation stayed unrecorded and `size "hi"` was
  -- ACCEPTED.  The signature is the one source both halves can agree on.
  -- the unconstrained standalone: nothing to solve, no unify, no perturbation at all.
  -- This short-circuit is what makes the change provably fixpoint-safe — all 5 of the
  -- compiler's own definer shadows are unconstrained and never reach the unify below.
  if isEmptyL (fst slots) then ([], [])
  else match mscheme
    None => ([], [])
    Some s =>
      let inst = instantiateTracked s
      let r = freshVar ()
      let eff = openRow ()
      let _ = unify (fst inst) (TFun xt eff r)
      shadowStandaloneDictSlots slots (snd inst)

-- the standalone's dict ROUTE slots for this call: its declared constraint ids mapped
-- through the call's instantiation subst.  Expanded with transitive supers EXACTLY as
-- inferDictAtFound does (WS-1b) — dictArityOf sizes the DEFINITION's leading dict params
-- from the same expanded table, so call and def must agree slot-for-slot or we have
-- merely moved the arity disagreement.  Empty on the plain `check` path
-- (funConstraintsRef is empty there) — check stamps no routes, so that is correct;
-- recordStandaloneSigObligations is what gives `check` its reject direction.
shadowStandaloneDictSlots : (List Int, List String) -> List (Int, Mono) -> (List Mono, List String)
shadowStandaloneDictSlots slots subst =
  if isEmptyL (fst slots) then ([], [])
  else
    let expanded = expandSupersPairs driverState.value.superDeclsRef.value (zipL (snd slots) (fst slots))
    (constraintMonosOf (map snd expanded) subst, map fst expanded)

-- ── S-1, REJECT direction on the plain `check` path ───────────────────────────
-- Record the standalone's declared `=>` constraints as CALL obligations, resolved from
-- its own SIGNATURE.
--
-- ⚠️ Why the SIGNATURE and not schemeObligationsRef: for a signatured binding those are
-- two different ID SPACES (sigToSchemeTvs's fresh quantification vs generalization's), so
-- schemeObligationsRef's ids are not keys of the env scheme's instantiation subst and
-- recordSchemeCallObligations silently records nothing.  That is exactly why the dict
-- ROUTES came out right (they key off funConstraintsRef, which IS the signature's id
-- space) while `check` still ACCEPTED `size "hi"` (⇒ `Num String`, which has no impl).
--
-- ⚠️ sigToSchemeTvs's `tvs` are the scheme's BOUND tyvars, and `instantiate` RE-freshens
-- them — so unifying the *instantiated* type against the receiver does not touch `tvs` at
-- all.  We must instantiateTracked and map each constraint tyvar's ID through the subst,
-- which is precisely what that function's own doc-comment prescribes ("so a caller can
-- recover the id of a specific constraint variable … a call-site instantiation's subst
-- maps them").  Getting this wrong records nothing and reads as "the fix does not work".
--
-- Keyed by the BARE name on the un-mangled run/check path and by the MANGLED symbol on
-- the emit path (sigsOf runs over the already-renamed decls there) — try both, exactly as
-- shadowDomainFor has to.
recordStandaloneSigObligations : String -> String -> Mono -> Unit
recordStandaloneSigObligations name sym xt = match standaloneShadowSigTy name sym
  None => ()
  Some ty => match sigConstraintsOf ty
    [] => ()
    cs =>
      let pr = sigToSchemeTvs ty
      let inst = instantiateTracked (fst pr)
      let r = freshVar ()
      let eff = openRow ()
      let _ = unify (fst inst) (TFun xt eff r)
      recordSigConstraintObls cs (snd pr) (snd inst)

-- the standalone's declared signature Ty, under whichever key this path stores it.
standaloneShadowSigTy : String -> String -> Option Ty
standaloneShadowSigTy name sym = match lookupAssoc name perRun.value.definerShadowSigsRef.value
  Some ty => Some ty
  None =>
    if sym == "" then
      None
    else
      lookupAssoc sym perRun.value.definerShadowSigsRef.value

-- every `=>` constraint in the signature.  Recurses the spine like constraintVarNamesTy
-- rather than matching only the top node, so an effect-annotated sig still yields them.
sigConstraintsOf : Ty -> List Constraint
sigConstraintsOf (TyConstrained cs t) = cs ++ sigConstraintsOf t
sigConstraintsOf (TyEffect _ _ t) = sigConstraintsOf t
sigConstraintsOf _ = []

-- one obligation per `=>` PREDICATE in the signature, carrying its whole argument
-- vector at THIS call's instantiation (`Ix a i` => ONE ("Ix", [ma, mi]) entry, not
-- two 1-ary entries that ask an unanswerable question about each var alone).  If ANY
-- argument fails to resolve (not a plain tyvar, or absent from either map) the whole
-- predicate records nothing — a partial vector is a DIFFERENT, wrong predicate, so
-- dropping it is the never-over-reject direction (#607).
recordSigConstraintObls : List Constraint -> List (String, Mono) -> List (Int, Mono) -> Unit
recordSigConstraintObls [] _ _ = ()
recordSigConstraintObls ((Constraint iface args)::rest) tvs subst =
  let _ = match recordSigConstraintArgs args tvs subst
    Some monos => pushCallObl (iface, monos, currentLoc.value)
    None => ()
  recordSigConstraintObls rest tvs subst

-- the constraint arguments' monos at THIS call's instantiation, in declaration order;
-- None if any argument does not resolve (see recordSigConstraintObls).  Two hops:
-- argument -> the signature's tyvar mono (constraintArgMonos), then that tyvar's id ->
-- this instantiation's mono (substMonos).  Both are all-or-nothing, so this is too.
recordSigConstraintArgs : List Ty -> List (String, Mono) -> List (Int, Mono) -> Option (List Mono)
recordSigConstraintArgs args tvs subst = match constraintArgMonos args tvs
  Some tvMonos => substMonos (map monoTyvarId tvMonos) subst
  None => None

-- the tyvar NAME a constraint argument mentions (`Num a` → "a"); None for a non-tyvar
-- argument (a concrete head), which carries no per-call obligation here.
constraintArgVarName : Ty -> Option String
constraintArgVarName (TyVar v) = Some v
constraintArgVarName _ = None

-- set currentLoc only when the lambda carried a span (mirror the ELoc arm), so
-- a bare unlocated lambda doesn't blank out an enclosing position.
setLocIfSome : Option Loc -> Unit
setLocIfSome None = ()
setLocIfSome (Some l) = setRef currentLoc (Some l)

-- peek through an ELoc wrapper to a lambda; returns its loc, params and body.
-- Non-lambda arguments (the common case) take the ordinary inference path.
lamUnderLoc : Expr -> Option (Option Loc, List Pat, Expr)
lamUnderLoc (ELam pats body) = Some (None, pats, body)
lamUnderLoc (ELoc l (ELam pats body)) = Some (Some l, pats, body)
lamUnderLoc _ = None

-- the function's first parameter type (the lambda's expected type), or a fresh
-- var when `ft` is not yet a concrete arrow (then nothing is pushed; the trailing
-- `unify ft (TFun xt …)` reconciles).
funDomain : Mono -> Mono
funDomain (TFun a _ _) = a
funDomain _ = freshVar ()

-- peel up to `n` arrow domains off the (normalized) lambda's expected type; those
-- domains are the lambda's expected PARAM types.  Stops early if it is not (yet)
-- an arrow — those params fall back to a fresh var in inferLamChecked, and the
-- trailing `unify ft (TFun xt …)` reconciles them.
expectedParamTypes : Mono -> Int -> List Mono
expectedParamTypes _ 0 = []
expectedParamTypes (TFun a _ b) n =
  a :: expectedParamTypes (normalize b) (n - 1)
expectedParamTypes _ _ = []

-- like inferLam, but each lambda param is unified with the corresponding expected
-- param type (from the function being applied) right after inferPat — so the body
-- and the hover side-channel see the expected type, not a bare fresh var.
inferLamChecked : TcEnv -> List Mono -> List Pat -> Expr -> Mono
inferLamChecked env expected pats body =
  let pr = inferPats env pats
  let _ = unifyParamsExpected (fst pr) expected
  let saved = perRun.value.curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.curEffect (openRow ())
  let bodyTy = infer (extendVars env (snd pr)) body
  let bodyEff = perRun.value.curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.curEffect saved
  arrowsWithLastEffect (fst pr) bodyEff bodyTy

-- unify each param type with its expected type, for the prefix both lists cover.
unifyParamsExpected : List Mono -> List Mono -> Unit
unifyParamsExpected (p::ps) (e::es) =
  let _ = unify p e
  unifyParamsExpected ps es
unifyParamsExpected _ _ = ()

-- Chunk C: recover the ultimate callee name of an application spine (the
-- leftmost head), stripping transparent wrappers.  `None` when the head is not
-- a plain variable (so no name to blame in an over-application message).
appSpineName : Expr -> Option String
appSpineName (ELoc _ e) = appSpineName e
appSpineName (EDoOrigin _ e) = appSpineName e
appSpineName (EAnnot e _) = appSpineName e
appSpineName (EApp f _) = appSpineName f
appSpineName (EVar n) = Some n
appSpineName (EVarAt n _) = Some n
appSpineName _ = None

-- Chunk C: number of arguments applied in this spine (`f a b c` ⇒ 3).
appSpineArgCount : Expr -> Int
appSpineArgCount (ELoc _ e) = appSpineArgCount e
appSpineArgCount (EDoOrigin _ e) = appSpineArgCount e
appSpineArgCount (EAnnot e _) = appSpineArgCount e
appSpineArgCount (EApp f _) = 1 + appSpineArgCount f
appSpineArgCount _ = 0

-- Chunk C messages (T-NOT-A-FUNCTION): a value of a ground non-function type is
-- being applied.  Base wording names the offending type; the context-aware
-- form names the over-applied callee and its arity.
notAFunctionMsg : String -> String
notAFunctionMsg tyName = "This expression has type \{tyName}, which is not a function, so it cannot be applied to an argument."

-- F-dimension actionable-fix hint appended to `notAFunctionMsg` (2026-07-04):
-- names the offending value when the spine head is a plain variable, else
-- falls back to a name-free phrasing.  No mechanical `fix` — the caller
-- decides whether to drop the argument or call a different function.
notAFunctionHint : Option String -> String
notAFunctionHint (Some name) = " '\{name}' is a value, not a function; remove the argument, or call a function here."
notAFunctionHint None = " This is a value, not a function; remove the argument, or call a function here."

overAppliedMsg : String -> Int -> Int -> String
overAppliedMsg name takes applied = "'\{name}' takes \{intToString takes} argument(s) but is applied to \{intToString applied}."

-- Batch B #6 reframe (2026-07-04): a container-consuming HOF whose next declared
-- parameter is a higher-kinded container `f a` (an unresolved tyvar head applied
-- to an element, i.e. `TApp (TVar …) elem`) is being handed a SCALAR argument —
-- a bare arity-0 type (`String`/`Int`/…) that has no element slot for `f` to
-- bind.  Left to the generic `unify (f a) String` sink it leaks a raw
-- tyvar-application `Type mismatch: a b vs String`.  This detector recognises the
-- shape UPSTREAM (in inferApp, where the callee name + arg loc are still in
-- scope) so the diagnostic can be reframed as container-vs-scalar.  SOUND: a
-- `TApp _ _ ~ TCon n` unification can only ever FAIL — a genuine container arg is
-- itself a `TApp` (`List a`, `Maybe a`, …) and would UNIFY the element, never
-- reaching a mismatch; the reframe therefore never hijacks a legitimate
-- higher-kinded mismatch.  Returns the scalar type's name when it fires.
containerParamScalarArg : Mono -> Mono -> Option String
containerParamScalarArg ft xt = match normalize ft
  TFun dom _ _ => match normalize dom
    TApp _ _ => match monoHeadCon dom
      None => match normalize xt
        TCon n => Some n
        _ => None
      Some _ => None
    _ => None
  _ => None

containerArgMsg : Option String -> String -> String
containerArgMsg fn argTy =
  let who = match fn
    Some n => "'\{n}'"
    None => "This function"
  "\{who} expects a container (like List or Array) here, but got \{argTy}\{containerArgHint argTy}"

-- Actionable-fix hint appended to `containerArgMsg`.  Special-cases `String`
-- (the most common scalar handed to `map`): a String's characters are reachable
-- via `string.toChars`, so name that conversion rather than a generic one.
containerArgHint : String -> String
containerArgHint "String" = ". Pass a List or Array; to work over a string's characters, convert it with `string.toChars` first."
containerArgHint argTy =
  ". Pass a List or Array, or convert the \{argTy} to one first."
-- P0-16 beginner call-syntax hint.  A newcomer writes `add(1, 2)` (C/Python/JS
-- call syntax) meaning `add 1 2`; in Medaka `(1, 2)` is a TUPLE, so `add(1, 2)`
-- applies `add` to ONE tuple argument → a partial application / no-impl error
-- whose message ("No impl of Display for ((Int, Int) -> (Int, Int))") is
-- inscrutable.  We DETECT the shape at the application site — a curried arity-N
-- function applied to a single arity-N tuple literal whose first param is NOT
-- itself an N-tuple — and record the residual result type as a candidate; the
-- no-impl + type-mismatch surface sites then append the tailored hint when the
-- offending type IS that residual.  Recording is generous (a legitimate partial
-- application like `map (const (1, 2)) xs` also records) but the hint is only
-- ever ATTACHED to a genuine downstream error, so a legit partial application —
-- whose residual is consumed WITHOUT error — never shows it.  (residualMono,
-- help, optional (span, replacement) fix); reset by resetState.

-- number of leading `->` arrows in a (normalized) type — its curried arity.
funArity : Mono -> Int
funArity m = match normalize m
  TFun _ _ r => 1 + funArity r
  _ => 0

-- the elements of a tuple-literal argument (through the transparent ELoc wrapper).
tupleArgElems : Expr -> Option (List Expr)
tupleArgElems (ELoc _ e) = tupleArgElems e
tupleArgElems (ETuple es) = Some es
tupleArgElems _ = None

-- True iff [ft]'s first parameter is itself an arity-[n] tuple type — the genuine
-- "the tuple IS the intended single argument" shape (e.g. `f (a, b) c = …`), which
-- must NOT be flagged.  Read on the PRISTINE ft (before inferApp unifies its domain
-- with the tuple arg), so the domain here is the declared param type.
domainIsTupleOfArity : Mono -> Int -> Bool
domainIsTupleOfArity ft n = match normalize ft
  TFun dom _ _ => match monoHeadCon dom
    Some c => c == "__tuple\{intToString n}__"
    None => False
  _ => False

-- render a tuple element to source-ish text for the `add 1 2` fix, for the simple
-- literal/var/negation cases; None for anything a full printer would be needed for.
renderCallArg : Expr -> Option String
renderCallArg (ELoc _ e) = renderCallArg e
renderCallArg (EAnnot e _) = renderCallArg e
renderCallArg (ENumLit n _ _ _) = Some (intToString n)
renderCallArg (ELit (LInt n)) = Some (intToString n)
renderCallArg (ELit (LBool True)) = Some "True"
renderCallArg (ELit (LBool False)) = Some "False"
renderCallArg (ELit (LString s)) = Some ("\"" ++ s ++ "\"")
renderCallArg (ELit (LChar c)) = Some ("'" ++ c ++ "'")
renderCallArg (EVar n) = Some n
renderCallArg (EUnOp "-" e _) = map ("-" ++ _) (renderCallArg e)
renderCallArg _ = None

-- all-or-nothing render of every tuple element.
renderCallArgs : List Expr -> Option (List String)
renderCallArgs [] = Some []
renderCallArgs (e::rest) = match renderCallArg e
  None => None
  Some s => map (s :: _) (renderCallArgs rest)

-- source span covering the whole `name(args)` call: callee start → tuple end.
tupleCallSpanLoc : Expr -> Expr -> Option Loc
tupleCallSpanLoc f x = match (exprLoc f, exprLoc x)
  (Some (Loc file sl sc _ _), Some (Loc _ _ _ el ec)) =>
    Some (Loc file sl sc el ec)
  _ => None

-- build the (help, optional fix) for a detected tuple-call.  The help names the
-- rule (Medaka calls with spaces, `(…)` is a tuple) and gives the exact rewrite;
-- when every element renders, a machine-applicable `fix` rewrites `add(1, 2)` →
-- `add 1 2` verbatim.  ERROR-QUALITY: located (caller sets loc), names the issue,
-- actionable fix, carries a code (T-NO-IMPL / T-TYPE-MISMATCH at the surface site).
buildTupleCallHint : String -> List Expr -> Expr -> Expr -> (String, Option (Loc, String))
buildTupleCallHint name es f x = match renderCallArgs es
  Some parts =>
    let argsCsv = joinWith ", " parts
    let argsSpaced = joinWith " " parts
    let spread = "\{name} \{argsSpaced}"
    let help = "Medaka calls functions with spaces, not parentheses: `\{name}(\{argsCsv})` applies `\{name}` to one tuple `(\{argsCsv})`. Did you mean `\{spread}`?"
    let fix = map (span => (span, spread)) (tupleCallSpanLoc f x)
    (help, fix)
  None =>
    let n = listLen es
    let help = "Medaka calls functions with spaces, not parentheses: `\{name}(…)` passes a single \{intToString n}-tuple, not \{intToString n} arguments. Separate them with spaces, e.g. `\{name} x y`."
    (help, None)

-- detect the tuple-call shape on the PRISTINE ft (before inferApp) and return the
-- (help, fix) to record against the residual result type, else None.  Fires only
-- when the argument is a tuple literal of arity N ≥ 2, the callee has curried arity
-- EXACTLY N (spreading the tuple would saturate it), and its first param is not
-- itself an N-tuple (the genuine tuple-arg case), and the callee is a named spine.
tupleCallShapeHint : Expr -> Expr -> Mono -> Option (String, Option (Loc, String))
tupleCallShapeHint f x ft = match tupleArgElems x
  None => None
  Some es =>
    let n = listLen es
    if n < 2 then
      None
    else if funArity ft != n then
      None
    else if domainIsTupleOfArity ft n then
      None
    else
      map (name => buildTupleCallHint name es f x) (appSpineName f)

-- surface-site lookup: does [m] (a type appearing in a no-impl / mismatch error)
-- equal a recorded tuple-call residual?  Compared by normalized rendering — the
-- residual IS the very type that flows into the error, so they render identically.
tupleCallHintFor : Mono -> Option (String, Option (Loc, String))
tupleCallHintFor m =
  findTupleCand (ppMono (normalize m)) perRun.value.tupleCallCandidates.value

findTupleCand : String -> List (Mono, String, Option (Loc, String)) -> Option (String, Option (Loc, String))
findTupleCand _ [] = None
findTupleCand key ((res, help, fix)::rest)
  | ppMono (normalize res) == key = Some (help, fix)
  | otherwise = findTupleCand key rest

-- first tuple-call hint matching any of the offending types in an error.
firstTupleCallHint : List Mono -> Option (String, Option (Loc, String))
firstTupleCallHint [] = None
firstTupleCallHint (m::rest) = match tupleCallHintFor m
  Some hf => Some hf
  None => firstTupleCallHint rest

inferApp : List (String, Expr) -> Expr -> Mono -> Mono -> Mono
inferApp lets appExpr ft xt =
  let r = freshVar ()
  -- Chunk C: if the callee normalizes to a concrete NON-arrow head (a known type
  -- constructor like `Int`/`List Int`, never a tyvar or an arrow), applying it
  -- is genuinely impossible.  Emit a context-aware `T-NOT-A-FUNCTION` instead of
  -- letting `unify ft (TFun …)` render a leaked `Int vs a -> b` mismatch, and
  -- poison the fresh result var so no secondary obligation cascade fires (reuses
  -- Chunk A's poisoned-var set).  Fires ONLY on a ground non-arrow head: a real
  -- function (`TFun`) or an as-yet-unresolved var (`TVar`) is unaffected.
  match monoHeadCon ft
    Some _ => inferAppNotFunction appExpr ft r
    None => match containerParamScalarArg ft xt
      Some scalarTy =>
        -- Batch B #6: container-consuming HOF handed a scalar.  Reframe here
        -- (callee name + arg loc in scope) instead of letting the generic sink
        -- leak `a b vs String`.  Skip the unify (it would push the leaked
        -- mismatch as a SECOND diagnostic) and poison `r` to suppress cascade.
        let hint = containerArgHint scalarTy
        let _ = pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt "T-TYPE-MISMATCH" currentLoc.value (containerArgMsg (appSpineName appExpr) scalarTy) hint None
        -- Poison the container tyvar + element vars (in ft) exactly as the generic
        -- unify sink would (`monoUnboundIds a ++ b`), so the dangling `Mappable f`
        -- obligation sees a poisoned var and suppresses its own cascade.
        let _ = poisonMismatchVars ft xt
        let _ = poisonMismatchVars r r
        r
      None =>
        let eff = openRow ()
        -- Type-error span (Bite 3, fork 3 = ARGUMENT): point a wrong-arg-type
        -- mismatch at the argument expression rather than the callee.  The live
        -- `currentLoc` after inferring the arg is its last atom; refine it to the
        -- whole argument span so `len 5` (len : String -> Int) squiggles the `5`.
        let _ = match appArgLoc appExpr
          Some al => setRef currentLoc (Some al)
          None => ()
        let _ = unify ft (TFun xt eff r)
        -- v2 Stage 2b: if f resolves to a leaf extern carrying an inferred-hole
        -- Prefix atom (`<Net _>`), fill it by α of the application spine's FIRST
        -- argument before the row propagates / is checked against any bound.  `lets`
        -- seeds α with the enclosing body's let scope (EFFECTS WS-2).
        let eff2 = fillHolesInRow lets eff (spineFirstArg appExpr)
        let _ = performEffect perRun.value.curEffect eff2
        r

-- Chunk C: the applied head is a ground non-function.  If the spine over-applies
-- a named function (callee name known AND at least one arg was consumed before
-- the type went non-arrow) use the arity-aware wording; otherwise fall back to
-- naming the non-function type.  Poison `r` to suppress downstream cascade.
inferAppNotFunction : Expr -> Mono -> Mono -> Mono
inferAppNotFunction appExpr ft r =
  let applied = appSpineArgCount appExpr
  let takes = applied - 1
  let _ = match appSpineName appExpr
    Some name => if takes >= 1 then pushTypeError "T-NOT-A-FUNCTION" (overAppliedMsg name takes applied)
    else
      let hint = notAFunctionHint (Some name)
      pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt
        "T-NOT-A-FUNCTION"
        currentLoc.value
        (notAFunctionMsg (ppMono (normalize ft)) ++ hint)
        hint
        None
    None =>
      let hint = notAFunctionHint None
      pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt
        "T-NOT-A-FUNCTION"
        currentLoc.value
        (notAFunctionMsg (ppMono (normalize ft)) ++ hint)
        hint
        None
  let _ = poisonMismatchVars r r
  r

inferLam : TcEnv -> List Pat -> Expr -> Mono
inferLam env pats body =
  -- Patterns carry no source Loc, so a pattern-level type error (e.g. an
  -- over-applied nullary constructor `A _`) would otherwise be recorded at the
  -- STALE `currentLoc` left by the previous clause/function's body inference —
  -- mis-blaming an innocent sibling clause.  Anchor `currentLoc` to THIS clause's
  -- body loc before inferring the patterns, so the error lands on the right
  -- clause (the body re-sets `currentLoc` via its own ELoc arm afterwards).
  let _ = match exprLoc body
    Some l => setRef currentLoc (Some l)
    None => ()
  let pr = inferPats env pats
  let saved = perRun.value.curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.curEffect (openRow ())
  let bodyTy = infer (extendVars env (snd pr)) body
  let bodyEff = perRun.value.curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.curEffect saved
  arrowsWithLastEffect (fst pr) bodyEff bodyTy

-- The first Bool (`mut`) is always False — `let mut` is a parser error (beta
-- mutability model), so no inline mut-let path remains here.
inferLet : TcEnv -> Bool -> Bool -> Pat -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferLet env _ isFun pat e1 e2
  | isFun = match pat
    PVar x => inferRecLet env x e1 e2
    _ => inferLetSimple env pat e1 e2
  | otherwise = inferLetSimple env pat e1 e2

-- T2: a recursive inline function binding (`let go n = … in …`) must
-- pre-bind `go` with a monomorphic placeholder so the RHS can reference
-- itself, then generalize (a function is a value — oracle `:1668-1681`).

-- Self-recursive inline `let` binding (mirrors blockRecLet + the oracle is_fun
-- arm): placeholder pre-bind, infer body, unify, generalize through the body.
inferRecLet : TcEnv -> String -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferRecLet env x e1 e2 =
  let _ = enterLevel ()
  let oblN0 = perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let placeholder = freshVar ()
  let envSelf = extendVar env x (monoScheme placeholder)
  let t1 = infer envSelf e1
  let _ = unify placeholder t1
  let _ = exitLevel ()
  let addedObls = takeFirst (perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value
  let _ = defaultAmbiguousNum addedObls t1  -- PLAN.md #11
  let _ = registerAmbiguousConstraints addedObls [t1]  -- RETPOS soundness
  let _ = recordLocalBind x t1  -- LSP hover (recursive PVar let bypasses inferPat)
  infer (seedAlphaLets (extendVar env x (generalize t1)) x e1) e2  -- WS-2: α scope-seed

inferLetSimple : TcEnv -> Pat -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferLetSimple env pat e1 e2 =
  let _ = enterLevel ()
  let oblN0 = perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let t1 = infer env e1
  let _ = exitLevel ()
  -- PLAN.md #11: ground an ambiguous Num-only var before this inner let
  -- generalizes, so `let z = 2 in y + z` (y:Float) is a clean type error and
  -- never leaks an unbound Num var.  Scoped to this binding's new obligations.
  let addedObls = takeFirst (perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value
  let _ = defaultAmbiguousNum addedObls t1
  let _ = registerAmbiguousConstraints addedObls [t1]   -- RETPOS soundness
  inferLetBody env pat t1 e1 e2

inferLetBody : TcEnv -> Pat -> Mono -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferLetBody env (PVar x) t1 e1 e2 =
  let _ = recordLocalBind x t1  -- LSP hover (PVar let bypasses inferPat)
  infer
    (seedAlphaLets
      (extendVar env x (genRestricted (isNonexpansive e1) t1))
      x
      e1)
    e2  -- WS-2: α scope-seed
inferLetBody env pat t1 _ e2 =
  let pr = inferPat env pat
  let _ = unify (fst pr) t1
  infer (extendVars env (snd pr)) e2

inferIf : TcEnv -> Expr -> Expr -> Expr -> Mono
inferIf env c t e =
  let _ = unify (infer env c) (TCon "Bool")
  let tt = infer env t
  let ee = infer env e
  let _ = noteNumlitCtx tt ee "if"  -- Chunk D: site-specific Num mis-framing wording
  let _ = unify tt ee
  tt

inferEach : TcEnv -> List Expr -> List Mono
inferEach _ [] = []
inferEach env (e::es) =
  let t = infer env e
  t :: inferEach env es

inferListLit : TcEnv -> List Expr -> Mono
inferListLit env es =
  let elem = freshVar ()
  let _ = unifyAllNumlit elem (inferEach env es)  -- Chunk D: tag list-element Num mismatch
  TApp (TCon "List") elem

-- Chunk D: like unifyAll but tags each element that is a still-unbound literal var
-- being forced against a concrete accumulator (or vice-versa) with the "list" ctx.
unifyAllNumlit : Mono -> List Mono -> Unit
unifyAllNumlit _ [] = ()
unifyAllNumlit t (x::xs) =
  let _ = noteNumlitCtx t x "list"
  let _ = unify t x
  unifyAllNumlit t xs

inferAnnot : TcEnv -> Expr -> Ty -> Mono
inferAnnot env e ty =
  let t = infer env e
  let pair = astTypeFreshTbl ty
  let annot = fst pair
  let _ = unify t annot
  let _ = checkAnnotTooGeneral ty (map snd (snd pair))
  annot

-- Phase 108 head-pin (`:~`, EHeadAnnot): like inferAnnot but WITHOUT the
-- checkAnnotTooGeneral skolemization guard — the pin's type variables are MEANT
-- to ground (the container literal's element types).  Fixes `e`'s head tycon to
-- the named container so `fromEntries`'s return-position dispatch resolves to
-- that container's impl.  Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's EHeadAnnot arm.
--
-- Phase 114 (was wrongly dropped here): ignore the *arity* the lowering happened
-- to supply and apply the head tycon to its *declared* arity of fresh vars.  The
-- parser can't tell empty `Map { }` from `Set { }` — both have no entries, so
-- classifyBrace falls through to `ESetLit name []` and the desugar pins a UNARY
-- `Map _a`, the wrong arity for `Map k v` (→ `Map vs Map …`).  Looking the arity
-- up by the head tycon makes empty literals work regardless of the lowering's
-- guess; element vars stay free and ground via inference as before.
inferHeadAnnot : TcEnv -> Expr -> Ty -> Mono
inferHeadAnnot env e ty =
  let t = infer env e
  let annot = headAnnotTy ty
  let _ = unify t annot
  t

-- The head-pin's target type: if the annotation's head is a TyCon with a known
-- declared arity (from `dataParamKindsRef`), rebuild it at that arity with fresh
-- vars (so an empty literal's wrong-arity pin is corrected); otherwise fall back
-- to the literal annotation type.
headAnnotTy : Ty -> Mono
headAnnotTy ty = match tyAppSpine ty
  (TyCon n _, _) => match lookupAssoc n perRun.value.dataParamKindsRef.value
    Some kinds => applyParams (TCon n) (freshVars (listLen kinds))
    None => fst (astTypeFreshTbl ty)
  _ => fst (astTypeFreshTbl ty)

-- C3 (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml:1821-1842): each type variable named in the
-- annotation asserts polymorphism, so after unification it must still be a
-- DISTINCT, unbound variable.  If one ground to a concrete type, or two
-- annotation variables collapsed to the same variable, the annotation is more
-- polymorphic than the expression — reject.  `sigTvarIds` keeps only the entries
-- that normalize to a TVar, so a grounded var drops out (count shrinks) and a
-- collapse shows as a duplicate id.  EHeadAnnot is exempt (no call to this).
checkAnnotTooGeneral : Ty -> List Mono -> Unit
checkAnnotTooGeneral ty resolved =
  let ids = sigTvarIds resolved
  if listLen ids < listLen resolved || hasDupI ids then
    pushTypeError "T-TYPE-TOO-GENERAL" (annotTooGeneralMsg ty)
  else
    ()

inferMatch : TcEnv -> Mono -> List Arm -> Mono
inferMatch env scrutTy arms =
  let result = freshVar ()
  let _ = inferArms env scrutTy result arms
  let _ = checkMatchExhaustive scrutTy arms
  let _ = checkMatchRedundant scrutTy arms
  result
-- Type-aware exhaustiveness (`check_match`): run AFTER the arms have unified
-- the scrutinee type, so `scrutTy` is fully resolved when we read its head.

-- The non-exhaustive-`match` warning (note the em-dash).  The redundancy pass
-- is not ported — only the exhaustiveness warning.  When the Maranget witness
-- algorithm can name the uncovered case (`nonExhaustiveMsg`) the message is
-- specialised to name the scrutinee type + the missing constructor; this
-- generic form is the fallback if no witness is recoverable.
nonExhaustiveMatchWarning : String
nonExhaustiveMatchWarning =
  "Warning: non-exhaustive match. Not all cases are covered"

-- Warn if an all-wildcard query is still useful against the matrix of the
-- non-guarded arms — i.e. some scrutinee value escapes every arm.  Guarded arms
-- are excluded (their guard may fail at runtime), matching the reference.
checkMatchExhaustive : Mono -> List Arm -> Unit
checkMatchExhaustive scrutTy arms =
  let rows = nonGuardedRows arms
  if useful driverState.value.matchOracle.value (matchCol0Type scrutTy) rows [PWild] then
    let (msg, help) = nonExhaustiveMsg scrutTy rows
    setRef
      driverState.value.matchWarnings
      (TcDiag "" 2 currentLoc.value msg help None :: driverState.value.matchWarnings.value)
  else ()

-- ── unreachable / redundant match-arm warning (W-UNREACHABLE-ARM) ──────────
-- An arm is unreachable when its pattern is already fully covered by an EARLIER
-- *unguarded* arm: those arms fire unconditionally, so no value ever reaches
-- this one.  We fold arms left-to-right, accumulating a matrix of the preceding
-- UNGUARDED arm patterns, and flag any arm whose pattern is not `useful` against
-- it (`patUnreachable`).  Guarded predecessors are excluded (their guard may fail
-- at runtime, leaving a later same-shape arm reachable), which keeps the check
-- conservative — we flag only arms provably dead via unconditional predecessors.
-- The warning carries the ARM's own loc (its body span) so the caret points at
-- the dead arm, not the match head.
unreachableArmWarning : String
unreachableArmWarning = "Warning: unreachable match arm. This pattern is already covered by an earlier arm"

checkMatchRedundant : Mono -> List Arm -> Unit
checkMatchRedundant scrutTy arms =
  checkRedundantGo (matchCol0Type scrutTy) [] omEmpty False arms

-- PERF: `prec` accumulates the preceding arms' patterns and is PREPENDED to, so
-- it holds them in REVERSE arm order.  It used to be built with `prec ++ [[dp]]`,
-- which copies the whole accumulator once per arm — O(N²) allocation on a match
-- with many arms, and the dominant allocation source in the redundancy check.
--
-- Reversing the rows is sound because `useful` is a PREDICATE OVER THE SET of
-- matrix rows ("does some value match the query and no row?"): `specializeCon` /
-- `specializeLit` / `defaultMatrix` are row-wise filters, and the only decision
-- that reads rows in order is `inferCol0Type`, which just needs SOME column-0
-- constructor to name the column's type — and it is consulted only when `col0`
-- is `None`, i.e. when the scrutinee type has no concrete head, in which case no
-- arm carries a constructor pattern and there is no head constructor to find in
-- either order.
checkRedundantGo : Option String -> List (List Pat) -> OrdMap Unit -> Bool -> List Arm -> Unit
checkRedundantGo _ _ _ _ [] = ()
checkRedundantGo col0 prec heads offCol ((Arm pat gs body)::rest) =
  let dp = desugarPat driverState.value.matchOracle.value pat
  let _ = if armUnreachable col0 prec heads offCol dp then
    setRef driverState.value.matchWarnings (TcDiag "" 2 (orElseLoc (exprLoc body) currentLoc.value) unreachableArmWarning None None :: driverState.value.matchWarnings.value)
  else ()
  -- Only UNGUARDED, range-free arms contribute to the covered set: a guarded arm
  -- may not fire, and a range collapses to a wildcard that over-states coverage
  -- (patHasRange) — either would produce a false "redundant" on a later arm.
  let contributes = armIsUnguarded gs && not (patHasRange pat)
  let prec2 = if contributes then [dp] :: prec else prec
  let heads2 = if contributes then noteHeadCtor dp heads else heads
  let offCol2 = offCol || (contributes && not (isConPat dp))
  checkRedundantGo col0 prec2 heads2 offCol2 rest

-- PERF fast path.  `patUnreachable` runs the full Maranget `useful` recursion, which
-- re-scans EVERY preceding row — so the fold as a whole is O(arms²) on a wide match.
-- But a constructor arm `C …` can only be unreachable if some preceding row could
-- match a `C` value, and the only rows that can are (a) rows headed by `C` itself and
-- (b) rows with a WILDCARD head, which match anything.  When neither exists,
-- `specializeCon C` yields the EMPTY matrix and `useful _ _ [] _` is `True` by
-- definition — the arm is reachable — so the recursion is guaranteed to say
-- "reachable" and we can skip it outright.
--
-- `heads` is the set of head constructors of the preceding contributing rows and
-- `offCol` records whether any of them has a NON-constructor head.  `offCol` is a
-- deliberate over-approximation of "has a wildcard head" (it also trips on a literal
-- head, which `specializeCon` would in fact discard): erring towards `True` only ever
-- costs us the fast path and falls back to the exact check, never an answer.
armUnreachable : Option String -> List (List Pat) -> OrdMap Unit -> Bool -> Pat -> Bool
armUnreachable col0 prec heads offCol (PCon c args)
  | not offCol && not (omHasKey c heads) = False
  | otherwise =
    patUnreachable driverState.value.matchOracle.value col0 prec (PCon c args)
armUnreachable col0 prec _ _ dp =
  patUnreachable driverState.value.matchOracle.value col0 prec dp

noteHeadCtor : Pat -> OrdMap Unit -> OrdMap Unit
noteHeadCtor (PCon c _) heads = omInsert c () heads
noteHeadCtor _ heads = heads

isConPat : Pat -> Bool
isConPat (PCon _ _) = True
isConPat _ = False

-- Only UNGUARDED arms contribute to the covered set (a guarded arm may not fire).
armIsUnguarded : List Guard -> Bool
armIsUnguarded [] = True
armIsUnguarded (_::_) = False

-- Build the specialised warning: recover a concrete uncovered pattern (the
-- Maranget witness) and name it, plus the scrutinee type when it has a real
-- head name.  Falls back to the generic wording if no witness is recoverable
-- (keeps the warning firing whenever `useful` should, never naming a wrong
-- constructor).  Actionable-fix hint (F dimension): when a witness IS
-- recoverable, append a concrete-edit suggestion using the witness string
-- VERBATIM (so `Triangle _` reads as `Triangle _`, not `Triangle`) and return it
-- as the `Some` help so `--json`'s `help` field carries it too.  #159: returns
-- `(message, Option help)` so the caller can stash both in the warning's TcDiag —
-- no more message-keyed `matchWarningHelp` side channel.
nonExhaustiveMsg : Mono -> List (List Pat) -> (String, Option String)
nonExhaustiveMsg scrutTy rows = match usefulWitness driverState.value.matchOracle.value (matchCol0Type scrutTy) rows 1
  Some (w::_) =>
    let witnessStr = renderWitness w
    let hint = "add a '\{witnessStr} => …' arm, or a '_' wildcard arm to catch the rest."
    let msg = "Warning: non-exhaustive match\{tyOfMsg (matchCol0Type scrutTy)}. Missing case: '\{witnessStr}'; \{hint}"
    (msg, Some hint)
  _ => (nonExhaustiveMatchWarning, None)

-- Name the scrutinee type (skip the synthetic tuple type names, which aren't
-- user-facing — the witness pattern already reads as a tuple).
tyOfMsg : Option String -> String
tyOfMsg None = ""
tyOfMsg (Some t) = if isTupleTyName t then "" else " of '\{t}'"

isTupleTyName : String -> Bool
isTupleTyName t = stringLength t >= 7 && stringSlice 0 7 t == "__tuple"

-- one single-column matrix row per non-guarded arm (its desugared pattern)
nonGuardedRows : List Arm -> List (List Pat)
nonGuardedRows [] = []
nonGuardedRows ((Arm pat [] _)::rest) =
  [desugarPat driverState.value.matchOracle.value pat] :: nonGuardedRows rest
nonGuardedRows ((Arm _ _ _)::rest) = nonGuardedRows rest

-- the head type name of the scrutinee, for the matrix's column-0 oracle: a
-- TCon/TApp head gives the type name; a tuple has no TCon head, so it maps to a
-- synthetic per-arity tuple type (mirrors the reference's col0_type).
matchCol0Type : Mono -> Option String
matchCol0Type ty = map (n => n) (headTyconMono ty)

inferArms : TcEnv -> Mono -> Mono -> List Arm -> Unit
inferArms _ _ _ [] = ()
inferArms env scrutTy result ((Arm pat gs body)::rest) =
  let _ = inferArm env scrutTy result pat gs body
  inferArms env scrutTy result rest

inferArm : TcEnv -> Mono -> Mono -> Pat -> List Guard -> Expr -> Unit
inferArm env scrutTy result pat gs body =
  let pr = inferPat env pat
  let _ = unify (fst pr) scrutTy
  let envBody = inferArmGuards (extendVars env (snd pr)) gs
  unify result (infer envBody body)

-- Thread an arm's guard qualifiers left-to-right (mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's
-- EMatch guard fold): a `GBool` must be `Bool` and leaves the env unchanged; a
-- `GBind p e` infers `e`, binds `p` against it, and extends the env so the bound
-- vars are in scope for LATER qualifiers and the arm body.
inferArmGuards : TcEnv -> List Guard -> TcEnv
inferArmGuards env [] = env
inferArmGuards env ((GBool g)::rest) =
  let _ = unify (infer env g) (TCon "Bool")
  inferArmGuards env rest
inferArmGuards env ((GBind p e)::rest) =
  let te = infer env e
  let pr = inferPat env p
  let _ = unify (fst pr) te
  inferArmGuards (extendVars env (snd pr)) rest

inferLetGroup : TcEnv -> List LetBind -> Expr -> Mono
inferLetGroup env binds body =
  let env2 = processLetGroup env binds
  infer env2 body

-- ── data declarations → constructor schemes ───────────────────────────────
registerData : TcEnv -> Decl -> TcEnv
registerData env (DData _ name params variants _) =
  registerVariants env name params variants
registerData env (DTypeAlias _ name params rhs) =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.aliasTableRef ((name, (params, rhs))::perRun.value.aliasTableRef.value)
  env
registerData env (DNewtype _ name params con fty _) =
  registerVariants env name params [Variant con (ConPos [fty])]
registerData env _ = env

-- ── record metadata registration & instantiation ──────────────────────────
-- registerRecordInfoKeyed uses a distinct registry KEY and result TYPE name:
-- for a record they coincide, but a named-field data variant is keyed by its
-- constructor (DInterface) while its values have the data type (Decl).
registerRecordInfoKeyed : String -> String -> List String -> List Field -> Unit
registerRecordInfoKeyed key typeName params fields =
  let paramVars = freshVars (listLen params)
  let tvs = zipL params paramVars
  let result = applyParams (TCon typeName) paramVars
  let fieldMonos = map (recFieldMono tvs) fields
  let ri = RecordInfo (map monoTyvarId paramVars) result fieldMonos
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.recordsRef ((key, ri)::perRun.value.recordsRef.value)
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.recordByNameRef (omInsert key ri perRun.value.recordByNameRef.value)
  setRef
    perRun.value.fieldOwnersRef
    (addFieldOwners key (map fst fieldMonos) perRun.value.fieldOwnersRef.value)

-- Add `key` as an owner of each of its field names in the field→owners multimap.
-- Order/dedup is not maintained here — fieldOwnerNames `sortUniqS`es the value on
-- read — so this only needs to preserve the SET of owner keys per field.
addFieldOwners : String -> List String -> OrdMap (List String) -> OrdMap (List String)
addFieldOwners _ [] m = m
addFieldOwners key (fn::rest) m =
  addFieldOwners key rest (omInsert fn (key :: fromOption [] (omLookup fn m)) m)

recFieldMono : List (String, Mono) -> Field -> (String, Mono)
recFieldMono tvs (Field fn ty) = (fn, fromAstType tvs ty)

lookupRecordByName : String -> Option RecordInfo
lookupRecordByName name = omLookup name perRun.value.recordByNameRef.value

-- instantiate a record's quantified params with fresh vars
instantiateRecord : RecordInfo -> (Mono, List (String, Mono))
instantiateRecord (RecordInfo paramIds result fields) =
  let subst = freshSubst paramIds
  (substMono subst [] result, map (substField subst) fields)

substField : List (Int, Mono) -> (String, Mono) -> (String, Mono)
substField subst (fn, fm) = (fn, substMono subst [] fm)

registerVariants : TcEnv -> String -> List String -> List Variant -> TcEnv
registerVariants env name params variants =
  let kinds = inferParamKinds params variants
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.dataParamKindsRef ((name, kinds)::perRun.value.dataParamKindsRef.value)
  let paramReprs = mintParamReprs kinds
  let tvs = ktypeTvs params kinds paramReprs
  let etbl = krowEtbl params kinds paramReprs
  let paramTyIds = collectTyIds paramReprs
  let rowEffvarIds = collectRowEffvarIds paramReprs
  let result = applyParams (TCon name) paramReprs
  let _ = registerNamedFieldVariants name params variants
  addVariants env tvs etbl paramTyIds rowEffvarIds result variants

-- A data param is KRow iff its name appears in a field's effect-tail position
-- (`<e>`); else KType (the common case).  Inferred from usage — no syntax.
inferParamKinds : List String -> List Variant -> List Kind
inferParamKinds params variants =
  let tailNames = flatMap variantFieldTailNames variants
  map (p => if contains p tailNames then KRow else KType) params

variantFieldTailNames : Variant -> List String
variantFieldTailNames (Variant _ payload) =
  flatMap effTailNames (payloadAstTypes payload)

payloadAstTypes : ConPayload -> List Ty
payloadAstTypes (ConPos tys) = tys
payloadAstTypes (ConNamed fields _) = map fieldTyOf fields

fieldTyOf : Field -> Ty
fieldTyOf (Field _ ty) = ty

-- one representation per param shared across all ctors: KType → fresh TVar,
-- KRow → a TEff wrapping a fresh effvar (the row the param stands for).
mintParamReprs : List Kind -> List Mono
mintParamReprs [] = []
mintParamReprs (KType::ks) = freshVar () :: mintParamReprs ks
mintParamReprs (KRow::ks) =
  TEff (EffRow [] (Some (freshEffvar ()))) :: mintParamReprs ks

-- KType param names → their TVar (the tvs map fromAstTypeE consults for tyvars)
ktypeTvs : List String -> List Kind -> List Mono -> List (String, Mono)
ktypeTvs (p::ps) (KType::ks) (r::rs) = (p, r) :: ktypeTvs ps ks rs
ktypeTvs (p::ps) (_::ks) (_::rs) = ktypeTvs ps ks rs
ktypeTvs _ _ _ = []

-- KRow param names → the effvar cell inside their TEff repr, so a field's `<e>`
-- and a nested `Async e a` row-arg resolve to the SAME cell the result carries.
krowEtbl : List String -> List Kind -> List Mono -> List (String, Ref Effvar)
krowEtbl (p::ps) (KRow::ks) ((TEff (EffRow _ (Some cell)))::rs) =
  (p, cell) :: krowEtbl ps ks rs
krowEtbl (p::ps) (_::ks) (_::rs) = krowEtbl ps ks rs
krowEtbl _ _ _ = []

collectTyIds : List Mono -> List Int
collectTyIds ((TVar cell)::rs) = tyvarId cell :: collectTyIds rs
collectTyIds (_::rs) = collectTyIds rs
collectTyIds [] = []

collectRowEffvarIds : List Mono -> List Int
collectRowEffvarIds ((TEff (EffRow _ (Some cell)))::rs) =
  effvarId cell :: collectRowEffvarIds rs
collectRowEffvarIds (_::rs) = collectRowEffvarIds rs
collectRowEffvarIds [] = []

-- a named-field data variant (`C { f : T, … }`, e.g. ast's DInterface) carries a
-- record-shaped payload: register its field layout keyed by the CONSTRUCTOR name
-- but with the DATA TYPE as result type, so `C { f, … }` patterns / `C { … }`
-- literals resolve (the positional ctor scheme is still added by addVariants).
registerNamedFieldVariants : String -> List String -> List Variant -> Unit
registerNamedFieldVariants _ _ [] = ()
registerNamedFieldVariants tyName params ((Variant cname (ConNamed fields _))::rest) =
  let _ = registerRecordInfoKeyed cname tyName params fields
  registerNamedFieldVariants tyName params rest
registerNamedFieldVariants tyName params (_::rest) =
  registerNamedFieldVariants tyName params rest

freshVars : Int -> List Mono
freshVars n
  | n <= 0 = []
  | otherwise = freshVar () :: freshVars (n - 1)

monoTyvarId : Mono -> Int
monoTyvarId (TVar cell) = tyvarId cell
monoTyvarId _ = 0 - 1

applyParams : Mono -> List Mono -> Mono
applyParams head [] = head
applyParams head (p::ps) = applyParams (TApp head p) ps

addVariants : TcEnv -> List (String, Mono) -> List (String, Ref Effvar) -> List Int -> List Int -> Mono -> List Variant -> TcEnv
addVariants env _ _ _ _ _ [] = env
addVariants env tvs etbl paramTyIds rowEffvarIds result ((Variant cname payload)::rest) =
  let ctorType = foldrFun (payloadTypesE etbl tvs payload) result
  let env2 = addCtor env cname (Forall paramTyIds rowEffvarIds ctorType)
  addVariants env2 tvs etbl paramTyIds rowEffvarIds result rest

payloadTypesE : List (String, Ref Effvar) -> List (String, Mono) -> ConPayload -> List Mono
payloadTypesE etbl tvs (ConPos tys) = map (fromAstTypeE etbl tvs) tys
payloadTypesE etbl tvs (ConNamed fields _) = map (fieldTypeE etbl tvs) fields

fieldTypeE : List (String, Ref Effvar) -> List (String, Mono) -> Field -> Mono
fieldTypeE etbl tvs (Field _ ty) = fromAstTypeE etbl tvs ty

-- ── top-level driver ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
funClausePair : FunClause -> (List Pat, Expr)
funClausePair (FunClause pats body) = (pats, body)

funDefs : List Decl -> List (String, (List Pat, Expr))
funDefs [] = []
funDefs ((DFunDef _ n pats body)::rest) = (n, (pats, body)) :: funDefs rest
-- Top-level `let rec … with …` (DLetGroup): flatten each binding's clauses to
-- (name, (pats, body)) entries, exactly like a multi-clause DFunDef, so the SCC
-- builder groups the mutually-recursive members and assigns them schemes.  Mirrors
-- lib/typecheck.ml, which feeds DLetGroup bindings into the same letrec-group path.
funDefs ((DLetGroup _ binds)::rest) = letGroupDefs binds ++ funDefs rest
funDefs ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) = funDefs (d::rest)
funDefs (_::rest) = funDefs rest

letGroupDefs : List LetBind -> List (String, (List Pat, Expr))
letGroupDefs [] = []
letGroupDefs ((LetBind n clauses)::rest) = map (tcClauseDef n) clauses
  ++ letGroupDefs rest

tcClauseDef : String -> FunClause -> (String, (List Pat, Expr))
tcClauseDef n c = (n, funClausePair c)

-- D2 fix: mirrors oracle is_syntactic_lambda (lib/typecheck.ml:2518-2521).
-- True iff the expression is an explicit lambda at the top level.
isSyntacticLambda : Expr -> Bool
isSyntacticLambda (ELam _ _) = True
isSyntacticLambda _ = False

-- D2 fix: check all top-level DLetGroup declarations for non-function recursive
-- bindings, mirroring the oracle's LetRecNonFunction guard
-- (lib/typecheck.ml:2596-2602, process_letrec_group).  A binding is illegal
-- when ALL its clauses have zero params AND none is a syntactic lambda.
-- Pushes the error into typeErrors for each violating member.
checkLetRecDecls : List Decl -> Unit
checkLetRecDecls [] = ()
checkLetRecDecls ((DLetGroup _ binds)::rest) =
  let _ = checkLetBinds binds
  checkLetRecDecls rest
checkLetRecDecls (_::rest) = checkLetRecDecls rest

checkLetBinds : List LetBind -> Unit
checkLetBinds [] = ()
checkLetBinds ((LetBind name clauses)::rest) =
  let _ = checkLetBind name clauses
  checkLetBinds rest

checkLetBind : String -> List FunClause -> Unit
checkLetBind name clauses
  | allList clauseIsNonFunction clauses =
    pushTypeError "T-NONREC-VALUE-LET" (letRecNonFunctionMsg name)
  | otherwise = ()

clauseIsNonFunction : FunClause -> Bool
clauseIsNonFunction (FunClause [] rhs) = not (isSyntacticLambda rhs)
clauseIsNonFunction (FunClause _ _) = False

letRecNonFunctionMsg : String -> String
letRecNonFunctionMsg name = "'"
  ++ name
  ++ "' is bound by 'let rec' but its right-hand side is not a function"

groupNames : List (String, (List Pat, Expr)) -> OrdMap Unit -> List String
groupNames [] _ = []
groupNames ((n, _)::rest) seen = match omLookup n seen
  Some _ => groupNames rest seen
  None => n :: groupNames rest (omInsert n () seen)

-- process a let-group (where clauses): bind names, infer, generalize
processLetGroup : TcEnv -> List LetBind -> TcEnv
processLetGroup env binds =
  let _ = enterLevel ()
  let oblN0 = perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let placeholders = map letBindPlaceholder binds
  let env2 = extendVars env placeholders
  let _ = inferLetBinds env2 (zipL binds placeholders)
  let _ = exitLevel ()
  -- PLAN.md #11: ground ambiguous Num-only vars (not arg-reachable from any
  -- where-binding's type) before this group generalizes.  Scoped to this group's
  -- newly added obligations.
  let addedObls = takeFirst (perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value
  let _ = defaultGroupNum addedObls (map (p => schemeMono (snd p)) placeholders)
  let _ = registerAmbiguousConstraints addedObls (map (p => schemeMono (snd p)) placeholders)   -- RETPOS soundness
  generalizeGroup env (zipL binds placeholders)

letBindPlaceholder : LetBind -> (String, Scheme)
letBindPlaceholder (LetBind name _) = (name, monoScheme (freshVar ()))

inferLetBinds : TcEnv -> List (LetBind, (String, Scheme)) -> Unit
inferLetBinds _ [] = ()
inferLetBinds env ((LetBind _ clauses, (_, sch))::rest) =
  let _ = unify (schemeMono sch) (inferClauses env (map funClausePair clauses))
  inferLetBinds env rest

schemeMono : Scheme -> Mono
schemeMono (Forall _ _ t) = t

-- Phase / GAP-2 phase-a: the tyvar ids that some ARG-position method occurrence
-- discriminates on (the receiver monos queued in pendingArgStamps).  A where-helper
-- whose own type shares one of these is *method-constrained by the enclosing fn's
-- dispatch* — its receiver var unifies (at the helper's call site) with the
-- enclosing fn's constraint var that activeDictVars maps to a dict param.  We must
-- NOT generalize over such a var, or the helper instantiates a fresh (id != the
-- enclosing constraint var) so resolveArgStamp can't see the in-scope dict → the
-- in-body method route degrades to RNone → arg-tag → native panic on a primitive
-- receiver.  Keeping it monomorphic links the ids (normalize collapses them) so the
-- existing resolveArgStamp routes RDict and dict_pass threads the enclosing dict in.
-- Sound: an UNconstrained helper var (e.g. `pair x = (x,x)`) never appears in a
-- method-usage mono, so it still generalizes; only dispatch-constrained vars (which
-- compiler cannot dict-pass per-where-helper anyway) are pinned, matching their
-- single enclosing-typed use.
methodConstrainedIds : Unit -> List Int
methodConstrainedIds _ = flatMap
  (s => monoUnboundIds (argStampMono s))
  perRun.value.pendingArgStamps.value

argStampMono : PendingEntry -> Mono
argStampMono (PendingEntry _ _ am _ _) = am

monoUnboundIds : Mono -> List Int
monoUnboundIds t = match normalize t
  TVar cell => [tyvarId cell]
  TCon _ => []
  TApp a b => monoUnboundIds a ++ monoUnboundIds b
  TFun a _ b => monoUnboundIds a ++ monoUnboundIds b
  TEff _ => []

anyIn : List Int -> List Int -> Bool
anyIn xs ys = anyList (x => containsI x ys) xs

-- PLAN.md #11 (locked §0.2): monomorphism-for-Num defaulting.  Before a type [t]
-- is generalized at a let-binding boundary, ground every *ambiguous* tyvar carrying
-- a `Num` constraint to `Int`.  "Ambiguous" = the var is NOT reachable from an
-- ARGUMENT position of [t] (a caller can't fix it).  This stops a bare `n = 1` (or
-- an inner `let z = 2`) from generalizing to `Num a => a` / leaking an unbound
-- `Num a` to eval, while leaving a genuinely polymorphic var live: `sum`'s element
-- var in `t a -> a` is reachable from the argument `t a`, so it stays polymorphic
-- (`sum [1.0,…]` still works); `x : Float; x = 0`'s var is already concrete Float
-- (not a generalizable var), so it is untouched.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's
-- default_ambiguous_num: a Num-constrained var (alone OR with another class) that is
-- ambiguous is grounded to Int — the literal's `Num`/`fromInt` representation IS
-- Int, so grounding keeps impl selection deterministic.
--
-- SCOPE (#518): "at a let-binding boundary" above is no longer the whole story — an
-- IMPL METHOD BODY is ALSO a defaulting boundary (inferImplMethod), for the same
-- reason a let-group is: it is a point past which an ambiguous Num var can never be
-- determined, so leaving it unbound hands an UNGROUNDED RECEIVER to the route
-- resolver, which then silently mis-selects an impl.  It is not a let-group, which is
-- why it needed its own call rather than inheriting one.  It defaults strictly LESS
-- than a let-group does — body-local vars only (defaultBodyLocalNum) — because an
-- impl method's vars can ALSO be caller-determined via a method DICT, a channel with
-- no let-group analogue.  This paragraph is the written scope extension; before #518
-- the widening would have existed only as a judgment call in code.
--
-- ⚠️ KNOWN GAP (pre-existing, NOT closed here): monoArgUnboundIds below walks only
-- the DOMAINS of the outermost arrow chain, so a var in RETURN position — or ANY var
-- of a nullary method like `Monoid.empty : a` (a non-TFun type ⇒ this returns [] and
-- protects nothing) — is invisible to defaultGroupNum's exclusion filter.  The two
-- pre-existing call sites (processLetGroup, processSCC) inherit that blind spot.
-- defaultBodyLocalNum sidesteps it at the impl-method site by testing membership in
-- the WHOLE type instead.  Tracked as #563 (which also records why tightening the
-- two let-group sites is NOT obviously safe: there, a return-only var frequently IS
-- ambiguous and SHOULD ground — `let n = 1` — so this filter is not simply wrong).
monoArgUnboundIds : Mono -> List Int
monoArgUnboundIds t = match normalize t
  TFun a _ b => monoUnboundIds a ++ monoArgUnboundIds b
  _ => []

-- the unbound var ids that any pending `Num` obligation in [obls] constrains (the
-- operand monos recorded by recordIfaceObligation / the literal infer arm)
numConstrainedIds : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List Int
numConstrainedIds obls = flatMap numObligIds obls

numObligIds : (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List Int
numObligIds (iface, _, _, occ, _)
  | iface == "Num" = monoUnboundIds occ
  | otherwise = []

defaultAmbiguousNum : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Mono -> Unit
defaultAmbiguousNum obls t =
  let argIds = monoArgUnboundIds t
  let inT = monoUnboundIds t
  let candidates = dedupI (numConstrainedIds obls)
  groundNumVars
    obls
    (filterList
      (id => containsI id inT && not (containsI id argIds))
      candidates)

-- group-level defaulting: ground every Num-constrained var NOT reachable from an
-- ARGUMENT position of ANY member's type.  [monos] = the group's member placeholder
-- monos; [obls] = ONLY this group's newly-added obligations (an earlier group's
-- already-generalized var must not be touched).  Unlike defaultAmbiguousNum this
-- does NOT require the var to appear in a member's type (a var consumed inside a
-- body surfaces in no type but must still ground — `let n = tag [1,2,3]`).  Mirrors
-- process_letrec_group's group_arg_ids over added_obligs/added_methods.
defaultGroupNum : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List Mono -> Unit
defaultGroupNum obls monos =
  let argIds = flatMap monoArgUnboundIds monos
  let candidates = dedupI (numConstrainedIds obls)
  groundNumVars obls (filterList (id => not (containsI id argIds)) candidates)

-- #518: BODY-LOCAL defaulting — ground only the Num-constrained vars that appear
-- NOWHERE in [memberMono].  Strictly narrower than defaultGroupNum, and the
-- distinction is SOUNDNESS-CRITICAL at an impl-method body, which is why this is a
-- separate primitive rather than a reuse.
--
-- defaultGroupNum's exclusion filter is monoArgUnboundIds, which walks ONLY the
-- domains of the outermost arrow chain.  A var living in RETURN position — including
-- nested inside a return application — is invisible to it and gets ZERO protection.
-- At a let-group that is (mostly) benign: the binding generalizes, so a return-only
-- var is usually ambiguous anyway.  At an IMPL METHOD BODY it is NOT, and the reason
-- is dict-passing: an impl method's OWN method-level constraint var
-- (`lift : Num b => a -> Box b`) is supplied by a DICT at the CALL SITE, so the
-- caller genuinely chooses it (`q : Box Float; q = lift 3` ⇒ b↦Float).  Grounding it
-- to Int here would permanently monomorphise a var the caller had already fixed —
-- MEASURED: `MkBox 0.0` before, `E-PANIC: floatToString: not a Float` after, i.e.
-- trading #518's S0 for a new one.  Return-position type params are exactly what
-- typeclass dispatch concentrates, so this site's density of the triggering shape is
-- far higher than a let-group's.
--
-- Membership in [memberMono] — arg position OR return position — therefore means
-- "determined by the caller (via an argument, an annotation, or a method dict)" and
-- is left alone.  #518's shape is the complement: the element var of `s [1, 2]` in
-- `t _ = s [1, 2]` is consumed entirely inside the body and surfaces nowhere in
-- `t : Option2 Int -> Int`, so nothing outside the body can ever determine it.  That
-- var, and only that var, is what this grounds.
--
-- (Note the membership test is the INVERSE of defaultAmbiguousNum's, which requires
-- the var to be IN the type — `containsI id inT` — and then excludes arg-reachable
-- ones.  Neither existing primitive expresses "in the type at all", hence this one.)
defaultBodyLocalNum : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Mono -> Unit
defaultBodyLocalNum obls memberMono =
  let inType = monoUnboundIds memberMono
  let candidates = dedupI (numConstrainedIds obls)
  groundNumVars obls (filterList (id => not (containsI id inType)) candidates)

-- per-binding defaulting: run defaultAmbiguousNum on each member's placeholder mono
defaultEachMember : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, Mono) -> Unit
defaultEachMember _ [] = ()
defaultEachMember obls ((_, m)::rest) =
  let _ = defaultAmbiguousNum obls m
  defaultEachMember obls rest

-- RETPOS soundness — ambiguous interface constraint (run≠build hole).
-- A method obligation recorded inside a binding whose discriminating tyvar is
-- UNBOUND, UNANCHORED, and appears NOWHERE in the binding's generalized type is
-- genuinely ambiguous (`make`'s result var in `f x = reveal (make (reveal x))`,
-- `f : Thing a => a -> Int`: `make`'s var is tied to neither `a` nor the result).
-- No annotation determines the instance and, with ≥2 impls, eval vs emit default
-- to DIFFERENT impls — a silent run≠build miscompile (RETPOS-DISPATCH-DESIGN.md).
-- We REJECT it by handing the genuine ambiguous DISPATCH mono to the existing
-- undetermined-obligation path (registering it as a call obligation), which
-- rejects (ambiguousImplMsg) under the SAME `Num` / active-dict / impl-count≥2
-- guards an undetermined call site already uses (so a sole-impl interface is never
-- over-rejected and `Num` stays on its literal-defaulting path).  Three filters
-- keep this from firing on legitimately-deferred constraints:
--   • member-type membership (defaultGroupNum's `monoUnboundIds`) — a var IN the
--     binding's type is determined by a caller/annotation;
--   • group LEVEL — a DEEPER var belongs to an inner let/where binding that already
--     generalized + checked it (its obligation merely leaks into our addedObls
--     window, e.g. `maximum`'s `where step` comparing at step's own element var);
--   • ANCHORED vars — a var that also appears inside a CONCRETE-HEADED dispatch mono
--     of this binding (`Set _a` from a `Set {…}` literal's FromEntries/Display) is
--     ground at emit by head-pinning, so it is sound-but-deferred, NOT ambiguous.
-- The obligation's record-site loc rides along (currentLoc is stale post-HM).
