  let names = dedup (flatMap tyVarNames tys)
  let tvs = freshTvMap names
  map (fromAstType tvs) tys

-- impls_overlap: do two impl head-arg lists share a common instance?  Every
-- TVar (fresh per impl, so distinct ids) is a unification wildcard with a shared
-- subst — `(List Int)` vs `(List a)` overlap via a:=Int; `a a` vs `Int Bool`
-- does NOT (resolve binds a:=Int, then Int vs Bool fails), mirroring the oracle.
cohOverlap : List Mono -> List Mono -> Bool
cohOverlap xs ys
  | listLen xs == listLen ys = cohOverlapGo (Ref []) (zipL xs ys)
  | otherwise = False

cohOverlapGo : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> List (Mono, Mono) -> Bool
cohOverlapGo _ [] = True
cohOverlapGo subst ((a, b)::rest)
  | cohGo subst a b = cohOverlapGo subst rest
  | otherwise = False

-- resolve a mono through the wildcard subst (chasing bound vars)
cohResolve : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Mono -> Mono
cohResolve subst m = match normalize m
  TVar cell => match lookupAssocI (tyvarId cell) subst.value
    Some m2 => cohResolve subst m2
    None => TVar cell
  other => other

cohGo : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohGo subst t1 t2 = cohGoR subst (cohResolve subst t1) (cohResolve subst t2)

cohGoR : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohGoR subst (TVar c1) (TVar c2)
  | tyvarId c1 == tyvarId c2 = True
  | otherwise = cohBind subst (tyvarId c1) (TVar c2)
cohGoR subst (TVar c1) t = cohBind subst (tyvarId c1) t
cohGoR subst t (TVar c2) = cohBind subst (tyvarId c2) t
cohGoR _ (TCon a) (TCon b) = a == b
cohGoR subst (TApp f1 a1) (TApp f2 a2) = cohGo subst f1 f2 && cohGo subst a1 a2
cohGoR subst (TFun a1 _ b1) (TFun a2 _ b2) = cohGo subst a1 a2
  && cohGo subst b1 b2
cohGoR _ (TEff _) (TEff _) = True
cohGoR _ _ _ = False

-- bind id := m in the wildcard subst and return True
cohBind : Ref (List (Int, Mono)) -> Int -> Mono -> Bool
cohBind subst id m =
  let _ = setRef subst ((id, m)::subst.value)
  True

-- ── shared one-sided matcher core (#156 S1) ────────────────────────────────
-- cohSubsumes (Mono-space), tySubsumes (AST-space) and matchTyMono (AST head-
-- pattern vs Mono) are the SAME one-sided, consistency-enforcing structural match
-- in three node spaces: only the GENERAL side's vars bind, a var bound twice must
-- be CONSISTENT (its two specific bindings structurally equal), and effect rows
-- are IGNORED (row-blind — §7's single-meaning law).  They differ only in (a) how
-- a node is destructured into a var / children / leaf and (b) the specific-side
-- equality used for the consistency check.  [matchOneSided] is that shared core:
-- it walks a worklist of (general, specific) pairs, threads a key→specific subst,
-- and returns it (None on a head-mismatch OR an inconsistent re-bind).  The Bool
-- frontends are `isSome ∘ matchOneSided`; matchTyMono returns the subst itself.
-- Enforcing consistency HERE is what makes matchTyMono a genuine SUBSTITUTION
-- (DICT-SEMANTICS §3 `inst`): the old `xs ++ ys` combine accepted an inconsistent
-- non-linear-head match (e.g. `Get (Box a) a` vs `Get (Box Int) Bool`) and so
-- could false-match and select the wrong impl's requires.  cohOverlap is NOT a
-- frontend of this core — it is two-sided unification (both sides' vars bind), the
-- coherence-overlap check, and stays separate.
--   MVar k   — the general node is a variable keyed [k]; bind/consistency-check it.
--   MKids ps — heads agree; recurse on these (general, specific) child pairs.
--   MOk      — leaf success (matching TCons, or two effect rows).
--   MFail    — head mismatch (no match).
data MStep k g s = MVar k | MKids (List (g, s)) | MOk | MFail

matchOneSided : (g -> s -> MStep k g s) -> (k -> k -> Bool) -> (s -> s -> Bool) -> List (g, s) -> List (k, s) -> Option (List (k, s))
matchOneSided _ _ _ [] acc = Some acc
matchOneSided step keyEq eqS ((g, s)::rest) acc = match step g s
  MFail => None
  MOk => matchOneSided step keyEq eqS rest acc
  MKids kids => matchOneSided step keyEq eqS (kids ++ rest) acc
  MVar k => match lookupBy keyEq k acc
    Some prev =>
      if eqS prev s then
        matchOneSided step keyEq eqS rest acc
      else
        None
    None => matchOneSided step keyEq eqS rest ((k, s)::acc)

-- NB: the comparison parameter must NOT be named `eq` — that is the prelude Eq
-- method, and the marker would rewrite the local reference into a method call,
-- importing a phantom `Eq k` constraint the signature can't discharge.
lookupBy : (k -> k -> Bool) -> k -> List (k, v) -> Option v
lookupBy _ _ [] = None
lookupBy keq k ((k2, v)::rest) =
  if keq k k2 then
    Some v
  else
    lookupBy keq k rest

-- subsumes ~general ~specific: is [specific] an instance of [general]?  Only the
-- general side's vars bind (consistently: a var bound twice must see structurally
-- equal types); the specific side's vars are rigid (match only the same id).
cohSubsumes : List Mono -> List Mono -> Bool
cohSubsumes general specific
  | listLen general == listLen specific =
    isSome (matchOneSided cohStep eqInt cohEqMono (zipL general specific) [])
  | otherwise = False

-- Mono-space step: normalize BOTH sides (chasing links), then classify the general
-- node.  Effect rows are row-blind (TEff/TEff ⇒ MOk; TFun's row slot ignored).
cohStep : Mono -> Mono -> MStep Int Mono Mono
cohStep g0 s0 =
  let g = normalize g0
  let s = normalize s0
  match g
    TVar c => MVar (tyvarId c)
    TCon a => match s
      TCon b => if a == b then MOk else MFail
      _ => MFail
    TApp f1 a1 => match s
      TApp f2 a2 => MKids [(f1, f2), (a1, a2)]
      _ => MFail
    TFun a1 _ b1 => match s
      TFun a2 _ b2 => MKids [(a1, a2), (b1, b2)]
      _ => MFail
    TEff _ => match s
      TEff _ => MOk
      _ => MFail

eqInt : Int -> Int -> Bool
eqInt a b = a == b

-- rigid structural equality (the specific side's vars match only the same id)
cohEqMono : Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohEqMono a b = cohEqR (normalize a) (normalize b)

cohEqR : Mono -> Mono -> Bool
cohEqR (TVar c1) (TVar c2) = tyvarId c1 == tyvarId c2
cohEqR (TCon a) (TCon b) = a == b
cohEqR (TApp f1 a1) (TApp f2 a2) = cohEqMono f1 f2 && cohEqMono a1 a2
cohEqR (TFun a1 _ b1) (TFun a2 _ b2) = cohEqMono a1 a2 && cohEqMono b1 b2
cohEqR (TEff _) (TEff _) = True
cohEqR _ _ = False

-- [a] strictly more specific than [b]: b subsumes a but not a subsumes b
cohStrictlyMoreSpecific : List Mono -> List Mono -> Bool
cohStrictlyMoreSpecific a b = cohSubsumes b a && not (cohSubsumes a b)

-- render two impl head-arg lists against ONE shared naming ctx (so a var shared
-- across both prints identically and distinct vars never collide) — mirrors the
-- oracle's pp_monos_pair.
cohPpPair : List Mono -> List Mono -> (String, String)
cohPpPair xs ys =
  let ctx = Ref []
  let cnt = Ref 0
  let s1 = joinWith " " (cohPpEach ctx cnt xs)
  let s2 = joinWith " " (cohPpEach ctx cnt ys)
  (s1, s2)

cohPpEach : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> List Mono -> List String
cohPpEach _ _ [] = []
cohPpEach ctx cnt (m::ms) = ppGo ctx cnt 0 m :: cohPpEach ctx cnt ms

-- the coherence error string for one conflicting pair (OverlappingImpls).
cohOverlapMsg : String -> List Mono -> List Mono -> String
cohOverlapMsg iface xs ys = match cohPpPair xs ys
  (s1, s2) => "Overlapping impls of \{iface}: \{s1} and \{s2} can match the same type. Make them disjoint, or wrap one type in a newtype"

-- scan all ordered pairs; return the FIRST conflict (mirrors the oracle raising on
-- the first incoherent pair).  None ⇒ coherent.  #414: the payload is the message
-- PLUS the span to blame — the scan runs over a `reverseL`-ed list, so the outer
-- element `e1` is the LATER-declared impl of the pair, which is also the head the
-- message names FIRST.  Blaming it keeps caret and wording consistent.
--
-- Every T-CONFLICTING-IMPL push reaches a conflict through HERE, so this one return
-- type is the seam that offers all of them a span.  The two cross-module pushes
-- deliberately drop it (see globalCoherenceConflict) — a span is only useful to a
-- consumer that files the diagnostic against the span's OWN file, which today only
-- the single-file `checkCoherence` path does.
cohFirstConflict : List CohImpl -> Option (String, Option Loc)
cohFirstConflict [] = None
cohFirstConflict (e::rest) = match cohConflictWith e rest
  Some hit => Some hit
  None => cohFirstConflict rest

cohConflictWith : CohImpl -> List CohImpl -> Option (String, Option Loc)
cohConflictWith _ [] = None
cohConflictWith (e1@(CohImpl if1 xs mid1 _)) ((CohImpl if2 ys mid2 _)::rest)
  | if1 == if2 && cohOverlap xs ys = cohAnonConflict e1 xs ys mid1 mid2 rest
  | otherwise = cohConflictWith e1 rest

-- two overlapping impls: a strict specialization (most-specific-wins) is coherent
-- — it falls through to the rest of the scan.  Otherwise it is an unresolvable
-- overlap (mirrors the oracle's OverlappingImpls).  When the two impls come from
-- DIFFERENT modules (mid1 /= mid2, both non-empty), the message names BOTH owning
-- modules (the cross-module global-coherence case).
cohAnonConflict : CohImpl -> List Mono -> List Mono -> String -> String -> List CohImpl -> Option (String, Option Loc)
cohAnonConflict e1 xs ys mid1 mid2 rest
  | cohStrictlyMoreSpecific xs ys = cohConflictWith e1 rest
  | cohStrictlyMoreSpecific ys xs = cohConflictWith e1 rest
  | mid1 != "" && mid2 != "" && mid1 != mid2 =
    Some (cohCrossModuleMsg (cohImplIface e1) mid1 mid2, cohImplLoc e1)
  | otherwise = Some (cohOverlapMsg (cohImplIface e1) xs ys, cohImplLoc e1)

-- cross-module conflict wording: names the interface and BOTH owning modules.
cohCrossModuleMsg : String -> String -> String -> String
cohCrossModuleMsg iface mid1 mid2 =
  "Conflicting `impl \{iface}`. Defined in \{mid2} and \{mid1}"

cohImplIface : CohImpl -> String
cohImplIface (CohImpl i _ _ _) = i

cohImplLoc : CohImpl -> Option Loc
cohImplLoc (CohImpl _ _ _ l) = l

-- run coherence over USER decls; push the first conflict (if any) into typeErrors.
-- The impls are scanned in REVERSE declaration order to mirror the oracle, whose
-- env.impls is built by prepending each entry (`entry :: !(env.impls)`) — so the
-- conflict message names the later-declared impl as the first head, byte-identical.
checkCoherence : List Decl -> Unit
checkCoherence userDecls = match cohFirstConflict (reverseL (cohCollectImpls userDecls))
  Some (msg, loc) => pushTypeErrorAt "T-CONFLICTING-IMPL" loc msg
  None => ()

-- ── D1 WS-1a: superinterface-existence gate ────────────────────────────────
-- Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's `check_superinterface_obligations`.  An
-- `impl C T` whose interface declares `interface C a requires D a` is unsound
-- unless a matching `impl D T` also exists.  This post-pass iterates the USER
-- impls (so a user impl that overrides a prelude impl isn't double-flagged) and,
-- for each, looks up its interface's supers in the FULL decl universe
-- ([allDecls], prelude included — the SUPER impl it needs may be a prelude impl).
-- It fires ONLY when the impl head's type args are all CONCRETE/ground (mirrors
-- the oracle's `is_concrete` gate); a parametric head like `impl Monoid (Bag a)`
-- (free tyvar) is deferred exactly as the oracle defers it.  Error wording is
-- byte-identical to the oracle's MissingSuperImpl message.
-- [userDecls] = the impls to check; [allDecls] = prelude + user, for both the
-- interface-supers lookup AND super-impl existence.
checkSuperImpls : List Decl -> List Decl -> Unit
checkSuperImpls userDecls allDecls =
  foreachUnit
    (pushTypeError "T-MISSING-SUPER-IMPL")
    (flatMap (superImplMsgsOf allDecls) userDecls)

foreachUnit : (a -> Unit) -> List a -> Unit
foreachUnit _ [] = ()
foreachUnit f (x::xs) =
  let _ = f x
  foreachUnit f xs

-- ── P0-17: impl-completeness check ─────────────────────────────────────────
-- Beta finding P0-17 (qa-beta-2026-07-07): an `impl Iface Ty…` that defines only
-- SOME of the interface's methods used to `check` clean, then PANIC at runtime
-- (`E-PANIC: unbound identifier: <method>`) when a missing method is dispatched —
-- a run≠check hole.  For each impl, verify every REQUIRED interface method (one
-- declared WITHOUT a default body) is defined by the impl body; each missing one
-- is a located `T-INCOMPLETE-IMPL` error, so check/run/build all reject instead of
-- deferring to a runtime panic.
--
-- Runs POST-desugar (from the typecheck cores), which is the ONLY stage where both
-- sets are cleanly visible with defaults distinguishable:
--   • desugar's mergeIfaceDefaults has coalesced each interface method's split
--     signature-entry (`lt : …`, default None) and body-entry (`lt x y = …`,
--     default Some) into ONE `IfaceMethod` carrying the merged Option MethodDefault.
--     PRE-merge the sig-only `None` entry would make every defaulted method (Ord's
--     lt/gt/…) look REQUIRED → hundreds of false positives across core/stdlib.
--   • desugar's fillImplDefaults has synthesized same-module default clauses INTO
--     the impl — which only ADDS to the defined set, never removes a required one,
--     so it cannot mask a genuinely-missing required method.
-- [lookupDecls] (prelude + accumulated modules + this module) resolves the
-- interface decl even when it is imported; [iterDecls] (this module's own decls)
-- are the impls to check.  Unknown interface (not in scope) → skip (a distinct
-- error is reported elsewhere), mirroring superImplMsgsOf.
checkImplCompleteness : List Decl -> List Decl -> Unit
checkImplCompleteness lookupDecls iterDecls =
  foreachUnit
    pushIncompleteImpl
    (flatMap (implCompletenessMsgsOf lookupDecls) iterDecls)

pushIncompleteImpl : (Option Loc, String) -> Unit
pushIncompleteImpl (loc, msg) = pushTypeErrorOnceAt "T-INCOMPLETE-IMPL" loc msg

-- the missing-required-method errors for one decl (a DImpl, possibly DAttrib-
-- wrapped); [] for a non-impl decl, an unknown interface, or a complete impl.
implCompletenessMsgsOf : List Decl -> Decl -> List (Option Loc, String)
implCompletenessMsgsOf lookupDecls (DAttrib _ d) =
  implCompletenessMsgsOf lookupDecls d
implCompletenessMsgsOf lookupDecls DImpl { iface, tys, methods, ... } =
  match ifaceRequiredMethods lookupDecls iface
    None => []   -- unknown interface (reported elsewhere) → skip
    Some required =>
      let defined = map implMethodName methods
      let loc = firstImplMethodLoc methods
      map
        (m => (loc, missingImplMethodMsg iface tys m))
        (filter (m => not (contains m defined)) required)
implCompletenessMsgsOf _ _ = []

implMethodName : ImplMethod -> String
implMethodName (ImplMethod n _ _) = n

-- a loc to attribute the error to: the first present impl-method body's span (the
-- impl head carries no Loc of its own).  None for a wholly-empty impl body.
firstImplMethodLoc : List ImplMethod -> Option Loc
firstImplMethodLoc [] = None
firstImplMethodLoc ((ImplMethod _ _ body)::_) = exprLoc body

-- the names of interface [target]'s REQUIRED methods (those whose merged
-- IfaceMethod carries NO default body); None if no such interface is in scope.
ifaceRequiredMethods : List Decl -> String -> Option (List String)
ifaceRequiredMethods [] _ = None
ifaceRequiredMethods ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) target = match ifaceRequiredMethods [d] target
  Some r => Some r
  None => ifaceRequiredMethods rest target
ifaceRequiredMethods ((DInterface { name, methods, ... })::rest) target
  | name == target = Some (requiredMethodNames methods)
  | otherwise = ifaceRequiredMethods rest target
ifaceRequiredMethods (_::rest) target = ifaceRequiredMethods rest target

requiredMethodNames : List IfaceMethod -> List String
requiredMethodNames [] = []
requiredMethodNames ((IfaceMethod n _ None)::rest) =
  n :: requiredMethodNames rest
requiredMethodNames ((IfaceMethod _ _ (Some _))::rest) =
  requiredMethodNames rest

-- #154 PR1: the multi-module analogue of checkImplCompleteness — identical, but the
-- per-impl `ifaceRequiredMethods fullUniverse iface` O(N) scan is replaced by an O(log N)
-- lookup in the persistent iface→required-methods map (universeIfaceRequiredRef).  The
-- map holds one entry per interface in the module universe (core + earlier modules +
-- this one), so a `None` here means the interface is genuinely out of scope — exactly
-- as the scan's `None` did.  Interface names are globally unique, so the map's
-- last-write-wins carries the same required-method list the scan's first-match returned.
checkImplCompletenessMap : OrdMap (List String) -> List Decl -> Unit
checkImplCompletenessMap reqMap iterDecls =
  foreachUnit
    pushIncompleteImpl
    (flatMap (implCompletenessMsgsOfMap reqMap) iterDecls)

implCompletenessMsgsOfMap : OrdMap (List String) -> Decl -> List (Option Loc, String)
implCompletenessMsgsOfMap reqMap (DAttrib _ d) =
  implCompletenessMsgsOfMap reqMap d
implCompletenessMsgsOfMap reqMap DImpl { iface, tys, methods, ... } =
  match omLookup iface reqMap
    None => []   -- unknown interface (reported elsewhere) → skip
    Some required =>
      let defined = map implMethodName methods
      let loc = firstImplMethodLoc methods
      map
        (m => (loc, missingImplMethodMsg iface tys m))
        (filter (m => not (contains m defined)) required)
implCompletenessMsgsOfMap _ _ = []

-- #154 PR1: fold each interface in [prog] into the required-methods map.  Mirrors
-- ifaceRequiredMethods' DAttrib-unwrapping + `requiredMethodNames methods` value.
insertIfaceRequired : List Decl -> OrdMap (List String) -> OrdMap (List String)
insertIfaceRequired [] m = m
insertIfaceRequired ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) m =
  insertIfaceRequired rest (insertIfaceRequired [d] m)
insertIfaceRequired ((DInterface { name, methods, ... })::rest) m =
  insertIfaceRequired rest (omInsert name (requiredMethodNames methods) m)
insertIfaceRequired (_::rest) m = insertIfaceRequired rest m

-- #154 PR2 (site #3): fold [prog]'s interface decls into the pair of accumulators that
-- feed methodIfaceParamsRef / registeredIfacesRef.  A PURE mirror of
-- registerMethodIfaceParamsAll (DAttrib-unwrap → per-method `omInsert mname (iface,
-- typarams, mty)` into the params map + `omInsert iface ()` into the registered-iface
-- set), updating the two passed maps in lockstep instead of the global refs, so the copy
-- in checkBodyImpl's Module arm lands the identical maps the per-module rebuild produced.
insertMethodIfaceParams : List Decl -> (OrdMap (String, List String, Ty), OrdMap Unit) -> (OrdMap (String, List String, Ty), OrdMap Unit)
insertMethodIfaceParams [] acc = acc
insertMethodIfaceParams ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) acc =
  insertMethodIfaceParams rest (insertMethodIfaceParams [d] acc)
insertMethodIfaceParams ((DInterface { name, typarams, methods, ... })::rest) acc = insertMethodIfaceParams rest (insertIfaceMethodsAcc name typarams methods acc)
insertMethodIfaceParams (_::rest) acc = insertMethodIfaceParams rest acc

insertIfaceMethodsAcc : String -> List String -> List IfaceMethod -> (OrdMap (String, List String, Ty), OrdMap Unit) -> (OrdMap (String, List String, Ty), OrdMap Unit)
insertIfaceMethodsAcc _ _ [] acc = acc
insertIfaceMethodsAcc iface typarams ((IfaceMethod mname mty _)::rest) (mp, ifs) = insertIfaceMethodsAcc iface typarams rest (omInsert mname (iface, typarams, mty) mp, omInsert iface () ifs)

missingImplMethodMsg : String -> List Ty -> String -> String
missingImplMethodMsg iface tys method = "'impl \{iface} \{joinWith " " (map ppTyAtom tys)}' is missing method '\{method}'. Every interface method without a default body must be implemented — define '\{method}' in the impl (or check for a typo in a method name)"

-- ── WS-5: phantom interface method detection ──────────────────────────────
-- A method whose declared type mentions NONE of the interface's type parameters
-- (neither in argument position nor in the result) cannot be dispatched: there
-- is no argument or return type to select an impl from.  Reject at check time
-- so the silent mis-dispatch at runtime is caught early.
--
-- Examples:
--   interface Phantom a where
--     name : String          -- PHANTOM: `a` appears nowhere in the type
--   interface Show a where
--     show : a -> String     -- OK: `a` appears in argument position
--   interface Pure f where
--     pure : a -> f a        -- OK: `f` appears in result position
--
-- The check uses the full method type (args AND result via tyVarNames), so
-- return-position methods like `pure : a -> f a` or `minBound : a` are NOT
-- flagged.  Multi-param interfaces: phantom iff ALL params absent.

checkPhantomMethods : List Decl -> Unit
checkPhantomMethods decls =
  foreachUnit
    (pushTypeError "T-PHANTOM-METHOD")
    (flatMap phantomMethodMsgs decls)

phantomMethodMsgs : Decl -> List String
phantomMethodMsgs (DAttrib _ d) = phantomMethodMsgs d
phantomMethodMsgs (DInterface { name, typarams, methods, ... }) =
  flatMap (phantomMethodMsg name typarams) methods
phantomMethodMsgs _ = []

phantomMethodMsg : String -> List String -> IfaceMethod -> List String
phantomMethodMsg ifaceName typarams (IfaceMethod mname mty _) =
  if isEmptyL typarams then
    []
  else if anyMember (tyVarNames mty) typarams then
    []
  else
    [
      "Method '\{mname}' in interface '\{ifaceName}' does not mention interface parameter(s) \{intercalateComma typarams}; cannot dispatch"
    ]

intercalateComma : List String -> String
intercalateComma [] = ""
intercalateComma [x] = "'" ++ x ++ "'"
intercalateComma (x::rest) = "'\{x}', \{intercalateComma rest}"

-- ── WS-4a: cyclic superinterface detection ────────────────────────────────
-- A cyclic `interface A requires B; interface B requires A` is ill-formed: no
-- impl can ever be constructed (each demands the other) and the transitive
-- evidence resolver would diverge.  DFS over the `requires` graph; if any node
-- is re-encountered on the current DFS stack, report the cycle.
-- Runs over ALL interfaces in scope (prelude + user) — a prelude cycle would
-- be equally ill-formed.  The `done` ref tracks fully-explored nodes to avoid
-- redundant traversal and re-reporting multi-root cycles.
checkInterfaceCycles : List Decl -> Unit
checkInterfaceCycles allDecls =
  let ifaceNames = collectIfaceNames allDecls
  let done = Ref []
  foreachUnit (checkOneIfaceCycle allDecls done) ifaceNames

-- #152: the whole-program final-checks tail, shared by all five entry points
-- (checkToLines / checkToLinesWithRuntime / checkErrorsWithRuntime /
-- checkProgramDiags / checkModuleFullDiags).  [cohDecls] feeds coherence AND
-- phantom-methods; [cycDecls] feeds interface-cycles AND checkSuperImpls's second
-- (whole-universe) argument.  ⚠️ trap #5: coherence must see USER decls only, so
-- this helper has NO access to `prog` — the caller decides [cohDecls] explicitly
-- (never a `prog` fallback).  Call ORDER is load-bearing (all push into typeErrors).
runFinalChecks : List Decl -> List Decl -> Unit
runFinalChecks cohDecls cycDecls =
  let _ = checkCoherence cohDecls
  let _ = checkInterfaceCycles cycDecls
  let _ = checkPhantomMethods cohDecls
  checkSuperImpls cohDecls cycDecls

checkOneIfaceCycle : List Decl -> Ref (List String) -> String -> Unit
checkOneIfaceCycle allDecls done ifaceName =
  if not (contains ifaceName done.value) then
    foreachUnit (pushTypeError "T-CYCLIC-SUPERINTERFACE") (ifaceDfsCycle allDecls done [ifaceName] ifaceName)
  else
    ()

collectIfaceNames : List Decl -> List String
collectIfaceNames [] = []
collectIfaceNames ((DInterface { name, ... })::rest) =
  name :: collectIfaceNames rest
collectIfaceNames ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) = collectIfaceNames (d::rest)
collectIfaceNames (_::rest) = collectIfaceNames rest

-- DFS from [current]; [path] is the stack of names visited so far (including
-- [current] as its last element, used to reconstruct the cycle message).
-- Marks [current] as done after all its supers are explored.
ifaceDfsCycle : List Decl -> Ref (List String) -> List String -> String -> List String
ifaceDfsCycle allDecls done path current = match ifaceSupersOf allDecls current
  None =>
    let _ = setRef done (current::done.value)
    []
  Some (_, supers) =>
    let msgs = ifaceDfsCycleSupers allDecls done path supers
    let _ = setRef done (current::done.value)
    msgs

ifaceDfsCycleSupers : List Decl -> Ref (List String) -> List String -> List Super -> List String
ifaceDfsCycleSupers _ _ _ [] = []
ifaceDfsCycleSupers allDecls done path ((Super superName _)::rest) =
  let msgs1 = ifaceDfsCycleOneSuper allDecls done path superName
  msgs1 ++ ifaceDfsCycleSupers allDecls done path rest

-- check one super edge: cycle if superName is on the DFS path; skip if done; else recurse.
ifaceDfsCycleOneSuper : List Decl -> Ref (List String) -> List String -> String -> List String
ifaceDfsCycleOneSuper allDecls done path superName =
  if contains superName path then
    [cyclicSuperIfaceMsg (path ++ [superName])]
  else if contains superName done.value then
    []
  else
    ifaceDfsCycle allDecls done (path ++ [superName]) superName

-- e.g. ["A", "B", "A"] → "cyclic superinterface: A requires B requires A"
cyclicSuperIfaceMsg : List String -> String
cyclicSuperIfaceMsg path = "Cyclic superinterface: "
  ++ joinWith " requires " path

-- the missing-superinterface error messages for one user decl (a DImpl, possibly
-- wrapped in DAttrib); [] for any non-impl decl or when every super impl exists.
superImplMsgsOf : List Decl -> Decl -> List String
superImplMsgsOf allDecls (DAttrib _ d) = superImplMsgsOf allDecls d
superImplMsgsOf allDecls DImpl { iface, tys, ... } =
  match ifaceSupersOf allDecls iface
    None => []   -- unknown interface (already reported elsewhere) → skip
    Some (typarams, supers) =>
      if listLen typarams == listLen tys && allList tyIsConcrete tys then
        flatMap (superMsgFor allDecls iface tys (zipL typarams tys)) supers
      else
        []   -- arity mismatch, or a non-concrete head (deferred, like the oracle)
superImplMsgsOf _ _ = []

-- check one `Super superName params` against the substitution: map each super
-- param name through [subst] to its concrete Ty; if every param mapped, all are
-- concrete, AND no matching `impl superName <concrete>` exists, emit the error.
superMsgFor : List Decl -> String -> List Ty -> List (String, Ty) -> Super -> List String
superMsgFor allDecls iface implTys subst (Super superName params) =
  let concrete = substParams params subst
  if listLen concrete == listLen params && allList tyIsConcrete concrete && not (superImplExists allDecls superName concrete) then
    [missingSuperImplMsg iface implTys superName concrete]
  else
    []

-- map each super-param NAME through [subst] to its Ty, dropping any unmapped (so a
-- short result vs [params] signals an incomplete substitution → caller skips).
substParams : List String -> List (String, Ty) -> List Ty
substParams [] _ = []
substParams (p::rest) subst = match lookupAssoc p subst
  Some t => t :: substParams rest subst
  None => substParams rest subst

-- byte-identical to lib/typecheck.ml's MissingSuperImpl rendering: both the impl
-- head and the super are printed with the same space-joined atom-position args.
missingSuperImplMsg : String -> List Ty -> String -> List Ty -> String
missingSuperImplMsg iface implTys superName superTys = "'impl \{iface} \{joinWith " " (map ppTyAtom implTys)}' requires a superinterface 'impl \{superName} \{joinWith " " (map ppTyAtom superTys)}', which is missing"

-- the (typarams, supers) of interface [target], scanning DInterface decls; None if
-- no such interface is in scope.
ifaceSupersOf : List Decl -> String -> Option (List String, List Super)
ifaceSupersOf [] _ = None
ifaceSupersOf ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) target = match ifaceSupersOf [d] target
  Some r => Some r
  None => ifaceSupersOf rest target
ifaceSupersOf ((DInterface { pub, def, name, typarams, supers, methods })::rest) target
  | name == target = Some (typarams, supers)
  | otherwise = ifaceSupersOf rest target
ifaceSupersOf (_::rest) target = ifaceSupersOf rest target

-- does any `impl superName <head>` with a head matching [concrete] exist? (head
-- may be parametric — a free tyvar in the impl head matches any concrete arg,
-- mirroring the oracle's mono_matches: pattern=impl head, concrete=super args.)
superImplExists : List Decl -> String -> List Ty -> Bool
superImplExists allDecls superName concrete =
  anyList (implMatchesSuper superName concrete) allDecls

implMatchesSuper : String -> List Ty -> Decl -> Bool
implMatchesSuper superName concrete (DAttrib _ d) =
  implMatchesSuper superName concrete d
implMatchesSuper superName concrete (DImpl { iface, tys, ... }) = iface == superName
  && listLen tys == listLen concrete
  && allList (p => tyMatchesAst (fst p) (snd p)) (zipL tys concrete)
implMatchesSuper _ _ _ = False

-- a Ty is concrete iff it contains no TyVar (mirror of the oracle's is_concrete,
-- which treats effect rows as transparent — TyEffect ignores its row).
tyIsConcrete : Ty -> Bool
tyIsConcrete (TyVar _) = False
tyIsConcrete (TyCon _ _) = True
tyIsConcrete (TyApp a b) = tyIsConcrete a && tyIsConcrete b
tyIsConcrete (TyFun a b) = tyIsConcrete a && tyIsConcrete b
tyIsConcrete (TyTuple ts) = allList tyIsConcrete ts
tyIsConcrete (TyEffect _ _ t) = tyIsConcrete t
tyIsConcrete (TyConstrained _ t) = tyIsConcrete t

-- one-directional structural match (pattern may carry free TyVar wildcards;
-- concrete is fully ground).  Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's mono_matches.
tyMatchesAst : Ty -> Ty -> Bool
tyMatchesAst (TyVar _) _ = True
tyMatchesAst (TyCon a _) (TyCon b _) = a == b
tyMatchesAst (TyApp f1 a1) (TyApp f2 a2) = tyMatchesAst f1 f2
  && tyMatchesAst a1 a2
tyMatchesAst (TyFun a1 b1) (TyFun a2 b2) = tyMatchesAst a1 a2
  && tyMatchesAst b1 b2
tyMatchesAst (TyTuple ts1) (TyTuple ts2) = listLen ts1 == listLen ts2
  && allList (p => tyMatchesAst (fst p) (snd p)) (zipL ts1 ts2)
tyMatchesAst (TyEffect _ _ t1) t2 = tyMatchesAst t1 t2
tyMatchesAst t1 (TyEffect _ _ t2) = tyMatchesAst t1 t2
tyMatchesAst (TyConstrained _ t1) t2 = tyMatchesAst t1 t2
tyMatchesAst t1 (TyConstrained _ t2) = tyMatchesAst t1 t2
tyMatchesAst _ _ = False

-- effectful concatMap of cohImplsOf (astTypeFresh allocates fresh tyvars → <Mut>)
cohCollectImpls : List Decl -> List CohImpl
cohCollectImpls [] = []
cohCollectImpls (d::rest) = cohImplsOf d ++ cohCollectImpls rest

-- ── D3 / WS-2 part-2: GLOBAL (cross-module) coherence ──────────────────────
-- Per-module coherence (checkCoherence prog) accepts two DIFFERENT modules that
-- each define `impl C T` for the SAME instance — each module is locally coherent,
-- but jointly they are incoherent (silent dispatch ambiguity).  This pass scans
-- the joint impl set over ALL USER modules (prelude EXCLUDED — a user `impl Eq Int`
-- legitimately overrides the prelude one, so prelude impls must NOT be in the set).
-- Each CohImpl carries the id of the module that PHYSICALLY declares it; imports do
-- NOT copy impl decls, so the joint set has exactly one entry per definition and a
-- structural (iface, head-tys) overlap already distinguishes distinct definitions —
-- no occurrence-dedup needed.  A conflict between two distinct modules is reported
-- naming BOTH (cohCrossModuleMsg via cohAnonConflict's mid1/=mid2 branch).
cohCollectModuleImpls : List (String, List Decl) -> List CohImpl
cohCollectModuleImpls [] = []
cohCollectModuleImpls ((mid, prog)::rest) = flatMap (cohImplsOfMid mid) prog
  ++ cohCollectModuleImpls rest

-- the first cross-module coherence conflict (if any) as a bare message — drivers
-- that thread errs as a VALUE (cmEntryCollect) prepend this themselves, rather than
-- relying on the typeErrors ref (cleared per-module by resetState).
--
-- ⚠️ #414: this deliberately DROPS the blame-span `cohFirstConflict` now returns.
-- Both consumers attach the diagnostic to the ENTRY module, but the span belongs to
-- whichever module declares the offending impl — and the `--json`/human renderers
-- key a diagnostic's file by the GROUP it is filed under, NOT by the filename
-- inside its own `Loc`.  Attaching a foreign span therefore renders a caret at that
-- line/col in the WRONG file.  Measured, with the impl 40 lines into a dependency:
--     ./main.mdk:42:7: Conflicting `impl C`. Defined in amod and bmod
-- — main.mdk is 5 lines long, so the caret block cannot even render.  That is worse
-- than the file-start default: a confidently wrong location beats an obviously
-- fabricated one only at misleading the reader.  The cross-module wording already
-- names BOTH owning modules, which is the locating information that actually helps
-- here.  Attaching a real span needs the renderers to honour `Loc`'s own filename
-- (a driver/diagnostics.mdk change, well beyond an S3 caret fix).
globalCoherenceConflict : List (String, List Decl) -> Option String
globalCoherenceConflict modules =
  map fst (cohFirstConflict (reverseL (cohCollectModuleImpls modules)))

-- the USER decls a driver wants coherence-checked.  Set by single-file drivers
-- (check.mdk / typecheck_main.mdk / check_batch.mdk) to the desugared USER program
-- (NO prelude), so a user impl that overrides a prelude impl is not flagged.  []
-- (the golden/profile drivers and the no-runtime path) ⇒ checkToLines uses its own
-- prelude-free arg; checkToLinesWithRuntime runs no coherence (byte-identical).

-- exported so the single-file front-end drivers set the user boundary before check
export setCoherenceUserDecls : List Decl -> Unit
setCoherenceUserDecls ds = setRef driverState.value.coherenceUserDecls ds

-- ── instance-`requires` impl-dict routing (Phase 83/84 single-level) ────────
-- One parametric impl that carries a `requires`: its head tag (the tycon eval
-- narrows by), the methods it defines (to disambiguate which impl a method site
-- picked), the head type pattern (matched against the call's concrete result mono
-- to recover the element type), and its requires constraints.
-- Fields: head tycon tag, defined method names, head type pattern, requires,
-- full impl type-args, and the INTERFACE this impl is for.  The iface field
-- disambiguates same-head impls: one type can carry several `requires`-bearing
-- impls at the same head tycon for DIFFERENT interfaces whose requires differ in
-- shape (e.g. `Applicative (VT e) requires Semigroup e` alongside
-- `Debug (VT e a) requires Debug e, Debug a`).  RKey element-dict routing must
-- pick the entry for the interface being dispatched, not merely the first by tag.
public export data ImplEntry =
  | ImplEntry String (List String) Ty (List Require) (List Ty) String

-- #148: the impl/key tables are keyed by head tycon TAG into a bucket of entries
-- kept in FORWARD declaration order (append; never re-sorted).  Every per-site
-- lookup filters `tag2 == tag` first, so it scans ONE bucket instead of the whole
-- impl universe — turning findImplEntry's O(impls) scan into O(bucket).  The
-- forward intra-bucket order is load-bearing for the RESIDUAL first-match consumer
-- (findImplEntry, the iface-unknown `""` fallback), so the earlier-declared of two
-- coherent siblings at the same head still wins there.  (#203: the element/requires
-- routers that USED to first-match this table — the former implDictRoutesIn /
-- argImplDictRoutesForGo — now select the most-specific impl over KeyBuckets via
-- `matchedEntry` / `selectImplEntryByIface`; forward order still decides their
-- tie-break when no unique min⊑ exists.)  This is NOT the coherence structure —
-- cohFirstConflict scans a separate reverseL-ed list; these buckets keep forward
-- order and coherence is not routed through them.
export type ImplBuckets = OrdMap (List ImplEntry)
export type KeyBuckets = OrdMap (List KeyEntry)

-- the entries at head tycon [tag] (empty when no impl heads there), in declaration
-- order.  Shared by both bucket flavours (List ImplEntry / List KeyEntry).
bucketOf : String -> OrdMap (List a) -> List a
bucketOf tag buckets = fromOption [] (omLookup tag buckets)

-- build the table from the program's impls-with-requires (others need no dicts),
-- bucketed by head tycon tag with each bucket in forward declaration order.
buildImplTable : List Decl -> ImplBuckets
buildImplTable prog =
  omMapValues reverseL (bucketImplEntries (flatMap implEntryOf prog) omEmpty)

-- fold the flat, forward-ordered entry list into tag buckets by PREPENDING (O(1)
-- per entry, vs O(bucket) tail-append → O(k²) for k same-tag entries).  Each
-- bucket ends up in reverse declaration order; buildImplTable applies one
-- `reverseL` per bucket to restore declaration order (first-match semantics).
bucketImplEntries : List ImplEntry -> ImplBuckets -> ImplBuckets
bucketImplEntries [] acc = acc
bucketImplEntries ((ImplEntry tag ms hty reqs itys ifn)::rest) acc =
  let e = ImplEntry tag ms hty reqs itys ifn
  bucketImplEntries rest (omInsert tag (e :: bucketOf tag acc) acc)

implEntryOf : Decl -> List ImplEntry
implEntryOf (DAttrib _ d) = implEntryOf d
implEntryOf (DImpl { iface, tys, reqs, methods, ... }) = match reqs
  [] => []
  _ => implEntryFromTys iface tys reqs methods
implEntryOf _ = []

implEntryFromTys : String -> List Ty -> List Require -> List ImplMethod -> List ImplEntry
implEntryFromTys _ [] _ _ = []
implEntryFromTys iface (headTy::rest) reqs methods = match headTyconTy headTy
  Some tag => [
    ImplEntry tag (map implMethodNameTc methods) headTy reqs (headTy::rest) iface
  ]
  None => []

implMethodNameTc : ImplMethod -> String
implMethodNameTc (ImplMethod n _ _) = n

-- ── TYPECHECK-AUDIT C7: canonical-impl-key registry ────────────────────────
-- One entry per USER impl (regardless of `requires`): the methods it defines (to
-- match a method site), its head tycon (to detect a head-tag collision), its
-- head-arg type pattern (matched against the site's concrete result mono), and its
-- canonical impl key.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's `matching_impls` + `entry.impl_key`.
-- The route normally keeps the bare head tycon (the established head-tag dispatch
-- the native Core-IR/LLVM backend keys symbols by); it is UPGRADED to the full
-- canonical key ONLY when two impls share that head tycon for the method (the C7
-- case, e.g. Pair Int Bool vs Pair Bool Int).  This keeps every single-impl-per-head
-- site — i.e. all existing code and the native backend — byte-identical, while
-- letting eval's narrowMethod pick the right VTypedImpl by its key field when the
-- heads genuinely collide.
-- #203: the last three fields (iface, full impl type-args, requires) were added so
-- this ALL-impls registry (every user impl, requires-bearing or not) can serve as the
-- single most-specific-wins selector for EVERY resolution position (§6 uniform
-- resolution).  ImplBuckets OMITS no-requires impls, so it cannot see the specific
-- `List Int` sibling next to a general `List a`; KeyBuckets carries every impl plus its
-- requires, so `selectImplEntryByIface` / `matchedEntry` return the min⊑ winner's OWN
-- requires ([] for a no-requires specific impl — never the general sibling's).
public export data KeyEntry =
  | KeyEntry (List String) String Ty String String (List Ty) (List Require)

buildKeyTable : List Decl -> KeyBuckets
buildKeyTable prog =
  omMapValues reverseL (bucketKeyEntries (flatMap keyEntryOf prog) omEmpty)

-- fold the flat, forward-ordered entry list into tag buckets by PREPENDING (O(1)
-- per entry; buildKeyTable applies one `reverseL` per bucket to restore
-- declaration order).
bucketKeyEntries : List KeyEntry -> KeyBuckets -> KeyBuckets
bucketKeyEntries [] acc = acc
bucketKeyEntries ((KeyEntry ms tag hty key ifn itys reqs)::rest) acc =
  let e = KeyEntry ms tag hty key ifn itys reqs
  bucketKeyEntries rest (omInsert tag (e :: bucketOf tag acc) acc)

keyEntryOf : Decl -> List KeyEntry
keyEntryOf (DAttrib _ d) = keyEntryOf d
keyEntryOf (DImpl { iface, tys, reqs, methods, ... }) = match tys
  headTy::_ => match headTyconTy headTy
    Some tag => [
      KeyEntry (map implMethodNameTc methods) tag headTy (implKeyTc iface tys) iface tys reqs
    ]
    None => []
  [] => []
keyEntryOf _ = []

-- the canonical impl key, byte-identical to eval.mdk's implKeyOf: `iface ++ "|" ++
-- <prec-2 args> ++ "|"` (the trailing empty name-component is retained so keys stay
-- byte-identical to the pre-removal format).
implKeyTc : String -> List Ty -> String
implKeyTc iface tys = "\{iface}|\{joinWith " " (map ppTyAtom tys)}|"

-- the canonical key to stamp for the impl of [name] matching the ground result
-- mono — but ONLY when another impl of the same method shares its head tycon (so
-- bare-head routing is ambiguous).  No collision (the common case) or no match →
-- None → the caller keeps the head tag, behaviour unchanged.
-- #609: [goals] is the interface's FULL param-mono vector `τ̄` at this site
-- (recovered by ifaceParamMonos), or the singleton [resultMono] when the caller
-- has no vector — see entryHeadMatches for the fallback.
keyForSite : KeyBuckets -> String -> List Mono -> Option String
keyForSite table name goals = match matchedEntry table name goals
  Some (KeyEntry _ tag _ key _ _ _) =>
    if headCollides table name tag then
      Some key
    else
      None
  None => None

-- the head tycon that buckets a goal vector: the spine head of its FIRST component
-- (the receiver).  Unchanged from the pre-#609 scalar bucketing — widening the
-- BUCKET key to the whole vector is exactly where a 14th `List`-as-a-map quadratic
-- would appear (compiler/AGENTS.md), and it buys nothing: two impls of one
-- interface that differ past arg 0 still share arg 0's tycon, so they must land in
-- the same bucket for the min⊑ comparison to see them at all.  The vector is
-- consulted by the MATCH (entryHeadMatches) and by ⊑ (entryCovers), both O(head
-- size) per bucket entry — a constant factor on an unchanged O(bucket) scan.
goalHeadCon : List Mono -> Option String
goalHeadCon (g::_) = monoHeadCon g
goalHeadCon [] = None

-- the entry whose method set contains [name] and whose head pattern matches the
-- concrete result mono (the impl the checker dispatches into at this site).  When
-- SEVERAL entries' head patterns match (overlapping instances, e.g. `Def (List a)`
-- and `Def (List Int)` both matching the ground result `List Int`), pick the
-- MOST-SPECIFIC one — the entry that is strictly more specific than (or equal to)
-- every other matching entry (WS-3/D4 return-position most-specific-wins).  No
-- unique most-specific (equal heads or incomparable partial overlap) → keep the
-- FIRST match, behaviour unchanged.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's
-- `strictly_more_specific` selection at a call site.
matchedEntry : KeyBuckets -> String -> List Mono -> Option KeyEntry
matchedEntry table name goals =
  pickMostSpecificEntry (matchingEntries table name goals)

-- all entries defining [name] whose head pattern matches [resultMono], in
-- declaration order.  #148: matchTyMono can only succeed when the entry's head
-- tycon equals [resultMono]'s spine head (a KeyEntry head is never a bare TyVar —
-- keyEntryOf only emits one when headTyconTy is Some), so scanning ONLY the bucket
-- at `monoHeadCon resultMono` collects exactly the same entries in the same order
-- as the old whole-table scan.  A headless mono (TVar/TFun) matched no entry then
-- and keys no bucket now → [].
matchingEntries : KeyBuckets -> String -> List Mono -> List KeyEntry
matchingEntries buckets name goals = match goalHeadCon goals
  Some tag => matchingEntriesGo (bucketOf tag buckets) name goals
  None => []

matchingEntriesGo : List KeyEntry -> String -> List Mono -> List KeyEntry
matchingEntriesGo [] _ _ = []
matchingEntriesGo ((KeyEntry methodNames tag headTy key ifn itys reqs)::rest) name goals
  | contains name methodNames && entryHeadMatches headTy itys goals = KeyEntry methodNames tag headTy key ifn itys reqs :: matchingEntriesGo rest name goals
  | otherwise = matchingEntriesGo rest name goals

-- pick the entry strictly more specific than every OTHER matching entry; if no
-- such unique entry exists (≤1 match, equal heads, or incomparable overlap) keep
-- the head of the list (the pre-existing first-match behaviour).
pickMostSpecificEntry : List KeyEntry -> Option KeyEntry
pickMostSpecificEntry [] = None
pickMostSpecificEntry (e::rest) = match findMostSpecificEntry (e::rest) (e::rest)
  Some best => Some best
  None => Some e

findMostSpecificEntry : List KeyEntry -> List KeyEntry -> Option KeyEntry
findMostSpecificEntry [] _ = None
findMostSpecificEntry (c::cs) all
  | entryCoversAllOthers c all = Some c
  | otherwise = findMostSpecificEntry cs all

-- is [cand]'s head pattern more specific than (or structurally equal to) every
-- entry in [all]?  Equal-head entries are covered trivially; a genuinely more-
-- general entry is NOT covered, so [cand] is rejected for it.
entryCoversAllOthers : KeyEntry -> List KeyEntry -> Bool
entryCoversAllOthers cand all = allList (entryCovers cand) all

-- #609: compares the entries' FULL head vectors (`itys`), not their arg-0 head
-- patterns.  Arg-0-only made `Ix Int Bool` and `Ix Int Char` mutually "tyHeadEq" —
-- ⊑-EQUAL, hence no unique min⊑ — so pickMostSpecificEntry fell to first-match and
-- DECLARATION ORDER decided the program's meaning.  For a single-param impl
-- `itys == [candTy]`, so this reduces to the old two lines algebraically (see
-- tySubsumesV).
entryCovers : KeyEntry -> KeyEntry -> Bool
entryCovers (KeyEntry _ _ _ _ _ candTys _) (KeyEntry _ _ _ _ _ otherTys _) = tyHeadEqV candTys otherTys
  || tyStrictlyMoreSpecificV candTys otherTys

-- ── #609: specificity over head VECTORS (DICT-SEMANTICS §3) ─────────────────
-- §3 defines a predicate as a class applied to a VECTOR (`π ::= C τ̄`) and
-- specificity as `I_A ⊑ I_B iff ∃σ. σ(C B̄) = C Ā` — ONE substitution σ matching
-- the WHOLE vector at once, NOT per-argument subsumption.  Per-argument would be a
-- plausible-looking wrong answer: it cannot see that a var bound in two argument
-- positions must receive the SAME type, so `Ix a a` would "subsume" `Ix Int Bool`.
-- Matching one worklist of pairs through matchOneSided is exactly σ: the shared
-- core binds each general-side TyVar once and re-checks (tyStructEq) every later
-- occurrence, so a non-linear pattern is only subsumed by a consistent instance.
-- Direction, as in the superseded scalar `tySubsumes`: is [specifics] an instance of
-- [generals]?  Only the GENERAL side's TyVars act as wildcards; the SPECIFIC side's
-- are rigid and match only the same name.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's `subsumes`.
--
-- ⚠️ n=1 reduction is ALGEBRAIC, not a fixture claim: a KeyEntry's `itys` for a
-- single-param impl is exactly `[hty]` (keyEntryOf destructures `tys` as `headTy::_`
-- and stores the SAME `tys` as itys), and `zipL [g] [s] == [(g, s)]`, so
--     tySubsumesV [g] [s] == matchOneSided tyStep eqStr tyStructEq [(g, s)] []
-- which is the REMOVED `tySubsumes g s` body term for term.  Every single-param
-- decision is therefore unchanged by construction, not by fixture census.  A length
-- mismatch is not a match (no σ can equate vectors of different arity); it is
-- unreachable between two impls of ONE interface, which share that interface's arity.
tySubsumesV : List Ty -> List Ty -> Bool
tySubsumesV generals specifics
  | listLen generals != listLen specifics = False
  | otherwise =
    isSome (matchOneSided tyStep eqStr tyStructEq (zipL generals specifics) [])

-- the two head VECTORS are the SAME pattern up to var renaming (mutual subsumption)
-- — no ordering needed, the candidate trivially covers it.  Supersedes the scalar
-- `tyHeadEq`, which compared arg-0 patterns only.
tyHeadEqV : List Ty -> List Ty -> Bool
tyHeadEqV a b = tySubsumesV a b && tySubsumesV b a

-- [a] ⊑ [b] strictly: [b]'s vector subsumes [a]'s but not vice versa.  Supersedes
-- the scalar `tyStrictlyMoreSpecific`.
tyStrictlyMoreSpecificV : List Ty -> List Ty -> Bool
tyStrictlyMoreSpecificV a b = tySubsumesV b a && not (tySubsumesV a b)

-- AST-space step: rigid structural, NO normalize (raw AST patterns).  A TyEffect/
-- TyConstrained wrapper on the general side is transparent — recurse into its body
-- with the specific side's own wrappers stripped (stripTyWrap), preserving the old
-- tySubsumeGo behaviour.  Exhaustive over Ty's 7 constructors, so no catch-all.
tyStep : Ty -> Ty -> MStep String Ty Ty
tyStep (TyVar gn) _ = MVar gn
tyStep (TyCon a _) (TyCon b _) = if a == b then MOk else MFail
tyStep (TyCon _ _) _ = MFail
tyStep (TyApp f1 a1) (TyApp f2 a2) = MKids [(f1, f2), (a1, a2)]
tyStep (TyApp _ _) _ = MFail
tyStep (TyFun a1 b1) (TyFun a2 b2) = MKids [(a1, a2), (b1, b2)]
tyStep (TyFun _ _) _ = MFail
tyStep (TyTuple ts1) (TyTuple ts2) =
  if listLen ts1 == listLen ts2 then
    MKids (zipL ts1 ts2)
  else
    MFail
tyStep (TyTuple _) _ = MFail
tyStep (TyEffect _ _ t1) s = MKids [(t1, stripTyWrap s)]
tyStep (TyConstrained _ t1) s = MKids [(t1, stripTyWrap s)]

eqStr : String -> String -> Bool
eqStr a b = a == b

stripTyWrap : Ty -> Ty
stripTyWrap (TyEffect _ _ t) = stripTyWrap t
stripTyWrap (TyConstrained _ t) = stripTyWrap t
stripTyWrap t = t

-- structural equality of two AST type patterns (rigid; TyVar names must match).
tyStructEq : Ty -> Ty -> Bool
tyStructEq (TyCon a _) (TyCon b _) = a == b
tyStructEq (TyVar a) (TyVar b) = a == b
tyStructEq (TyApp f1 a1) (TyApp f2 a2) = tyStructEq f1 f2 && tyStructEq a1 a2
tyStructEq (TyFun a1 b1) (TyFun a2 b2) = tyStructEq a1 a2 && tyStructEq b1 b2
tyStructEq (TyTuple ts1) (TyTuple ts2) = tyStructEqList ts1 ts2
tyStructEq (TyEffect _ _ t1) (TyEffect _ _ t2) = tyStructEq t1 t2
tyStructEq (TyConstrained _ t1) (TyConstrained _ t2) = tyStructEq t1 t2
tyStructEq _ _ = False

tyStructEqList : List Ty -> List Ty -> Bool
tyStructEqList [] [] = True
tyStructEqList (t1::ts1) (t2::ts2) = tyStructEq t1 t2 && tyStructEqList ts1 ts2
tyStructEqList _ _ = False

-- do two distinct impls of [name] share head tycon [tag]?  (≥2 entries that
-- define [name] at the same head tycon.)
headCollides : KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Bool
headCollides table name tag = countHead table name tag > 1

countHead : KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Int
countHead buckets name tag = countHeadGo (bucketOf tag buckets) name tag

countHeadGo : List KeyEntry -> String -> String -> Int
countHeadGo [] _ _ = 0
countHeadGo ((KeyEntry methodNames tag2 _ _ _ _ _)::rest) name tag
  | contains name methodNames && tag2 == tag = 1 + countHeadGo rest name tag
  | otherwise = countHeadGo rest name tag

-- ── #203: INTERFACE-keyed most-specific selector (§3 `inst` = min⊑(match)) ───
-- The shared min⊑ selector, keyed by INTERFACE (not method name), over the ALL-impls
-- KeyBuckets registry (every impl carries its iface + requires, unlike ImplBuckets
-- which OMITS no-requires impls).  Returns the unique most-specific matching impl's
-- KeyEntry — from which callers read the canonical key AND the impl's OWN requires
-- ([] for a no-requires specific impl).  No unique min⊑ (≤1 match, equal or
-- incomparable heads) → the FIRST match, byte-identical to the historical first-match
-- scans.  This is the ONE selector every resolution position (E/F/G/H) now funnels
-- through, discharging the §6 "uniform resolution" obligation.
selectImplEntryByIface : KeyBuckets -> String -> List Mono -> Option KeyEntry
selectImplEntryByIface buckets iface goals =
  pickMostSpecificEntry (matchingEntriesByIface buckets iface goals)

-- all impls of [iface] whose head pattern matches [receiverMono], in declaration
-- order.  Same bucket-by-head-tycon scan as matchingEntries, filtered on the impl's
-- iface instead of a method-name membership (so a specific impl that inherits a
-- method via a DEFAULT — hence not in its methodNames — is still seen).
matchingEntriesByIface : KeyBuckets -> String -> List Mono -> List KeyEntry
matchingEntriesByIface buckets iface goals = match goalHeadCon goals
  Some tag => matchingEntriesByIfaceGo (bucketOf tag buckets) iface goals
  None => []

matchingEntriesByIfaceGo : List KeyEntry -> String -> List Mono -> List KeyEntry
matchingEntriesByIfaceGo [] _ _ = []
matchingEntriesByIfaceGo ((KeyEntry ms tag hty key ifn itys reqs)::rest) iface goals
  | ifn == iface && entryHeadMatches hty itys goals = KeyEntry ms tag hty key ifn itys reqs :: matchingEntriesByIfaceGo rest iface goals
  | otherwise = matchingEntriesByIfaceGo rest iface goals

-- the canonical impl key to stamp at an iface-keyed site (EKNestedTop primary route),
-- mirroring keyForSite: UPGRADE the bare head tag to the winning impl's full canonical
-- key ONLY when two+ impls of [iface] share the head tycon (so bare-head dispatch is
-- ambiguous).  No collision (the common, non-overlapping case) → None → caller keeps
-- the bare tag, byte-identical.
keyForSiteByIface : KeyBuckets -> String -> List Mono -> Option String
keyForSiteByIface table iface goals = match selectImplEntryByIface table iface goals
  Some (KeyEntry _ tag _ key _ _ _) =>
    if headCollidesByIface table iface tag then
      Some key
    else
      None
  None => None

-- do two+ distinct impls of [iface] share head tycon [tag]?
headCollidesByIface : KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Bool
headCollidesByIface table iface tag = countHeadByIface table iface tag > 1

countHeadByIface : KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Int
countHeadByIface buckets iface tag =
  countHeadByIfaceGo (bucketOf tag buckets) iface tag

countHeadByIfaceGo : List KeyEntry -> String -> String -> Int
countHeadByIfaceGo [] _ _ = 0
countHeadByIfaceGo ((KeyEntry _ tag2 _ _ ifn _ _)::rest) iface tag
  | ifn == iface && tag2 == tag = 1 + countHeadByIfaceGo rest iface tag
  | otherwise = countHeadByIfaceGo rest iface tag

-- the head type constructor of an (unelaborated) AST type
headTyconTy : Ty -> Option String
headTyconTy (TyCon n _) = Some n
headTyconTy (TyApp a _) = headTyconTy a
headTyconTy (TyTuple ts) = Some (tupleHeadTagTc (listLen ts))
headTyconTy _ = None

-- Multi-param-interface variant: [paramMonos] is the FULL list of the interface's
-- type-parameter monos at this occurrence (recovered by ifaceParamMonos), e.g. for
-- `Set { 3,1,2 } : Set _a` the `FromEntries c e` occurrence has c↦Set _a, e↦Int.
-- The single-param result-only path lost the element grounding (`e`): matching the
-- impl head `Set a` against the result `Set _a` only binds a↦_a (a free var), so the
-- `requires Ord a` route fell to RNone → a null Ord dict → SIGSEGV.  Matching the
-- impl's FULL `implTys` (`[Set a, a]`) against ALL the param monos recovers a↦Int from
-- the second param, exactly as the OCaml oracle's impl_head_subst keys off the full
-- concrete_args (the interface's param_vars), not just the result.
-- #203 (G): the return-position element/requires routes.  Was a FIRST-MATCH scan of the
-- ImplBuckets [tag] bucket (which OMITS no-requires impls, so it could never see the
-- specific `List Int` sibling next to `List a` and always discharged `List a`'s requires).
-- Now selects the MOST-SPECIFIC matching impl over the ALL-impls KeyBuckets registry via
-- the shared method-keyed `matchedEntry` (§3 `inst` = min⊑) and discharges THAT impl's OWN
-- requires — [] for a no-requires specific impl.  Non-overlapping site (one match) → the
-- same impl the first-match scan returned ⇒ byte-identical routes.  [implTable] is still
-- threaded for the nested requires recursion (which re-buckets by each requires' own head).
-- The OUTER head match still uses headSubstWithParams (multi-param augmentation).
implDictRoutesForFull : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Mono -> List Route
-- #609: the impl is now selected on the SAME full param-mono vector the head subst
-- is built from (was: `matchedEntry … resultMono` — arg-0 only, so at n≥2 it could
-- hand headSubstWithParams the requires of an impl that does not match the goal).
implDictRoutesForFull implTable keyTable encl name tag resultMono paramMonos = match matchedEntry keyTable name paramMonos
  Some (KeyEntry _ _ headTy _ _ implTys reqs) => match headSubstWithParams headTy implTys resultMono paramMonos
    Some subst => argImplReqRoutes implTable keyTable encl subst reqs 0
    None => []
  None => []

-- Build the impl head substitution.  Always match the impl HEAD (`Set a`) against
-- the result mono (the historical single-param behaviour, byte-identical when no
-- extra param info is supplied).  THEN, when the caller has the interface's FULL
-- param monos (`[Set _a, Int]` for a multi-param interface) and they line up with
-- the impl's full type-arg patterns (`[Set a, a]`), additionally match the trailing
-- patterns against the trailing param monos and merge — preferring a concrete head
-- (Int) over a free var (_a) on a key collision.  This recovers the element grounding
-- (`a ↦ Int`) the head-only match lost.  When the param monos don't line up (a plain
-- result-only call, or a shape mismatch) the head match alone stands.
headSubstWithParams : Ty -> List Ty -> Mono -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono))
headSubstWithParams headTy implTys resultMono paramMonos = map
  (headSub => augmentWithParams headSub implTys paramMonos)
  (matchTyMono headTy resultMono)

-- When the impl's full type-arg patterns line up with the interface's full param
-- monos (a multi-param interface), fold the trailing element groundings into the
-- head subst (concrete-wins); otherwise return the head subst unchanged.
augmentWithParams : List (String, Mono) -> List Ty -> List Mono -> List (String, Mono)
augmentWithParams headSub implTys paramMonos
  | listLen implTys == listLen paramMonos && listLen paramMonos > 1 = match implHeadSubst implTys paramMonos
    Some full => mergeSubstPreferConcrete headSub full
    None => headSub
  | otherwise = headSub

-- Match the impl's full type-arg patterns [pats] (`[Set a, a]`) against the
-- interface's concrete param monos [concretes] (`[Set _a, Int]`), accumulating a
-- name→mono subst.  When the SAME impl var appears in several positions (here `a`
-- is both the head element and the standalone element param), prefer the binding
-- whose mono has a CONCRETE head tycon (Int) over a still-free var (_a) — that is
-- what recovers the element grounding the result-only match missed.  Mirrors the
-- oracle's impl_head_subst (Hashtbl.replace last-wins, with concrete winning here).
-- A length mismatch / structural failure → None (no match, try the next impl), so a
-- single-param interface ([resultMono] only) behaves exactly as the old matchTyMono.
implHeadSubst : List Ty -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono))
implHeadSubst pats concretes
  | listLen pats != listLen concretes = None
  | otherwise = implHeadSubstGo pats concretes (Some [])

implHeadSubstGo : List Ty -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono)) -> Option (List (String, Mono))
implHeadSubstGo [] [] acc = acc
implHeadSubstGo (p::ps) (c::cs) None = None
implHeadSubstGo (p::ps) (c::cs) (Some sofar) = match matchTyMono p c
  None => None
  Some s => implHeadSubstGo ps cs (Some (mergeSubstPreferConcrete sofar s))
implHeadSubstGo _ _ _ = None

-- Merge two name→mono substs; on a key collision keep whichever value has a
-- concrete head tycon (so the grounded element wins over a free result var).
mergeSubstPreferConcrete : List (String, Mono) -> List (String, Mono) -> List (String, Mono)
mergeSubstPreferConcrete acc [] = acc
mergeSubstPreferConcrete acc ((k, v)::rest) =
  mergeSubstPreferConcrete (mergeOne acc k v) rest

mergeOne : List (String, Mono) -> String -> Mono -> List (String, Mono)
mergeOne [] k v = [(k, v)]
mergeOne ((k2, v2)::rest) k v
  | k2 == k = (k2, preferConcrete v2 v)::rest
  | otherwise = (k2, v2) :: mergeOne rest k v

preferConcrete : Mono -> Mono -> Mono
preferConcrete a b = match headTyconMono a
  Some _ => a
  None => b

-- match an impl-head type PATTERN against a concrete mono, binding the impl's
-- type-variable names to monos (single-level: the pattern's args are bare vars).
-- #156 S1 / Fork B: matchTyMono is now a CONSISTENCY-ENFORCING frontend of the
-- shared matchOneSided core (specific side = Mono, so the consistency equality is
-- cohEqMono).  The old body combined element substs with `xs ++ ys` and NEVER
-- checked a var bound twice, so a non-linear impl head (`Get (Box a) a`) could
-- "match" `Get (Box Int) Bool` (binding a↦Int AND a↦Bool) even though NO
-- substitution exists — violating §3 `inst`'s "φ a most-general MATCHER" premise
-- and letting findImplEntryGo (iface=="") select the wrong impl's requires.  Now
-- a↦Int, a↦Bool fails cohEqMono ⇒ None (no false match); a CONSISTENT non-linear
-- head (`Get (Box Int) Int`, a↦Int twice) still matches (the live canary in
-- test/eval_typed_fixtures/return_only_param_dispatch.mdk).  The recovered subst
-- is consumed only by key-lookup (fromAstType / paramMonoOf), so dropping the
-- redundant duplicate binding is byte-identical for every previously-accepted
-- match.
matchTyMono : Ty -> Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono))
matchTyMono headTy resultMono =
  matchOneSided matchStep eqStr cohEqMono [(headTy, resultMono)] []

-- #609: the §3 matching judgement over a head VECTOR —
--     match(IE, C τ̄) = { I | ∃φ. φ(head(I)) = C τ̄ }
-- ONE most-general matcher φ for the whole vector, so a var appearing in several
-- argument positions must receive the same mono (matchOneSided's cohEqMono
-- consistency check, the same one matchTyMono relies on).  n=1 reduces to
-- matchTyMono term for term: `zipL [t] [m] == [(t, m)]`.
matchTyMonos : List Ty -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono))
matchTyMonos pats goals
  | listLen pats != listLen goals = None
  | otherwise = matchOneSided matchStep eqStr cohEqMono (zipL pats goals) []

-- Does an impl whose head vector is [itys] (arg-0 pattern [hty]) match the goal
-- vector [goals]?  When the caller recovered the interface's FULL param monos the
-- two vectors line up and we match ALL of them under one φ (§3).  When it could
-- not — a scalar-only caller passes the singleton [m] — the lengths disagree for a
-- multi-param impl and we fall back to matching the arg-0 pattern alone, which is
-- byte-identical to the pre-#609 scan.  For a single-param impl the lengths always
-- agree (both 1) and `matchTyMonos [hty] [m] == matchTyMono hty m`, so the
-- single-param path never takes the fallback and never changes.
entryHeadMatches : Ty -> List Ty -> List Mono -> Bool
entryHeadMatches hty itys goals
  | listLen itys == listLen goals = isSome (matchTyMonos itys goals)
  | otherwise = match goals
    g::_ => isSome (matchTyMono hty g)
    [] => False

-- AST-head-vs-Mono step: destructure the AST head pattern, normalizing the Mono
-- side at each level.  TyVar binds the WHOLE mono at that position.
--  • TyTuple: a tuple impl head (`(a, b)`) matches a same-arity tuple mono (Native
--    Gap C) — one element-var subst per position; mismatched arity ⇒ MFail.
--  • TyFun: recover typaram bindings from BOTH argument and result positions (used
--    by ifaceParamMonos); effect rows ignored (row-blind), result wrapper stripped.
--  • TyEffect/TyConstrained wrappers on the head are transparent.
matchStep : Ty -> Mono -> MStep String Ty Mono
matchStep (TyVar n) _ = MVar n
matchStep (TyCon n _) m = match normalize m
  TCon n2 => if n == n2 then MOk else MFail
  _ => MFail
matchStep (TyApp a b) m = match normalize m
  TApp ma mb => MKids [(a, ma), (b, mb)]
  _ => MFail
matchStep (TyTuple ts) m = match tupleSpine m
  Some ms => if listLen ts == listLen ms then MKids (zipL ts ms) else MFail
  _ => MFail
matchStep (TyFun a b) m = match normalize m
  TFun ma _ mb => MKids [(a, ma), (stripEff b, mb)]
  _ => MFail
matchStep (TyEffect _ _ t) m = MKids [(t, m)]
matchStep (TyConstrained _ t) m = MKids [(t, m)]
matchStep _ _ = MFail

-- #217: the return-position BLIND requires chain (implReqRoutes → reqRoute →
-- implRequiresRoutesRec[D]) has been UNIFIED onto the activeDictVar-AWARE arg chain
-- (argImplReqRoutes → argReqRoute → routeOf → argImplRequiresRoutesRec[D]).  The old
-- blind chain resolved an abstract element var to RNone (a null dict cell, the
-- historical SIGSEGV source) even when that var was an in-scope enclosing-method
-- constraint var whose dict param IS available; the aware chain routes it RDict per
-- DICT-SEMANTICS §3 `assum`.  The WS-4b depth fuse (max 32) was RETAINED — it now lives
-- on the aware recursion (argImplRequiresRoutesRecD) so the unified chain is aware +
-- fused, not aware-and-diverging on a non-shrinking impl context.

-- find the ImplEntry in [implTable] for interface [iface] whose head tycon is
-- [tag] and whose head pattern matches the concrete mono [m].  A type may carry
-- SEVERAL `requires`-bearing impls at one head tycon for different interfaces
-- whose requires differ in shape (arity/parameters); keying on the tag alone
-- picked the first such impl and produced the WRONG element-dict routes (e.g.
-- `Applicative (VT e) requires Semigroup e` stealing dispatch from
-- `Debug (VT e a) requires Debug e, Debug a`).  So we filter by [iface] too.
-- [iface] == "" means "no interface known" (the method-level path) → fall back to
-- the historical first-by-tag behaviour.  The head-pattern match against [m] is
-- also required so a same-iface but wrong-arity head (e.g. `VT e` vs `VT e a`) is
-- skipped rather than matched-then-dropped.
findImplEntry : ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> Option ImplEntry
findImplEntry buckets iface tag m =
  findImplEntryGo (bucketOf tag buckets) iface tag m

findImplEntryGo : List ImplEntry -> String -> String -> Mono -> Option ImplEntry
findImplEntryGo [] _ _ _ = None
findImplEntryGo ((ImplEntry tag2 ms hty reqs itys ifn)::rest) iface tag m
  | tag2 == tag && (iface == "" || ifn == iface) = match matchTyMono hty m
    Some _ => Some (ImplEntry tag2 ms hty reqs itys ifn)
    None => findImplEntryGo rest iface tag m
  | otherwise = findImplEntryGo rest iface tag m

-- fill each constrained-fn call site's routes: one route per constraint mono.  A
-- mono pinned to a concrete head → RKey; still a constraint variable (a nested
-- constrained call) → RDict; otherwise RNone.
resolveDictApps : List Decl -> ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> List (Ref (List Route), List Mono, List String) -> Unit
resolveDictApps _ _ _ [] = ()
resolveDictApps prog implTable keyTable ((routesRef, monos, ifaces)::rest) =
  let routes = routesOfMonosTop prog implTable keyTable monos ifaces
  let _ = setRef routesRef routes
  resolveDictApps prog implTable keyTable rest

-- Caller-supplied policy for the UNDETERMINED arm of routeOf (the constraint var is
-- pinned by NEITHER an argument nor the result).  KeepNone (the nested element-dict
-- recursion) keeps the existing RNone; CountImpls prog iface (a TOP-LEVEL
-- constrained-call site) counts impls of [iface] in [prog].  A VARIANT (not a
-- callback) so a nested element-position caller STRUCTURALLY cannot read [prog] or
-- raise T-AMBIGUOUS-INSTANCE — the type forbids it, so a blanket RNone→RKey can never
-- corrupt element-dict routing (the fear the three variants were "kept SEPARATE" for).
data Undetermined = KeepNone | CountImpls (List Decl) String

-- #156 S3b: the SiteKind-parametrized entailment core.  ONE `assum → inst → fallback`
-- ladder that both the genuine return-position method resolver (`resolveSite`, EKReturn)
-- and the nested/top constrained-call router (`routeOfD`, EKNestedTop) route through.
-- Returns `(primary route, element-dict route list)`.  Genuine sites carry their element
-- routes in the SEPARATE `implRef` (primary is `RKey key []`), so `snd` is non-empty and
-- the adapter stamps it into `implRef`; nested/top PACK their `requires` into the primary
-- RKey (byte-identical to the pre-S3b `routeOfD`) and leave `snd` empty.
--   * assum: EKNestedTop uses `activeDictVarForEncl`; EKReturn uses the enclDictVarOf-
--     preferring `activeDictVarOfEncl`.  EKReturn routes RDictFwd when the method is
--     return-position (name∈rpNames), else RDict; EKNestedTop routes RDict.
--   * inst: EKReturn upgrades the head tag to the canonical full-type key (`keyForSite`)
--     and fills element routes via `implDictRoutesForFull`; EKNestedTop keeps the bare
--     head tag + `argImplRequiresRoutesRecD`-packed requires (with the W2 depth fuse).
--   * fallback: EKReturn → RNone; EKNestedTop → the caller-supplied `Undetermined` policy.
-- #156 S3c wired arg-position + operator sites in (EKArg / EKOp below).
data EntailKind =
  -- return-position method site: keyTable (for keyForSite), the method's full
  -- (un-stripped) mono (for ifaceParamMonos), and whether the method is return-position.
  | EKReturn KeyBuckets Mono Bool
  -- nested-element / top-level constrained call: iface, undetermined policy, depth fuse.
  -- #203: now carries the ALL-impls KeyBuckets (was structurally unable to reach the
  -- min⊑ selector), so the arm can stamp the canonical key of the most-specific impl
  -- (mirroring EKReturn) instead of a bare ambiguous head tag.
  | EKNestedTop KeyBuckets String Undetermined Int
  -- #156 S3c: arg-position stamp (resolveArgStamp).  keyTable for keyForSite.  assum =
  -- activeDictVarOfEncl → RDict (never RDictFwd; arg-position); inst = same canonical
  -- RKey primary as EKReturn but element routes via argImplDictRoutesForEncl keyed on
  -- the (default-reduced) method; fallback = RNone (the load-bearing do-nothing arm).
  -- #609: also carries the method's full occurrence mono, so this kind can recover
  -- the interface's whole param vector (ifaceParamMonos) exactly as EKReturn does.
  -- Without it the arm could only ever see arg 0 — and the arg-position leg is the
  -- one an ordinary `ix 1 'x'` call takes, so this is where #609 landed.
  | EKArg KeyBuckets Mono
  -- #156 S3c: operator site (resolveOpSite).  isBinop, keyTable (for keyForSite), and the
  -- in-impl-body flag (for opDictVarOf's dict-var policy).  assum = opDictVarOf → RDict;
  -- inst = stampOpRouteVal (builtin head → RNone, else RKey with reqs PACKED in — no
  -- separate implRef); fallback = RNone.  Both do-nothing arms preserved by the adapter's
  -- RNone guard (op-site refs are always Ref RNone-seeded — parser/desugar).
  | EKOp Bool KeyBuckets Bool

entail : ImplBuckets -> String -> Mono -> String -> EntailKind -> (Route, List Route)
entail implTable name m encl kind = match entailAssum m encl name kind
  Some dname => (entailAssumRoute dname kind, [])
  None => match headTyconMono m
    Some tag => entailInst implTable name m encl tag kind
    None => (entailFallback implTable kind, [])

-- #412: `assum`, both halves.  DICT-SEMANTICS §3 pins the precedence — `assum`/`super`
-- takes precedence over `inst`; a predicate already in scope is USED, and `inst` fires only
-- when no assumption matches (GHC's rule) — which is what makes :160's "sub-evidence is
-- never pre-baked at the instance declaration" realisable.  The tyvar-keyed registry runs
-- first and unchanged; the predicate-keyed one (registerPredGiven) then answers the
-- STRUCTURED given (`requires S (List a)`) that `activeDictVarOf` structurally cannot see,
-- because it recurses to the spine head and a structured subject has a TCon there.
-- Strictly additive: every goal the tyvar path already answers routes exactly as before.
entailAssum : Mono -> String -> String -> EntailKind -> Option String
entailAssum m encl name kind = match entailAssumVar m encl name kind
  Some dname => Some dname
  None => match ifaceOfMethodName name
    Some iface => activeDictPredOf iface m encl
    None => None

-- assum: which in-scope dict-param (if any) does [m]'s head constraint var read?  The
-- genuine/arg kinds prefer the enclosing method's own slot (activeDictVarOfEncl); the
-- nested/top kind uses the plain encl-aware lookup (activeDictVarForEncl); the op kind
-- routes through opDictVarOf (isBinop/inImpl-keyed, needs the method [name]).  [name] is
-- ignored by the non-op kinds.
entailAssumVar : Mono -> String -> String -> EntailKind -> Option String
entailAssumVar m encl _ (EKReturn _ _ _) = activeDictVarOfEncl m encl
entailAssumVar m encl _ (EKNestedTop _ _ _ _) = activeDictVarForEncl m encl
entailAssumVar m encl _ (EKArg _ _) = activeDictVarOfEncl m encl
entailAssumVar m encl name (EKOp isBinop _ inImpl) =
  opDictVarOf isBinop name m inImpl encl

entailAssumRoute : String -> EntailKind -> Route
entailAssumRoute dname (EKReturn _ _ isRp) =
  if isRp then
    RDictFwd dname
  else
    RDict dname
entailAssumRoute dname (EKNestedTop _ _ _ _) = RDict dname
entailAssumRoute dname (EKArg _ _) = RDict dname
entailAssumRoute dname (EKOp _ _ _) = RDict dname

-- inst: a concrete head [tag].  EKReturn → canonical full-type key + split element routes
-- (implRef-bound); EKNestedTop → bare head tag with requires PACKED into the primary RKey;
-- EKArg → same canonical RKey primary as EKReturn but element routes keyed on the
-- (default-reduced) method via argImplDictRoutesForEncl; EKOp → stampOpRouteVal (builtin
-- head → RNone do-nothing, else RKey with reqs packed in, no separate element routes).
entailInst : ImplBuckets -> String -> Mono -> String -> String -> EntailKind -> (Route, List Route)
-- #609: the goal is the interface's FULL param-mono vector `τ̄` (§5: "the dispatch
-- type is the instantiated vector"), not the result mono alone — and it is the SAME
-- vector `implDictRoutesForFull` already recovered for element grounding, so the
-- selector and the requires router now agree on one goal by construction.  A method
-- with no registered interface (ifaceParamMonos → None) still selects on [m],
-- exactly as before.
entailInst implTable name m encl tag (EKReturn keyTable fullMono _) =
  let paramMonos = fromOption [m] (ifaceParamMonos name fullMono)
  let routeKey = fromOption tag (keyForSite keyTable name paramMonos)
  (
    RKey routeKey [],
    implDictRoutesForFull implTable keyTable encl name tag m paramMonos,
  )
-- #203 (E, the root fix): mirror EKReturn.  Was `RKey tag …` — a BARE ambiguous head
-- tag whose constructor carried no KeyBuckets, so it could not reach the min⊑ selector;
-- every nested-`requires` and super-projection goal funnels through here.  Now the
-- primary route is the canonical key of the MOST-SPECIFIC impl (iface-keyed, head-
-- collision-gated exactly like keyForSite ⇒ a non-overlapping site keeps its bare tag,
-- byte-identical), and the packed requires come from that same winner (F).
entailInst implTable _ m encl tag (EKNestedTop keyTable iface _ depth) =
  -- #609: a nested/top `requires` goal arrives as a single subject mono, so the
  -- goal vector is the singleton — unchanged behaviour.  Threading the full
  -- predicate vector down this leg needs the n-ary obligation representation and
  -- is #607's scope, not this fix's.
  let routeKey = fromOption tag (keyForSiteByIface keyTable iface [m])
  (
    RKey routeKey (argImplRequiresRoutesRecD implTable keyTable iface encl tag m depth),
    [],
  )
-- #609: select on the interface's FULL param vector (§5 — the dispatch type is the
-- instantiated vector `T̄`), not on the discriminating ARGUMENT's mono alone.
-- [goals] is bound ONCE and fed to BOTH arms: the method route (`routeKey`) and the
-- selected impl's own `requires`-context routes.  That is not a tidiness point — §6
-- C2 requires the impl dispatched to and the impl whose context is discharged to be
-- the SAME impl, and the first cut of this fix computed the vector here and then
-- handed the scalar `m` to argImplDictRoutesForEncl, so the two arms could select
-- differently at n≥2.  Sharing one binding makes them agree by construction.
entailInst implTable name m encl tag (EKArg keyTable fullMono) =
  let goals = fromOption [m] (ifaceParamMonos name fullMono)
  let routeKey = fromOption tag (keyForSite keyTable name goals)
  let dictName = if implDefinesMethodAt implTable name tag then
    name
  else
    innerDefaultMethod name
  (
    RKey routeKey [],
    argImplDictRoutesForEncl implTable keyTable encl dictName tag m goals,
  )
entailInst implTable name m _ tag (EKOp isBinop keyTable _) =
  (stampOpRouteVal isBinop implTable keyTable name m tag, [])

entailFallback : ImplBuckets -> EntailKind -> Route
entailFallback _ (EKReturn _ _ _) = RNone
entailFallback implTable (EKNestedTop keyTable _ policy _) =
  undeterminedRoute implTable keyTable policy
entailFallback _ (EKArg _ _) = RNone
entailFallback _ (EKOp _ _ _) = RNone

-- ONE constrained-call-arg route resolver — collapses routeOfMono (encl="",
-- KeepNone) / routeOfMonoEncl (KeepNone) / routeOfMonoTop (encl="", CountImpls).
-- [encl] == "" ⇒ global activeDictVarOf (activeDictVarForEncl short-circuits); else
-- the enclosing method's own constraint-var dict.  [policy] decides the undetermined
-- arm (see Undetermined).
routeOf : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Undetermined -> Mono -> Route
routeOf implTable keyTable iface encl policy m =
  routeOfD implTable keyTable iface encl policy m 0

-- #217: depth-carrying core of routeOf.  [depth] threads the WS-4b fuse (max 32)
-- through the aware recursion (routeOfD → argImplRequiresRoutesRecD → argImplReqRoutes
-- → argReqRoute → routeOfD), the guard retained from the removed blind chain so a
-- non-shrinking impl context (`impl C (T a) requires C (T (T a))`) terminates instead of
-- diverging at route-resolution time.  Depth is unchanged here; it increments once per
-- impl level in argImplRequiresRoutesRecD.
routeOfD : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Undetermined -> Mono -> Int -> Route
-- #156 S3b: a thin adapter over `entail` (EKNestedTop kind).  The nested/top ladder
-- packs its `requires` into the primary RKey, so `snd` is empty and only `fst` is read —
-- byte-identical to the pre-S3b arm-by-arm body.  The requires recursion still lands back
-- here via argImplRequiresRoutesRecD → argImplReqRoutes → argReqRoute → routeOfD, with
-- [depth] threaded through the EKNestedTop payload.
routeOfD implTable keyTable iface encl policy m depth =
  fst (entail implTable "" m encl (EKNestedTop keyTable iface policy depth))

undeterminedRoute : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> Undetermined -> Route
undeterminedRoute _ _ KeepNone = RNone
undeterminedRoute implTable keyTable (CountImpls prog iface) =
  routeUndeterminedTop prog implTable keyTable iface

-- TOP-LEVEL constraint routes (a constrained-fn CALL site, e.g. `roundtrip 10`).
-- Like routesOfMonos but passes the CountImpls policy to routeOf, so the UNDETERMINED
-- case resolves via routeUndeterminedTop instead of the nested-recursion RNone (which
-- yields an empty dict → runtime `intToString: not an Int` / a null build dict): look
-- up how many impls of the slot's interface exist:
--   • exactly ONE  → stamp a concrete RKey to that impl's head tag (deterministic
--                    dispatch on both run and build);
--   • two or more  → genuinely ambiguous → pushTypeError AmbiguousImpl (DELIBERATELY
--                    diverges from the frozen oracle's silent first-wins);
--   • zero         → keep RNone (existing no-impl behaviour/error).
routesOfMonosTop : List Decl -> ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> List Mono -> List String -> List Route
routesOfMonosTop _ _ _ [] _ = []
routesOfMonosTop prog implTable keyTable (m::rest) [] =
  routeOf implTable keyTable "" "" (CountImpls prog "") m ::
    routesOfMonosTop prog implTable keyTable rest []
routesOfMonosTop prog implTable keyTable (m::rest) (iface::ifacesRest) =
  routeOf implTable keyTable iface "" (CountImpls prog iface) m ::
    routesOfMonosTop prog implTable keyTable rest ifacesRest

-- the undetermined-constraint resolution: count impls of [iface] in [prog].
routeUndeterminedTop : List Decl -> ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> Route
routeUndeterminedTop prog implTable keyTable iface
  | iface == "" = RNone
  | otherwise = match implHeadTagsForIface prog iface
    [] => RNone
    [tag] => RKey tag (argImplRequiresRoutesRec implTable keyTable iface "" tag (TCon tag))
    _ => reportAmbiguousImpl iface

reportAmbiguousImpl : String -> Route
reportAmbiguousImpl iface =
  let _ = pushTypeError "T-AMBIGUOUS-INSTANCE" (ambiguousImplMsg iface)
  RNone
