-- every impl head tycon for [iface] declared in [prog] (a single-arg interface has
-- one head ty per impl; we read its head tycon).  Dedups so a prelude impl imported
-- twice via multi-module loading doesn't read as ambiguous.
implHeadTagsForIface : List Decl -> String -> List String
implHeadTagsForIface prog iface =
  dedupStr (flatMap (implHeadTagForIface iface) prog)

implHeadTagForIface : String -> Decl -> List String
implHeadTagForIface want (DAttrib _ d) = implHeadTagForIface want d
implHeadTagForIface want (DImpl { iface, tys, ... })
  | iface == want = match tys
    headTy::_ => match headTyconTy headTy
      Some tag => [tag]
      None => []
    [] => []
  | otherwise = []
implHeadTagForIface _ _ = []

dedupStr : List String -> List String
dedupStr xs = dedupStrGo [] xs

dedupStrGo : List String -> List String -> List String
dedupStrGo _ [] = []
dedupStrGo seen (x::rest)
  | contains x seen = dedupStrGo seen rest
  | otherwise = x :: dedupStrGo (x::seen) rest

ambiguousImplMsg : String -> String
ambiguousImplMsg iface = "Ambiguous instance for `"
  ++ iface
  ++ "`. Cannot determine which impl; add a type annotation"

routesOfMonos : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> List Mono -> List Route
routesOfMonos _ _ [] = []
routesOfMonos implTable keyTable (m::rest) =
  routeOf implTable keyTable "" "" KeepNone m ::
    routesOfMonos implTable keyTable rest

-- Native #54/#21 residual: the activeDictVar-AWARE requires recursion.  Recursing
-- via routeOf (not the removed blind reqRoute) keeps every nesting level
-- activeDictVar-aware: e.g. a `Debug (Map k v)` impl body's `debug (toList m)` arg-
-- stamps `List (k, v)` → RKey "List" recursing into the tuple `(k, v)` whose element
-- vars `k`/`v` ARE the enclosing impl's `requires Debug k, Debug v` dict params
-- (registered in activeDictVars), so they route RDict $dict_debug_<slot>, NOT RNone.
-- The emitted element-dict cell carries the live impl dicts instead of a null (0)
-- field — the null field was the SIGSEGV (the inner per-element debug/eq/compare
-- dispatched through a 0 dict).
-- #217: this is now the SOLE requires recursion — the return position
-- (implDictRoutesIn) routes through here too, no longer through a blind twin.  [encl]
-- threads the enclosing method so nested element dicts forward its own
-- `$dict_<encl>_<slot>` params (encl == "" recovers the global routing).
argImplRequiresRoutesRec : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Route
argImplRequiresRoutesRec implTable keyTable iface encl tag m =
  argImplRequiresRoutesRecD implTable keyTable iface encl tag m 0

-- #217/WS-4b: depth-fused core.  [depth] guards a non-shrinking impl context (`impl C
-- (T a) requires C (T (T a))`) — the fuse RETAINED from the removed blind chain, now on
-- the aware recursion so unifying the two chains did not import a typecheck hang.  Depth
-- 32 is ample for valid programs (real nesting rarely exceeds 3–4 levels); on limit-hit
-- we return [] (RNone element → null cell), safe because such an impl is ill-formed and
-- never produces a valid concrete instantiation in a well-typed program.
argImplRequiresRoutesRecD : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> String -> Mono -> Int -> List Route
argImplRequiresRoutesRecD implTable keyTable iface encl tag m depth =
  if depth >= 32 then []
  else match selectReqImpl keyTable implTable iface tag m
    Some (headTy, reqs) => match matchTyMono headTy m
      Some subst =>
        argImplReqRoutes implTable keyTable encl subst reqs (depth + 1)
      None => []
    None => []

-- #203 (F): pick the impl whose `requires` this nested obligation must discharge.
--   • iface known → the MOST-SPECIFIC impl of that iface over the ALL-impls KeyBuckets
--     registry (§3 `inst` = min⊑) and ITS OWN requires — [] for a no-requires specific
--     impl (the `List Int` sibling), never the general `List a`'s.  The old
--     `findImplEntry` searched ImplBuckets (no-requires impls OMITTED) and first-matched,
--     so it was structurally blind to `List Int` and always returned `List a`'s requires.
--   • iface unknown ("" — the method-dict / untyped-fallback path, never the typed
--     dispatch of #203) → the historical first-by-tag `findImplEntry`, byte-identical.
selectReqImpl : KeyBuckets -> ImplBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> Option (Ty, List Require)
selectReqImpl keyTable implTable iface tag m
  | iface == "" = map ((ImplEntry _ _ headTy reqs _ _) => (headTy, reqs)) (findImplEntry implTable iface tag m)
  | otherwise = map ((KeyEntry _ _ headTy _ _ _ reqs) => (headTy, reqs)) (selectImplEntryByIface keyTable iface [m])

-- ── D3b-1: arg-position element-dict routing ────────────────────────────────
-- The argument-position analogue of implDictRoutesFor.  Same impl-table match +
-- head-pattern unification against the discriminating-argument mono, but each
-- requires route is computed by routeOf (activeDictVar-aware): a recursive
-- element of the SAME parametric type (the impl's own element tyvar `a`, still a
-- registered constraint var) forwards the in-scope `$dict_<method>_<slot>` param
-- (RDict); a concrete element (`MyList Int`) materializes a static RKey.  #217: the
-- return position (implDictRoutesIn) now shares this same aware requires recursion — the
-- two chains were unified; this entry runs for emit-path arg stamps specifically.
-- #609: the operator entry.  The goal is the singleton [mono] — and unlike the
-- deferred EKNestedTop case this is not a scope cut, it is EXACT: every interface
-- owning an operator method is single-param (`Eq a`, `Ord a`, `Num a`,
-- `Semigroup a`, `Monoid a` in stdlib/core.mdk), so `itys` has length 1, the
-- lengths agree, and the singleton IS the full vector — matchTyMonos [hty] [m]
-- reduces to matchTyMono hty m.  Should a multi-param interface ever own an
-- operator, entryHeadMatches degrades to the arg-0 fallback rather than
-- mis-selecting.
argImplDictRoutesFor : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Route
argImplDictRoutesFor implTable keyTable name tag mono =
  argImplDictRoutesForEncl implTable keyTable "" name tag mono [mono]

-- encl-aware variant: thread the ENCLOSING method [encl] so the recursively-routed
-- element dicts forward [encl]'s own `$dict_<encl>_<slot>` params (not whichever
-- method registered the shared impl tyvar id LAST — see activeDictVarForEncl).  encl
-- == "" recovers the original global routing (binop path, single-method impls).
-- #203 (H): the arg/op-position element routes.  Was a FIRST-MATCH scan of the
-- ImplBuckets [tag] bucket (blind to no-requires siblings, see implDictRoutesForFull);
-- now selects the MOST-SPECIFIC matching impl over the ALL-impls KeyBuckets registry via
-- the shared method-keyed `matchedEntry` and discharges THAT impl's OWN requires.  A
-- non-overlapping site (one match) selects the same impl the first-match scan did ⇒
-- byte-identical routes.
-- #609 (arg position): selects on the goal VECTOR [goals] and binds the subst from
-- the winner's FULL head types, exactly as its return-position twin
-- implDictRoutesForFull does.  This is the SAME defect as #609 proper, one leg over:
-- selecting on `[mono]` length-mismatches a multi-param impl's `itys`, so
-- entryHeadMatches fell back to arg-0 and two siblings differing only past arg 0 both
-- matched.  They are genuinely disjoint, so coherence never rejected them and
-- entryCovers finds them incomparable ⇒ pickMostSpecificEntry took the FIRST-DECLARED,
-- and the impl whose `requires`-context was routed could differ from the impl
-- `routeKey` dispatched to — evidence for instance A attached to methods of instance
-- B (§2 "evidence is a tree"; §6 C2 coherence), order-dependently (§6 C3).
--
-- ⚠️ Only observable when the siblings' requires differ in COUNT.  The dict cell is a
-- TYPE tag (`@mdk_dc_0 = [1 x i64] [193460240]` — the `Int` tag, shared with
-- println's dict), and the routes are built from the subst, so two siblings whose
-- requires range over the SAME tyvar produce the SAME dict whichever is picked; the
-- interface is re-chosen at the use site by a method-specific dispatcher
-- (`@mdk_disp_bar_0_1`).  Differing COUNTS thread the wrong NUMBER of dicts, shifting
-- every later argument.  Regression:
-- test/eval_typed_fixtures/multiparam_impl_requires_arity{,_swapped}.mdk.
argImplDictRoutesForEncl : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> String -> Mono -> List Mono -> List Route
argImplDictRoutesForEncl implTable keyTable encl name _tag mono goals = match matchedEntry keyTable name goals
  Some (KeyEntry _ _ headTy _ _ implTys reqs) => match headSubstWithParams headTy implTys mono goals
    Some subst => argImplReqRoutes implTable keyTable encl subst reqs 0
    None => []
  None => []

-- #217: [depth] carries the WS-4b fuse through the aware recursion (see
-- argImplRequiresRoutesRecD).  Arg-position entry points seed 0; the return position
-- (implDictRoutesForFull) also seeds 0.
argImplReqRoutes : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> List Require -> Int -> List Route
argImplReqRoutes _ _ _ _ [] _ = []
argImplReqRoutes implTable keyTable encl subst ((Require rIface rargs)::rest) depth = argReqRoute implTable keyTable rIface encl subst rargs depth :: argImplReqRoutes implTable keyTable encl subst rest depth

argReqRoute : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> String -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> List Ty -> Int -> Route
argReqRoute _ _ _ _ _ [] _ = RNone
argReqRoute implTable keyTable iface encl subst (arg::_) depth =
  routeOfD implTable keyTable iface encl KeepNone (fromAstType subst arg) depth

-- Phase 69.x-e: fill each method occurrence's method-dicts ref — one route per
-- method-level constraint, from the instantiated constraint mono.  A concrete head
-- → RKey (eval supplies a literal VDict for the body's `empty`/… to read); a
-- constraint variable still (the caller is itself constrained) → RDict forwarding
-- the caller's dict.  Mirrors resolveDictApps; runs after inference.
resolveMethodDicts : ImplBuckets -> KeyBuckets -> List (Ref (List Route), List Mono) -> Unit
resolveMethodDicts _ _ [] = ()
resolveMethodDicts implTable keyTable ((methodRef, monos)::rest) =
  let _ = setRef methodRef (routesOfMonos implTable keyTable monos)
  resolveMethodDicts implTable keyTable rest

-- ── recursive dict-app realization (self/mutual recursion) ──────────────────
-- A deferred recursive call records one route per the CALLEE's constraint slot
-- (matching the dict params dict_pass prepends to its def), each forwarding the
-- ENCLOSING fn's own dict param.  Run after inference, when funConstraintsRef is
-- fully populated.
realizeRecDictApps : List RecDictApp -> Unit
realizeRecDictApps [] = ()
realizeRecDictApps ((RecDictApp routesRef callee encl mono)::rest) =
  let ids = fromOption [] (lookupAssocSL2 callee perRun.value.funConstraintsRef.value)
  let routes = recRoutes encl mono ids
  let _ = setRef routesRef routes
  realizeRecDictApps rest

recRoutes : String -> Mono -> List Int -> List Route
recRoutes _ _ [] = []
recRoutes encl mono (id::rest) =
  recRoute encl mono id :: recRoutes encl mono rest

-- For one of the callee's constraint ids, recover the live occurrence type from
-- [mono] (the var the body unified it to).  Concrete head → RKey; still a
-- constraint variable that is one of [encl]'s own → forward [encl]'s dict param
-- (RDict, the slot it sits at); otherwise fall back to the global routing.
recRoute : String -> Mono -> Int -> Route
recRoute encl mono id = match findTvarInMono mono id
  None => RNone
  Some m => resolveRecMono encl m

resolveRecMono : String -> Mono -> Route
resolveRecMono encl m = match headTyconMono m
  Some tag => RKey tag []
  None => match enclSlot encl m
    Some slot => RDict (dictParamName encl slot)
    None => routeOf omEmpty omEmpty "" "" KeepNone m

-- the dict-param slot at which [m]'s tyvar sits among the enclosing fn's
-- constraint ids (its position in funConstraintsRef[encl] = the kept slot)
enclSlot : String -> Mono -> Option Int
enclSlot encl m = match normalize m
  TVar cell => indexOfId (tyvarId cell) (fromOption [] (lookupAssocSL2 encl perRun.value.funConstraintsRef.value))
  _ => None

indexOfId : Int -> List Int -> Option Int
indexOfId target ids = indexOfIdGo 0 target ids

indexOfIdGo : Int -> Int -> List Int -> Option Int
indexOfIdGo _ _ [] = None
indexOfIdGo i target (x::xs)
  | x == target = Some i
  | otherwise = indexOfIdGo (i + 1) target xs

-- the tyvar in [t] whose id is [id], if any (mirrors the reference's
-- find_tvar_in_mono): recovers the enclosing constraint var the body unified into
-- the recursive call's type
findTvarInMono : Mono -> Int -> Option Mono
findTvarInMono t id = findTvarN (normalize t) id

findTvarN : Mono -> Int -> Option Mono
findTvarN (TVar cell) id = if tyvarId cell == id then Some (TVar cell) else None
findTvarN (TCon _) _ = None
findTvarN (TApp a b) id = firstTvar a b id
findTvarN (TFun a _ b) id = firstTvar a b id
-- An effect row is reachable here as a KRow type-application argument
-- (`TApp con (TEff row)`); a value-level tyvar id is never inside an effect row
-- (its tail is an effvar, a separate namespace), so the answer is None.
findTvarN (TEff _) _ = None

firstTvar : Mono -> Mono -> Int -> Option Mono
firstTvar a b id = match findTvarInMono a id
  Some m => Some m
  None => findTvarInMono b id

-- ── dict_pass: prepend dict parameters to constrained function definitions ──
-- For each top-level def of a name in [dictNames], prepend one `$dict_<fn>_<slot>`
-- parameter per constraint (arity from funConstraintsRef, populated during
-- inference), matching the dicts EDictAt applies at call sites.  Multi-clause
-- functions are several DFunDef decls; each clause gets the same leading params.
dictPass : List String -> List Decl -> List Decl
dictPass names prog =
  map
    (dictPassDecl names (returnPosMethodNames prog))
    (mapProg rewriteBinopExpr prog)

-- Phase 151 / Gap G: rewrite each *stamped* comparison/equality EBinOp into its
-- Eq/Ord method application via EMethodAt (the same dispatch node `eq`/`lt` calls
-- become), so eval routes to the user/derived impl.  Only nodes resolveBinopSites
-- stamped (ground non-primitive operand) carry a non-RNone route; primitives /
-- arithmetic stay the literal EBinOp the eval builtin handles (no recursion).
-- Native Gap C: a parametric impl (tuple `Eq (a,b)`) has element `requires` dicts
-- the body reads; resolveBinopSite stamped them into the RKey route's reqs, which
-- binopMethodApp now forwards as the EMethodAt impl-dict routes (the 3rd ref).  A
-- non-parametric concrete impl stamps empty reqs → the plain direct call.
--   <→lt  >→gt  <=→lte  >=→gte  ==→eq  !=→not (eq a b)
rewriteBinopExpr : Expr -> Expr
rewriteBinopExpr (EBinOp op l r routeRef) = match routeRef.value
  RNone => EBinOp op l r routeRef
  -- SHARED-FLOAT-RESIDUAL §3(C): an RScalar route is NOT a typeclass dispatch — it
  -- is the scalar-type tag typecheck stamped on a monomorphic arithmetic binop
  -- (resolveArithSite, "Float").  The EBinOp's own route ref does NOT survive the
  -- dict-pass→lower boundary (only NODE values do — verified: an op-string change in
  -- this arm reaches lower but a setRef on routeRef does not), so we carry the scalar
  -- type in a NODE by wrapping the binop in `EAnnot … (TyCon tag)`.  Eval ignores the
  -- annotation (`eval (EAnnot e _) = eval e`) and evaluates the binop dynamically
  -- (Float-correct); lowering reads the annotation to stamp CBinPrim's scalar tag so
  -- the native emitter picks the Float primitive (bypassing the type-lost
  -- staticIsFloat).  This is the dispatch route READ-OUT the design names, relocated
  -- from the (lost) ref cell to a (surviving) node.
  RScalar tag => EAnnot (EBinOp op l r routeRef) (TyCon tag None)
  _ => binopMethodApp op l r routeRef
-- PLAN.md #11: re-tag a source integer literal to its inferred runtime rep, a 3-way
-- decision mirroring lib/dict_pass.ml's e7031e6 ENumLit arm.  setNumlitFloats already
-- decided: float cell `Some f` ⇒ concrete Float ⇒ `ELit (LFloat f)`; otherwise the
-- route cell [dref] distinguishes concrete-Int (RNone, cleared) from a surviving
-- polymorphic `Num a` (a non-RNone RDictFwd/RKey route, the soundness case) — the
-- latter dispatches through the runtime Num dict via `fromInt n` (the SAME dict the
-- enclosing `Num a => …` function threads), closing the hole where `inc x = x + 1`
-- instantiated at Float crashed `VFloat + VInt`.  The constructed EMethodAt carries
-- [dref]'s route on its tag ref; impl-dicts/method-dicts refs are empty (`Num` has no
-- element `requires`).  Done in the same mapProg walk that rewrites binops, so
-- eval/lower never see ENumLit and eval_arith / the emitter get matching tags.
rewriteBinopExpr (ENumLit n fref dref _) = match fref.value
  Some f => ELit (LFloat f)
  None => match dref.value
    RNone => ELit (LInt n)
    route =>
      EApp (EMethodAt "fromInt" (Ref route) (Ref []) (Ref [])) (ELit (LInt n))
-- Roadmap #18c: rewrite each *stamped* EUnOp (`-` → `negate`) into its Num
-- method application via EMethodAt, exactly like the EBinOp arm above.  Only
-- nodes resolveUnopSites stamped (ground non-primitive operand) carry a
-- non-RNone route; Int/Float stay the literal EUnOp the eval builtin handles
-- structurally (no recursion — Int/Float's own `negate` bodies use the BINOP
-- `0 - a`, never unary minus).  `!` never routes (stays RNone unconditionally).
rewriteBinopExpr (EUnOp op e routeRef) = match routeRef.value
  RNone => EUnOp op e routeRef
  _ => unopMethodApp op e routeRef
rewriteBinopExpr e = e

-- split the stamped RKey route into a bare-tag route (reqs dropped) + the element
-- dict routes, so the dicts ride on the EMethodAt impl-dict ref (where emitMethod /
-- eval read leading dict words) rather than the route reqs (which the RKey call
-- path ignores).  A non-RKey / empty-reqs route forwards no dicts.
binopRouteSplit : Route -> (Route, List Route)
binopRouteSplit (RKey tag reqs) = (RKey tag [], reqs)
binopRouteSplit r = (r, [])

binopMethodApp : String -> Expr -> Expr -> Ref Route -> Expr
binopMethodApp op l r routeRef =
  let method = fromOption "eq" (binopMethod op)
  let split = binopRouteSplit routeRef.value
  let call = EApp (EApp (EMethodAt method (Ref (fst split)) (Ref (snd split)) (Ref [])) l) r
  if op == "!=" then EApp (EVar "not") call else call

-- The EUnOp analogue of binopMethodApp: one operand, no `!=`-style postfix
-- (unopMethod only ever routes `-`).  Reuses binopRouteSplit (a generic
-- Route-ripping helper — nothing about it is binop-specific).
unopMethodApp : String -> Expr -> Ref Route -> Expr
unopMethodApp op e routeRef =
  let method = fromOption "negate" (unopMethod op)
  let split = binopRouteSplit routeRef.value
  EApp (EMethodAt method (Ref (fst split)) (Ref (snd split)) (Ref [])) e

dictPassDecl : List String -> List String -> Decl -> Decl
dictPassDecl names _ (DFunDef pub n pats body)
  | contains n names = DFunDef pub n (dictParams n (dictArityOf n) ++ pats) body
  | otherwise = DFunDef pub n pats body
-- Top-level `let rec … with …` (DLetGroup): each binding is dict-passed exactly
-- like a DFunDef — a constrained member gets `dictParams n (dictArityOf n)`
-- prepended to every clause's params, so the dict-passed call sites (which DO get
-- rewritten) line up with the definition.  Mirror of lib/dict_pass.ml's DLetGroup
-- run_decl arm.  (Without this, a `Num a => a -> a` group member's call site
-- supplies a leading dict the body never binds → the dict lands in the first user
-- param → arithmetic over a dict value panics `unknown op`.)
dictPassDecl names _ (DLetGroup pub binds) =
  DLetGroup pub (map (dictPassLetBind names) binds)
-- Phase 83/84 single-level + D3b-1: a parametric impl with `requires` gets one
-- leading `$dict_<method>_<slot>` param on each method (return- OR argument-
-- position) whose body actually reads an impl dict (so a terminal body like
-- `eq Nil Nil = True` would take none — but see implDictPassMethods: the param is
-- added UNIFORMLY across all clauses of a method when ANY clause uses the dict, so
-- a multi-clause method keeps one arity).  The routes resolveSite/resolveArgStamp
-- filled on the call-site EMethodAt's impl-dicts ref line up with these params.
dictPassDecl _ _ (d@(DImpl { reqs, methods, ... })) =
  DImpl { d | methods = implDictPassMethods (listLen reqs) methods }
-- Phase 69.x-e: a method with method-level constraints (foldMap's `Monoid m`) gets
-- one leading `$dict_<method>_<slot>` param per constraint on its DEFAULT body, so a
-- caller can supply the dictionary the body's `empty`/… reads (routed RDict by
-- inferDefaultMethod).  Mirror of lib/dict_pass.ml's DInterface arm.
dictPassDecl _ _ (d@(DInterface { methods, ... })) =
  DInterface { d | methods = map ifaceDictPassMethod methods }
dictPassDecl names ret (DAttrib attrs d) =
  DAttrib attrs (dictPassDecl names ret d)
dictPassDecl _ _ d = d

-- DLetGroup binding dict-pass: prepend the member's dict params to every clause.
-- `dictArityOf n` is 0 for an unconstrained member (no change), matching the
-- oracle's `if k = 0 then (n, clauses)` guard.  Gated on `names` (the constrained
-- set) so an unconstrained member with a coincidental arity entry is untouched.
dictPassLetBind : List String -> LetBind -> LetBind
dictPassLetBind names (LetBind n clauses)
  | contains n names =
    LetBind n (map (prependDictClause n (dictArityOf n)) clauses)
  | otherwise = LetBind n clauses

prependDictClause : String -> Int -> FunClause -> FunClause
prependDictClause n k (FunClause pats body) =
  FunClause (dictParams n k ++ pats) body

ifaceDictPassMethod : IfaceMethod -> IfaceMethod
ifaceDictPassMethod (IfaceMethod n ty (Some (MethodDefault pats body))) =
  ifaceDictPassDefault n ty pats body (methodDictArityOf n)
ifaceDictPassMethod m = m

ifaceDictPassDefault : String -> Ty -> List Pat -> Expr -> Int -> IfaceMethod
ifaceDictPassDefault n ty pats body k
  | k > 0 =
    IfaceMethod n ty (Some (MethodDefault (dictParams n k ++ pats) body))
  | otherwise = IfaceMethod n ty (Some (MethodDefault pats body))

methodDictArityOf : String -> Int
methodDictArityOf n =
  listLen (fromOption
    []
    (lookupAssocSL2 n perRun.value.methodConstraintsRef.value))

-- D3b-1: add the leading `$dict_<method>_<slot>` params UNIFORMLY across all
-- clauses of a method.  An impl method is many ImplMethod entries (one per
-- clause), so a per-clause `usesImplDict` test would give clauses different
-- arities (`eq Nil Nil = True` uses no dict; `eq (Cons …) (Cons …) = … eq x y …`
-- does) and break coalescing.  Compute the set of method NAMES any of whose
-- clauses reads the dict, then prepend the params to every clause of those names.
-- No rpNames gate: usesImplDict already requires an in-body RDict route to one of
-- these params, which on the non-emit path no arg-position site ever has (its
-- occurrences stay bare EVar), so return-position goldens are unaffected.
-- TYPECHECK-AUDIT S2: each method gets, in oracle slot order, its METHOD-LEVEL
-- dict params (`dictParams n k`, k = methodDictArityOf n — e.g. `build`'s `Num e`)
-- FOLLOWED BY the impl-`requires` element-dict params (at slots k..k+nReq-1, only
-- when the body reads one), then the original pats.  Direct mirror of
-- lib/dict_pass.ml:99-119 (`dict_pats n k @ impl_pats @ pats`) and the
-- `method_off + slot` impl-dict naming in lib/typecheck.ml:3682-3690.  Eval applies
-- method dicts unconditionally first (eval.mdk), so dropping the leading method-level
-- params (the old `match reqs []=>d` early-out) mis-bound the dict into the first
-- value parameter.
implDictPassMethods : Int -> List ImplMethod -> List ImplMethod
implDictPassMethods nReq methods =
  let users = dedup (flatMap (clauseImplDictUser nReq) methods)
  map (prependImplDictIfUser nReq users) methods

clauseImplDictUser : Int -> ImplMethod -> List String
clauseImplDictUser nReq (ImplMethod n _ body)
  | usesImplDict n nReq body = [n]
  | otherwise = []

-- params = method-level dicts (slots 0..k-1) ++ impl-`requires` dicts (slots
-- k..k+nReq-1, only when this method's body consumes one) ++ original pats.  Skip
-- entirely only when there are neither (k = 0 and no impl-dict use), matching the
-- oracle's `if k = 0 && not uses_impl_dict then (n, pats, body)`.
prependImplDictIfUser : Int -> List String -> ImplMethod -> ImplMethod
prependImplDictIfUser nReq users (ImplMethod n pats body) =
  let k = methodDictArityOf n
  let implPats = if contains n users then dictParamsFrom n k nReq else []
  if k == 0 && isEmptyL implPats then
    ImplMethod n pats body
  else
    ImplMethod n (dictParams n k ++ implPats ++ pats) body

-- does [body] reference an in-body return-position method already routed RDict to
-- one of THIS method's would-be impl-dict params? (the gate that distinguishes a
-- body that consumes the element dict from a terminal one that doesn't).  Impl-dict
-- slots are offset by the method's own method-level arity (S2), so probe at k..k+nReq-1.
usesImplDict : String -> Int -> Expr -> Bool
usesImplDict mname nReq body =
  anyMember (bodyRDictNames body) (implDictNames mname nReq)

implDictNames : String -> Int -> List String
implDictNames mname nReq =
  map (dictParamName mname) (rangeFromCount (methodDictArityOf mname) nReq)

-- [start, start+1, .. start+count-1]
rangeFromCount : Int -> Int -> List Int
rangeFromCount start count
  | count <= 0 = []
  | otherwise = start :: rangeFromCount (start + 1) (count - 1)

-- the dict-param names every EMethodAt in [e] routes RDict to (post-resolveSites)
-- plus every EDictAt route-LIST that forwards RDict/RDictFwd to an impl param
-- (a `=>`-constrained helper call that threads the impl dict through, e.g.
-- `debug xs = "[\{debugListItems xs}]"` where `debugListItems` is an EDictAt).
bodyRDictNames : Expr -> List String
bodyRDictNames e = flatMap siteRDictName (collectMethodSites e)
  ++ flatMap siteRDictNamesL (collectDictSites e)

siteRDictName : Ref Route -> List String
siteRDictName r = match r.value
  RDict d => [d]
  RDictFwd d => [d]
  _ => []

siteRDictNamesL : Ref (List Route) -> List String
siteRDictNamesL r = flatMap routeRDictName r.value

routeRDictName : Route -> List String
routeRDictName (RDict d) = [d]
routeRDictName (RDictFwd d) = [d]
-- Native #54/#21 residual: an element-dict RKey route can NEST an RDict forwarding
-- the enclosing impl's `requires` dict (e.g. `RKey "$tuple" [RDict $dict_debug_0,
-- RDict $dict_debug_1]` for `debug (toList m)` in a `Debug (Map k v)` body).  The
-- gate that decides whether to PREPEND those impl-dict params (usesImplDict) must
-- see the nested name, else the route references a param the clause never declares
-- → emitter panics "unbound dict witness".  Recurse into the nested reqs.
routeRDictName (RKey _ reqs) = flatMap routeRDictName reqs
routeRDictName _ = []

-- every EMethodAt route ref reachable in [e] (a pure structural walk; reading a
-- ref's `.value` is done later by siteRDictName, not here)
collectMethodSites : Expr -> List (Ref Route)
collectMethodSites (EMethodAt _ r _ _) = [r]
collectMethodSites (ELoc _ e) = collectMethodSites e
collectMethodSites (EDoOrigin _ e) = collectMethodSites e
collectMethodSites (EApp f x) = collectMethodSites f ++ collectMethodSites x
collectMethodSites (ELam _ b) = collectMethodSites b
collectMethodSites (ELet _ _ _ e1 e2) = collectMethodSites e1
  ++ collectMethodSites e2
collectMethodSites (ELetGroup binds body) = flatMap letBindSites binds
  ++ collectMethodSites body
collectMethodSites (EMatch e arms) = collectMethodSites e
  ++ flatMap armSites arms
collectMethodSites (EIf c t e) = collectMethodSites c
  ++ collectMethodSites t
  ++ collectMethodSites e
collectMethodSites (EBinOp _ l r _) = collectMethodSites l
  ++ collectMethodSites r
collectMethodSites (EUnOp _ e _) = collectMethodSites e
collectMethodSites (EInfix _ l r) = collectMethodSites l ++ collectMethodSites r
collectMethodSites (EFieldAccess e _ _) = collectMethodSites e
collectMethodSites (ETuple es) = flatMap collectMethodSites es
collectMethodSites (EListLit es) = flatMap collectMethodSites es
collectMethodSites (EArrayLit es) = flatMap collectMethodSites es
collectMethodSites (ERangeList a b _) = collectMethodSites a
  ++ collectMethodSites b
collectMethodSites (ERangeArray a b _) = collectMethodSites a
  ++ collectMethodSites b
collectMethodSites (ESlice e a b _ _) = collectMethodSites e
  ++ collectMethodSites a
  ++ collectMethodSites b
collectMethodSites (EIndex e i _) = collectMethodSites e ++ collectMethodSites i
collectMethodSites (EAnnot e _) = collectMethodSites e
collectMethodSites (EBlock stmts) = flatMap doStmtSites stmts
collectMethodSites (ERecordCreate _ fs) = flatMap fieldAssignSites fs
collectMethodSites (ERecordUpdate e fs _) = collectMethodSites e
  ++ flatMap fieldAssignSites fs
collectMethodSites (EVariantUpdate _ e fs) = collectMethodSites e
  ++ flatMap fieldAssignSites fs
collectMethodSites _ = []

letBindSites : LetBind -> List (Ref Route)
letBindSites (LetBind _ clauses) = flatMap funClauseSites clauses

funClauseSites : FunClause -> List (Ref Route)
funClauseSites (FunClause _ body) = collectMethodSites body

armSites : Arm -> List (Ref Route)
armSites (Arm _ guards body) = flatMap guardSites guards
  ++ collectMethodSites body

guardSites : Guard -> List (Ref Route)
guardSites (GBool e) = collectMethodSites e
guardSites (GBind _ e) = collectMethodSites e

doStmtSites : DoStmt -> List (Ref Route)
doStmtSites (DoExpr e) = collectMethodSites e
doStmtSites (DoLet _ _ _ e) = collectMethodSites e
doStmtSites (DoBind _ e) = collectMethodSites e
doStmtSites (DoAssign _ e) = collectMethodSites e
doStmtSites (DoFieldAssign _ _ e) = collectMethodSites e

fieldAssignSites : FieldAssign -> List (Ref Route)
fieldAssignSites (FieldAssign _ e) = collectMethodSites e

-- mirror of collectMethodSites's structural walk, but collecting EDictAt's
-- route-LIST refs (`Ref (List Route)`) instead of EMethodAt's single route ref.
collectDictSites : Expr -> List (Ref (List Route))
collectDictSites (EDictAt _ rs) = [rs]
-- D3b-1: an EMethodAt arg-position site carries its element-dict routes in the
-- impl-/method-dicts route LISTS (not tagRef).  A nested `requires` impl whose
-- body dispatches a method on its own element tyvar routes RDict $dict_<m>_<slot>
-- here — scan both so implDictPassMethods' usesImplDict gate sees it and prepends
-- the matching param.  [] on the golden path (resolveArgStamp never runs).
collectDictSites (EMethodAt _ _ implR methR) = [implR, methR]
collectDictSites (ELoc _ e) = collectDictSites e
collectDictSites (EDoOrigin _ e) = collectDictSites e
collectDictSites (EApp f x) = collectDictSites f ++ collectDictSites x
collectDictSites (ELam _ b) = collectDictSites b
collectDictSites (ELet _ _ _ e1 e2) = collectDictSites e1 ++ collectDictSites e2
collectDictSites (ELetGroup binds body) = flatMap letBindDictSites binds
  ++ collectDictSites body
collectDictSites (EMatch e arms) = collectDictSites e
  ++ flatMap armDictSites arms
collectDictSites (EIf c t e) = collectDictSites c
  ++ collectDictSites t
  ++ collectDictSites e
collectDictSites (EBinOp _ l r _) = collectDictSites l ++ collectDictSites r
collectDictSites (EUnOp _ e _) = collectDictSites e
collectDictSites (EInfix _ l r) = collectDictSites l ++ collectDictSites r
collectDictSites (EFieldAccess e _ _) = collectDictSites e
collectDictSites (ETuple es) = flatMap collectDictSites es
collectDictSites (EListLit es) = flatMap collectDictSites es
collectDictSites (EArrayLit es) = flatMap collectDictSites es
collectDictSites (ERangeList a b _) = collectDictSites a ++ collectDictSites b
collectDictSites (ERangeArray a b _) = collectDictSites a ++ collectDictSites b
collectDictSites (ESlice e a b _ _) = collectDictSites e
  ++ collectDictSites a
  ++ collectDictSites b
collectDictSites (EIndex e i _) = collectDictSites e ++ collectDictSites i
collectDictSites (EAnnot e _) = collectDictSites e
collectDictSites (EBlock stmts) = flatMap doStmtDictSites stmts
collectDictSites (ERecordCreate _ fs) = flatMap fieldAssignDictSites fs
collectDictSites (ERecordUpdate e fs _) = collectDictSites e
  ++ flatMap fieldAssignDictSites fs
collectDictSites (EVariantUpdate _ e fs) = collectDictSites e
  ++ flatMap fieldAssignDictSites fs
collectDictSites _ = []

letBindDictSites : LetBind -> List (Ref (List Route))
letBindDictSites (LetBind _ clauses) = flatMap funClauseDictSites clauses

funClauseDictSites : FunClause -> List (Ref (List Route))
funClauseDictSites (FunClause _ body) = collectDictSites body

armDictSites : Arm -> List (Ref (List Route))
armDictSites (Arm _ guards body) = flatMap guardDictSites guards
  ++ collectDictSites body

guardDictSites : Guard -> List (Ref (List Route))
guardDictSites (GBool e) = collectDictSites e
guardDictSites (GBind _ e) = collectDictSites e

doStmtDictSites : DoStmt -> List (Ref (List Route))
doStmtDictSites (DoExpr e) = collectDictSites e
doStmtDictSites (DoLet _ _ _ e) = collectDictSites e
doStmtDictSites (DoBind _ e) = collectDictSites e
doStmtDictSites (DoAssign _ e) = collectDictSites e
doStmtDictSites (DoFieldAssign _ _ e) = collectDictSites e

fieldAssignDictSites : FieldAssign -> List (Ref (List Route))
fieldAssignDictSites (FieldAssign _ e) = collectDictSites e

dictArityOf : String -> Int
dictArityOf n =
  listLen (fromOption
    []
    (lookupAssocSL2 n perRun.value.funConstraintsRef.value))

dictParams : String -> Int -> List Pat
dictParams n k = dictParamsGo n 0 k

dictParamsGo : String -> Int -> Int -> List Pat
dictParamsGo n slot k
  | slot >= k = []
  | otherwise = PVar (dictParamName n slot) :: dictParamsGo n (slot + 1) k

-- [count] dict params for [n] starting at slot [start]: $dict_n_start ..
-- $dict_n_(start+count-1).  Used for impl-`requires` element-dict params, which
-- follow the method's own method-level params (S2 offset = methodDictArityOf).
dictParamsFrom : String -> Int -> Int -> List Pat
dictParamsFrom n start count
  | count <= 0 = []
  | otherwise =
    PVar (dictParamName n start) :: dictParamsFrom n (start + 1) (count - 1)

dictParamName : String -> Int -> String
dictParamName fn slot = "$dict_\{fn}_\{intToString slot}"

-- head type constructor, normalizing at each level (the head may be a TVar
-- linked to a concrete type after unification — normalize follows only the top
-- link, so recurse through normalize)
headTyconMono : Mono -> Option String
headTyconMono t = match normalize t
  TApp a _ => headTyconMono a
  TCon n => Some n
  _ => None

-- Native Gap C: the arity-distinguished tuple dispatch tag (`__tuple2__`, …),
-- byte-identical to eval.mdk's `tupleHeadTag`.  Stamped as the RKey route tag at a
-- tuple method site so each arity routes to its own impl group / lifted define.
tupleHeadTagTc : Int -> String
tupleHeadTagTc n = "__tuple" ++ intToString n ++ "__"

-- An n-tuple TYPE is a saturated left-spine TApp over the synthetic nullary
-- constructor `TCon (tupleHeadTagTc n)` — the constructor NAME string IS the
-- existing `__tupleN__` dispatch tag, so tag/eval parity is automatic and no
-- `Mono.TTuple` constructor is needed.  (Surface `Ty.TyTuple` is unchanged.)
tupleMono : List Mono -> Mono
tupleMono ts = tupleSpineOf (TCon (tupleHeadTagTc (listLen ts))) ts

tupleSpineOf : Mono -> List Mono -> Mono
tupleSpineOf acc [] = acc
tupleSpineOf acc (t::rest) = tupleSpineOf (TApp acc t) rest

-- collect a left-spine `TApp` into (head, args-in-application-order),
-- normalizing at each level so a linked head resolves.
spineParts : Mono -> (Mono, List Mono)
spineParts t = match normalize t
  TApp a b => match spineParts a
    (h, args) => (h, args ++ [b])
  other => (other, [])

-- recognise a fully-saturated tuple spine (arity >= 2 — `()`/Unit stays
-- `TCon "Unit"`, never a tuple); return its element monos in order.
export tupleSpine : Mono -> Option (List Mono)
tupleSpine t = match spineParts t
  (TCon n, args) =>
    if listLen args >= 2 && n == tupleHeadTagTc (listLen args) then
      Some args
    else
      None
  _ => None

-- arity a `__tupleN__` tag string names (else None) — bounded probe so a bare
-- or PARTIAL tuple ctor never leaks the raw `__tupleN__` tag into a message.
tupleTagArity : String -> Option Int
tupleTagArity n = tupleTagArityGo n 2

tupleTagArityGo : String -> Int -> Option Int
tupleTagArityGo n k
  | k > 32 = None
  | tupleHeadTagTc k == n = Some k
  | otherwise = tupleTagArityGo n (k + 1)

commaStr : Int -> String
commaStr n = if n <= 0 then "" else "," ++ commaStr (n - 1)

-- render a bare/partial tuple constructor as the surface-less `(,)`/`(,,)` head
-- (only reached when a tuple spine is UNSATURATED, e.g. an arity-mismatch
-- message); a saturated spine renders `(e1, …)` via ppApp before reaching here.
ppConName : String -> String
ppConName n = match tupleTagArity n
  Some k => "(" ++ commaStr (k - 1) ++ ")"
  None => n

-- Two monos genuinely CANNOT unify because a tuple spine meets a differing
-- concrete head/arity — used only to pick the readable full-type mismatch
-- message.  A TVar-headed other side returns False (it can still bind).
tupleUnifyClash : Mono -> Mono -> Bool
tupleUnifyClash a b = match (tupleSpine a, tupleSpine b)
  (Some xs, Some ys) => listLen xs != listLen ys
  (Some _, None) => monoSpineHeadIsCon b
  (None, Some _) => monoSpineHeadIsCon a
  (None, None) => False

-- DRIVER-COLLAPSE Phase 5: scheme extraction for repl/lsp `:type`/hover + the
-- `=== TYPES ===` golden (checkToLines), the surviving replacement for the
-- deleted flat `checkProgram`.  implInferEnabled OFF + the seeded path, which
-- keeps the checkLetRecDecls / LetRecNonFunction guard checkModuleFull skips.
-- Callers pass the 1-module decl list (preludeDecls ++ user buffer) and read
-- schemes by name; no eval, so this is the typecheck-only sibling of the
-- 1-module path.
export checkProgramSchemes : List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
checkProgramSchemes coreProg userProg =
  let _ = setRef driverState.value.implInferEnabled False
  checkProgramSeededSplit [] coreProg userProg

-- Like checkProgramSchemes but ALSO seeds runtime.mdk's externs (putStrLn,
-- readLine, …) into scope, so a buffer using them type-checks instead of leaving
-- the bindings that consume them unconstrained.  Mirrors the OCaml reference's
-- `check_program`, which seeds `Runtime.entries` into the initial env — the LSP
-- hover/completion/inlay handlers run this so externs resolve and a `let input =
-- readLine ()` hovers as `String`, not a free `a`.
export checkProgramSchemesWithRuntime : List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
checkProgramSchemesWithRuntime runtimeDecls coreProg userProg =
  let _ = setRef driverState.value.implInferEnabled False
  checkProgramSeededSplit (externSchemes runtimeDecls) coreProg userProg

-- [seed] are extern schemes from runtime.mdk: in scope for lookup (so core.mdk's
-- use of putStrLn/etc. resolves) but NOT part of the output (mirrors the
-- reference seeding Runtime.entries into initial_env, which aren't returned).
checkProgramSeeded : List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
checkProgramSeeded seed prog = checkProgramSeededSplit seed [] prog

-- Same pass, but told WHERE THE PRELUDE ENDS.  Every driver that flattens the prelude
-- into the program it checks (`desugar coreP ++ desugared`) hands the two halves in
-- separately instead of pre-concatenating them, which is the only thing the flat path
-- was missing to make SHADOW-SEMANTICS S1 (shadow-hood is PER-MODULE, per-name) hold:
-- with the boundary known, a user standalone that shadows an interface-method name is a
-- shadow inside the USER's decls and NOT inside the prelude's.  See flatShadowScopingRef
-- for the bug this closes.  [coreProg] = [] ⇒ prelude-free program ⇒ scoping is inert and
-- this is byte-for-byte the old checkProgramSeeded (that is the multi-module discovery,
-- elaborateDict, and probe-entry path).
checkProgramSeededSplit : List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
checkProgramSeededSplit seed coreProg userProg =
  checkBodyImpl seed (Flat coreProg) userProg

-- #80: the SINGLE whole-program typecheck body, shared by the flat/single-file path
-- (checkProgramSeededSplit, `Flat coreProg`) and the per-module path
-- (checkModuleFullImpl, `Module mid accData implDecls`).  Institutionalizes
-- DICT-SEMANTICS §7's single-evaluator law: one elaboration ⇒ `check` and `emit`
-- cannot silently disagree on a dispatch decision.  Everything is shared EXCEPT the
-- SIX load-bearing divergences, each a `match mode` branch below:
--   (1) decl universe (prog / persistent DATA-universe / fullUniverse / groundUniverse)
--   (2) inference plan — flat's TWO-PHASE prelude-then-user vs module's single pass
--   (3) cross-module dict-threading restore + snapshot (module only; flat SKIPS)
--   (4) importer/definer shadows + the shadowMethodSchemes env layer
--   (5) the implInferEnabled obligation gate (INVERTED between the two emit paths)
--   (6) superDeclsRef ownership (set from the mode)
-- The mode carries the inputs each path adds on top of `seed` + `prog0`:
--   Flat   coreProg               — the prelude prefix (empty ⇒ prelude-free program)
--   Module mid accData implDecls  — module id, prior modules' public data, impl universe
data CheckMode = Flat (List Decl) | Module String (List Decl) (List Decl)

checkBodyImpl : List (String, Scheme) -> CheckMode -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
checkBodyImpl seed mode prog0 =
  let _ = resetState ()
  -- (#1) decl universes, derived from the mode.  On Flat, prog = coreProg ++ userProg
  -- and every universe collapses to it; on Module they fan out over accData/implDecls.
  let prog = match mode
    Flat coreProg => coreProg ++ prog0
    Module _ _ _ => prog0
  -- the full visible universe (shadows / key table / iface-param registration /
  -- completeness / obligations): Flat = whole flattened program; Module = imported
  -- data + impl universe + this module.
  -- #154 PR-A: every consumer of `fullUniverse` is a `Flat _ =>` arm (grep confirms:
  -- buildDefinerShadows / buildKeyTable / registerMethodIfaceParamsAll / checkImplCompleteness
  -- / checkImplObligations / checkCallObligations — all Flat).  Medaka is STRICT, so the
  -- Module arm's `accData ++ implDecls ++ prog0` allocated an O(|accData|) list every module
  -- and discarded it unread — a dead per-module O(N) concat.  Bind `[]` on Module.
  let fullUniverse = match mode
    Flat _ => prog
    Module _ _ _ => []
  -- registerAllData universe (#154 PR-A): the Module path no longer builds `accData ++ prog0`
  -- here — prior modules' public data lives in the persistent DATA-universe accumulators
  -- (universeRecords/…/universeDataEnv), copied+overlaid at the `dataEnv` binding below.
  -- groundMultiParamObligations universe: Flat grounds over prog; Module over implDecls.
  let groundUniverse = match mode
    Flat _ => prog
    Module _ _ implDecls => implDecls
  -- (#6) superDeclsRef: unified body OWNS it — Flat = coreProg ++ userProg, Module =
  -- implDecls.  On the emit path elabModuleStamp already sets the same implDecls; on the
  -- multi-module CHECK path this closes the latent stale/empty-ref gap (behavior change).
  let superDecls = match mode
    Flat _ => prog
    Module _ _ implDecls => implDecls
  let _ = setRef driverState.value.superDeclsRef superDecls
  -- (BREAK #3) effect domains: FLAT populates inline; MODULE relies on the driver
  -- preamble (checkModulesPreamble / elaborateModules) populating ONCE over the whole
  -- import graph — a per-module populate would wipe another module's domains.
  let _ = match mode
    Flat _ =>
      let _ = populateEffectDomains prog
      ()
    Module _ _ _ => ()
  let _ = checkEffectParams prog
  let _ = checkLetRecDecls prog
  -- #154 PR1: on the Module path, grow the persistent universe accumulators by THIS
  -- module's decls BEFORE the shadow/key reads below consult them — so they see the
  -- same universe `fullUniverse` (which appended prog0) did.  The Flat path leaves them
  -- untouched and rebuilds over its whole `prog` (already O(N)).
  let _ = match mode
    Flat _ => ()
    Module _ _ _ => appendUniverseAccums prog0
  -- (#4) shadows.  definerShadows uses the SHARED form — buildDefinerShadows over the
  -- full visible universe, scoped to THIS module's decls (prog0).  On Flat, prog0 =
  -- userProg and fullUniverse = prog, so this is exactly the old flat `buildDefinerShadows
  -- prog userProg`.  #154 PR1: the Module path reads the accumulated interface-method
  -- SET instead (byte-identical membership).  importerShadows exist only across a module
  -- boundary; the `Module _ _ []` arm (cmCheckWorker / core pass, implDecls = []) keeps
  -- the old empty result — `funDefs [] = []` gave no imported fns there.
  let definerShadows = match mode
    Flat _ => buildDefinerShadows fullUniverse prog0
    Module _ _ _ => definerShadowsFromSet crossRun.value.universeIfaceMethodsRef.value prog0
  let importerShadows = match mode
    Flat _ => []
    Module _ _ [] => []
    Module _ _ _ => standaloneShadowsFromSet crossRun.value.universeIfaceMethodsRef.value crossRun.value.universeFunNamesRef.value prog0
  let sigs = sigsOf prog
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef definerShadows
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.definerShadowSigsRef sigs
  -- #154 PR1: implExistsForHead's existence table — the accumulated buckets on the
  -- Module path (existence is order/dup-insensitive), the per-program rebuild on Flat.
  let _ = match mode
    Flat _ => setRef perRun.value.shadowKeyTableRef (buildKeyTable fullUniverse)
    Module _ _ _ => setRef perRun.value.shadowKeyTableRef crossRun.value.universeKeyBucketsRef.value
  -- (#4 + #3-restore) mode-specific pre-inference ref setup (all pure/independent of the
  -- register+globalS block below, so order-safe).  Flat arms the per-body shadow-scoping
  -- toggles; Module installs importer-shadow state and restores the cross-module dict
  -- accumulators resetState just wiped.
  let _ = match mode
    Flat coreProg =>
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.flatShadowScopingRef (declsNonEmpty coreProg)
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.flatUserShadowNamesRef definerShadows
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.flatCoreFnNamesRef (namesToSet (map fst (funDefs coreProg)) omEmpty)
      ()
    Module _ _ _ =>
      let _ = setRef driverState.value.standaloneValuesRef (dedup (importerShadows ++ definerShadows))
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.shadowStandaloneSchemesRef (pickStandaloneSchemes importerShadows seed)
      let _ = setRef driverState.value.methodDispatchIdxRef crossRun.value.universeMethodDispatchIdxRef.value
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.funConstraintsRef crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintsRef.value
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.funConstraintIfacesRef crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesRef.value
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.funConstraintsRef (aliasConstraintEntries prog crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef.value
        ++ perRun.value.funConstraintsRef.value)
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.funConstraintIfacesRef (aliasConstraintEntries prog crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesQualRef.value
        ++ perRun.value.funConstraintIfacesRef.value)
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.currentImportDefinersRef (importDefinersOf prog)
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.currentImportOriginsRef (importOriginsOf prog)
      ()
  -- #11 soundness: the interface-method→iface-param map (+ the registered-iface set).
  -- #154 PR2 (site #3): FLAT rebuilds both refs over its whole `fullUniverse` (already
  -- O(N)); the Module path COPIES the persistent accumulators — grown one module at a
  -- time by `appendUniverseAccums prog0` above (which ran BEFORE this) and seeded by the
  -- core pass — into the two refs resetState just wiped, exactly as PR1 copies
  -- universeKeyBucketsRef into shadowKeyTableRef.  Method/interface names are globally
  -- unique, so the accumulated maps carry the same entries the per-module rebuild over
  -- `fullUniverse` (= accData ++ implDecls ++ prog0) did for every name this module names.
  let _ = match mode
    Flat _ => registerMethodIfaceParamsAll fullUniverse
    Module _ _ _ =>
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.methodIfaceParamsRef crossRun.value.universeMethodIfaceParamsRef.value
      setRef perRun.value.registeredIfacesRef crossRun.value.universeRegisteredIfacesRef.value
  -- #154 PR-A: the dominant remaining O(N²) in the multi-module typecheck.  The Module arm
  -- used to `registerAllData initialEnv (accData ++ prog0)` — re-registering (and re-minting
  -- the tyvars of) EVERY earlier module's public data ONCE PER MODULE.  Now: COPY the six
  -- persistent DATA-universe accumulators (prior modules' public data + ctor env, each minted
  -- exactly once) into the resetState-wiped working refs, then register ONLY this module's
  -- `prog0` as a transient FRONT overlay.  Ordering is byte-identical: the persistent list is
  -- [pub_{k-1}..pub_1] and `registerData` conses prog0 on top → [prog0, pub_{k-1}..pub_1],
  -- the same lookupAssoc (first-wins) / omInsert (last-write-wins) winner the old left fold
  -- over `accData ++ prog0` produced.  Per module O(|prog0|); total O(N).  Flat is unchanged.
  let dataEnv = match mode
    Flat _ => registerAllData initialEnv prog
    Module _ _ _ =>
      let _ = loadDataUniverse ()
      let base = registerAllData crossRun.value.universeDataEnv.value prog0
      -- #674: OVERLAY the ctors of the types THIS module imports with `(..)` so a
      -- bare ctor name resolves to the module the unit imports it from, not the
      -- universe's load-order winner.  Registers LAST → wins the omInsert tie-break.
      -- Reads the persistent universe DECLS (not the Module `accData` field — that is
      -- dead `[]` on the elaborate/emit path, #154 PR-C), so the overlay fires on the
      -- run/build gate too, not just `check`.
      registerAllData base (importedCtorTypeDecls prog0 crossRun.value.universeDataDecls.value)
  let _ = rejectCyclicAliases ()
  let globalS = ifaceMethodSchemes prog ++ externSchemes prog
  -- methodNames: FLAT's SECOND ifaceMethodSchemes call over prog — dedup-skipped by
  -- registerMethodConstraints so globalS's registered (first-call) ids stay authoritative.
  -- Module checks core in isolation and never needs it, so NO extra call there.
  let methodNames = match mode
    Flat _ => map fst (ifaceMethodSchemes prog)
    Module _ _ _ => []
  -- (#4 cont) post-globalS mode setup.  Module: shadowMethodSchemes env layer (rebinding
  -- imported method names to the interface-method scheme) + the method-constraint cross
  -- seed (AFTER globalS's ifaceMethodSchemes registration, per registerMethodConstraints).
  -- Flat: record the interface-method names for processSCC's method-shadow scoping.
  let shadowLayer = match mode
    Flat _ =>
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.methodShadowNamesRef methodNames
      []
    Module _ _ implDecls =>
      let sm = pickSchemes importerShadows (ifaceMethodSchemes implDecls)
      let _ = setRef perRun.value.methodConstraintsRef (perRun.value.methodConstraintsRef.value ++ crossRun.value.crossModuleMethodConstraintsRef.value)
      sm
  let env1 = extendVars (extendVars (extendVars dataEnv seed) globalS) shadowLayer
  let defs = funDefs prog
  let topSchemes = processTopGroups env1 sigs defs (groupNames defs omEmpty)
  -- (#2 / BREAK #1) inference plan.  FLAT runs the TWO-PHASE prelude-then-user scoped
  -- inference (dropSchemesNamed drops user schemes shadowing an interface method so the
  -- prelude's own bodies see the METHOD; scopeShadowsCore/User gate the per-body shadow
  -- set) — reproducing the isolation the module path gets by checking core as its own
  -- invocation.  Collapsing this to one pass reintroduces the prelude-method-shadow
  -- miscompile (List Int vs Int).  MODULE runs the single pass.
  let _ = match mode
    Flat coreProg =>
      let preludeBodyEnv = extendVars env1 (dropSchemesNamed methodNames topSchemes)
      let _ = scopeShadowsCore ()
      let _ = inferImplBodiesIfEnabledIn preludeBodyEnv prog coreProg
      let _ = inferDefaultBodiesIfEnabled preludeBodyEnv coreProg
      let _ = inferPropBodies preludeBodyEnv coreProg
      let _ = inferTestBodies preludeBodyEnv coreProg
      let _ = scopeShadowsUser ()
      let _ = inferImplBodiesIfEnabledIn preludeBodyEnv prog prog0
      let _ = inferDefaultBodiesIfEnabled preludeBodyEnv prog0
      let _ = inferPropBodies preludeBodyEnv prog0
      let _ = inferTestBodies preludeBodyEnv prog0
      ()
    Module _ _ implDecls =>
      let bodyEnv = extendVars env1 topSchemes
      let _ = inferModuleImplBodiesIfEnabled bodyEnv implDecls prog
      let _ = inferDefaultBodiesIfEnabled bodyEnv prog
      let _ = inferPropBodies bodyEnv prog
      let _ = inferTestBodies bodyEnv prog
      ()
  -- (#3) cross-module dict-threading SNAPSHOT — MODULE ONLY (flat SKIPS: running it would
  -- pollute the cross-module accumulators with a phantom single-file attribution).
  let _ = match mode
    Flat _ => ()
    Module mid _ _ =>
      let _ = expandSupersTable driverState.value.superDeclsRef.value
      let _ = setRef crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintsRef perRun.value.funConstraintsRef.value
      let _ = setRef crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef
        (attributeModuleArities mid prog perRun.value.funConstraintsRef.value ++ crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintsQualRef.value)
      let _ = setRef crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesQualRef
        (attributeModuleArrIfaces mid prog perRun.value.funConstraintIfacesRef.value ++ crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesQualRef.value)
      let _ = setRef crossRun.value.crossModuleFunConstraintIfacesRef perRun.value.funConstraintIfacesRef.value
      let _ = setRef crossRun.value.crossModuleMethodConstraintsRef perRun.value.methodConstraintsRef.value
      let _ = setRef crossRun.value.crossModuleMethodConstraintsQualRef
        (attributeMethodModuleArities mid prog perRun.value.methodConstraintsRef.value ++ crossRun.value.crossModuleMethodConstraintsQualRef.value)
      ()
  let _ = groundMultiParamObligations groundUniverse perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value
  -- P0-17: impl missing a required method.  #154 PR1: the Module path looks the required
  -- methods up in the accumulated iface→required map instead of rescanning fullUniverse.
  let _ = match mode
    Flat _ => checkImplCompleteness fullUniverse prog
    Module _ _ _ => checkImplCompletenessMap crossRun.value.universeIfaceRequiredRef.value prog
  -- (#5 / BREAK #2) obligation gate.  FLAT runs checkImplObligations + setNumlitFloats
  -- UNCONDITIONALLY (flat-emit via elaborateDict needs the Float int-literal stamp — its
  -- resolve chain doesn't redo it).  MODULE runs them iff `not implInferEnabled` (the emit
  -- path stamps AFTER arg-stamp resolution; calling here would double-stamp).  The
  -- setNumlitFloats-vs-checkCallObligations ordering also differs per path — preserved.
  let _ = match mode
    Flat _ =>
      let _ = checkImplObligations fullUniverse perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value
      let _ = checkCallObligations fullUniverse perRun.value.pendingCallObligations.value
      let _ = setNumlitFloats ()   -- PLAN.md #11: stamp Float-typed int literals
      ()
    Module _ _ _ =>
      -- #154 PR3: consume the PERSISTENT keyed impl universe (grown per module in
      -- appendUniverseAccums) — NO per-module `implDeclsWithReqs`/`implHeadsOf` rebuild
      -- over the growing `fullUniverse`.  The accumulator holds the SAME impls; existence/
      -- count reads are byte-identical, and findMatchingImplReqsU's most-specific-first
      -- requires-selection is a deliberate soundness fix (see its doc-comment).  Site #8
      -- (groundMultiParamObligations groundUniverse) is left on the list path above
      -- because groundUniverse = implDecls EXCLUDES this module's prog0, which the
      -- accumulator (appended before this point) includes — and it is a no-op for the
      -- single-param obligations the perf `modules` shape exercises.
      let obUniv = ImplUniverse crossRun.value.obUnivConcreteRef.value crossRun.value.obUnivHeadlessRef.value crossRun.value.obUnivIfaceTagsRef.value
      let _ = if not driverState.value.implInferEnabled.value then
                let _ = checkImplObligationsU obUniv perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value
                setNumlitFloats ()
              else ()
      let _ = checkCallObligationsU obUniv [] perRun.value.pendingCallObligations.value
      ()
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.localSchemesOut (mapLocalSchemes perRun.value.localBindRefs.value)
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.seedSchemesOut seed
  -- #154 PR-A: grow the persistent DATA-universe by THIS module's PUBLIC data, AFTER the
  -- module's inference is complete (so the copy+overlay above saw pub 1..k-1 and NEVER this
  -- module's own pub twice) — the register-time mirror of foldModules' `accData ++
  -- publicDataDecls prog` threading.  appendDataUniverse clobbers the working table refs,
  -- which are DEAD here (the next module's resetState wipes them).  Flat leaves it untouched.
  let _ = match mode
    Flat _ => ()
    Module _ _ _ => appendDataUniverse (publicDataDecls prog0)
  globalS ++ topSchemes
-- D2: check top-level `let rec` bindings for non-function members before
-- type-checking the DFunDef groups (mirrors oracle's LetRecNonFunction guard,
-- lib/typecheck.ml:2596-2602, which fires in process_letrec_group for
-- is_letrec=true groups — i.e. those coming from DLetGroup).

-- Phase 83/84 single-level: AFTER top-level inference (so the scheme output is
-- unchanged), and ONLY on the elaborate/dict path, infer each parametric impl's
-- return-position method bodies, registering the requires' element dict so the
-- inner ref routes RDict and the call site routes RKey + impl dicts.

-- Phase 69.x-e: likewise infer each interface DEFAULT body that carries a
-- method-level constraint (foldMap's default), registering the constraint var so
-- the in-body return-position ref (`empty`) routes RDict to `$dict_<method>_<slot>`.

-- Phase 4.55 (mirror lib/typecheck.ml:4254-4262): type-check each prop body with
-- its declared params in scope.  This is what RESOLVES the EDictAt/EMethodAt route
-- refs the prepass marked inside a prop body — without it the routes stay empty,
-- and on the dict-passed eval path (DRIVER-COLLAPSE Phase 2 promotes the file's own
-- `=>`-constrained fns) the prop body would call a now-dict-passed `wf`/`fromList`
-- with no dict argument → under-application → `force` a partial closure → not VBool
-- → every prop "fails" (set's `fromList []` reported ill-formed).  The compiler
-- typechecker previously skipped props entirely (funDefs ignores DProp), so this
-- gap was latent until Phase 2's promotion exposed it.

-- Shape A (gap #44): ground a container literal's free result-element var THROUGH
-- the impl's method type, BEFORE the resolve* passes read the cells.

-- Error-path B4: now every method occurrence's dispatch type is fully resolved;
-- reject any whose concrete head type has no matching impl (mirrors the oracle's
-- check_method_usages, lib/typecheck.ml:3845).

-- Phase 4.55: infer each prop body with its declared params in scope, so the
-- prepass-marked EDictAt/EMethodAt route refs inside resolve (mirror of
-- lib/typecheck.ml:4254-4262).  Runs unconditionally (props are eval-only; the
-- emit/golden paths have no props), like the OCaml `infer prop_body; unify t_bool`.
-- A prop body is eval-only and is NOT dict-passed for operator dispatch: like the
-- OCaml oracle (which evals raw prop bodies against the marked env), its comparison
-- operators must stay PRIMITIVE EBinOps the eval builtin handles structurally, not
-- get stamped and rewritten to eq/lt method apps (whose dict route would then be
-- applied to a non-dict-passed comparison, panic: applied non-function).  So
-- snapshot pendingBinopSites around prop inference and restore it after: the routes
-- the prop body NEEDS (EDictAt for the file's promoted constrained fns, via
-- pendingDictApps/pendingSites) are still collected; only the binop-rewrite sites
-- the prop body would otherwise contribute are dropped.
inferPropBodies : TcEnv -> List Decl -> Unit
inferPropBodies env prog =
  let savedBinop = perRun.value.pendingBinopSites.value
  let savedUnop = perRun.value.pendingUnopSites.value
  let savedArith = perRun.value.pendingArithSites.value
  let _ = inferPropBodiesGo env prog
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.pendingArithSites savedArith
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.pendingUnopSites savedUnop
  setRef perRun.value.pendingBinopSites savedBinop

inferPropBodiesGo : TcEnv -> List Decl -> Unit
inferPropBodiesGo _ [] = ()
inferPropBodiesGo env ((DProp _ _ params body)::rest) =
  let local = extendPropParams env params
  let _ = infer local body
  inferPropBodiesGo env rest
inferPropBodiesGo env (_::rest) = inferPropBodiesGo env rest

-- Phase 127 (restored 2026-07-11): typecheck each `test "…" = body` body,
-- requiring it to have type `Expectation` (a nullary TCon, so a plain nominal
-- unify).  Unlike props, test bodies ARE dict-passed (their `expectEqual`/… calls
-- come from `import test`), so this runs on the normal inference path with binop
-- stamping enabled (no snapshot/restore).  Without this, `test "x" = 1 + "str"`
-- and `test "x" = 42` slipped through `check` with zero diagnostics.
inferTestBodies : TcEnv -> List Decl -> Unit
inferTestBodies _ [] = ()
inferTestBodies env ((DTest _ _ body)::rest) =
  let _ = unify (infer env body) (TCon "Expectation")
  inferTestBodies env rest
inferTestBodies env (_::rest) = inferTestBodies env rest

-- bind each prop param `x : ty` as a monomorphic scheme in the env (fresh tyvars
-- for any free type variable in ty; props are normally concrete, e.g. `List Int`).
extendPropParams : TcEnv -> List PropParam -> TcEnv
extendPropParams env [] = env
extendPropParams env ((PropParam x ty)::rest) =
  let scheme = monoScheme (fromAstType (freshTvMap (dedup (tyVarNames ty))) ty)
  extendPropParams (extendVar env x scheme) rest

-- gate: impl-body inference runs only when the elaborate/dict path turned it on.
-- FLAT-PATH SHADOW SCOPING: the impl decls to WALK [decls] are given separately from the
-- [allProg] universe the return-position/method tables are built from — so the flat path
-- can walk core's impls (shadows off) and the user's impls (shadows on) in two bracketed
-- calls without perturbing either table.
inferImplBodiesIfEnabledIn : TcEnv -> List Decl -> List Decl -> Unit
inferImplBodiesIfEnabledIn env allProg decls
  | driverState.value.implInferEnabled.value =
    inferImplBodies env allProg (returnPosMethodNames allProg) decls
  | otherwise = ()

-- C8b: default-body type-checking runs UNCONDITIONALLY (mirrors the oracle's
-- register_interface default-body loop at lib/typecheck.ml:3122-3160 which checks
-- ALL default bodies regardless of whether they carry method-level constraints).
-- Previously gated on implInferEnabled — that blocked unconstrained defaults
-- (Ord.lt/gt/min/max, Foldable.length/isEmpty, Filterable.filter, …) from being
-- type-checked on the plain-check path, so bad unconstrained default bodies were
-- silently accepted.  The constrained branch still registers dict slots (needed
-- for the emit/dict path); the unconstrained branch only type-checks — safe on
-- the plain-check path (activeDictVars mutation is harmless; reset by resetState).
inferDefaultBodiesIfEnabled : TcEnv -> List Decl -> Unit
inferDefaultBodiesIfEnabled env prog = inferDefaultBodies env prog

-- drop every (name, scheme) whose name is in [names] (the flat-path prelude-body
-- shadow fix: remove user schemes that shadow an interface-method name so the
-- method scheme stays visible to the prelude's own bodies).
dropSchemesNamed : List String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
dropSchemesNamed names schemes =
  filterList (s => not (contains (fst s) names)) schemes

inferDefaultBodies : TcEnv -> List Decl -> Unit
inferDefaultBodies _ [] = ()
inferDefaultBodies env (d::rest) =
  let _ = inferOneIfaceDefaults env d
  inferDefaultBodies env rest

inferOneIfaceDefaults : TcEnv -> Decl -> Unit
inferOneIfaceDefaults env (DAttrib _ d) = inferOneIfaceDefaults env d
-- Mirror lib/typecheck.ml:3116-3126: type each default body against the
-- interface's OWN method scheme (built from [typarams]+[methods]), NOT
-- `lookupVar env mname`.  On the flat single-file check path a user top-level
-- funDef can SHADOW a method name (e.g. map.mdk's `isEmpty : Map k v -> Bool`
-- shadowing Foldable's `isEmpty`); looking the name up in env would instantiate
-- that Map-typed shadow and infer the default `isEmpty t = match toList t`
-- against it, grounding `toList`'s container var to `Map` → a spurious
-- `No impl of Foldable for Map` obligation.  The interface scheme is shadow-free.
-- Also extend env with these schemes so PEER-method refs in a default body
-- resolve to the interface methods (oracle's env_with_methods), not user shadows.
inferOneIfaceDefaults env (DInterface { typarams, methods, ... }) =
  let ifaceSchemes = methodSchemesPure methods
  inferDefaultMethods (extendVars env ifaceSchemes) typarams methods
inferOneIfaceDefaults _ _ = ()

inferDefaultMethods : TcEnv -> List String -> List IfaceMethod -> Unit
inferDefaultMethods _ _ [] = ()
inferDefaultMethods env typarams (m::rest) =
  let _ = inferDefaultMethod env typarams m
  inferDefaultMethods env typarams rest

-- C8b: type-check ALL interface default bodies, unconditionally.
--
-- Constrained arm (non-empty ids): method has method-level constraints (e.g. foldMap : Monoid m =>):
--   Instantiate scheme, register constraint vars → dict param names (for dict-passing
--   emit path), infer body, unify.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml:3132-3142 + 3143-3150.
--   CRITICAL: the constraint slot ids AND the default-body instantiation MUST come
--   from the SAME sigToSchemeTvs build of THIS method's type.  Reading the ids from
--   methodConstraintsRef (filled by a SEPARATE ifaceMethodSchemes build with its own
--   fresh tyvar ids) and instantiating an unrelated methodSchemesPure scheme gave ids
--   that did not appear in the instantiation subst → registerOneMethodDict's
--   `lookupAssocI id subst` returned None → activeDictVars got no entry → the in-body
--   `empty`/`++` routed RNone → eval's `++` saw a non-monoid acc and panicked
--   ('++' requires Semigroup).  The oracle (lib/typecheck.ml:3067-3093 + 3126-3142)
--   uses ONE method_schemes build for both, which is mirrored here.
--
-- Unconstrained arm (empty ids): method has no method-level constraints (e.g. Ord.lt):
--   Instantiate scheme, infer body, unify — same but no dict registration.
--
-- Double-error guard: snapshot typeErrors length before body inference; skip the
-- outer unify if new errors were added (matches the oracle's single-error raise).
inferDefaultMethod : TcEnv -> List String -> IfaceMethod -> Unit
inferDefaultMethod env typarams (IfaceMethod mname mty (Some (MethodDefault pats body))) =
  let st = sigToSchemeTvs mty
  let ids = methodConstraintSlotIds typarams mty (snd st)
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.currentFn ""
  let inst = instantiateTracked (fst st)
  let _ = inferDefaultMethodBody mname env (fst inst) pats body
  -- register AFTER body inference, so each constraint var's id in [snd inst] is
  -- its SURVIVING (normalized) union-find root — the same id the in-body return-
  -- position `empty`/`++` site normalizes to (activeDictVarOf reads that root).
  -- Registering BEFORE inference recorded the pre-unification id; the in-body
  -- site could become the surviving root instead, so activeDictVarOf missed it →
  -- RNone → `'++' requires Semigroup`.  Mirrors registerImplRequires's ordering.
  registerMethodDictSlots mname 0 ids (snd inst)
inferDefaultMethod _ _ _ = ()

-- Infer a default method body against [expectedTy], suppressing the outer
-- unify if the body itself already pushed a type error (prevents duplicates
-- that would arise when the accumulating compiler differs from the oracle's
-- raise-on-first behaviour: oracle's TypeMismatch from inside `infer` never
-- reaches the outer `unify`, whereas compiler would report both).
--
-- C8b Gap 2: the outer unify runs with `currentMethodMismatch = Some mname` so a
-- leaf TypeMismatch reports the oracle's single specialized line
-- `Method '<mname>': expected type <e> but got <a>` rather than a bare
-- `Type mismatch`.  When that unify pushed a mismatch, the body's own added
-- impl obligations are TRUNCATED back to the pre-body snapshot — the oracle's
-- `fail (MethodTypeMismatch …)` RAISES, so its later check_method_usages never
-- sees the body's `Num <wrong-type>` usage (e.g. `(5 : Bool)` ⇒ `Num Bool`);
-- dropping them here mirrors that single-error outcome instead of cascading a
-- spurious `No impl of Num for Bool`.
inferDefaultMethodBody : String -> TcEnv -> Mono -> List Pat -> Expr -> Unit
inferDefaultMethodBody mname env expectedTy pats body =
  let oblSnapshot = perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value
  let oblSnapshotN = perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let errsBefore = listLen perRun.value.typeErrors.value
  let actualTy = inferClauses env [(pats, body)]
  let errsAfter = listLen perRun.value.typeErrors.value
  if errsAfter != errsBefore then ()
  else
    let errsPreUnify = listLen perRun.value.typeErrors.value
    let _ = setRef currentMethodMismatch (Some mname)
    let _ = unify expectedTy actualTy
    let _ = setRef currentMethodMismatch None
    -- mismatch reported ⇒ restore the pre-body obligation list (drop the body's
    -- own added `Num <wrong-type>` cascade, prepended at the front)
    if listLen perRun.value.typeErrors.value != errsPreUnify then restorePendingObls oblSnapshot oblSnapshotN else ()

-- Restore the obligation list (and its length counter) to a snapshot.
restorePendingObls : List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Int -> Unit
restorePendingObls obls n =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.pendingImplObligations obls
  setRef perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN n

-- ── Error-path B4: missing-impl dispatch-obligation check ──────────────────
-- Mirror lib/typecheck.ml's check_method_usages (the `No impl of … for …` reject,
-- :3845).  For each recorded interface-method occurrence, recover the types the
-- interface params resolved to at this site (dispatchMonosOf), and — ONLY when
-- they are all CONCRETE (a resolved head tycon, no remaining polymorphic var, so
-- a constrained-generic `Ord a =>` site whose receiver is still `a` is correctly
-- deferred to the caller) — verify the program declares a matching impl.  None →
-- `No impl of <iface> for <args>`.  An impl block (even one defaulting every
-- method to its interface default body) creates a head entry, so a default-method
-- call on a type WITH an impl is satisfied; a default-method call on a type with
-- NO impl is rejected — both matching the oracle.
-- #154 PR3: the KEYED impl universe consumed by the obligation checkers below.  Bundles
-- the three accumulator refs' contents (see obUnivConcreteRef): concrete-head impls
-- keyed "iface|tag", HEADLESS-receiver impls keyed by iface (matched against ANY
-- receiver), and iface→concrete-head-tag SET (for implCountForIfaceU).  The FLAT path
-- builds one from its whole `prog` via `buildImplUniverse` (O(N) once — the same cost
-- as the old `implDeclsWithReqs`+`implHeadsOf` it replaces); the Module path passes the
-- PERSISTENT accumulator (grown one module at a time in appendUniverseAccums).
public export data ImplUniverse =
  | ImplUniverse (OrdMap (List (List Ty, List Require))) (OrdMap (List (List Ty, List Require))) (OrdMap (OrdMap Unit))

emptyImplUniverse : ImplUniverse
emptyImplUniverse = ImplUniverse omEmpty omEmpty omEmpty

buildImplUniverse : List Decl -> ImplUniverse
buildImplUniverse prog =
  growImplUniverse (implDeclsWithReqs prog) emptyImplUniverse

-- fold each (iface, tys, reqs) triple into the universe, APPENDING to keep forward
-- declaration order within every bucket (matching the old whole-list first-match scan).
growImplUniverse : List (String, List Ty, List Require) -> ImplUniverse -> ImplUniverse
growImplUniverse [] univ = univ
growImplUniverse (t::rest) univ = growImplUniverse rest (insertUnivImpl univ t)

-- concrete receiver head (Some tag) → the "iface|tag" bucket + record the tag in the
-- iface's tag set; headless receiver (TyVar/function/empty head — None) → the iface's
-- headless bucket, which every lookup also consults (matchTyMono wildcard matches any).
insertUnivImpl : ImplUniverse -> (String, List Ty, List Require) -> ImplUniverse
insertUnivImpl (ImplUniverse conc hl tags) (iface, tys, reqs) = match univReceiverTag tys
  Some tag =>
    let k = "\{iface}|\{tag}"
    ImplUniverse
      (omInsert k (bucketOf k conc ++ [(tys, reqs)]) conc)
      hl
      (omInsert
        iface
        (omInsert tag () (fromOption omEmpty (omLookup iface tags)))
        tags)
  None => ImplUniverse conc (omInsert iface (bucketOf iface hl ++ [(tys, reqs)]) hl) tags

-- the receiver-position head tycon tag of an impl's head types (None for a headless
-- receiver or an empty head list) — the SAME tag `headTyconMono` computes for a
-- dispatch mono, so an impl and the args it matches land under the same key.
univReceiverTag : List Ty -> Option String
univReceiverTag (headTy::_) = headTyconTy headTy
univReceiverTag [] = None

-- ── PR3 keyed lookups (replace implMatches / implMatchesReceiver / findMatchingImplReqs /
-- implCountForIface).  Each first scans the concrete "iface|tag" bucket, then the iface's
-- headless bucket.  implMatchesU / implMatchesReceiverU are EXISTENCE tests, so the
-- concrete-then-headless order is byte-identical to the old flat scan.  findMatchingImplReqsU
-- is first-MATCH and DOES depend on that order — a deliberate soundness/conformance fix,
-- documented on it below. ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- does SOME impl of [iface] match the concrete dispatch monos [args]? (existence)
implMatchesU : ImplUniverse -> String -> List Mono -> Bool
implMatchesU _ _ [] = False
implMatchesU univ iface (a0::rest) = bucketArgsMatch (univConcreteBucket univ iface (headTyconMono a0)) (a0::rest)
  || bucketArgsMatch (univHeadless univ iface) (a0::rest)

bucketArgsMatch : List (List Ty, List Require) -> List Mono -> Bool
bucketArgsMatch [] _ = False
bucketArgsMatch ((tys, _)::rest) args = implHeadMatchesArgs tys args
  || bucketArgsMatch rest args

-- does some impl of [iface] have a RECEIVER (first head type) matching the single
-- dispatch mono [dm]?  (the call-obligation path carries only the receiver mono.)
implMatchesReceiverU : ImplUniverse -> String -> Mono -> Bool
implMatchesReceiverU univ iface dm = bucketRecvMatch (univConcreteBucket univ iface (headTyconMono dm)) dm
  || bucketRecvMatch (univHeadless univ iface) dm

bucketRecvMatch : List (List Ty, List Require) -> Mono -> Bool
bucketRecvMatch [] _ = False
bucketRecvMatch ((tys, _)::rest) dm = match tys
  recv::_ => match matchTyMono recv dm
    Some _ => True
    None => bucketRecvMatch rest dm
  [] => bucketRecvMatch rest dm

-- the impl of [iface] whose `requires` we must check at a dispatch to [args] (the
-- receiver-head mono [args[0]]), with its (subst, reqs) — the impl checkNestedReqs
-- then discharges.  The concrete RECEIVER-head bucket is consulted BEFORE the iface's
-- headless bucket, so a specific impl always outranks a `impl Foo a` catch-all.
--
-- ⚠️ Coherence ACCEPTS a specific-vs-general overlap (`impl Foo Int` + `impl Foo a`,
-- most-specific-wins), so TWO impls can match one concrete mono.  Selecting the WRONG
-- one's requires is a soundness hole: parametric `impl Foo a` (no reqs) first + concrete
-- `impl Foo Int requires Bar Int` (no `impl Bar`) → an ACCEPT then a runtime null-dict
-- crash, because `foo (3:Int)` dispatches to the MOST-SPECIFIC `impl Foo Int`.
--
-- #326: the within-bucket winner is now chosen by the SHARED min⊑ selector
-- `selectImplEntryByIface` over the all-impls `KeyBuckets` registry (shadowKeyTableRef —
-- the SAME registry, and the SAME selector, the dispatch ROUTER stamps with; PR #319
-- fixed the router, this makes the CHECKER agree).  It returns the most-specific impl's
-- OWN requires — [] for a no-requires specific `impl Foo (List Int)` sibling declared
-- ALONGSIDE a `impl Foo (List a) requires Show a`, both in the same `List` head bucket —
-- never the general sibling's.  The prior concrete-then-headless first-match ranked
-- concrete-vs-headless correctly but took DECLARATION-ORDER first-match WITHIN a shared
-- head bucket, so `Foo (List Int)` could inherit `List a`'s `Show a` requirement (a false
-- reject/accept).  A non-overlapping receiver head has exactly one match, which
-- pickMostSpecificEntry returns unchanged ⇒ byte-identical to the old first-match.
-- Headless impls carry no head tycon and so are absent from KeyBuckets — the headless
-- bucket fallback (still over the ImplUniverse) covers them.  Regression:
-- test/typecheck_error_fixtures/overlap_specific_req_wins.mdk (concrete-vs-headless) and
-- overlap_same_head_req_wins{,_swapped}.mdk (#326 within-bucket min⊑).
findMatchingImplReqsU : ImplUniverse -> String -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono), List Require)
findMatchingImplReqsU univ iface [] = firstReqMatch (univHeadless univ iface) []
findMatchingImplReqsU univ iface (a0::rest) = match concreteReqMatchByIface iface (a0::rest)
  Some r => Some r
  None => firstReqMatch (univHeadless univ iface) (a0::rest)

-- #326: the concrete-receiver-head requires match, routed through the shared min⊑
-- selector over the all-impls KeyBuckets registry (the router's `selectReqImpl` /
-- `argImplRequiresRoutesRecD` select the SAME entry the SAME way).  Bind the winning
-- impl's typaram vars by matching its FULL head types against [args] (multi-param subst
-- preserved), then hand back that impl's OWN requires.  A selected entry whose full head
-- fails to match [args] yields None, so findMatchingImplReqsU falls through to the
-- headless bucket, mirroring the old scan.
-- #609: select on [args] — the FULL head-mono vector this function already matches
-- the winner's `itys` against two lines down.  Selecting on the receiver alone while
-- substituting against the whole vector was the same split #609 names: the checker
-- saw the vector, the selector saw arg 0.  With one goal for both, a selected entry
-- can no longer fail the subst for a reason the selector could have seen.
concreteReqMatchByIface : String -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono), List Require)
concreteReqMatchByIface iface args = match selectImplEntryByIface perRun.value.shadowKeyTableRef.value iface args
  Some (KeyEntry _ _ _ _ _ itys reqs) =>
    map (sub => (sub, reqs)) (implHeadSubst itys args)
  None => None

firstReqMatch : List (List Ty, List Require) -> List Mono -> Option (List (String, Mono), List Require)
firstReqMatch [] _ = None
firstReqMatch ((tys, reqs)::rest) args = match implHeadSubst tys args
  Some sub => Some (sub, reqs)
  None => firstReqMatch rest args

-- the number of DISTINCT concrete-head impls of [iface] (headless impls contribute no
-- tag, exactly as the old implHeadTagsFromHeads skipped them).
implCountForIfaceU : ImplUniverse -> String -> Int
implCountForIfaceU (ImplUniverse _ _ tags) iface =
  omSize (fromOption omEmpty (omLookup iface tags))

-- the concrete "iface|tag" bucket (empty when the tag is None / no such impl).
univConcreteBucket : ImplUniverse -> String -> Option String -> List (List Ty, List Require)
univConcreteBucket (ImplUniverse conc _ _) iface (Some t) =
