  bucketOf "\{iface}|\{t}" conc
univConcreteBucket _ _ None = []

univHeadless : ImplUniverse -> String -> List (List Ty, List Require)
univHeadless (ImplUniverse _ hl _) iface = bucketOf iface hl

-- ── Error-path B4: missing-impl dispatch-obligation check ──────────────────
-- Mirror lib/typecheck.ml's check_method_usages (the `No impl of … for …` reject,
-- :3845).  For each recorded interface-method occurrence, recover the types the
-- interface params resolved to at this site (dispatchMonosOf), and — ONLY when
-- they are all CONCRETE (a resolved head tycon, no remaining polymorphic var, so
-- a constrained-generic `Ord a =>` site whose receiver is still `a` is correctly
-- deferred to the caller) — verify the program declares a matching impl.  None →
-- `No impl of <iface> for <args>`.
-- FLAT entry: builds the keyed universe from `prog`.  MODULE entry: checkImplObligationsU
-- with the persistent accumulated universe (no per-module rebuild — #154 PR3).
checkImplObligations : List Decl -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Unit
checkImplObligations prog obligations =
  checkImplObligationsU (buildImplUniverse prog) obligations

checkImplObligationsU : ImplUniverse -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Unit
checkImplObligationsU _ [] = ()
checkImplObligationsU univ ((iface, typarams, mty, occ, loc)::rest) =
  let _ = checkOneImplObligation univ iface typarams mty occ loc
  checkImplObligationsU univ rest

-- #11 soundness (native obligation hole): check each constrained-fn CALL site's
-- instantiated obligation.  When `f : C a => …` is applied (`g = f "hello"`),
-- inferDictAtFound recorded (iface, the instantiated constraint mono) here.  After
-- inference the mono is ground: a CONCRETE-head mono (`String`) with no matching impl
-- is an ERROR; a still-polymorphic head (`a`, the obligation propagated to a further-
-- out caller — `id x = x; y = id 5`) is DEFERRED.  Exactly the accept/reject decision
-- of checkOneImplObligation's tail, and the mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's
-- check_constraint_obligations (which checks fun_constraints instantiated at the call).
checkCallObligations : List Decl -> List (String, List Mono, Option Loc) -> Unit
checkCallObligations prog obligations =
  checkCallObligationsU (buildImplUniverse prog) [] obligations

-- [seen] dedups by (iface, EVERY argument's concrete-head-tycon): the SAME
-- unsatisfiable obligation can be recorded by more than one path (a use marked both
-- EVar and, on the emit path, EDictAt) or by repeated call sites, but the oracle
-- raises ONE error per missing impl.  The key spans the WHOLE argument vector — a
-- head-of-arg-0-only key conflated `Ix Int Bool` with `Ix Int Char` and reported
-- only the first (#607).
-- ORDER of errors follows the OBLIGATION list (unchanged), never the impl universe.
checkCallObligationsU : ImplUniverse -> List String -> List (String, List Mono, Option Loc) -> Unit
checkCallObligationsU _ _ [] = ()
checkCallObligationsU univ seen ((iface, occs, loc)::rest) =
  let key = joinWith "," (iface :: map (o => fromOption "" (headTyconMono o)) occs)
  if contains key seen then checkCallObligationsU univ seen rest
  else
    let _ = checkOneCallObligation univ iface occs loc
    checkCallObligationsU univ (key::seen) rest

-- [occs] is the predicate's whole argument vector.  At arity 1 the match test stays
-- implMatchesReceiverU (byte-identical to the pre-#607 behaviour: for a 1-param
-- interface the receiver IS the vector).  At arity >= 2 it is implMatchesU, which
-- matches the FULL head vector — the difference between "is there an impl of Ix whose
-- receiver is String" (yes-ish, vacuously) and "is there an impl of Ix for
-- (String, Bool)" (no).
checkOneCallObligation : ImplUniverse -> String -> List Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
checkOneCallObligation univ iface occs loc =
  let args = map normalize occs
  if isEmptyL args then ()
  else if iface == "Num" && anyListM numUnimplementableHead args then
    pushNoImplError iface loc args
  -- A function-typed constraint obligation is fully ground yet has no impl — report
  -- `No impl of <iface> for <a -> b>` (not the emit path's misleading "Ambiguous").
  else if anyListM monoIsFunction args && not (implMatchesArgsU univ iface args) then
    pushNoImplError iface loc args
  else if not (allConcreteHeads args) then
    checkUndeterminedObligations univ iface args loc
  else if implMatchesArgsU univ iface args then
    -- Recurse into a matched CONDITIONAL instance's `requires` (`impl Display (a, b)
    -- requires Display a, Display b`) so an unsatisfied element constraint surfaces
    -- (`main = (Red, 5)` → `No impl of Display for Color`) instead of the head-only
    -- match silently accepting (→ null element dict → SIGSEGV at runtime).
    checkNestedReqs univ iface args loc
  else
    pushNoImplError iface loc args

-- match test for a call obligation's argument vector: receiver-only at arity 1 (the
-- historical, deliberately lenient 1-param shape), full head-vector at arity >= 2.
implMatchesArgsU : ImplUniverse -> String -> List Mono -> Bool
implMatchesArgsU univ iface [a] = implMatchesReceiverU univ iface a
implMatchesArgsU univ iface args = implMatchesU univ iface args

-- an undetermined-var check per argument of the predicate (at arity 1 this is exactly
-- the old single call).
checkUndeterminedObligations : ImplUniverse -> String -> List Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
checkUndeterminedObligations _ _ [] _ = ()
checkUndeterminedObligations univ iface (a::rest) loc =
  let _ = checkUndeterminedObligation univ iface a loc
  checkUndeterminedObligations univ iface rest loc

-- WS-1c: a constrained call whose constraint var stays UNDETERMINED (pinned by
-- neither arg nor result, `roundtrip 14` where `roundtrip : Sub a => Int -> Int`).
-- If the var is a FORWARDED enclosing-fn dict (activeDictVarOf Some), it's resolved
-- by dict-passing — fine.  Otherwise it is genuinely free, and the route must pick
-- an impl with no type to guide it: ONE impl of [iface] → sole-impl default (no
-- error; routeUndeterminedTop stamps it concretely); TWO or more → genuinely
-- ambiguous → AmbiguousImpl (the deliberate divergence from the oracle's silent
-- first-wins).  Skip "Num" (its literal defaulting handles ambiguity) and ifaces
-- with no user impls (zero → existing no-impl path).  This runs on BOTH the check
-- path (checkModuleFullImpl) and the emit/eval path, so `check` rejects too.
checkUndeterminedObligation : ImplUniverse -> String -> Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
checkUndeterminedObligation univ iface occ loc
  | iface == "Num" = ()
  | isSome (activeDictVarOf occ) = ()
  | anyIn (monoUnboundIds occ) perRun.value.poisonedVars.value = ()  -- Chunk A: primary mismatch already explained this var
  | implCountForIfaceU univ iface >= 2 =
    pushTypeErrorOnceAt "T-AMBIGUOUS-INSTANCE" loc (ambiguousImplMsg iface)
  | otherwise = ()

-- PLAN.md #11: after HM + defaulting every source integer literal's type var is
-- ground (or a genuine `Num a` survivor).  Decide each literal's runtime rep
-- (3-way, mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's set_numlit_floats):
--   • ground Float ⇒ stamp the float cell + CLEAR the fromInt route (dref RNone) ⇒
--     dictPass emits `ELit (LFloat f)` (eval is value-tagged; eval_arith rejects
--     mixed int/float tags, so a Float literal must carry a real float).
--   • ground Int (incl. via ambiguous→Int defaulting) ⇒ clear both cells ⇒
--     `ELit (LInt n)`, no dict dispatch.
--   • NOT a primitive head (a still-polymorphic `Num a` — the soundness case, `1`
--     in `inc x = x + 1` — or an exotic user numeric type) ⇒ leave [dref]'s
--     resolveSites-stamped route in place ⇒ dictPass emits
--     `EApp (EMethodAt "fromInt" …) (ELit (LInt n))` so the literal dispatches
--     through the runtime Num dict (Int→identity, Float→intToFloat).  A dref left
--     RNone (route never filled, e.g. unreachable position) falls to Int below.
-- Reads numlitRefs (populated by inferNumLit during THIS run/module's inference).
setNumlitFloats : Unit -> Unit
setNumlitFloats _ = setNumlitFloatsGo perRun.value.numlitRefs.value

setNumlitFloatsGo : List (Mono, Ref (Option Float), Int, Ref Route) -> Unit
setNumlitFloatsGo [] = ()
setNumlitFloatsGo ((var, fref, n, dref)::rest) =
  let _ = match normalize var
    TCon "Float" =>
      let _ = setRef fref (Some (intToFloat n))
      setRef dref RNone
    TCon "Int" => setRef dref RNone
    _ => ()
  setNumlitFloatsGo rest

-- ── Multi-param-interface element grounding (Shape A / gap #44) ─────────────
-- A container LITERAL (`Set { 3,1,2 }` / `Map { 1 => "a" }`) desugars to a
-- return-position `fromEntries [..] :~ Set _a`.  The head-pin fixes the RESULT to
-- `Set _a`, and the list arg grounds the interface's ELEMENT param `e` (= Int),
-- but nothing ties the result's element var `_a` to `e` — HM does not propagate the
-- impl's `FromEntries (Set a) a` variable-sharing.  So `_a` stays free, and BOTH the
-- return-position `requires Ord a` route AND the enclosing `display`/`debug`'s
-- arg-position element dict drop their dictionaries (null dict → SIGSEGV on the build
-- path; eval recovers via runtime arg-tag fallback, which is why `medaka run` worked).
-- Fix: for each multi-param-interface method occurrence whose dispatch type heads a
-- UNIQUE impl, instantiate that impl's method type (interface typarams ↦ the impl's
-- own type args, freshened) and unify it with the occurrence mono.  This grounds `_a`
-- := Int THROUGH THE SHARED CELL, so every downstream site reading that cell (the
-- `display` arg-stamp) and the return-position route both see `Set Int`.  Runs after
-- inference, before the resolve* passes read the cells.  Single-param interfaces and
-- already-ground occurrences are no-ops.  Mirrors the intent of the oracle's
-- impl_head_subst keying off the FULL concrete_args (the interface's param_vars).
groundMultiParamObligations : List Decl -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> Unit
groundMultiParamObligations _ [] = ()
groundMultiParamObligations prog ((iface, typarams, mty, occ, _)::rest) =
  let _ = groundOneObligation prog iface typarams mty occ
  groundMultiParamObligations prog rest

groundOneObligation : List Decl -> String -> List String -> Ty -> Mono -> Unit
groundOneObligation prog iface typarams mty occ
  | listLen typarams <= 1 = ()
  | otherwise = match dispatchMonosOf typarams mty occ
    [] => ()
    dispMono::_ => match headTyconMono dispMono
      None => ()
      Some tag => match uniqueImplTysFor prog iface tag
        None => ()
        Some implTys =>
          let baseMap = zipL typarams implTys
          let implMethodTy = substTyVars baseMap mty
          let inst = fst (astTypeFreshTbl implMethodTy)
          unify occ inst
-- build impl method type: interface typaram ↦ impl ty, then freshen the
-- impl's own vars, then unify with the occurrence.

-- the type args of the UNIQUE impl of [iface] whose head tycon is [tag]; None when
-- absent or ambiguous (more than one), so an over-determined case is left untouched.
uniqueImplTysFor : List Decl -> String -> String -> Option (List Ty)
uniqueImplTysFor prog iface tag = match filterImplTysFor prog iface tag
  [tys] => Some tys
  _ => None

filterImplTysFor : List Decl -> String -> String -> List (List Ty)
filterImplTysFor prog iface tag = flatMap (implTysIfMatch iface tag) prog

implTysIfMatch : String -> String -> Decl -> List (List Ty)
implTysIfMatch iface tag (DAttrib _ d) = implTysIfMatch iface tag d
implTysIfMatch iface tag (DImpl { iface = ifn, tys, ... })
  | ifn == iface = match tys
    headTy::_ => match headTyconTy headTy
      Some t => if t == tag then [tys] else []
      None => []
    [] => []
  | otherwise = []
implTysIfMatch _ _ _ = []

-- substitute interface-typaram NAMES → impl type PATTERNS inside a declared Ty.
substTyVars : List (String, Ty) -> Ty -> Ty
substTyVars sub (TyVar n) = fromOption (TyVar n) (lookupAssoc n sub)
substTyVars _ (TyCon n _) = TyCon n None
substTyVars sub (TyApp a b) = TyApp (substTyVars sub a) (substTyVars sub b)
substTyVars sub (TyFun a b) = TyFun (substTyVars sub a) (substTyVars sub b)
substTyVars sub (TyTuple ts) = TyTuple (map (substTyVars sub) ts)
substTyVars sub (TyEffect es eff t) = TyEffect es eff (substTyVars sub t)
substTyVars sub (TyConstrained cs t) = TyConstrained cs (substTyVars sub t)

checkOneImplObligation : ImplUniverse -> String -> List String -> Ty -> Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
checkOneImplObligation univ iface typarams mty occ loc =
  let args = dispatchMonosOf typarams mty occ
  -- PLAN.md #11 soundness: under Num-polymorphic literals a literal's var can unify
  -- with a *function* type (`5 1` ⇒ the `5` var becomes `Int -> b`) or be forced
  -- into an abstract HKT position (`do x <- 42` ⇒ `Num (m a)` with an unbound head
  -- `m`).  Pre-#11 these failed at unification (the literal was concretely Int); now
  -- the obligation is non-concrete and would be deferred-then-DROPPED → a silent
  -- accept.  A function / unbound-headed application can never have a `Num` impl, so
  -- REJECT here even though inner vars are still free, restoring the structural error.
  -- Mirror of lib/typecheck.ml's check_method_usages Num guards.
  --
  -- No-prelude (`Num` unregistered): a numeric literal still records a Num obligation
  -- (inferNumLitBare) so value-level defaulting can ground it to Int, but the bare-HM
  -- oracle imposes NO `impl Num` constraint — so SKIP the reject here (there is no
  -- `impl Num Int` to match in the prelude-free program).  This keeps the no-prelude
  -- `typecheck_main`/tc_probe oracle parity: a grounded `Num Int` literal types Int
  -- without a spurious `No impl of Num for Int`.  The unimplementable-head reject
  -- (function/unbound `m`) stays even no-prelude — it is a structural unification
  -- error, not a missing-impl one.
  if iface == "Num" && anyListM numUnimplementableHead args then pushNoImplError iface loc args else if iface == "Eq" && anyListM eqUnimplementableHead args then pushNoImplError iface loc args else if iface == "Ord" && anyListM ordUnimplementableHead args then pushNoImplError iface loc args else if iface == "Num" && not (ifaceRegistered "Num") then () else if isEmptyL args then () else if anyListM monoIsFunction args && not (implMatchesU univ iface args) then pushNoImplError iface loc args else if not (allConcreteHeads args) then () else if implMatchesU univ iface args then checkNestedReqs univ iface args loc else reportNumOrNoImpl iface args loc

-- A function-typed dispatch mono is fully GROUND — its head is `->`, structurally
-- un-implementable for an ordinary interface (no `impl Display (a -> b)` exists).
-- So a function-typed obligation with no matching impl is a genuine `No impl of
-- <iface> for <a -> b>` (bug: `main = f` where `f : Int -> Int` used to fall
-- through to the emit path's route resolution and mis-report "Ambiguous instance"
-- — but a monomorphic function type is not ambiguous, it simply has no impl).
-- Reserved for the un-matched case so a real `impl Display (a -> b)` would satisfy.
monoIsFunction : Mono -> Bool
monoIsFunction m = match normalize m
  TFun _ _ _ => True
  _ => False

-- After the impl head for (iface, args) matched, a CONDITIONAL instance's
-- `requires` constraints must ALSO be satisfied (`impl Display (a, b) requires
-- Display a, Display b`).  Recover the impl's typaram bindings from the head match
-- and check each substituted requirement recursively — so `main = (Red, 5)` (Red
-- has no Display) surfaces `No impl of Display for Color` at the deepest failing
-- leaf, exactly like the bare `main = Red`, instead of the head-only match silently
-- accepting the unsatisfiable element constraint (→ null element dict → SIGSEGV).
-- A satisfied requirement (`Display Int`, `Display String`) recurses into ITS own
-- (empty) requires and accepts, so `(5, "hi")` / `[1,2,3]` still build.
checkNestedReqs : ImplUniverse -> String -> List Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
checkNestedReqs univ iface args loc =
  checkReqObligations univ loc (reqObligationsFor univ iface args)

-- Impl decls paired with their `requires` (implHeadsOf drops reqs; this keeps them).
implDeclsWithReqs : List Decl -> List (String, List Ty, List Require)
implDeclsWithReqs prog = flatMap implDeclWithReqs prog

implDeclWithReqs : Decl -> List (String, List Ty, List Require)
implDeclWithReqs (DAttrib _ d) = implDeclWithReqs d
implDeclWithReqs (DImpl { iface, tys, reqs, ... }) = [(iface, tys, reqs)]
implDeclWithReqs _ = []

-- The substituted requirement obligations of the impl of [iface] whose head
-- matches [args]: bind the impl's typaram vars from the head match, then apply that
-- binding to each `Require`'s type args.  No matching impl / no reqs → [].
-- #154 PR3: the FIRST-match impl lookup is now the keyed `findMatchingImplReqsU`
-- (bucket by iface|tag, headless fallback) instead of a flat scan over every impl.
reqObligationsFor : ImplUniverse -> String -> List Mono -> List (String, List Mono)
reqObligationsFor univ iface args = match findMatchingImplReqsU univ iface args
  None => []
  Some (subst, reqs) => map (reqToObligation subst) reqs

reqToObligation : List (String, Mono) -> Require -> (String, List Mono)
reqToObligation subst (Require ifaceR tysR) =
  (ifaceR, map (reqTyToMono subst) tysR)

-- Convert a `Require`'s declared type arg to a mono under the head-match subst.
-- Impl-typaram vars (the common case, `Display a`) resolve to their bound mono; an
-- unbound name gets a fresh var (defers, never a spurious concrete head).
reqTyToMono : List (String, Mono) -> Ty -> Mono
reqTyToMono sub (TyVar n) = fromOption (freshVar ()) (lookupAssoc n sub)
reqTyToMono _ (TyCon n _) = TCon n
reqTyToMono sub (TyApp a b) = TApp (reqTyToMono sub a) (reqTyToMono sub b)
reqTyToMono sub (TyFun a b) =
  TFun (reqTyToMono sub a) (openRow ()) (reqTyToMono sub b)
reqTyToMono sub (TyTuple ts) =
  tupleSpineOf (TCon (tupleHeadTagTc (listLen ts))) (map (reqTyToMono sub) ts)
reqTyToMono sub (TyEffect _ _ t) = reqTyToMono sub t
reqTyToMono sub (TyConstrained _ t) = reqTyToMono sub t

-- Check each substituted requirement with the SAME accept/reject logic as a
-- top-level obligation, recursing into a satisfied conditional impl's own reqs.
checkReqObligations : ImplUniverse -> Option Loc -> List (String, List Mono) -> Unit
checkReqObligations _ _ [] = ()
checkReqObligations univ loc ((ifaceR, monoArgs)::rest) =
  let _ = checkReqOne univ ifaceR monoArgs loc
  checkReqObligations univ loc rest

-- A function-typed requirement arg is fully GROUND yet structurally un-Ord/Eq/etc
-- (no `impl Ord (a -> b)` exists), so it must REJECT — but its head has no tycon,
-- so `allConcreteHeads` reads it as non-concrete.  Order the function-typed reject
-- BEFORE the concreteness defer (mirroring checkOneCallObligation): otherwise a
-- `requires Ord k` with k = (Int -> Int) — e.g. `Map { f => 1 }` selecting
-- `FromEntries (Map k v) (k, v) requires Ord k` — would DEFER into a silent accept,
-- and `check` would pass a program `run` rejects at runtime (P0-18).
checkReqOne : ImplUniverse -> String -> List Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
checkReqOne univ iface args loc
  | anyListM monoIsFunction args && not (implMatchesU univ iface args) =
    pushNoImplError iface loc args
  | not (allConcreteHeads args) = ()
  | implMatchesU univ iface args = checkNestedReqs univ iface args loc
  | otherwise = pushNoImplError iface loc args

-- a mono no `Ord` impl can ever match: a function type (`<` on functions).  Like
-- Eq (and unlike Num) an unbound spine head DEFERS — a downstream `Ord a =>`
-- constraint resolves it — so only functions are structurally un-Ord.
ordUnimplementableHead : Mono -> Bool
ordUnimplementableHead m = match normalize m
  TFun _ _ _ => True
  _ => False

-- a mono no `Eq` impl can ever match: a function type (`==` on functions).  Unlike
-- Num we do NOT reject an unbound spine head (`m a`) — for Eq that is a genuinely
-- polymorphic operand that a downstream `Eq a =>` constraint resolves, so it must
-- DEFER (allConcreteHeads), not reject.  Only functions are structurally un-Eq.
eqUnimplementableHead : Mono -> Bool
eqUnimplementableHead m = match normalize m
  TFun _ _ _ => True
  _ => False

-- a mono no `Num` impl can ever match: a function type, or an application whose
-- spine head is still an unbound tyvar (a literal forced into `m a` monad position)
numUnimplementableHead : Mono -> Bool
numUnimplementableHead m = match normalize m
  TFun _ _ _ => True
  TApp a b => unboundSpineHead (spineHeadMono (TApp a b))
  _ => False

-- True iff [m] normalizes to an unbound tyvar (the spine head of `m a`)
unboundSpineHead : Mono -> Bool
unboundSpineHead m = match normalize m
  TVar _ => True
  _ => False

spineHeadMono : Mono -> Mono
spineHeadMono t = match normalize t
  TApp f _ => spineHeadMono f
  other => other

noImplFoundMsg : String -> List Mono -> String
noImplFoundMsg iface args = "No impl of \{iface} for \{ppMonosShared args}"

-- Actionable-fix hint (F dimension) for a `No impl of <iface> for <ty>` reject:
-- only when [args] is a single user-declared data type (a `dataParamKindsRef`
-- hit) does "add deriving" / "write an impl" read as concrete, applicable
-- advice — a builtin (String/Int/Bool/…) can't take `deriving`, and a function
-- type is structurally un-implementable, so both stay hint-free (unchanged
-- message) rather than print misleading advice.
noImplHint : String -> List Mono -> Option String
noImplHint iface [arg] = match headTyconMono arg
  Some n => map (_ => noImplAdvice iface n) (lookupAssoc n perRun.value.dataParamKindsRef.value)
  None => None
noImplHint _ _ = None

-- Roadmap #18a: only a DERIVABLE interface may be suggested via `deriving`.  A
-- `deriving <iface>` for anything outside that set is silently DROPPED by desugar's
-- `deriveImpls` (its `deriveForData`/`deriveForNewtype` return None), so the old
-- unconditional "add 'deriving X'" sent the user down a dead end for every other
-- interface — including the `Semigroup` reject `a ++ b` now raises, and the
-- pre-existing user-interface case (`No impl of Printable for Foo`).  For those the
-- only real fix is to write the impl, so that is all we advise.
noImplAdvice : String -> String -> String
noImplAdvice iface n
  | derivableIface iface = "add 'deriving \{iface}' to the '\{n}' type, or write an 'impl \{iface} \{n}'."
  | otherwise = "write an 'impl \{iface} \{n}'."

-- The interfaces desugar.mdk can actually synthesize an impl for (deriveForData /
-- deriveForNewtype).  `Generic` is data-only; the other four cover both.
derivableIface : String -> Bool
derivableIface iface =
  contains iface ["Eq", "Ord", "Debug", "Display", "Generic"]

-- Append the hint (when applicable) to the plain `noImplFoundMsg`, mirroring
-- the did-you-mean append convention (base message ++ " — " ++ hint).
noImplFoundMsgHinted : String -> List Mono -> (String, Option String)
noImplFoundMsgHinted iface args =
  let base = noImplFoundMsg iface args
  match noImplHint iface args
    Some hint => ("\{base}; \{hint}", Some hint)
    None => (base, None)

-- Push a `No impl of …` type error, appending + recording the actionable-fix
-- hint (via `pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt`) when one applies; otherwise behaves
-- exactly like the previous plain `pushTypeErrorOnceAt "T-NO-IMPL" …` call.
pushNoImplError : String -> Option Loc -> List Mono -> Unit
pushNoImplError iface loc args = match firstTupleCallHint args
  -- P0-16: the un-implementable type IS a tuple-call residual (e.g. `println
  -- (add(1, 2))` → `No impl of Display for ((Int, Int) -> (Int, Int))`) — append
  -- the "call with spaces, not parentheses" hint + machine fix.
  Some (help, fix) =>
    pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt "T-NO-IMPL" loc "\{noImplFoundMsg iface args} — \{help}" help fix
  None =>
    let (msg, mhint) = noImplFoundMsgHinted iface args
    match mhint
      Some hint => pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt "T-NO-IMPL" loc msg hint None
      None => pushTypeErrorOnceAt "T-NO-IMPL" loc msg

-- Chunk D (Num mis-framing reframe): at the final no-impl branch, a `Num`
-- obligation forced to a fully-ground concrete non-Num head (`args` already passed
-- allConcreteHeads + failed implMatches) is reframed to a structural type mismatch
-- WHEN it traces to a bare numeric LITERAL (`numlitReframeLoc`: recorded at this
-- loc, and NOT an arithmetic operand).  An operator-sourced `Num` failure
-- (`x + 1`, `1 + x`) keeps `No impl of Num for T` — the `+` is the real culprit.
reportNumOrNoImpl : String -> List Mono -> Option Loc -> Unit
reportNumOrNoImpl iface args loc
  | iface == "Num" && numlitReframeLoc loc =
    let hint = numlitMismatchHint loc args
    pushTypeErrorHelpFixAt
      "T-TYPE-MISMATCH"
      loc
      (numlitMismatchMsg loc args ++ hint)
      hint
      None
  | otherwise = pushNoImplError iface loc args

-- True iff [loc] is a literal-sourced Num obligation eligible for reframe: some
-- literal was recorded at [loc] and that loc was NOT operator-tainted.
numlitReframeLoc : Option Loc -> Bool
numlitReframeLoc (Some l) = anyList (p => locEq (snd p) l) perRun.value.numlitVarLocs.value
  && not (anyList (ol => locEq ol l) perRun.value.numlitOpLocs.value)
numlitReframeLoc None = False

-- site-specific reframe wording (if / list / cons) when the context is known, else
-- a bare literal-vs-type mismatch.  `Int` is the numeric default the literal takes.
numlitMismatchMsg : Option Loc -> List Mono -> String
numlitMismatchMsg loc args = match numlitCtxKind loc
  Some "if" =>
    "'if' branches have different types: Int vs \{ppMonosShared args}"
  Some "list" =>
    "List elements have different types: Int vs \{ppMonosShared args}"
  Some "cons" => "Cons (::) type mismatch: head is Int but the list holds \{ppMonosShared args}"
  _ => "Type mismatch: Int literal vs \{ppMonosShared args}"

-- F-dimension actionable-fix hint appended to `numlitMismatchMsg` (2026-07-04):
-- mirrors its per-context wording, reusing the SAME two type strings the
-- message already interpolates (never recomputed).  No hint for the
-- context-less fallback (`_`) — there's no branch/element/head to name.
numlitMismatchHint : Option Loc -> List Mono -> String
numlitMismatchHint loc args = match numlitCtxKind loc
  Some "if" => ". Both branches must have the same type; change the else branch to Int, or the then branch to \{ppMonosShared args}."
  Some "list" => ". All elements must have the same type; convert the \{ppMonosShared args} element, or make the list hold \{ppMonosShared args}."
  Some "cons" => ". The head's type must match the list's elements; change the head to \{ppMonosShared args}, or use a list of Int."
  _ => ""

numlitCtxKind : Option Loc -> Option String
numlitCtxKind (Some l) = lookupCtxKind l perRun.value.numlitCtxTags.value
numlitCtxKind None = None

lookupCtxKind : Loc -> List (Loc, String) -> Option String
lookupCtxKind _ [] = None
lookupCtxKind l ((l2, k)::rest) =
  if locEq l l2 then
    Some k
  else
    lookupCtxKind l rest

locEq : Loc -> Loc -> Bool
locEq (Loc f1 a1 b1 c1 d1) (Loc f2 a2 b2 c2 d2) = f1 == f2
  && a1 == a2
  && b1 == b2
  && c1 == c2
  && d1 == d2

-- the dispatch (interface-param) monos at one occurrence: parallel-walk the
-- declared method type against the normalized occurrence mono; wherever the
-- declared type is a bare interface-param tyvar, read the corresponding sub-mono.
-- Returns one entry per interface param, in typaram order (deduplicated by name).
-- An empty result means the walk found no iface-param position (shadowed method /
-- shape mismatch) — the caller then skips, so a shadow never produces a false
-- reject.
dispatchMonosOf : List String -> Ty -> Mono -> List Mono
dispatchMonosOf typarams mty occ =
  let pairs = walkDispatch typarams (stripTyConstraints mty) occ
  collectByParam typarams pairs

-- strip leading `=>` constraints / effect rows so the walk aligns the method's
-- value type with the occurrence mono.
stripTyConstraints : Ty -> Ty
stripTyConstraints (TyConstrained _ t) = stripTyConstraints t
stripTyConstraints (TyEffect _ _ t) = stripTyConstraints t
stripTyConstraints t = t

-- parallel structural walk collecting (paramName, mono) wherever the declared
-- type is a bare iface-param tyvar.  Other positions recurse; a shape mismatch
-- (declared has structure the occurrence lacks) simply stops that branch.
walkDispatch : List String -> Ty -> Mono -> List (String, Mono)
walkDispatch typarams (TyVar n) m
  | contains n typarams = [(n, normalize m)]
  | otherwise = []
walkDispatch typarams (TyFun a b) m = match normalize m
  TFun ma _ mb => walkDispatch typarams (stripTyConstraints a) ma
    ++ walkDispatch typarams (stripTyConstraints b) mb
  _ => []
walkDispatch typarams (TyApp a b) m = match normalize m
  TApp ma mb => walkDispatch typarams a ma ++ walkDispatch typarams b mb
  _ => []
walkDispatch typarams (TyTuple ts) m = match tupleSpine m
  Some ms => flatMap (p => walkDispatch typarams (fst p) (snd p)) (zipL ts ms)
  _ => []
walkDispatch _ _ _ = []

-- one mono per interface param, in declaration order; if ANY param has no
-- recovered binding the whole obligation is abandoned (return [] → caller skips).
collectByParam : List String -> List (String, Mono) -> List Mono
collectByParam typarams pairs = match collectByParamGo typarams pairs
  None => []
  Some ms => ms

collectByParamGo : List String -> List (String, Mono) -> Option (List Mono)
collectByParamGo [] _ = Some []
collectByParamGo (p::rest) pairs = match lookupAssoc p pairs
  None => None
  Some m => map (m :: _) (collectByParamGo rest pairs)

-- every dispatch mono has a resolved concrete head tycon (no remaining bare
-- TVar at the head) — the oracle's is_concrete gate, restricted to the head (a
-- concrete head with free args, e.g. `Maybe a`, still uniquely picks the impl).
allConcreteHeads : List Mono -> Bool
allConcreteHeads [] = True
allConcreteHeads (m::rest) = isSome (headTyconMono m) && allConcreteHeads rest

-- #154 PR3: implMatches / implMatchesReceiver (flat whole-universe scans) are replaced
-- by the keyed implMatchesU / implMatchesReceiverU above.  implHeadMatchesArgs (used by
-- both the keyed matcher and matchTyMono callers) stays: a head-arg type pattern is
-- matched against the corresponding mono via matchTyMono (a TyVar in the head is a
-- wildcard; a parametric impl `impl Eq (List a)` thus matches `List Int`).
implHeadMatchesArgs : List Ty -> List Mono -> Bool
implHeadMatchesArgs tys args
  | listLen tys == listLen args = allHeadArgsMatch (zipL tys args)
  | otherwise = False

allHeadArgsMatch : List (Ty, Mono) -> Bool
allHeadArgsMatch [] = True
allHeadArgsMatch ((ty, m)::rest) = match matchTyMono ty m
  Some _ => allHeadArgsMatch rest
  None => False

-- render the dispatch monos space-separated under ONE shared naming context
-- (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's pp_monos), so distinct free vars don't all collapse
-- to "a".  Concrete heads (the only case that reaches an error) render as the
-- type name.
ppMonosShared : List Mono -> String
ppMonosShared ms =
  let ctx = Ref []
  let cnt = Ref 0
  joinWith " " (ppEachShared ctx cnt ms)

ppEachShared : Ref (List (Int, String)) -> Ref Int -> List Mono -> List String
ppEachShared _ _ [] = []
ppEachShared ctx cnt (m::rest) = ppGo ctx cnt 2 m :: ppEachShared ctx cnt rest

-- register, per slot, the default body's constraint var id → `$dict_<method>_<slot>`
-- in activeDictVars, so an in-body method ref unified to that var routes RDict.
-- Run AFTER body inference so the id is the SURVIVING (normalized) union-find root —
-- the same id the in-body site normalizes to (mirrors registerImplRequires).
registerMethodDictSlots : String -> Int -> List Int -> List (Int, Mono) -> Unit
registerMethodDictSlots _ _ [] _ = ()
registerMethodDictSlots mname slot (id::rest) subst =
  let _ = registerOneMethodDict mname slot id subst
  registerMethodDictSlots mname (slot + 1) rest subst

registerOneMethodDict : String -> Int -> Int -> List (Int, Mono) -> Unit
registerOneMethodDict mname slot id subst = match lookupAssocI id subst
  None => ()
  Some m => match normalize m
    TVar cell => setRef perRun.value.activeDictVars ((tyvarId cell, dictParamName mname slot)::perRun.value.activeDictVars.value)
    _ => ()

-- infer each impl-with-`requires`' return-position method bodies; the interface
-- declarations live in [allProg] (method types + typarams).
inferImplBodies : TcEnv -> List Decl -> List String -> List Decl -> Unit
inferImplBodies _ _ _ [] = ()
inferImplBodies env allProg rpNames (d::rest) =
  let _ = inferOneImpl env allProg rpNames d
  inferImplBodies env allProg rpNames rest

inferOneImpl : TcEnv -> List Decl -> List String -> Decl -> Unit
inferOneImpl env allProg rpNames (DAttrib _ d) =
  inferOneImpl env allProg rpNames d
inferOneImpl env allProg rpNames (DImpl { iface, tys, reqs, methods, ... }) = match reqs
  [] => inferPlainImpl env allProg iface tys methods
  _ => inferRequiresImpl env allProg rpNames iface tys reqs methods
inferOneImpl _ _ _ _ = ()

-- D3a: a NON-`requires` impl body, inferred on every path (single elaboration mode)
-- so its ARG-position method occurrences (e.g. `display` of a String field inside a
-- `Display`/`Debug` impl — the 19 `display@String` sites) reach inferMethodAt and
-- get stamped.  Mirrors inferRequiresImpl's head-mono substitution but with no
-- requires to register (an unconstrained impl threads no element dict).
inferPlainImpl : TcEnv -> List Decl -> String -> List Ty -> List ImplMethod -> Unit
inferPlainImpl env allProg iface tys methods =
  let implTvMap = freshTvMap (dedup (flatMap tyVarNames tys))
  let headMonos = map (fromAstType implTvMap) tys
  inferImplMethods env allProg iface implTvMap headMonos methods

-- one impl `impl Iface (List a) requires Iface a where …`: build ONE tyvar map
-- shared between the head (`List a`) and the requires (`Iface a`) so the body's
-- inner method ref unifies its discriminating var to the head's `a`; infer each
-- method body against the method type instantiated at the head; THEN register the
-- requires' post-unification ids in activeDictVars keyed to `$dict_<method>_<slot>`.
inferRequiresImpl : TcEnv -> List Decl -> List String -> String -> List Ty -> List Require -> List ImplMethod -> Unit
inferRequiresImpl env allProg rpNames iface tys reqs methods =
  let implTvMap = freshTvMap (dedup (flatMap tyVarNames tys))
  let headMonos = map (fromAstType implTvMap) tys
  let _ = inferImplMethods env allProg iface implTvMap headMonos methods
  registerImplRequires implTvMap rpNames reqs methods

inferImplMethods : TcEnv -> List Decl -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> List Mono -> List ImplMethod -> Unit
inferImplMethods _ _ _ _ _ [] = ()
inferImplMethods env allProg iface implTvMap headMonos (m::rest) =
  let _ = inferImplMethod env allProg iface implTvMap headMonos m
  inferImplMethods env allProg iface implTvMap headMonos rest

inferImplMethod : TcEnv -> List Decl -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> List Mono -> ImplMethod -> Unit
inferImplMethod env allProg iface implTvMap headMonos (ImplMethod mname pats body) = match ifaceMethodTy allProg iface mname
  None => ()
  Some (ifaceParams, mty) =>
    -- currentFn = the method name (NOT "") so arg-/return-position sites in THIS
    -- body record `encl = mname`.  registerImplRequires keys every method of an impl
    -- under the SAME impl tyvar id (`impl Ord (List a)` registers `a` once per
    -- compare/lt/min/max/…), so the global first-match route resolution returns the
    -- LAST-registered method's `$dict_<m>_<slot>` for ALL bodies — corrupting e.g.
    -- compare's element dispatch to `$dict_max_0` (unbound in compare's scope).  With
    -- encl = mname, activeDictVarOfEncl/argImplDictRoutesForEncl prefer THIS method's
    -- own `$dict_<mname>_<slot>`.  Pre-specialization each Ord impl defined only
    -- compare so there was no collision; universal default specialization exposes it.
    let _ = setRef perRun.value.currentFn mname
    let _ = setRef perRun.value.currentImplBody True
    -- #518: window this body's newly-added obligations, exactly as processLetGroup /
    -- checkProgramSeeded do for a let-group, so the Num defaulting below is scoped to
    -- THIS method and can never touch an already-generalized outer var.
    let oblN0 = perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value
    -- build the method type's tv map explicitly (instead of expectedMethodType,
    -- which hides it) so the impl method's OWN method-level constraint vars
    -- (`Thenable m => …`) can be registered into activeDictVars below.
    let baseMap = zipL ifaceParams headMonos
    let extraMap = freshTvMap (removeAllS ifaceParams (dedup (tyVarNames mty)))
    let expected = fromAstType (baseMap ++ extraMap) mty
    let actual = inferClauses env [(pats, body)]
    let _ = unify expected actual
    -- #518 (S0, silent wrongness): PLAN.md #11 Num defaulting NEVER RAN over an impl
    -- method body — this path is not a let-group, so nothing grounded its ambiguous
    -- Num vars.  A literal consumed entirely inside the body (`t _ = s [1, 2]`) left
    -- its element var UNBOUND, so the receiver the route resolver saw was `List _a`,
    -- not `List Int` — and `List _a` matches only the GENERAL `impl S (List a)`, never
    -- the specific `impl S (List Int)`.  The min⊑ selector was never at fault: with one
    -- match there is nothing to select between.  It resolved to the general impl and
    -- routed its `requires S a` to RNone (a null dict) — hence `build` silently printing
    -- the general instance's answer and `run` panicking E-NOT-A-FUNCTION.
    -- The same expression at TOP LEVEL was always correct precisely because
    -- checkProgramSeeded's group defaulting grounded the var to Int first.
    --
    -- MUST run AFTER `unify expected actual` (so [expected] reflects the body's
    -- aliasing) and defaults ONLY BODY-LOCAL vars — see defaultBodyLocalNum, which
    -- explains at length why defaultGroupNum is the WRONG primitive HERE even though
    -- it is what processLetGroup/processSCC use: its arg-position-only exclusion filter
    -- leaves a method-level constraint var in RETURN position (`lift : Num b => a ->
    -- Box b`) unprotected, and at an impl method body that var is dict-supplied by the
    -- CALLER.  Using defaultGroupNum here measurably traded #518's S0 for a new one.
    --
    -- NOT paired with registerAmbiguousConstraints, unlike processLetGroup/processSCC:
    -- that helper reads `currentLevel + 1` and documents itself as running POST-
    -- exitLevel, but this function has no enterLevel/exitLevel at all, so the level it
    -- would compute here is meaningless.  It is also orthogonal to #518 (oblDispatchMonos
    -- returns [] for Num).  Tracked as #564.
    let addedObls = takeFirst (perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value
    let _ = defaultBodyLocalNum addedObls expected
    -- Eval-path method-dict threading (parity with inferDefaultMethod's
    -- registerMethodDictSlots): an impl method with its OWN method-level constraint
    -- (`wrap : Thenable m => … -> m (List b)`) whose body returns a constraint-var
    -- method (`pure []`) needs that inner site routed RDictFwd $dict_<mname>_<slot>
    -- — the leading method-dict param implDictPassMethods prepends — so eval narrows
    -- it by the dict supplied at the call site (resolveMethodDicts) instead of
    -- falling to arg-tag dispatch and panicking `no matching impl for dispatch`.
    -- The EMIT path already worked because the emitter restamps impl-body
    -- return-position methods to the dict param directly; the eval path relies on
    -- the route, so register the var → dict-param mapping here (AFTER body inference,
    -- so each var is its surviving union-find root, mirroring registerMethodDictSlots).
    let _ = registerImplMethodDicts mname ifaceParams mty extraMap
    let _ = setRef perRun.value.currentImplBody False
    setRef perRun.value.currentFn ""
-- G4: mark binop sites recorded while inferring this impl body so resolveBinopSite
-- routes their element dict (RDict) — and ONLY theirs, not top-level operators.

-- Register an impl method's OWN method-level constraints (`Thenable m => …`) into
-- activeDictVars, one slot per constraint, so a return-position method in the impl
-- body whose result type is the constraint var routes RDictFwd $dict_<mname>_<slot>.
-- [tvMap] maps the method type's non-interface-param tyvar names → the fresh vars
-- expected/the body unified against; normalize each to its surviving root before
-- registering.  Slot numbering matches methodConstraintSlotIds (same flatMap order),
-- so it lines up with implDictPassMethods' prepended params and the call-site
-- methodRoutes resolveMethodDicts fills.
registerImplMethodDicts : String -> List String -> Ty -> List (String, Mono) -> Unit
registerImplMethodDicts mname ifaceParams mty tvMap =
  registerImplMethodDictsGo
    mname
    0
    (methodLevelConstraintVarNames ifaceParams mty)
    tvMap

registerImplMethodDictsGo : String -> Int -> List String -> List (String, Mono) -> Unit
registerImplMethodDictsGo _ _ [] _ = ()
registerImplMethodDictsGo mname slot (vname::rest) tvMap =
  let _ = registerImplMethodDictOne mname slot vname tvMap
  registerImplMethodDictsGo mname (slot + 1) rest tvMap

registerImplMethodDictOne : String -> Int -> String -> List (String, Mono) -> Unit
registerImplMethodDictOne mname slot vname tvMap = match lookupAssoc vname tvMap
  None => ()
  Some m => match normalize m
    TVar cell => setRef perRun.value.activeDictVars ((tyvarId cell, dictParamName mname slot)::perRun.value.activeDictVars.value)
    _ => ()

-- the non-interface-param dispatch var name of each method-level constraint, in
-- declaration order — the name-returning mirror of methodConstraintSlotIds (which
-- returns the ids).  One name per constraint slot; a constraint over only interface
-- params contributes nothing (it dispatches via the impl, not a dict).
methodLevelConstraintVarNames : List String -> Ty -> List String
methodLevelConstraintVarNames typarams (TyConstrained cs t) = flatMap (constraintFirstNonParam typarams) cs
  ++ methodLevelConstraintVarNames typarams t
methodLevelConstraintVarNames typarams (TyEffect _ _ t) =
  methodLevelConstraintVarNames typarams t
methodLevelConstraintVarNames _ _ = []

constraintFirstNonParam : List String -> Constraint -> List String
constraintFirstNonParam typarams c = match constraintNonParamVars typarams c
  [] => []
  n::_ => [n]

removeAllS : List String -> List String -> List String
removeAllS _ [] = []
removeAllS drop (x::xs)
  | contains x drop = removeAllS drop xs
  | otherwise = x :: removeAllS drop xs

-- the (interface typarams, declared method type) of method [mname] in interface
-- [iface], searched across [allProg]'s interface decls
ifaceMethodTy : List Decl -> String -> String -> Option (List String, Ty)
ifaceMethodTy [] _ _ = None
ifaceMethodTy ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) iface mname = match ifaceMethodTy [d] iface mname
  Some r => Some r
  None => ifaceMethodTy rest iface mname
ifaceMethodTy ((DInterface { name, typarams, methods, ... })::rest) iface mname
  | name == iface = match methodTyOf mname methods
    Some ty => Some (typarams, ty)
    None => ifaceMethodTy rest iface mname
  | otherwise = ifaceMethodTy rest iface mname
ifaceMethodTy (_::rest) iface mname = ifaceMethodTy rest iface mname

methodTyOf : String -> List IfaceMethod -> Option Ty
methodTyOf _ [] = None
methodTyOf mname ((IfaceMethod n ty _)::rest)
  | n == mname = Some ty
  | otherwise = methodTyOf mname rest

-- AFTER body inference: for each method of the impl that dispatches the element
-- (return-position OR, on the emit path, argument-position), register the requires'
-- surviving (normalized) tyvar ids → its `$dict_<method>_<slot>` param, so the
-- inner ref's pendingSite/pendingArgStamp (also normalizing to that id) routes
-- RDict.  D3b-1: arg-position methods (`eq`/`compare`/`debug`/… on the element)
-- register whenever argDispatchOf finds a dispatch index (single elaboration mode;
-- return-position-only methods are unaffected).  (The element tyvar is shared
-- across an impl's methods, so per-method dict names only disambiguate for
-- single-method interfaces — exactly the D3b-1 targets Eq/Ord/Debug/Display/Hashable.)
registerImplRequires : List (String, Mono) -> List String -> List Require -> List ImplMethod -> Unit
registerImplRequires _ _ _ [] = ()
registerImplRequires implTvMap rpNames reqs ((ImplMethod mname _ _)::rest)
  | registersElementDict mname rpNames =
    let _ = registerReqSlots mname implTvMap (methodDictArityOf mname) reqs
    registerImplRequires implTvMap rpNames reqs rest
  | otherwise = registerImplRequires implTvMap rpNames reqs rest

-- a method registers an element dict if it is return-position (rpNames) or — on
-- the emit path only — an arg-position dispatch method (argDispatchOf is Some).
registersElementDict : String -> List String -> Bool
registersElementDict mname rpNames = contains mname rpNames
  || isSome (argDispatchOf mname)

registerReqSlots : String -> List (String, Mono) -> Int -> List Require -> Unit
registerReqSlots _ _ _ [] = ()
registerReqSlots mname implTvMap slot ((Require iface rargs)::rest) =
  let ids = flatMap (a => monoTyvarIds (fromAstType implTvMap a)) rargs
  let _ = registerEachActive ids (dictParamName mname slot)
  let _ = registerPredGiven iface (map (a => fromAstType implTvMap a) rargs) (dictParamName mname slot)
  registerReqSlots mname implTvMap (slot + 1) rest

-- #412: the PREDICATE-keyed half of the given registry.  `activeDictVars` above is keyed
-- by TYVAR ID, which cannot express a STRUCTURED given: `requires S (List a)` registers
-- only `a`'s id (monoTyvarIds descends to the leaves), and the goal `S (List a)` looks up
-- through `activeDictVarOf`, which recurses to the SPINE HEAD (`TCon "List"`) and finds no
-- tyvar — so `assum` was unreachable by construction and `entail` fell through to
-- `entailInst`, statically committing the body to the GENERAL instance and discarding the
-- most-specific dict the construction site had correctly built and passed.  DICT-SEMANTICS
-- §3 (assum before inst) + :155-166 (sub-evidence is never pre-baked at the declaration).
-- Keyed by (iface, subject mono) so it is neither iface-blind nor structure-blind, and
-- matched by `monoSameGiven` — structural, on rigid-tyvar IDENTITY, never unification.
-- Only single-parameter ifaces get a subject (multi-param `requires` registers nothing —
-- the tyvar-id path is unchanged for them).
--
-- §6.1.3's rigid commit is preserved TWICE OVER, and the guard below is the load-bearing
-- one: `requires S a` is a bare-tyvar given, so it never reaches this registry at all, and
-- the body's goal `S (List a)` finds no assumption ⇒ `inst` fires on the rigid goal ⇒ the
-- general instance ⇒ 2, unchanged.  Even had it been registered, `monoSameGiven` compares
-- structurally and would not match subject `a` against goal `List a` — only unification
-- would, and this is deliberately not unification.  Pinned by
-- test/build_diff_fixtures/impl_requires_structured_rigid_control.mdk.
--
-- ⚠️ STRUCTURED subjects ONLY (`monoIsBareTyvar` guard) — a MINIMALITY guard, kept because
-- the bare case buys nothing and costs surface, not because a gate caught it.  A bare-tyvar
-- given (`requires S a`) is already answered by `activeDictVars`, which `entailAssum`
-- consults FIRST, so registering it here could only ever produce a second answer to an
-- already-answered goal.  It would also opt that goal into this registry's one sharp edge:
-- `monoSameGiven` compares TVar to TVar by ID, and ids here are not unique forever — see
-- the G4 note at `currentImplBody` ("an operand could normalize to an unsolved tyvar whose
-- id collides with a stale activeDictVars entry left by a previously-inferred impl"), which
-- `activeDictVarOf` is scope-gated against by the `inImpl` flag and this lookup is not.
-- A STRUCTURED subject is matched head-first down a TCon spine, so it answers only a goal
-- of the same shape at the same interface, and the id comparison is reached only after that
-- shape already agrees.
registerPredGiven : String -> List Mono -> String -> Unit
registerPredGiven iface [subject] dname
  | monoIsBareTyvar subject = ()
  | otherwise = setRef perRun.value.activeDictPreds ((iface, subject, dname)::perRun.value.activeDictPreds.value)
registerPredGiven _ _ _ = ()

-- True iff [m] normalizes to a bare type VARIABLE (the given shape `activeDictVars`
-- already covers). `TApp`/`TCon`/`TFun` are the STRUCTURED shapes it cannot see.
monoIsBareTyvar : Mono -> Bool
monoIsBareTyvar m = match normalize m
  TVar _ => True
  _ => False

-- structural equality of two monos that may contain RIGID tyvars, compared by tyvar
-- IDENTITY (`monoSameShape` is documented var-free and answers False on any TVar, so it
-- cannot serve here).  Deliberately NOT unification: a given must match a goal only when
-- it IS that goal, never by binding the given's variables.
monoSameGiven : Mono -> Mono -> Bool
monoSameGiven a b = match normalize a
  TVar c1 => match normalize b
    TVar c2 => tyvarId c1 == tyvarId c2
    _ => False
  TCon x => match normalize b
    TCon y => x == y
    _ => False
  TApp a1 b1 => match normalize b
    TApp a2 b2 => monoSameGiven a1 a2 && monoSameGiven b1 b2
    _ => False
  TFun a1 _ b1 => match normalize b
    TFun a2 _ b2 => monoSameGiven a1 a2 && monoSameGiven b1 b2
    _ => False
  _ => False

-- assum lookup over the predicate-keyed givens: the in-scope dict param holding evidence
-- for `iface subject`.  PREFERS the entry owned by the enclosing method [encl] (`$dict_
-- <encl>_<slot>`) for exactly the reason `activeDictVarForEncl` does: registerImplRequires
-- registers every method of an impl against the SAME given, so a global first-match would
-- return whichever method registered LAST — a dict param out of scope in this body.
activeDictPredOf : String -> Mono -> String -> Option String
activeDictPredOf iface subject encl =
  let preds = perRun.value.activeDictPreds.value
  match firstPredForEncl iface subject "$dict_\{encl}_" preds
    Some dname => Some dname
    None => firstPredForEncl iface subject "" preds

firstPredForEncl : String -> Mono -> String -> List (String, Mono, String) -> Option String
firstPredForEncl _ _ _ [] = None
firstPredForEncl iface subject pfx ((eiface, esubject, ename)::rest)
  | eiface == iface && startsWith pfx ename && monoSameGiven esubject subject =
    Some ename
  | otherwise = firstPredForEncl iface subject pfx rest

-- the interface a method NAME belongs to, for the predicate-keyed assum lookup.  A
-- non-method [name] (a top-level constrained call routed through EKNestedTop) is absent
-- from methodIfaceParamsRef → None → no predicate route, tyvar-keyed behaviour unchanged.
ifaceOfMethodName : String -> Option String
ifaceOfMethodName name = map
  ((iface, _, _) => iface)
  (omLookup name perRun.value.methodIfaceParamsRef.value)

registerEachActive : List Int -> String -> Unit
registerEachActive [] _ = ()
registerEachActive (id::rest) dname =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.activeDictVars ((id, dname)::perRun.value.activeDictVars.value)
  registerEachActive rest dname

-- the tyvar ids in a (normalized) mono — single var for a bare constraint arg
monoTyvarIds : Mono -> List Int
monoTyvarIds m = match normalize m
  TVar cell => [tyvarId cell]
  TApp a b => monoTyvarIds a ++ monoTyvarIds b
  TFun a _ b => monoTyvarIds a ++ monoTyvarIds b
  _ => []

-- extern declarations become top-level schemes (like interface methods)
externSchemes : List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
externSchemes [] = []
externSchemes ((DExtern _ n ty)::rest) =
  (n, sigToScheme ty) :: externSchemes rest
externSchemes (_::rest) = externSchemes rest

-- interface method schemes: each method's declared type, free tyvars (incl. the
-- interface param) quantified — the bare scheme the === TYPES === output shows
-- (the typeclass constraint itself is never rendered).  Impl bodies add no
-- top-level bindings, so DImpl needs no output here.
ifaceMethodSchemes : List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
ifaceMethodSchemes [] = []
ifaceMethodSchemes (d::rest) = declIfaceMethods d ++ ifaceMethodSchemes rest

declIfaceMethods : Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
declIfaceMethods (DInterface { typarams, methods, ... }) =
  methodSchemes typarams methods
declIfaceMethods _ = []

-- Error-path B4: register each interface method → (iface, typarams, declared Ty)
-- so checkImplObligations can recover its dispatch (iface-param) types at a call
-- site.  Built from the SAME prog the impl lookup uses, so an interface absent
-- from prog (e.g. a prelude method in the no-prelude path) is never checked.
registerMethodIfaceParamsAll : List Decl -> Unit
registerMethodIfaceParamsAll [] = ()
registerMethodIfaceParamsAll (d::rest) =
  let _ = registerMethodIfaceParamsDecl d
  registerMethodIfaceParamsAll rest

registerMethodIfaceParamsDecl : Decl -> Unit
registerMethodIfaceParamsDecl (DAttrib _ d) = registerMethodIfaceParamsDecl d
registerMethodIfaceParamsDecl (DInterface { name, typarams, methods, ... }) =
  registerMethodIfaceParamsMethods name typarams methods
registerMethodIfaceParamsDecl _ = ()

registerMethodIfaceParamsMethods : String -> List String -> List IfaceMethod -> Unit
registerMethodIfaceParamsMethods _ _ [] = ()
registerMethodIfaceParamsMethods iface typarams ((IfaceMethod mname mty _)::rest) =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.methodIfaceParamsRef (omInsert mname (iface, typarams, mty) perRun.value.methodIfaceParamsRef.value)
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.registeredIfacesRef (omInsert iface () perRun.value.registeredIfacesRef.value)
  registerMethodIfaceParamsMethods iface typarams rest

-- build each method's scheme AND register its method-level constraints (foldMap's
-- `Monoid m`) into methodConstraintsRef, keyed by the scheme's own quantified ids
-- (from the same tvs map), so a call-site instantiation can route the dict.
methodSchemes : List String -> List IfaceMethod -> List (String, Scheme)
methodSchemes _ [] = []
methodSchemes typarams ((IfaceMethod mname mty _)::rest) =
  let st = sigToSchemeTvs mty
  let _ = registerMethodConstraints typarams mname mty (snd st) (schemeIds (fst st))
  (mname, fst st) :: methodSchemes typarams rest

-- like methodSchemes but WITHOUT the registerMethodConstraints side effect — for
-- callers (inferOneIfaceDefaults) that only need the schemes to look up a method's
-- own type and must not double-register methodConstraintsRef entries (those are
-- registered once during ifaceMethodSchemes at env setup).
methodSchemesPure : List IfaceMethod -> List (String, Scheme)
methodSchemesPure [] = []
methodSchemesPure ((IfaceMethod mname mty _)::rest) =
  (mname, sigToScheme mty) :: methodSchemesPure rest

-- the method-level-constraint slot ids of [mname]: one id per `=>` constraint over
-- a non-interface-param tyvar, looked up in the scheme's tvs map.  Registered only
-- when non-empty (so methodConstraintsRef holds only genuinely-constrained methods).
registerMethodConstraints : List String -> String -> Ty -> List (String, Mono) -> List Int -> Unit
registerMethodConstraints typarams mname mty tvs fullIds =
  let ids = methodConstraintSlotIds typarams mty tvs
  if isEmptyL ids || hasAssocSL2 mname perRun.value.methodConstraintsRef.value then ()
  else
    let _ = setRef perRun.value.methodConstraintsRef ((mname, ids)::perRun.value.methodConstraintsRef.value)
    setRef
      perRun.value.methodConstraintPositionsRef
      ((mname, map (id => indexOfInt id fullIds) ids)::perRun.value.methodConstraintPositionsRef.value)

-- index of [x] in [xs] (0-based; -1 if absent — absent shouldn't happen, slot ids
-- are always quantified ids of the same scheme).
indexOfInt : Int -> List Int -> Int
indexOfInt x xs = indexOfIntGo x xs 0
indexOfIntGo : Int -> List Int -> Int -> Int
indexOfIntGo _ [] _ = 0 - 1
indexOfIntGo x (y::ys) i = if x == y then i else indexOfIntGo x ys (i + 1)
-- DRIVER-COLLAPSE Phase 2: skip when [mname] is ALREADY registered in THIS pass.
-- The ids must be the SAME quantified ids the call-site instantiation maps through
-- its subst (see methodConstraintsRef doc).  The module path's checkModuleFullImpl
-- can call ifaceMethodSchemes more than once per pass (env setup + impl/default body
-- inference re-resolving schemes), and a SECOND registration prepends DIFFERENT
-- freshly-instantiated ids → lookupAssocSL2 (first-match) returns the stale ids →
-- constraintMonosOf drops them → empty method-dict route (method_constraint_impl_offset:
-- methodN=0).  Keeping the FIRST (env-setup) registration — whose scheme is the one
-- lookupVar returns at call sites — restores the flat path's single-registration
-- invariant.  resetState clears methodConstraintsRef between modules, so this only
-- de-dups WITHIN a pass.

hasAssocSL2 : String -> List (String, List Int) -> Bool
hasAssocSL2 n xs = match lookupAssocSL2 n xs
  Some _ => True
  None => False

methodConstraintSlotIds : List String -> Ty -> List (String, Mono) -> List Int
methodConstraintSlotIds typarams (TyConstrained cs t) tvs = flatMap (constraintSlotId typarams tvs) cs
  ++ methodConstraintSlotIds typarams t tvs
methodConstraintSlotIds typarams (TyEffect _ _ t) tvs =
  methodConstraintSlotIds typarams t tvs
methodConstraintSlotIds _ _ _ = []

-- one slot per method-level constraint: the id of its first non-interface-param
-- tyvar (the dispatch var the supplied dict narrows by).  A constraint over only
-- interface params contributes no slot (it dispatches via the impl, not a dict).
constraintSlotId : List String -> List (String, Mono) -> Constraint -> List Int
constraintSlotId typarams tvs c = match constraintNonParamVars typarams c
  [] => []
  n::_ => match lookupAssoc n tvs
    Some m => [monoTyvarId m]
    None => []

sigsOf : List Decl -> List (String, Ty)
sigsOf [] = []
sigsOf ((DTypeSig _ n ty)::rest) = (n, ty) :: sigsOf rest
sigsOf (_::rest) = sigsOf rest

-- all free variable references in an expr (for the dependency graph); over-
-- approximate (includes bound vars), filtered to top-level group names by caller
allEVars : Expr -> List String
allEVars (EVar x) = [x]
allEVars (EMethodAt n _ _ _) = [n]
allEVars (EDictAt n _) = [n]
allEVars (ELit _) = []
allEVars (EApp f x) = allEVars f ++ allEVars x
allEVars (ELam _ b) = allEVars b
allEVars (ELet _ _ _ e1 e2) = allEVars e1 ++ allEVars e2
allEVars (ELetGroup binds body) = flatMap letBindEVars binds ++ allEVars body
allEVars (EMatch e arms) = allEVars e ++ flatMap armEVars arms
allEVars (EIf c t e) = allEVars c ++ allEVars t ++ allEVars e
allEVars (EBinOp _ l r _) = allEVars l ++ allEVars r
allEVars (EUnOp _ e _) = allEVars e
allEVars (EInfix op l r) = op :: allEVars l ++ allEVars r
allEVars (EFieldAccess e _ _) = allEVars e
allEVars (ETuple es) = flatMap allEVars es
allEVars (EListLit es) = flatMap allEVars es
allEVars (EArrayLit es) = flatMap allEVars es
allEVars (ERangeList a b _) = allEVars a ++ allEVars b
allEVars (ERangeArray a b _) = allEVars a ++ allEVars b
allEVars (ESlice e a b _ _) = allEVars e ++ allEVars a ++ allEVars b
allEVars (EIndex e i _) = allEVars e ++ allEVars i
allEVars (EAnnot e _) = allEVars e
allEVars (EBlock stmts) = flatMap doStmtEVars stmts
allEVars (ERecordCreate _ fs) = flatMap fieldAssignEVars fs
allEVars (ERecordUpdate e fs _) = allEVars e ++ flatMap fieldAssignEVars fs
allEVars (EVariantUpdate _ e fs) = allEVars e ++ flatMap fieldAssignEVars fs
allEVars (ELoc _ e) = allEVars e
allEVars (EDoOrigin _ e) = allEVars e
allEVars _ = []

letBindEVars : LetBind -> List String
letBindEVars (LetBind _ clauses) = flatMap funClauseEVars clauses

funClauseEVars : FunClause -> List String
funClauseEVars (FunClause _ body) = allEVars body

armEVars : Arm -> List String
armEVars (Arm _ guards body) = flatMap guardEVars guards ++ allEVars body

guardEVars : Guard -> List String
guardEVars (GBool e) = allEVars e
guardEVars (GBind _ e) = allEVars e

doStmtEVars : DoStmt -> List String
doStmtEVars (DoExpr e) = allEVars e
doStmtEVars (DoLet _ _ _ e) = allEVars e
doStmtEVars (DoBind _ e) = allEVars e
doStmtEVars (DoAssign _ e) = allEVars e
doStmtEVars (DoFieldAssign _ _ e) = allEVars e

fieldAssignEVars : FieldAssign -> List String
fieldAssignEVars (FieldAssign _ e) = allEVars e

-- builtin constructors available without the prelude (mirrors initial_env)
initialEnv : TcEnv
initialEnv =
  TcEnv
    omEmpty
    (omInsert
      "True"
      (monoScheme (TCon "Bool"))
      (omInsert "False" (monoScheme (TCon "Bool")) omEmpty))
    []

registerAllData : TcEnv -> List Decl -> TcEnv
registerAllData env [] = env
registerAllData env (d::rest) = registerAllData (registerData env d) rest

-- ── dependency-ordered, SCC-merged letrec processing ──────────────────────
-- The prelude has forward references and mutual recursion throughout, so groups
-- must be type-checked in dependency order (a callee generalized before its
-- callers instantiate it polymorphically) with mutually-recursive cycles merged
-- into one letrec group (generalized together).  Mirrors the reference's
-- order_groups_by_deps + process_letrec_group (Phase 136).

-- ── Tarjan strongly-connected components (O(V+E)) ─────────────────────────
-- Replaces the former all-pairs-reachability + mutual-reachability SCC + topo
-- sort, which was O(n^3) in a module's top-level binding count and dominated
-- the multi-module typecheck (check_modules).  Emits SCCs in dependency-first
-- order (a "sink" component — no outside dependencies — finishes and is emitted
-- first), exactly what processSCCs needs.  State is held in module-level refs,
-- reset at the start of each run (processTopGroups runs sequentially per module).

depGraphMap : List String -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> OrdMap (List String)
depGraphMap names grouped =
  buildAdj names (namesToSet names omEmpty) grouped omEmpty

-- name-membership set, built ONCE, so `keepGroupNames` is O(log n)/ref instead of
-- O(names)/ref (the dep-graph build was O(names²·refs)). Output-order-preserving:
-- `keepGroupNames`'s `filterList` is unchanged, only its membership test is faster.
namesToSet : List String -> OrdMap Unit -> OrdMap Unit
namesToSet [] m = m
namesToSet (n::rest) m = namesToSet rest (omInsert n () m)

-- defs grouped by name ONCE (name -> its clauses, in defs order), so `depsOf`
-- does an O(log n) lookup instead of re-scanning all defs per name (was
-- O(names·defs)). Built by prepend (O(1)); `clausesOf` reverses on read to
-- restore defs order — so the per-name clause list is identical to the old
-- `clausesFor name defs`, keeping dep lists / SCC order / output byte-identical.
groupClauses : List (String, (List Pat, Expr)) -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr))
groupClauses defs = groupClausesGo defs omEmpty
groupClausesGo : List (String, (List Pat, Expr)) -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr))
groupClausesGo [] m = m
groupClausesGo ((n, c)::rest) m =
  groupClausesGo rest (omInsert n (c :: clausesAt n m) m)

clausesAt : String -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> List (List Pat, Expr)
clausesAt n m = match omLookup n m
  Some xs => xs
  None => []

clausesOf : String -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> List (List Pat, Expr)
clausesOf n m = revClauses (clausesAt n m) []

revClauses : List (List Pat, Expr) -> List (List Pat, Expr) -> List (List Pat, Expr)
revClauses [] acc = acc
revClauses (x::xs) acc = revClauses xs (x::acc)

buildAdj : List String -> OrdMap Unit -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> OrdMap (List String) -> OrdMap (List String)
buildAdj [] _ _ m = m
buildAdj (n::rest) nameSet cbn m =
  buildAdj rest nameSet cbn (omInsert n (depsOf n nameSet cbn) m)

-- sig-name presence set (which top-level names carry a type signature), set once
-- per module in `processTopGroups` so `memberPeelSource`/`memberSigIsFun` do an
-- O(log n) presence check instead of an O(sigs) `lookupAssoc` scan per SCC member
-- (was O(members·sigs) over the whole compiler). Module-level ref, matching the
-- `currentFn`/`curEffect` idiom; processing is sequential per module.

sigNamesToSet : List (String, Ty) -> OrdMap Unit -> OrdMap Unit
sigNamesToSet [] m = m
sigNamesToSet ((n, _)::rest) m = sigNamesToSet rest (omInsert n () m)

-- PERF: name → declared signature, the same `sigs` assoc list as an index.  Three
-- sites (preunifySigsEx, checkEffectEscape, checkSigConstraintOne) did
-- `lookupAssoc m sigs` — an O(N) scan of EVERY top-level signature, run per member
-- of every group, so O(N²) over a large file.  Companion to `sigNameSetRef`, seeded
-- from the same `sigs` in processTopGroups.
--
-- Built from the REVERSED list: `omFromPairs` is last-write-wins, while the
-- `lookupAssoc` it replaces was first-match-wins, so reversing preserves which
-- signature a duplicated name resolves to.

processTopGroups : TcEnv -> List (String, Ty) -> List (String, (List Pat, Expr)) -> List String -> List (String, Scheme)
processTopGroups env sigs defs names =
  let _ = setRef driverState.value.sigNameSetRef (sigNamesToSet sigs omEmpty)
  let _ = setRef driverState.value.sigTyMapRef (omFromPairs (reverseL sigs) omEmpty)
  let grouped = groupClauses defs
  let ordered = tarjanSCCs names (depGraphMap names grouped)
  processSCCs env sigs grouped ordered

-- per-name dependencies: the other top-level names a binding's bodies reference
-- (a conservative over-approximation via all free EVars is fine — only names
-- that are themselves groups matter).  Consumed by depGraphMap → Tarjan.
depsOf : String -> OrdMap Unit -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> List String
depsOf name nameSet cbn =
  filterNonSelf
    name
    (keepGroupNames nameSet (dedup (groupRefs (clausesOf name cbn))))

groupRefs : List (List Pat, Expr) -> List String
groupRefs clauses = flatMap clauseRefs clauses

clauseRefs : (List Pat, Expr) -> List String
clauseRefs (_, body) = allEVars body

keepGroupNames : OrdMap Unit -> List String -> List String
keepGroupNames nameSet refs = filterList (r => omHasKey r nameSet) refs

filterNonSelf : String -> List String -> List String
filterNonSelf name xs = filterList (!= name) xs

-- process each SCC as a letrec group in topological order
processSCCs : TcEnv -> List (String, Ty) -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> List (List String) -> List (String, Scheme)
processSCCs _ _ _ [] = []
processSCCs env sigs grouped (scc::rest) =
  let er = processSCC env sigs grouped scc
  snd er ++ processSCCs (fst er) sigs grouped rest

-- #23 support: synthesize impl-obligation-shaped Num entries from a group's
-- pendingCallObligations / pendingDictApps deltas.  The Num constraint of an
-- ambiguous var reaching a group ONLY through a constrained-callee instantiation
-- (`main = println (app2 (==))`) is recorded on those refs, never on
-- pendingImplObligations, so the group-level Num defaulting (defaultGroupNum /
-- groundNumVars, which both reads candidate ids AND finds the live var to ground
-- from impl-shaped entries) never saw it — leaving an ungrounded var whose Num dict
-- route resolves RNone → a NULL dict word → build-time SIGTRAP (run is correct).
-- Only field 0 (iface) and field 3 (the live operand mono) are read downstream;
-- the rest mirror inferNumLitBare's placeholder shape.
-- Num is 1-param, so its obligation vector is a singleton; a wider predicate can
-- never be "Num" and is filtered out anyway.
numCallObls : List (String, List Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc)
numCallObls [] = []
numCallObls ((iface, monos, loc)::rest) = match (iface, monos)
  ("Num", [mono]) => ("Num", ["a"], TyVar "a", mono, loc) :: numCallObls rest
  _ => numCallObls rest

numDictObls : List (Ref (List Route), List Mono, List String) -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc)
numDictObls [] = []
numDictObls ((_, monos, ifaces)::rest) = flatMap (p => if fst p == "Num" then [("Num", ["a"], TyVar "a", snd p, currentLoc.value)] else []) (zipL ifaces monos)
  ++ numDictObls rest

-- #23 (two-hop forward): the impl-obligation-shaped entries for EVERY iface (not
-- just Num) a group's constrained-callee instantiations recorded on the call/dict
-- deltas.  Unlike numCallObls/numDictObls (which feed only Num-defaulting), these
-- feed registerSchemeObligations so a constraint FORWARDED from a constrained callee
-- (`useApp g = app2 g`, whose Num lives on the call/dict deltas, never on
-- pendingImplObligations) is recorded in schemeObligationsRef — so a caller of the
-- forwarder (`main = println (useApp (==))`) re-instantiates it (instantiateVarTracked),
-- surfacing the constraint on the caller's own delta where group Num-defaulting can
-- ground the shared ambiguous var.  Without this the forwarded var never grounds on
-- the check path and a downstream Eq/Ord obligation on it is falsely ambiguous.
-- #23 two-hop forward, VECTOR-PRESERVING.  A constraint forwarded from a constrained
-- callee lives on the pendingCallObligations delta, never in addedObls, so a caller of
-- this group must re-instantiate it from schemeObligationsRef.
--
-- This used to detour through a SYNTHETIC addedObls entry (iface, ["a"], TyVar "a",
-- mono, loc) that schemeObligationOne re-read via dispatchMonosOf.  That entry
-- structurally carries ONE Mono, so a joint predicate could not pass through it: it was
-- flat-mapped per ARGUMENT, and because each 1-ary shard lands on a bound tyvar,
-- boundTyvarIds SUCCEEDED and FABRICATED `Ix a` + `Ix i` alongside the real `Ix a i`
-- (one violation reported three times; `(Ix a, Ix a b, Ix b) =>` displayed).
--
-- The detour was pure overhead: dispatchMonosOf ["a"] (TyVar "a") m is an IDENTITY —
-- walkDispatch hits `contains "a" ["a"]` giving [("a", normalize m)], and collectByParam
-- reads it straight back out as [normalize m].  A pendingCallObligations entry ALREADY
-- carries its joint `List Mono`, so map it to bound tyvar ids DIRECTLY and the vector
-- survives at every arity.  Identical at arity 1 (boundTyvarIds normalizes too), and it
-- closes the UNSIGNED forwarding hole, which the declared-context path cannot reach
-- (lookupSigTvMap is None for an unsigned binding).
forwardedVecObls : List Int -> List (String, List Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, List Int)
forwardedVecObls _ [] = []
forwardedVecObls boundIds ((iface, monos, _)::rest) = forwardedVecOblOne boundIds iface monos
  ++ forwardedVecObls boundIds rest

forwardedVecOblOne : List Int -> String -> List Mono -> List (String, List Int)
forwardedVecOblOne boundIds iface monos
  | iface == "" = []
  | otherwise = match boundTyvarIds boundIds monos
    Some ids => [(iface, ids)]
    None => []

allDictObls : List (Ref (List Route), List Mono, List String) -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc)
allDictObls [] = []
allDictObls ((_, monos, ifaces)::rest) = flatMap (p => if fst p == "" then [] else [(fst p, ["a"], TyVar "a", snd p, currentLoc.value)]) (zipL ifaces monos)
  ++ allDictObls rest

-- FLAT-PATH SHADOW SCOPING (S1): a letrec group whose members are ALL prelude fns is a
-- CORE group — no user standalone is a shadow inside it.  Empty group ⇒ not core (it
-- cannot occur, and "user" is the conservative arm: it preserves the pre-fix behavior).
flatGroupIsCore : List String -> Bool
flatGroupIsCore [] = False
flatGroupIsCore members = allCoreFns members

allCoreFns : List String -> Bool
allCoreFns [] = True
allCoreFns (m::rest)
  | omHasKey m perRun.value.flatCoreFnNamesRef.value = allCoreFns rest
  | otherwise = False

-- FLAT-PATH SHADOW SCOPING (S1): point definerShadowNamesRef at the shadow set that is
-- actually IN SCOPE for the decls about to be inferred — [] inside the prelude, the
-- user's shadows inside the user's module.  Inert (and byte-identical) unless the flat
-- path prepended a prelude; see flatShadowScopingRef.
scopeShadowsForGroup : List String -> Unit
scopeShadowsForGroup members
  | not perRun.value.flatShadowScopingRef.value = ()
  | flatGroupIsCore members = setRef perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef []
  | otherwise = setRef perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef perRun.value.flatUserShadowNamesRef.value

scopeShadowsCore : Unit -> Unit
scopeShadowsCore _ =
  if perRun.value.flatShadowScopingRef.value then
    setRef perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef []
  else
    ()

scopeShadowsUser : Unit -> Unit
scopeShadowsUser _ =
  if perRun.value.flatShadowScopingRef.value then
    setRef perRun.value.definerShadowNamesRef perRun.value.flatUserShadowNamesRef.value
  else
    ()

-- did the caller prepend a prelude?  (`listLen ds > 0` without walking the list.)
declsNonEmpty : List Decl -> Bool
declsNonEmpty [] = False
declsNonEmpty _ = True

processSCC : TcEnv -> List (String, Ty) -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> List String -> (TcEnv, List (String, Scheme))
processSCC env sigs grouped members =
  let _ = scopeShadowsForGroup members
  let _ = enterLevel ()
  let oblN0 = perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value
  let callN0 = perRun.value.pendingCallObligationsN.value
  let dictN0 = perRun.value.pendingDictAppsN.value
  let placeholders = map (m => (m, freshVar ())) members
  let env2 = extendVars env (map placeholderBinding placeholders)
  let (regs, sigTvMaps) = preunifySigsEx sigs placeholders
  -- P0-19 (constrained-receiver arm): publish THIS group's `=>`-constraint vars for
  -- the duration of body inference, so a definer-shadow method occurrence on one of
  -- them (`neq x y = not (eq x y)` under `neq : Eq a => …`) is seen as a DISPATCH and
  -- not monomorphised to the shadowing standalone's declared domain.
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.groupConstraintMonosRef (flatMap snd regs)
  let _ = inferMembers env2 sigs grouped placeholders
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.groupConstraintMonosRef []
  let _ = checkSigsTooGeneral sigTvMaps
  let _ = registerConstraintRegs regs
  let _ = exitLevel ()
  -- PLAN.md #11 (locked §0.2): GROUP-LEVEL + per-binding Num defaulting.  Ground
  -- every ambiguous Num-only var (one not reachable from an ARGUMENT position of
  -- ANY member's type) to Int before this group generalizes.  Covers both a var
  -- that surfaces in a member's type (`n = 1`) and one consumed entirely inside a
  -- body (`n = tag [1,2,3]`, element var in no member type).  An arg-reachable var
  -- (`sum`'s/`inc`'s element var) stays polymorphic.  Scoped to THIS group's newly
  -- added obligations (prepended at the front) so an earlier group's already-
  -- generalized var is never touched.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's
  -- process_letrec_group group-level pass + per-binding default_ambiguous_num.
  let addedObls = takeFirst (perRun.value.pendingImplObligationsN.value - oblN0) perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value
  -- #23: also ground an ambiguous Num var that reaches this group ONLY through a
  -- constrained-callee instantiation (its Num obligation lives on the call/dict deltas,
  -- not pendingImplObligations).  See numCallObls/numDictObls.  The synthetic entries
  -- feed ONLY the Num-defaulting search; addedObls alone still drives the
  -- ambiguous-constraint/scheme-obligation registration below.
  let extraNumObls =
        numCallObls (takeFirst (perRun.value.pendingCallObligationsN.value - callN0) perRun.value.pendingCallObligations.value)
        ++ numDictObls (takeFirst (perRun.value.pendingDictAppsN.value - dictN0) perRun.value.pendingDictApps.value)
  let defaultObls = addedObls ++ extraNumObls
  let _ = defaultGroupNum defaultObls (map snd placeholders)
  let _ = defaultEachMember defaultObls placeholders
  let _ = registerAmbiguousConstraints addedObls (map snd placeholders)   -- RETPOS soundness
  let schemes = sccSchemes sigs grouped (isLetrecGroup members) placeholders
  -- #23 two-hop forward: also record constraints FORWARDED from a constrained
  -- callee (living on the call/dict deltas, never in addedObls) into
  -- schemeObligationsRef, so a caller of this group re-instantiates them and its
  -- own group can ground the shared ambiguous var.
  -- The CALL delta is passed RAW (it already carries each predicate's joint `List
  -- Mono`); registerSchemeObligations maps it with forwardedVecObls so a multi-param
  -- predicate survives whole.  Only the DICT delta still goes through the synthetic
  -- 1-Mono addedObls shape — its entries are registerMember's per-TYVAR dict slots,
  -- which are 1-ary by construction (see allDictObls).
  let callOblsDelta = takeFirst (perRun.value.pendingCallObligationsN.value - callN0) perRun.value.pendingCallObligations.value
  let forwardedObls = allDictObls (takeFirst (perRun.value.pendingDictAppsN.value - dictN0) perRun.value.pendingDictApps.value)
  let _ = registerSchemeObligations sigTvMaps callOblsDelta (addedObls ++ forwardedObls) schemes
  -- SIGNATURE CONSTRAINT SOUNDNESS: a signed member whose body induces an
  -- interface constraint on one of its own quantified vars that its declared `=>`
  -- context (superclass-closed) does NOT cover severs the constraint for callers
  -- → wrong-dictionary dispatch (a silent wrong answer).  Reject at the def-site.
  -- Runs on every path; the census over compiler+stdlib+sqlite was 0 new failures.
  let _ = checkSigConstraintCoverage regs sigs sigTvMaps callOblsDelta addedObls schemes
  let _ = registerInferredConstraints sigs schemes
  -- FLAT-PATH SHADOW FIX (facet 1): thread forward only the NON-method-shadowing
  -- schemes, so a method name a user fn shadows keeps resolving to the METHOD
  -- (base env / globalS) for later prelude bindings.  The shadowing scheme is still
  -- RETURNED (second tuple slot) for the scheme dump + final env.
  (extendVars env (dropSchemesNamed perRun.value.methodShadowNamesRef.value schemes), schemes)

-- A multi-member SCC is a mutually-recursive group (always a function group, like
-- the oracle's DLetGroup); a singleton is a plain binding gated on its RHS.  Self-
-- recursive singletons have had their self-edge stripped (filterNonSelf) so they
-- arrive as size-1 SCCs — strict evaluation rules out a self-recursive zero-arg
-- value, so gating them on the RHS is sound and matches the oracle's DFunDef path.
isLetrecGroup : List String -> Bool
isLetrecGroup [] = False
isLetrecGroup [_] = False
isLetrecGroup _ = True

placeholderBinding : (String, Mono) -> (String, Scheme)
placeholderBinding (m, v) = (m, monoScheme v)

-- Extended Pass A: same as preunifySigs but also returns the tyvar maps (for
-- the signature-too-general check after body inference).  Returns a pair:
-- (constraintRegs, sigTvMaps) where sigTvMaps is [(name, sig_ast, tvs)] for
-- each signed member.
preunifySigsEx : List (String, Ty) -> List (String, Mono) -> (List (String, List (String, Mono)), List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)))
preunifySigsEx _ [] = ([], [])
preunifySigsEx sigs ((m, v)::rest) =
  let (restRegs, restTvs) = preunifySigsEx sigs rest
  match omLookup m driverState.value.sigTyMapRef.value
    Some ty =>
      let inst = instantiateSigTracked ty
      let _ = unify v (fst inst)
      let regs = [(m, constraintVarIfaceMonos (sigConstraints ty) (snd inst))]
      (regs ++ restRegs, (m, ty, snd inst)::restTvs)
    None => (restRegs, restTvs)

-- like astTypeFresh but also returns the tyvar-name → fresh-var map
instantiateSigTracked : Ty -> (Mono, List (String, Mono))
instantiateSigTracked ty =
  let tvs = freshTvMap (removeAllS (rowArgNames ty) (tyVarNamesWithConstraints ty))
  let etbl = freshEffMap (dedup (effTailNames ty ++ rowArgNames ty))
  (fromAstTypeE etbl tvs ty, tvs)

-- the (iface, tyvarName) pairs of a signature's `=>` constraints (each constraint
-- argument that is a single type variable; multi-param/structured args skipped)
sigConstraints : Ty -> List (String, String)
sigConstraints (TyConstrained cs _) = flatMap constraintTyVars cs
sigConstraints _ = []

constraintTyVars : Constraint -> List (String, String)
constraintTyVars (Constraint iface tys) =
