  map (n => (iface, n)) (tyVarArgNames tys)

tyVarArgNames : List Ty -> List String
tyVarArgNames [] = []
tyVarArgNames ((TyVar n)::rest) = n :: tyVarArgNames rest
tyVarArgNames (_::rest) = tyVarArgNames rest

-- like constraintVarMonos but KEEPS the iface name paired with each constraint var's
-- mono (slot order preserved), so registerMember can register call-obligation ifaces.
constraintVarIfaceMonos : List (String, String) -> List (String, Mono) -> List (String, Mono)
constraintVarIfaceMonos [] _ = []
constraintVarIfaceMonos ((iface, name)::rest) tvMap = match lookupAssoc name tvMap
  Some mono => (iface, mono) :: constraintVarIfaceMonos rest tvMap
  None => constraintVarIfaceMonos rest tvMap

-- After body inference, register each member's constraint variables by their
-- *surviving* (normalized) tyvar id: in activeDictVars (id → dict param name, for
-- in-body RDict routing) and per-fn in funConstraintsRef (for call-site dict
-- application).  Done per group so a callee's constraints are known before its
-- (later-group) callers infer.
registerConstraintRegs : List (String, List (String, Mono)) -> Unit
registerConstraintRegs [] = ()
registerConstraintRegs ((m, ifaceMonos)::rest) =
  let _ = registerMember m ifaceMonos
  registerConstraintRegs rest

-- register a member's constraints: ids into funConstraintsRef + activeDictVars (the
-- kept/abstract slots), and the SLOT-ALIGNED iface names into funConstraintIfacesRef.
-- registerMemberSlots keeps a slot only when its mono is still an abstract TVar (a
-- body-forced-concrete constraint takes no dict slot); the iface list is filtered the
-- same way so it stays parallel to the id list.
registerMember : String -> List (String, Mono) -> Unit
-- An UNCONSTRAINED member (empty constraint list) gets NO funConstraintsRef /
-- funConstraintIfacesRef entry.  These refs are keyed by BARE NAME and accumulate
-- across the per-module elaboration; an unconstrained top-level fn that shares a
-- name with a constrained one in another module (e.g. array.mdk's `find : (a ->
-- Bool) -> Array a -> Option a` vs core.mdk's `find : Foldable t => …`) would
-- otherwise register a `(find, [])` entry that, prepended later, wins the
-- first-match lookup over core's `(find, [foldableId])`.  inferDictAt then reads 0
-- constraint ids → 0 dict args at the call site → `unbound identifier:
-- $dict_find_0` at eval.  An unconstrained fn is never marked EDictAt, so it needs
-- no entry (mirrors registerInferredFor's empty-ids guard).
registerMember _ [] = ()
registerMember m ifaceMonos =
  let ids = registerMemberSlots m 0 (map snd ifaceMonos)
  let keptIfaces = keptConstraintIfaces ifaceMonos
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.funConstraintsRef ((m, ids)::perRun.value.funConstraintsRef.value)
  setRef
    perRun.value.funConstraintIfacesRef
    ((m, keptIfaces)::perRun.value.funConstraintIfacesRef.value)

-- the iface names of the constraints registerMemberSlots KEEPS (mono still a TVar),
-- in the same slot order — parallel to registerMember's id list.
keptConstraintIfaces : List (String, Mono) -> List String
keptConstraintIfaces [] = []
keptConstraintIfaces ((iface, mono)::rest) = match normalize mono
  TVar _ => iface :: keptConstraintIfaces rest
  _ => keptConstraintIfaces rest

-- [kept] is the next dict-param slot; a constraint variable forced concrete by the
-- body takes no dict and doesn't advance the slot (so param names stay 0..k-1,
-- matching dict_pass).
registerMemberSlots : String -> Int -> List Mono -> List Int
registerMemberSlots _ _ [] = []
registerMemberSlots m kept (mono::rest) = match normalize mono
  TVar cell =>
    let id = tyvarId cell
    let _ = setRef perRun.value.activeDictVars ((id, dictParamName m kept)::perRun.value.activeDictVars.value)
    id :: registerMemberSlots m (kept + 1) rest
  _ => registerMemberSlots m kept rest

-- Pass B: infer each member's clauses, unifying with its placeholder.
-- Phase 73 (bidirectional, signature-driven parameter typing): a member WITH a
-- signature peels its declared arrow types onto the clause parameter patterns
-- BEFORE inferring the body, so the body sees concrete parameter types (a
-- receiver-directed field access `f p = p.x` or a `String`-indexed `s.[0]`
-- resolves from the signature alone).  [peel] is Some(placeholder) for a signed
-- member, None otherwise; the placeholder already holds the sig instantiation
-- (preunifySigsEx unified it), and its shared tyvars accumulate unifications
-- across clauses exactly as the oracle's `sig_t` does.  Purely additive: the
-- per-clause `unify v arrow` below imposes the same equalities anyway.
inferMembers : TcEnv -> List (String, Ty) -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> List (String, Mono) -> Unit
inferMembers _ _ _ [] = ()
inferMembers env sigs grouped ((m, v)::rest) =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.currentFn m
  let peel = memberPeelSource sigs m v
  let effs = inferMemberClauses env peel v (clausesOf m grouped)
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.currentFn ""
  let _ = checkEffectEscape sigs m effs
  inferMembers env sigs grouped rest

-- the peel source for a member: Some placeholder if it has a signature (peel the
-- declared arrow domains onto the clause params), None otherwise
memberPeelSource : List (String, Ty) -> String -> Mono -> Option Mono
memberPeelSource sigs m v =
  map (_ => v) (omLookup m driverState.value.sigNameSetRef.value)

-- peel up to [n] arrow domains off [t], normalizing at each level.  A signature
-- with fewer arrows than the clause has params stops early and returns fewer
-- domains (the mismatch then surfaces at the per-clause `unify v arrow`).
peelArrows : Int -> Mono -> List Mono
peelArrows n t
  | n <= 0 = []
  | otherwise = match normalize t
    TFun a _ b => a :: peelArrows (n - 1) b
    _ => []

-- zip-unify the equal-length prefix of param types and peeled sig domains; a
-- shorter [qs] (partial sig) pushes nothing past its end
zipUnify : List Mono -> List Mono -> Unit
zipUnify (p::ps) (q::qs) =
  let _ = unify p q
  zipUnify ps qs
zipUnify _ _ = ()

-- infer each clause, unifying it to the member placeholder [v] and collecting the
-- concrete effect labels the bodies perform (Phase 79e binding-boundary check).
-- [peel] threads the Phase-73 sig peel source (Some placeholder when signed).
inferMemberClauses : TcEnv -> Option Mono -> Mono -> List (List Pat, Expr) -> List Atom
inferMemberClauses _ _ _ [] = []
inferMemberClauses env peel v (cl::rest) =
  let ce = inferClauseEff env peel cl
  let _ = unify v (fst ce)
  atomsUnion (snd ce) (inferMemberClauses env peel v rest)

-- infer one clause, returning its arrow type AND the concrete effect labels its
-- body performs (captured under a fresh open ambient, then the ambient restored —
-- building a closure performs nothing).  Mirrors inferLam but exposes the effect.
-- Phase 73: when [peel] is Some(sig source) and the clause has params, peel the
-- signature's arrow domains and zip-unify them onto the clause param types BEFORE
-- inferring the body (bidirectional checking mode).
inferClauseEff : TcEnv -> Option Mono -> (List Pat, Expr) -> (Mono, List Atom)
inferClauseEff env peel (pats, body) =
  -- Anchor currentLoc to THIS clause's body loc before inferring patterns:
  -- patterns carry no source Loc, so an over-applied-constructor / arity pattern
  -- error would otherwise be recorded at the STALE loc left by the previous
  -- clause's (or a previous function's) body inference — mis-blaming an innocent
  -- sibling clause.  See the twin note in inferLam.
  let _ = match exprLoc body
    Some l => setRef currentLoc (Some l)
    None => ()
  let pr = inferPats env pats
  let _ = peelOntoParams peel (fst pr) pats
  let saved = perRun.value.curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.curEffect (openRow ())
  let bodyTy = infer (extendVars env (snd pr)) body
  let bodyEff = perRun.value.curEffect.value
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.curEffect saved
  (arrowsWithLastEffect (fst pr) bodyEff bodyTy, effrowLabels bodyEff)

-- Phase 73 peel: for a signed clause with params, zip-unify the peeled sig
-- domains onto the inferred param types.  No-op for an unsigned member, a value
-- binding (no params), or a sig with no remaining arrows.
peelOntoParams : Option Mono -> List Mono -> List Pat -> Unit
peelOntoParams None _ _ = ()
peelOntoParams (Some _) _ [] = ()
peelOntoParams (Some src) patTypes pats =
  zipUnify patTypes (peelArrows (listLen pats) src)

-- a signatured binding's body must not perform a concrete effect its signature
-- doesn't declare; effect *variables* (polymorphic `<e>`) carry no labels and so
-- never escape (Phase 79e)
checkEffectEscape : List (String, Ty) -> String -> List Atom -> Unit
checkEffectEscape sigs m effs = match omLookup m driverState.value.sigTyMapRef.value
  None => ()
  Some ty => match atomsEscape effs (declaredEffects ty)
    [] => ()
    extras => pushTypeError "T-EFFECT-LEAK" (effectEscapeMsg m (declaredEffects ty) extras)

effectEscapeMsg : String -> List Atom -> List Atom -> String
effectEscapeMsg name declared extras = "Function '\{name}' declared with <\{renderAtoms declared}> but also performs <\{renderAtoms extras}>"

-- generalize each member's (sig-and-body-unified) placeholder.  A signature was
-- already unified into the placeholder by preunifySigs, so generalizing the
-- result respects it AND any further specialization the body forces (e.g.
-- sum : t a -> a with body `fold (+) 0` reports a Int -> Int, not the raw sig).
-- Value restriction (T1): generalize a member only if it is a *value* —
--   plainVal = isLetrec (multi-member SCC, always a function group) OR every
--              clause has params / a non-expansive zero-arg RHS;
--   sigIsFun = the member is signed AND its (post-unify) type is an arrow
--              (Phase 89 point-free relaxation: a signed arrow-typed point-free
--              binding like `maximum = fold step None` stays generalizable even
--              though its RHS is an application);
--   isVal    = plainVal || sigIsFun.
-- A non-function expansive unsigned binding (`r = Ref []`) is value-restricted and
-- stays monomorphic.  Mirrors oracle lib/typecheck.ml:2705-2710 + 2756.
sccSchemes : List (String, Ty) -> OrdMap (List (List Pat, Expr)) -> Bool -> List (String, Mono) -> List (String, Scheme)
sccSchemes _ _ _ [] = []
sccSchemes sigs grouped isLetrec ((m, v)::rest) =
  let plainVal = isLetrec || allList memberClauseIsValue (clausesOf m grouped)
  let sigIsFun = memberSigIsFun sigs m v
  let isVal = plainVal || sigIsFun
  (m, genRestricted isVal v) :: sccSchemes sigs grouped isLetrec rest

-- a top-level clause is a value if it has params (function) or a non-expansive RHS
memberClauseIsValue : (List Pat, Expr) -> Bool
memberClauseIsValue ([], rhs) = isNonexpansive rhs
memberClauseIsValue (_, _) = True

-- Phase 89: a signed binding whose (post-unify) type is an arrow is generalizable
memberSigIsFun : List (String, Ty) -> String -> Mono -> Bool
memberSigIsFun sigs m v = match omLookup m driverState.value.sigNameSetRef.value
  None => False
  Some _ => match normalize v
    TFun _ _ _ => True
    _ => False

-- ── signature-too-general check ──────────────────────────────────────────────
-- After body inference, the declared signature's type variables must map to
-- DISTINCT TVar refs.  If two declared tyvars (e.g. `a` and `b` in `a -> b`)
-- collapse to the SAME TVar after unification (body forces them equal), the
-- declared signature claims more polymorphism than the body allows.
-- We only reject the "two tvars become one" case (not concrete grounding), so
-- `sum = fold (+) 0` (which grounds one tyvar to Int) passes.
checkSigsTooGeneral : List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> Unit
checkSigsTooGeneral [] = ()
checkSigsTooGeneral ((m, ty, tvs)::rest) =
  let _ = checkSigTooGeneral m ty tvs
  checkSigsTooGeneral rest

checkSigTooGeneral : String -> Ty -> List (String, Mono) -> Unit
checkSigTooGeneral name ty tvs =
  let tvarIds = sigTvarIds (map snd tvs)
  if hasDupI tvarIds then
    pushTypeError "T-TYPE-TOO-GENERAL" (sigTooGeneralMsg name ty)
  else
    ()

-- collect the TVar ids of those entries in tvs that normalize to a TVar
-- (concrete-grounded entries are skipped — only same-TVar collapses are an error)
sigTvarIds : List Mono -> List Int
sigTvarIds [] = []
sigTvarIds (m::rest) = match normalize m
  TVar cell => tyvarId cell :: sigTvarIds rest
  _ => sigTvarIds rest

-- true if the list has any duplicate Int
hasDupI : List Int -> Bool
hasDupI [] = False
hasDupI (x::xs) = containsI x xs || hasDupI xs

sigTooGeneralMsg : String -> Ty -> String
sigTooGeneralMsg name ty = "Declared signature of '\{name}' is more general than its body. '\{ppTy ty}' claims type variables that are the same type after inference"

-- C3: mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's AnnotationTooGeneral message byte-for-byte.
annotTooGeneralMsg : Ty -> String
annotTooGeneralMsg ty = "Type annotation '"
  ++ ppTy ty
  ++ "' is more polymorphic than the expression. A type variable in the annotation is actually a specific type (or two annotation variables are the same type)"

-- pretty-print an AST type (mirrors lib/ast.ml's pp_ty byte-for-byte)
ppTy : Ty -> String
ppTy (TyCon n _) = n
ppTy (TyVar n) = n
ppTy (TyApp a b) = "\{ppTy a} \{ppTyAtom b}"
ppTy (TyFun a b) = "\{ppTyFunArg a} -> \{ppTy b}"
ppTy (TyTuple ts) = "(" ++ joinWith ", " (map ppTy ts) ++ ")"
ppTy (TyEffect effs tail t) =
  let labs = map ppEffAtomTy effs
  let inside = match tail
    None => joinWith ", " labs
    Some v => match effs
      [] => v
      _ => "\{joinWith ", " labs} | \{v}"
  "<\{inside}> \{ppTy t}"
ppTy (TyConstrained cs t) = "\{ppConstraints cs} => \{ppTy t}"

-- effect atom renderer for ppTy (mirrors OCaml pp_atom inside pp_ty_prec / TEff)
ppEffAtomTy : (String, Option String) -> String
ppEffAtomTy (l, None) = l
-- Intentional cross-file duplicate of the same helper in doc.mdk; not consolidating (tiny helper / divergent-by-design backend pair).
-- lint-disable-next-line rule-duplicate-body
ppEffAtomTy (l, Some s) = if s == "_" then l ++ " _" else "\{l} \{escStr s}"

-- single constraint: no outer parens; multiple: wrap in parens (mirrors OCaml)
ppConstraints : List Constraint -> String
ppConstraints [c] = ppConstraint c
ppConstraints cs = "(" ++ joinWith ", " (map ppConstraint cs) ++ ")"

-- argument of `->`: wrap TyFun (prec>=1) but not TyApp (prec<2)
ppTyFunArg : Ty -> String
ppTyFunArg (TyFun a b) = "(" ++ ppTy (TyFun a b) ++ ")"
ppTyFunArg t = ppTy t

-- argument of application: wrap TyFun (prec>=1) and TyApp (prec>=2)
ppTyAtom : Ty -> String
ppTyAtom (TyFun a b) = "(" ++ ppTy (TyFun a b) ++ ")"
ppTyAtom (TyApp a b) = "(" ++ ppTy (TyApp a b) ++ ")"
ppTyAtom t = ppTy t

ppConstraint : Constraint -> String
ppConstraint (Constraint iface []) = iface
ppConstraint (Constraint iface tys) =
  "\{iface} \{joinWith " " (map ppTyAtom tys)}"

-- ── inferred-constraint promotion (unsignatured `=>` — the two-pass layer) ──
-- After a group is generalized, an UNSIGNATURED eligible member that uses a
-- return-position method (methodSiteFns) — or calls a constrained fn (dictAppFns)
-- — at one of its OWN quantified tyvars carries an INFERRED constraint.  Register
-- it exactly as a signatured one (funConstraintsRef + activeDictVars, keyed by the
-- *surviving* normalized id) so its body's method routes RDict and dict_pass
-- prepends a dict param; and record it in promotedRef so elaborateDict marks its
-- occurrences EDictAt on the next pass.  Signatured members are skipped (their
-- constraints came from preunifySigs); prelude fns are skipped (not eligible).
registerInferredConstraints : List (String, Ty) -> List (String, Scheme) -> Unit
registerInferredConstraints _ [] = ()
registerInferredConstraints sigs ((m, sch)::rest) =
  let _ = maybeInferConstraint sigs m sch
  registerInferredConstraints sigs rest

maybeInferConstraint : List (String, Ty) -> String -> Scheme -> Unit
maybeInferConstraint sigs m sch
  | not (omHasKey m driverState.value.dictEligibleSetRef.value) = ()
  | omHasKey m driverState.value.sigNameSetRef.value = ()
  | otherwise = registerInferredFor m (inferredConstraintIds m (schemeIds sch))

-- #11 soundness (ALWAYS-ON): for each generalized member, record the deferred impl
-- obligations it quantifies over — an addedObls entry whose discriminating mono is a
-- TVar among the member's scheme ids becomes (iface, that id).  A signed member's
-- declared `=>` constraints come from its sig instead (registered via funConstraints);
-- but recording the inferred obligation list here too is harmless (a satisfiable
-- concrete instantiation passes; an unsatisfiable one SHOULD error).  Runs on every
-- path (NOT gated on dictEligibleSetRef), so the plain check path catches the hole.
-- SOURCING (#619 DECLARED-ONLY; supersedes the #607/#610 UNION): the predicates a
-- binding abstracts are its PRINCIPAL context, and per DICT-SEMANTICS §4 `gen`/`var`
-- an ASCRIBED signature IS that context — a contract the body must satisfy, not a
-- floor it can raise (Jones; Hall-Peyton-Jones-Wadler; Haskell).  So:
--   • SIGNED binding   → obligations come SOLELY from the declared `=>` context
--       (declaredSchemeOblsFor).  The body's inferred obligations
--       (schemeObligationsOf / forwardedVecObls) are NOT merged in — they are
--       CHECKED as entailed by the declared context (checkSigConstraintCoverage);
--       a body that genuinely needs MORE than the signature declares is a def-site
--       type error (T-MISSING-CONSTRAINT), not a silent widening.
--   • UNSIGNED binding → no declared context exists, so the inferred principal
--       context stands: direct body dispatch (schemeObligationsOf) plus any
--       constraint forwarded from a constrained callee (forwardedVecObls).  Its
--       inferred constraints are promoted, not checked (see checkSigConstraintOne).
-- #610 stays satisfied: a declared constraint the body never dispatches on
-- (`sz2 : Sz a => a -> Int; sz2 x = 9`) is still sourced from declaredSchemeOblsFor,
-- so the scheme keeps `Sz a` and every caller discharges it.  #619 additionally drops
-- the union's redundant superclass display — `when : Thenable m => …` whose body's
-- `pure` infers Applicative now renders just `Thenable a =>` (Applicative is entailed
-- by `Thenable requires Applicative`, so it is checked-and-dropped, not merged).
registerSchemeObligations : List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> List (String, List Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, Scheme) -> Unit
registerSchemeObligations _ _ _ [] = ()
registerSchemeObligations sigTvMaps callObls addedObls ((m, sch)::rest) =
  let obs = dedupVecObls (schemeOblsForMember sigTvMaps callObls addedObls m sch)
  let _ = if isEmptyL obs then
    ()
  else
    setRef perRun.value.schemeObligationsRef ((m, obs)::perRun.value.schemeObligationsRef.value)
  registerSchemeObligations sigTvMaps callObls addedObls rest

-- #619: a SIGNED binding's scheme context is EXACTLY its declared `=>` predicates
-- (authoritative); an UNSIGNED binding's is its inferred principal context (direct
-- dispatch + forwarded).  Signedness is `lookupSigTvMap … = Some` — true for any
-- signed member, including a signed binding with an EMPTY `=>` (whose declared
-- context is correctly empty), which is what distinguishes it from an unsigned one.
schemeOblsForMember : List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> List (String, List Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> String -> Scheme -> List (String, List Int)
schemeOblsForMember sigTvMaps callObls addedObls m sch = match lookupSigTvMap m sigTvMaps
  Some _ => declaredSchemeOblsFor sigTvMaps m (schemeIds sch)
  None => schemeObligationsOf (schemeIds sch) addedObls
    ++ forwardedVecObls (schemeIds sch) callObls

-- the DECLARED `=>` predicates of signed member [m], each as its argument vector of
-- scheme-quantified tyvar ids.  A predicate is kept only if EVERY argument is a plain
-- tyvar that instantiated to one of this scheme's quantified vars: a concrete-grounded
-- argument (`Sz Int =>`) carries no per-use obligation here, and a partial vector would
-- be a different predicate.  An unsigned member contributes nothing.
declaredSchemeOblsFor : List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> String -> List Int -> List (String, List Int)
declaredSchemeOblsFor sigTvMaps m boundIds = match lookupSigTvMap m sigTvMaps
  None => []
  Some (ty, tvMap) =>
    flatMap (declaredOblOne boundIds tvMap) (sigConstraintsOf ty)

declaredOblOne : List Int -> List (String, Mono) -> Constraint -> List (String, List Int)
declaredOblOne boundIds tvMap (Constraint iface args) = match constraintArgMonos args tvMap
  None => []
  Some monos => match boundTyvarIds boundIds monos
    Some ids => [(iface, ids)]
    None => []

-- the monos a predicate's arguments resolve to under a tyvar-name -> mono map, in
-- declaration order; None if ANY argument is not a plain tyvar or is absent from the
-- map (a partial vector is a DIFFERENT predicate, so the whole thing is dropped).
-- Shared by the declared-context sourcing and recordSigConstraintArgs.
constraintArgMonos : List Ty -> List (String, Mono) -> Option (List Mono)
constraintArgMonos [] _ = Some []
constraintArgMonos (a::rest) tvMap = match constraintArgMono a tvMap
  None => None
  Some mono => map (mono :: _) (constraintArgMonos rest tvMap)

-- the mono ONE predicate argument resolves to: None unless it is a plain tyvar
-- present in [tvMap] (a concrete-grounded argument like `Sz Int =>` carries no
-- per-use obligation here).
constraintArgMono : Ty -> List (String, Mono) -> Option Mono
constraintArgMono a tvMap = match constraintArgVarName a
  None => None
  Some v => lookupAssoc v tvMap

lookupSigTvMap : String -> List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> Option (Ty, List (String, Mono))
lookupSigTvMap _ [] = None
lookupSigTvMap m ((n, ty, tvMap)::rest)
  | m == n = Some (ty, tvMap)
  | otherwise = lookupSigTvMap m rest

dedupVecObls : List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int)
dedupVecObls xs = dedupVecOblsGo xs []

dedupVecOblsGo : List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int)
dedupVecOblsGo [] _ = []
dedupVecOblsGo (p::rest) seen
  | containsVecObl p seen = dedupVecOblsGo rest seen
  | otherwise = p :: dedupVecOblsGo rest (p::seen)

containsVecObl : (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int) -> Bool
containsVecObl _ [] = False
containsVecObl (i, ids) ((i2, ids2)::rest) = i == i2 && eqIntList ids ids2
  || containsVecObl (i, ids) rest

eqIntList : List Int -> List Int -> Bool
eqIntList [] [] = True
eqIntList (a::xs) (b::ys) = a == b && eqIntList xs ys
eqIntList _ _ = False

-- the (iface, var-id VECTOR) entries of the obligations whose discriminating monos are
-- all quantified ids of this scheme.  Deduped on (iface, ids).
schemeObligationsOf : List Int -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, List Int)
schemeObligationsOf _ [] = []
schemeObligationsOf boundIds ((iface, typarams, mty, occ, _)::rest) = schemeObligationOne boundIds iface typarams mty occ
  ++ schemeObligationsOf boundIds rest

-- a single obligation contributes ONE joint (iface, ids) entry iff EVERY one of its
-- dispatch monos is a bound tyvar of the scheme.  Before #607 the multi-mono case fell
-- into a `_ => []` wildcard and the obligation was DISCARDED — which is why an
-- unsatisfiable `Ix a i =>` reached neither the displayed scheme nor any caller's
-- obligation list, and `check` accepted it at exit 0 while the built binary did raw
-- pointer arithmetic.  A non-var-headed dispatch mono is still skipped (left to the
-- concrete-obligation checker), and an EMPTY dispatch list still contributes nothing.
schemeObligationOne : List Int -> String -> List String -> Ty -> Mono -> List (String, List Int)
schemeObligationOne boundIds iface typarams mty occ = match dispatchMonosOf typarams mty occ
  [] => []
  ds => match boundTyvarIds boundIds ds
    Some ids => [(iface, ids)]
    None => []

-- the tyvar ids of [monos], in order, iff EVERY mono is a TVar bound by this scheme;
-- None otherwise (a partial vector is a different predicate — never over-reject).
boundTyvarIds : List Int -> List Mono -> Option (List Int)
boundTyvarIds _ [] = Some []
boundTyvarIds boundIds (d::rest) = match normalize d
  TVar cell if containsI (tyvarId cell) boundIds =>
    map (tyvarId cell :: _) (boundTyvarIds boundIds rest)
  _ => None

schemeIds : Scheme -> List Int
schemeIds (Forall ids _ _) = ids

-- shatter vector obligations into (iface, id) pairs — the currency of the
-- coverage subset test.  See checkSigConstraintCoverage's header.
pairsOfVecObls : List (String, List Int) -> List (String, Int)
pairsOfVecObls [] = []
pairsOfVecObls ((iface, ids)::rest) = map (id => (iface, id)) ids
  ++ pairsOfVecObls rest

-- ── signature-constraint soundness check ─────────────────────────────────────
-- For each SIGNED member, the interface constraints its body INFERS on the
-- member's own quantified vars must be covered by the DECLARED `=>` context,
-- closed under the interface `requires` (superclass) graph.  An uncovered
-- inferred constraint ⇒ def-site type_error (the constraint analogue of
-- checkEffectEscape).  Under #619 DECLARED-ONLY this is the SOLE enforcement of the
-- body's inferred obligations: registerSchemeObligations no longer merges them into
-- the scheme, so a body needing more than its signature declares is caught HERE or
-- silently severed.  The inferred obligations are the UNION of the flat-path
-- (schemeObligationsOf over addedObls), the FORWARDED path (forwardedVecObls over the
-- constrained-callee call delta — #619: a signed forwarder that under-declares is no
-- longer backstopped by the union, so it must be checked here), and the marked-path
-- (inferredConstraintIds + ifaceForInferredId) sources — a return-position-method
-- constraint lands only in the last.
-- #607: the PAIR test runs in (iface, var-id) currency on both sides — a multi-param
-- predicate is shattered per argument before the subset test (pairsOfVecObls /
-- declaredPairsFor).  Shattering both sides is sound for a COVERAGE (subset) question
-- — declared `Ix a i` covers inferred `Ix a` and `Ix i` — and it makes the reject
-- direction live at n>=2.  On its own it UNDER-rejects the exotic cross-pairing
-- (declared `Ix a b, Ix c d` vs inferred `Ix a d`: all four pairs a,b,c,d are declared,
-- so pair coverage passes though the JOINT `Ix a d` is undeclared).
-- #619/#607 item 4: reportUncoveredVec closes that gap — an inferred JOINT vector
-- (arity >= 2) whose EXACT argument vector is not among the declared vectors
-- (vector-valued superclass closure, censusSuperCloseVec / superSlotVecOf) is
-- uncovered, and is reported when the pair test did not already flag one of its args.
checkSigConstraintCoverage : List (String, List (String, Mono)) -> List (String, Ty) -> List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> List (String, List Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, Scheme) -> Unit
checkSigConstraintCoverage _ _ _ _ _ [] = ()
checkSigConstraintCoverage regs sigs sigTvMaps callObls addedObls ((m, sch)::rest) =
  let _ = checkSigConstraintOne regs sigs sigTvMaps callObls addedObls m sch
  checkSigConstraintCoverage regs sigs sigTvMaps callObls addedObls rest

checkSigConstraintOne : List (String, List (String, Mono)) -> List (String, Ty) -> List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> List (String, List Mono, Option Loc) -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> String -> Scheme -> Unit
checkSigConstraintOne regs sigs sigTvMaps callObls addedObls m sch = match omLookup m driverState.value.sigTyMapRef.value
  None => ()   -- unsigned member: its inferred constraints are promoted, not checked
  Some _ =>
    let declaredPairs = censusSuperClose driverState.value.superDeclsRef.value (declaredPairsFor m regs)
    let inferredA = pairsOfVecObls (schemeObligationsOf (schemeIds sch) addedObls)
    let inferredFwd = pairsOfVecObls (forwardedVecObls (schemeIds sch) callObls)
    let inferredB = inferredIfacePairs m (schemeIds sch)
    let inferred = dedupPairs (inferredA ++ inferredFwd ++ inferredB)
    let _ = reportUncovered m sigTvMaps declaredPairs inferred
    let declaredVecs = censusSuperCloseVec driverState.value.superDeclsRef.value (declaredSchemeOblsFor sigTvMaps m (schemeIds sch))
    let inferredVecs = schemeObligationsOf (schemeIds sch) addedObls ++ forwardedVecObls (schemeIds sch) callObls
    reportUncoveredVec m sigTvMaps declaredPairs declaredVecs inferredVecs

-- inferred (iface, id) pairs from the marked path: each inferred constraint var id
-- paired with its recovered iface (skip an id whose iface can't be recovered).
inferredIfacePairs : String -> List Int -> List (String, Int)
inferredIfacePairs m boundIds =
  flatMap (pairIface m) (inferredConstraintIds m boundIds)

pairIface : String -> Int -> List (String, Int)
pairIface m id = match ifaceForInferredId m id
  "" => []
  iface => [(iface, id)]

-- the declared (iface, id) constraint pairs of member m: each declared constraint
-- mono normalized to its surviving tyvar id (single-param shape; concrete-grounded
-- constraints — no tyvar — carry no dict slot and are skipped).
declaredPairsFor : String -> List (String, List (String, Mono)) -> List (String, Int)
declaredPairsFor m regs = match lookupAssoc m regs
  None => []
  Some ifaceMonos => flatMap declaredPairOne ifaceMonos

declaredPairOne : (String, Mono) -> List (String, Int)
declaredPairOne (iface, mono) = match normalize mono
  TVar cell => [(iface, tyvarId cell)]
  _ => []

-- UNGATED transitive super-closure over declared (iface, id) slots — mirrors
-- expandSupersPairs but WITHOUT the userIfaceNamesRef gate, so prelude super chains
-- (Applicative requires Mappable, Thenable requires Applicative, …) are included.
-- (The gated expandSupersPairs stays user-only because its slots drive dict arity;
-- this closure only feeds the read-only subset test, so gating is not needed here.)
censusSuperClose : List Decl -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int)
censusSuperClose allDecls declared =
  censusSuperCloseFix allDecls declared declared

censusSuperCloseFix : List Decl -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int)
censusSuperCloseFix allDecls acc frontier =
  let next = dedupPairs (acc ++ flatMap (censusSuperSlotsOf allDecls) frontier)
  if listLen next == listLen acc then
    acc
  else
    censusSuperCloseFix allDecls next (dropPairs (listLen acc) next)

censusSuperSlotsOf : List Decl -> (String, Int) -> List (String, Int)
censusSuperSlotsOf allDecls (iface, id) = match ifaceSupersOf allDecls iface
  None => []
  Some (typarams, supers) =>
    let subst = zipL typarams (replicate (listLen typarams) id)
    flatMap (superSlotOf subst) supers

-- #619/#607 item 4: VECTOR-valued transitive super-closure over declared constraint
-- vectors.  Unlike censusSuperClose (which collapses each predicate to per-arg pairs),
-- this preserves the whole argument vector, so an inferred JOINT predicate can be
-- tested for membership as a unit — the exotic cross-pairing `Ix a d` is NOT a member
-- of {`Ix a b`, `Ix c d`} even though every one of its args appears in some declared
-- predicate.  Ungated (mirrors censusSuperClose) so prelude super chains are included.
censusSuperCloseVec : List Decl -> List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int)
censusSuperCloseVec allDecls declared =
  censusSuperCloseVecFix allDecls declared declared

censusSuperCloseVecFix : List Decl -> List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int)
censusSuperCloseVecFix allDecls acc frontier =
  let next = dedupVecObls (acc ++ flatMap (censusSuperSlotsVecOf allDecls) frontier)
  if listLen next == listLen acc then
    acc
  else
    censusSuperCloseVecFix allDecls next (dropVecObls (listLen acc) next)

-- the direct super vectors of one (iface, ids) predicate: the interface's typarams map
-- POSITIONALLY onto ids (so a multi-param super carries the right per-arg ids, not a
-- single shared one), and each `Super superName params` emits its own arg vector.
censusSuperSlotsVecOf : List Decl -> (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int)
censusSuperSlotsVecOf allDecls (iface, ids) = match ifaceSupersOf allDecls iface
  None => []
  Some (typarams, supers) =>
    let subst = zipL typarams ids
    flatMap (superSlotVecOf subst) supers

superSlotVecOf : List (String, Int) -> Super -> List (String, List Int)
superSlotVecOf subst (Super superName params) =
  let mapped = filterMaybeIds (map (p => lookupAssoc p subst) params)
  if listLen mapped == listLen params then [(superName, mapped)] else []

dropVecObls : Int -> List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int)
dropVecObls n xs
  | n <= 0 = xs
  | otherwise = match xs
    [] => []
    _::rest => dropVecObls (n - 1) rest

-- one type_error per inferred JOINT vector (arity >= 2) whose exact vector the declared
-- (super-closed) set does not contain.  Skipped when the pair test already flagged one
-- of the vector's args (allPairsIn False) — so a genuinely absent constraint reports
-- ONCE, via reportUncovered, and only the cross-pairing (all args present, joint
-- vector absent) reports here.  Arity < 2 is fully handled by the pair test.
reportUncoveredVec : String -> List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, List Int) -> List (String, List Int) -> Unit
reportUncoveredVec _ _ _ _ [] = ()
reportUncoveredVec m sigTvMaps declaredPairs declaredVecs ((iface, ids)::rest) =
  let _ = if listLen ids < 2 then
    ()
  else if containsVecObl (iface, ids) declaredVecs then
    ()
  else if not (allPairsIn iface ids declaredPairs) then
    ()
  else
    pushTypeError "T-MISSING-CONSTRAINT" (missingConstraintVecMsg m iface (map (varNameForId sigTvMaps m) ids))
  reportUncoveredVec m sigTvMaps declaredPairs declaredVecs rest

allPairsIn : String -> List Int -> List (String, Int) -> Bool
allPairsIn iface ids declaredPairs =
  allList (id => containsPair (iface, id) declaredPairs) ids

missingConstraintVecMsg : String -> String -> List String -> String
missingConstraintVecMsg name iface varNames =
  let pred = joinWith " " (iface::varNames)
  "Could not deduce '\{pred}' from the signature of '\{name}'. Its body requires it; add '\{pred} =>' to the declared type"

-- one type_error per inferred (iface, id) not covered by the declared-closed set.
reportUncovered : String -> List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> List (String, Int) -> List (String, Int) -> Unit
reportUncovered _ _ _ [] = ()
reportUncovered m sigTvMaps declared ((iface, id)::rest) =
  let _ = if containsPair (iface, id) declared then
    ()
  else
    pushTypeError "T-MISSING-CONSTRAINT" (missingConstraintMsg m iface (varNameForId sigTvMaps m id))
  reportUncovered m sigTvMaps declared rest

containsPair : (String, Int) -> List (String, Int) -> Bool
containsPair _ [] = False
containsPair (i, d) ((i2, d2)::rest) = i == i2 && d == d2
  || containsPair (i, d) rest

-- recover the sig-tyvar NAME for a constraint var id (for a readable message);
-- falls back to "a" if the id isn't found among the member's sig tyvars.
varNameForId : List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> String -> Int -> String
varNameForId sigTvMaps m id = match findSigTvMap m sigTvMaps
  None => "a"
  Some tvMap => match findTvName id tvMap
    None => "a"
    Some n => n

findSigTvMap : String -> List (String, Ty, List (String, Mono)) -> Option (List (String, Mono))
findSigTvMap _ [] = None
findSigTvMap m ((n, _, tvMap)::rest)
  | n == m = Some tvMap
  | otherwise = findSigTvMap m rest

findTvName : Int -> List (String, Mono) -> Option String
findTvName _ [] = None
findTvName id ((n, mono)::rest) = match normalize mono
  TVar cell => if tyvarId cell == id then Some n else findTvName id rest
  _ => findTvName id rest

missingConstraintMsg : String -> String -> String -> String
missingConstraintMsg name iface varName = "Could not deduce '\{iface} \{varName}' from the signature of '\{name}'. Its body requires it; add '\{iface} \{varName} =>' to the declared type"

-- the member's own quantified tyvar ids that a body method site / propagated
-- constrained-call sits at (deduped, slot order = discovery order)
inferredConstraintIds : String -> List Int -> List Int
inferredConstraintIds m boundIds =
  dedupI
    (flatMap (boundTvarId boundIds) (sitesFor m perRun.value.methodSiteFns.value) ++ flatMap (monosTvarIds boundIds) (sitesFor m perRun.value.dictAppFns.value))

monosTvarIds : List Int -> List Mono -> List Int
monosTvarIds boundIds monos = flatMap (boundTvarId boundIds) monos

-- [mono] normalizing to a tyvar this scheme generalized → that (constraint var)
-- id; a pinned-concrete or foreign var → none
boundTvarId : List Int -> Mono -> List Int
boundTvarId boundIds mono = match normalize mono
  TVar cell => if containsI (tyvarId cell) boundIds then [tyvarId cell] else []
  _ => []

registerInferredFor : String -> List Int -> Unit
registerInferredFor _ [] = ()
registerInferredFor m ids =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.funConstraintsRef ((m, ids)::perRun.value.funConstraintsRef.value)
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.funConstraintIfacesRef ((m, map (ifaceForInferredId m) ids)::perRun.value.funConstraintIfacesRef.value)
  let _ = registerActiveDictVars m 0 ids
  setRef perRun.value.promotedRef (m::perRun.value.promotedRef.value)

-- #23: the iface name for an inferred constraint var.  `ifaceForConstraintId` only
-- recovers it from a pendingImplObligation whose `occ` is a BARE `TVar id`; a
-- constraint induced by a `fromInt`/method site records a COMPOUND occ (the whole
-- method type), so it returns "" — leaving the call site's dict route unkeyed
-- (`routeUndeterminedTop ""` → RNone → a NULL Num dict word → build SIGTRAP, while
-- `medaka run` is correct).  Two FALLBACKS recover the name when ifaceForConstraintId
-- returns "" (so existing non-"" routes stay byte-identical):
--   (1) `schemeObligationsRef` pairs each scheme var with its iface via
--       `dispatchMonosOf` (robust to compound occs); covers a fn whose own body has
--       the literal/method site (`app2 f = f 2 3`).
--   (2) a constraint FORWARDED from a constrained callee (`useApp g = app2 g`) never
--       lands in pendingImplObligations/schemeObligations — its iface lives in the
--       `pendingDictApps` entry the callee's inferDictAtFound recorded (slot-parallel
--       monos/ifaces).  Scan it for the var at a slot whose iface is now known.
-- Both run AFTER the callee's own constraints are registered (topological SCC order),
-- so the forwarded callee's ifaces are populated.
ifaceForInferredId : String -> Int -> String
ifaceForInferredId m id =
  let direct = ifaceForConstraintId id
  if direct != "" then direct
  else match lookupSchemeIface m id perRun.value.schemeObligationsRef.value
    Some s => s
    None => ifaceFromDictApps id perRun.value.pendingDictApps.value

lookupSchemeIface : String -> Int -> List (String, List (String, List Int)) -> Option String
lookupSchemeIface _ _ [] = None
lookupSchemeIface m id ((m2, obs)::rest)
  | m == m2 = match lookupIfaceById id obs
    Some iface => Some iface
    None => lookupSchemeIface m id rest
  | otherwise = lookupSchemeIface m id rest

-- the iface of the first recorded predicate whose ARGUMENT VECTOR mentions [id].
-- (#607: an obligation's ids became a vector; at arity 1 this is the old `id == oid`.)
-- This per-id recovery is inherently 1-ary — it answers "which interface constrains
-- this var", which for a multi-param predicate has no single right answer; it feeds
-- the dict-slot/route tables, whose per-tyvar shattering is punch-list item 3.
lookupIfaceById : Int -> List (String, List Int) -> Option String
lookupIfaceById _ [] = None
lookupIfaceById id ((iface, oids)::rest)
  | containsI id oids = Some iface
  | otherwise = lookupIfaceById id rest

-- Scan pendingDictApps for [id] at a slot (bare-TVar mono) whose iface is non-empty.
ifaceFromDictApps : Int -> List (Ref (List Route), List Mono, List String) -> String
ifaceFromDictApps _ [] = ""
ifaceFromDictApps id ((_, monos, ifaces)::rest) = match ifaceAtMonoId id monos ifaces
  Some s => s
  None => ifaceFromDictApps id rest

ifaceAtMonoId : Int -> List Mono -> List String -> Option String
ifaceAtMonoId _ [] _ = None
ifaceAtMonoId _ _ [] = None
ifaceAtMonoId id (m::ms) (i::is) = match normalize m
  TVar cell =>
    if tyvarId cell == id && i != "" then
      Some i
    else
      ifaceAtMonoId id ms is
  _ => ifaceAtMonoId id ms is

-- recover the interface name of an INFERRED constraint var (unsignatured fn like
-- `f x = x + 1`): the var was recorded in a pendingImplObligation by the method
-- occurrence that induced the constraint (`+` → a `Num <thisvar>` entry).  Slot-
-- aligned with registerInferredFor's id list.  "" if unresolvable — recordCallObligations
-- still pairs it positionally, and checkOneCallObligation with an empty iface finds no
-- matching impl head ⇒ it only fires for a CONCRETE-head mono whose iface genuinely had
-- no impl, which for an inferred Num constraint is exactly the hole we close.  A still-
-- polymorphic head defers regardless of iface, so a blank iface never over-rejects.
ifaceForConstraintId : Int -> String
ifaceForConstraintId id =
  ifaceForConstraintIdGo id perRun.value.pendingImplObligations.value

ifaceForConstraintIdGo : Int -> List (String, List String, Ty, Mono, Option Loc) -> String
ifaceForConstraintIdGo _ [] = ""
ifaceForConstraintIdGo id ((iface, _, _, occ, _)::rest) = match normalize occ
  TVar cell =>
    if tyvarId cell == id then
      iface
    else
      ifaceForConstraintIdGo id rest
  _ => ifaceForConstraintIdGo id rest

registerActiveDictVars : String -> Int -> List Int -> Unit
registerActiveDictVars _ _ [] = ()
registerActiveDictVars m slot (id::rest) =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.activeDictVars ((id, dictParamName m slot)::perRun.value.activeDictVars.value)
  registerActiveDictVars m (slot + 1) rest

-- Render accumulated type errors as `TYPE ERROR: <msg>` lines (in order of
-- occurrence — typeErrors accumulates newest-first, so reverse).
typeErrorLines : List String -> List String
typeErrorLines [] = []
typeErrorLines (e::rest) = "TYPE ERROR: " ++ e :: typeErrorLines rest

export checkToLines : List Decl -> String
checkToLines prog =
  let schemes = checkProgramSchemes [] prog
  let _ = runFinalChecks prog prog
  let errs = reverseL perRun.value.typeErrors.value
  match errs
    [] => joinNl (schemeLines schemes)
    _ => joinNl (typeErrorLines (map tcMsg errs))
-- coherence over the WHOLE prog: this path (typecheck_main single-arg) is
-- prelude-free, so every impl is a user impl (mirrors check_program_no_prelude).

-- #415 item 1: the seeding PREAMBLE every runtime-seeded entry point runs before its
-- typecheck pass — the head half of the sandwich whose tail is `runFinalChecks`.
-- [oracleDecls] is the decl set the ctor oracle is built from: the ONE axis the callers
-- legitimately differ on (the flat paths pass `prog`; checkModuleFullDiags passes
-- `prog ++ accData` own-module-FIRST — see the P0-9 note there, that order is load-bearing).
-- matchWarnings is cleared explicitly because resetState does NOT clear it.
-- Call ORDER is preserved from the four bodies this replaces.
seedCheckRun : List Decl -> Unit
seedCheckRun oracleDecls =
  let _ = setRef driverState.value.implInferEnabled False
  let _ = setRef driverState.value.matchWarnings []
  setRef driverState.value.matchOracle (buildOracle oracleDecls)

-- #415 item 1: seed-and-run for the three FLAT runtime-seeded entry points
-- (checkToLinesWithRuntime / checkErrorsWithRuntime / checkProgramDiags).  All three
-- used to repeat this body verbatim under a "Mirrors checkToLinesWithRuntime's
-- side-effects EXACTLY" comment — a mirror obligation enforced only by that comment.
-- They now differ ONLY in what they HARVEST from the accumulators afterwards, so the
-- seeding+coherence they share has exactly one body and cannot drift.
-- ⚠️ trap #5, same as runFinalChecks: coherence sees the driver-staged USER decls only,
-- NEVER `prog`.  A user impl deliberately overrides a seeded prelude impl, so feeding
-- the seeded set to checkCoherence false-positives on the stdlib itself.
seedAndCheckSplit : List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
seedAndCheckSplit runtimeDecls coreProg userProg =
  let prog = coreProg ++ userProg
  let _ = seedCheckRun prog
  let schemes = checkProgramSeededSplit (externSchemes runtimeDecls) coreProg userProg
  let _ = runFinalChecks driverState.value.coherenceUserDecls.value prog
  schemes

-- like checkToLines but seeds runtime.mdk's externs (for typechecking core.mdk,
-- which references putStrLn / etc. declared in runtime.mdk).  [coreProg] is the PRELUDE
-- the caller would otherwise have pre-concatenated onto [userProg]; keeping the two
-- apart is what lets the flat path scope shadow-hood per module (SHADOW-SEMANTICS S1 —
-- see flatShadowScopingRef).  Prelude-free callers pass [].
export checkToLinesWithRuntime : List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> String
checkToLinesWithRuntime runtimeDecls coreProg userProg =
  let schemes = seedAndCheckSplit runtimeDecls coreProg userProg
  let errs = reverseL perRun.value.typeErrors.value
  let warns = map tcMsg (reverseL driverState.value.matchWarnings.value)
  match errs
    [] => joinNl (schemeLines schemes ++ warns)
    _ => joinNl (typeErrorLines (map tcMsg errs))
-- B5: seed the exhaustiveness oracle and clear any stale match warnings so the
-- composed check.mdk path surfaces non-exhaustive-match warnings, mirroring the
-- OCaml oracle's `medaka check` output.

-- coherence over the USER decls the driver staged (NO prelude) — empty when the
-- driver staged nothing (golden/profile paths) ⇒ no check, byte-identical.

-- G1 (SOUNDNESS): does the runtime-seeded typecheck pass produce any TYPE ERROR?
-- Shares checkToLinesWithRuntime's seeding + coherence BY CONSTRUCTION (the one
-- seedAndCheckSplit body), and differs only in the harvest: a Bool keyed on the
-- typeErrors accumulator, ignoring the success-path scheme dump and the match WARNINGS
-- (which are not errors).  Used by check.mdk's checkHasErrors to gate the CLI exit code
-- WITHOUT altering runCheck's reported stdout.
export checkErrorsWithRuntime : List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> Bool
checkErrorsWithRuntime runtimeDecls coreProg userProg =
  let _ = seedAndCheckSplit runtimeDecls coreProg userProg
  match perRun.value.typeErrors.value
    [] => False
    _ => True

-- ── type-aware non-exhaustive-match check (the `check_match` driver) ────────
-- Run the full typecheck pass over [prog] (runtime externs seeded, NO prelude —
-- mirrors the reference's check_program_no_prelude oracle) and return one
-- `Warning: non-exhaustive match …` line per `match` that isn't exhaustive.
-- The oracle is built from the program's own data decls + the syntactic builtins
-- (same set check_match's env-backed oracle has in the no-prelude path).  Diffs
-- against `dev/diagdump.exe --check-match` (the harness sorts).
export checkMatchToLines : List Decl -> List Decl -> String
checkMatchToLines runtimeDecls prog =
  let _ = setRef driverState.value.matchWarnings []
  let _ = setRef driverState.value.matchOracle (buildOracle prog)
  let _ = checkProgramSeeded (externSchemes runtimeDecls) prog
  joinNl (map tcMsg (reverseL driverState.value.matchWarnings.value))

-- ── diagnostics entry point (compiler/diagnostics.mdk bridge) ────────────
-- Run the full typecheck pass (runtime-seeded) over [prog] and return the
-- accumulated errors and non-exhaustive-match warnings as `TcDiag`s — each
-- carrying its own code, span (B.10.2b / S4), message, and optional help/fix —
-- so diagnostics.mdk maps them straight to structured `Diag` values.
export checkProgramDiags : List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> (List TcDiag, List TcDiag)
checkProgramDiags runtimeDecls coreProg userProg =
  let _ = seedAndCheckSplit runtimeDecls coreProg userProg
  (
    reverseL perRun.value.typeErrors.value,
    reverseL driverState.value.matchWarnings.value,
  )

-- ── multi-module typecheck (bootstrap front-end) ───────────────────────────
-- Threads per-module exports in dependency order.  The prelude (core) is checked
-- ONCE and ALL its schemes + ctors seed every module (it's implicitly visible);
-- each compiler module then contributes only its PUBLIC value schemes — private
-- helpers stay module-local, so same-named helpers across modules never collide
-- (the per-module-frame property, without a frame) — plus its public ctors.
-- Returns each module's OWN value schemes, in load order.

-- typecheck one program with seeded value schemes + accumulated data decls.
-- Like checkProgramSeeded but ALSO re-registers prior modules' public data decls
-- (`accData ++ prog`) so imported constructors AND named-field-variant record
-- info (e.g. ast's DInterface) resolve.  Returns the module's own value schemes.
checkModuleFull : List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
checkModuleFull seedVars accData prog =
  checkModuleFullImpl "" seedVars accData [] prog

-- E5: like checkModuleFull but takes [implDecls] (core + every EARLIER module +
-- this module) as the impl-body inference universe.  Under implInferEnabled (now ON
-- unconditionally) it ALSO infers THIS module's parametric impl
-- bodies — mirroring checkProgramSeeded's inferImplBodiesIfEnabled — so their
-- ARG-position ELEMENT dispatch (`eq`/`compare`/… on the impl head tyvar) reaches
-- inferMethodAt and lands in pendingArgStamps, which elabModuleStamp's
-- resolveArgStamps then stamps RDict (via the requires dict registered by
-- registerImplRequires).  [implDecls] gives ifaceMethodTy the interface decls
-- (core's Eq/Ord/…) AND lets a body dispatch against an earlier module's impl.
checkModuleFullImpl : String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List Decl -> List (String, Scheme)
checkModuleFullImpl mid seedVars accData implDecls prog =
  checkBodyImpl seedVars (Module mid accData implDecls) prog
-- C5: compute this module's standalone-shadow set BEFORE inference, so the
-- inferMethodAt arg-position record can gate on it (resetState cleared it via
-- pendingRLocalSites, but standaloneValuesRef is set fresh here per module).
--
-- Two flavours of standalone-shadow, BOTH needing RLocal recording so a method
-- call on a no-impl receiver routes to the standalone instead of mis-dispatching:
--   • IMPORTER shadow (`importerShadows`): a name imported from a sibling that is
--     also an interface method (box importing map's `toList`).  Its scheme must be
--     REBOUND to the interface-method scheme (`shadowMethodSchemes`) so the call
--     site types polymorphically; RLocal then redirects the no-impl receiver back.
--   • DEFINER shadow (`definerShadows`): a name THIS module defines as a top-level
--     funDef that ALSO names an interface method (map-as-root's own `toList` /
--     `isEmpty`).  The MARK pass (`buildStandaloneShadowsGraph`) rewrites these to
--     `EMethodAt`, so without a matching RLocal record the route falls back to the
--     result-type `RKey` (e.g. `toList`'s `List` result), dispatching the method on
--     the WRONG impl (List's `toList = identity`) → `debug`/`display` recurse on the
--     unchanged receiver forever (Map Debug/Eq/Ord impl bodies call standalone
--     `toList`).  These must be RECORDED for RLocal but must NOT be rebound to the
--     method scheme — their local standalone scheme (`Map k v -> …`) is what the
--     impl body types against.

-- E6: re-install the cross-module constraint accumulator that resetState just
-- wiped, so this module's `=>`-constrained call sites into EARLIER modules resolve.
-- Post-ARGSTAMP-UNIFY (Phase 4): eval and emit both thread dicts, so this runs
-- UNCONDITIONALLY on every path (single elaboration mode).

-- resetState wiped methodIfaceParamsRef; re-register it from the FULL impl
-- universe [implDecls] (core/earlier modules ++ this prog) so resolveSite's
-- ifaceParamMonos can recover a multi-param interface's full param monos (the
-- element grounding `e` for `FromEntries c e`).  The flat checkProgramSeeded does
-- the same against its whole-program [prog].  Without it the module path leaves the
-- table empty → ifaceParamMonos returns None → a Set/Map-literal return-position
-- `fromEntries` route drops its `Ord` element dict → null dict → SIGSEGV.

-- C5: a standalone-shadow method name (box's `toList`/`isEmpty`) is bound in
-- seedVars to its imported MONOMORPHIC standalone scheme (`Box k v -> …`), which
-- would shadow the polymorphic interface-method scheme and mis-type a genuine
-- method call (`isEmpty [1,2,3]` unifying List with Box).  Re-bind those names to
-- the graph-wide METHOD scheme so the call site types against the method (oracle
-- behaviour); RLocal then redirects only the no-impl receiver back to the standalone
-- at eval.  Mirrors lib/typecheck.ml: the EApp method-head arm types against the
-- method scheme even when standalone_values holds the name.
-- DEFINER shadows are EXCLUDED here: the module's OWN funDef (map-root's `toList`)
-- must type against its local standalone scheme (`Map k v -> List (k, v)`), not the
-- imported method scheme — the impl bodies call it ON a Map.  Only IMPORTER shadows
-- (imported-from-sibling method names) get the method-scheme rebind.

-- E5: infer this module's parametric impl bodies (gated, emit path only), seeing
-- [implDecls] for interface method types + cross-module impl dispatch.  The sites
-- it records stay in pendingArgStamps/pendingSites for elabModuleStamp to resolve.

-- C8(b): also infer THIS module's interface DEFAULT bodies (gated identically to
-- the single-file path's inferDefaultBodiesIfEnabled) so a default body's type
-- error is caught on the module path too — mirrors checkProgramSeeded.

-- Phase 4.55 (mirror checkProgramSeeded + lib/typecheck.ml:4254-4262): infer this
-- module's prop bodies so their prepass-marked EDictAt/EMethodAt routes resolve.
-- The multi-module test path (runPropsMulti) evaluates the ELABORATED prop body
-- against the dict-passed env, so the body's calls into the file's own promoted
-- `=>`-constrained fns (set's `wellFormed`/`fromList`) need their dict-arg routes
-- filled here — otherwise the call under-applies the dict-passed callee → not VBool
-- → every prop "fails".  funDefs ignores DProp, so without this the module path
-- never typed props at all.

-- E6: fold THIS module's freshly-registered fn-constraints into the cross-module
-- accumulator so a LATER module's call site finds them after its own reset.
-- Post-ARGSTAMP-UNIFY (Phase 4): unconditional on every path (single elaboration mode).

-- Shape A (gap #44): ground a container literal's free result-element var THROUGH
-- the unique impl's method type, so this module's pending sites/arg-stamps resolve
-- against the GROUNDED element (Set Int, not Set _a).  [implDecls] is the full impl
-- universe (core ++ earlier modules ++ prog), so the Set/Map impls are visible.

-- gate: per-module impl-body inference runs only on the emit path (implInferEnabled).
-- allProg = [implDecls] (interfaces + accumulated impls); rpNames computed over it.
inferModuleImplBodiesIfEnabled : TcEnv -> List Decl -> List Decl -> Unit
inferModuleImplBodiesIfEnabled env implDecls prog
  | driverState.value.implInferEnabled.value =
    inferImplBodies env implDecls (returnPosMethodNames implDecls) prog
  | otherwise = ()

-- public top-level value names (the pub flag may sit on the sig, def, or extern).
-- C8(a): a PUBLIC interface ALSO exports its method names so an importer can see
-- their schemes (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's pub_iface_schemes — methods of an
-- `is_pub = true` interface are appended to the exported value schemes).
publicValNames : List Decl -> List String
publicValNames [] = []
publicValNames ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) = publicValNames [d] ++ publicValNames rest
publicValNames ((DFunDef True n _ _)::rest) = n :: publicValNames rest
publicValNames ((DTypeSig True n _)::rest) = n :: publicValNames rest
publicValNames ((DExtern True n _)::rest) = n :: publicValNames rest
publicValNames ((DInterface { pub = True, methods, ... })::rest) = ifaceMethodNames methods
  ++ publicValNames rest
publicValNames (_::rest) = publicValNames rest

ifaceMethodNames : List IfaceMethod -> List String
ifaceMethodNames [] = []
ifaceMethodNames ((IfaceMethod n _ _)::rest) = n :: ifaceMethodNames rest

-- C5: every interface method name declared anywhere in [decls] (unwrapping DAttrib).
allIfaceMethodNames : List Decl -> List String
allIfaceMethodNames [] = []
allIfaceMethodNames ((DAttrib _ d)::rest) = allIfaceMethodNames [d]
  ++ allIfaceMethodNames rest
allIfaceMethodNames ((DInterface { methods, ... })::rest) = ifaceMethodNames methods
  ++ allIfaceMethodNames rest
allIfaceMethodNames (_::rest) = allIfaceMethodNames rest

-- C5 (DEFINER shadow): the names THIS module declares as a top-level funDef that
-- ALSO name an interface method visible in [implDecls] (the imported prelude/core).
-- E.g. map-as-root defines `toList`/`isEmpty`, which shadow Foldable's methods.  The
-- MARK pass (`buildStandaloneShadowsGraph`) rewrites occurrences of these to
-- `EMethodAt`, so they MUST be in `standaloneValuesRef` for `recordRLocalSite` to
-- record an RLocal candidate — otherwise the route falls back to the method's
-- RESULT-type RKey (`toList`'s `List`) and the method dispatches on the WRONG impl.
-- Unlike importer shadows these keep their LOCAL standalone scheme (NOT rebound to the
-- method scheme), since the impl bodies apply them to the concrete receiver.
buildDefinerShadows : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String
buildDefinerShadows implDecls prog =
  -- A method name that is ALSO bound by a LOCAL pattern anywhere in the module (a
  -- clause/lambda param, a let/match binder) is NOT a definer shadow at that
  -- occurrence: lexical scope resolves it to the local.  Without this guard the
  -- definer-shadow application path (inferDefinerShadowVarApp / inferDefinerShadowApp)
  -- types `applyEq eq x y = eq x y` — where `eq` is a PARAMETER — against the TOP-LEVEL
  -- standalone `eq : Int -> Int -> Int`, ignoring the binder entirely.  Mirrors the
  -- MARK pass's identical exclusion (marker.mdk's shadowRenames `keepNotIn
  -- (localBoundNames prog)`), so check/run/build agree on which occurrences are shadows.
  let methodNames = allIfaceMethodNames implDecls
  let localNames = dedup (map fst (funDefs prog))
  let direct = filter (n => contains n methodNames) localNames
  -- P0-18: on the EMIT path this module's funDefs are MANGLED, so `direct` finds no
  -- shadow.  For each mangled funDef that the mangle map flags as an iface-method
  -- shadow, register the BARE method name — that is the name the mark pass stamped
  -- on the `EMethodAt`, which `definerShadowArgHead`/`resolveRLocalSite` key off.
  let bares = flatMap (n => match lookupAssoc n driverState.value.mangledShadowMapRef.value
    Some bare => [bare]
    None => []) localNames
  removeAllS (localBoundNames prog) (dedup (direct ++ bares))

-- #154 PR1: fold THIS module's decls into the four persistent universe accumulators
-- (see their declarations near shadowKeyTableRef).  Called from checkBodyImpl's Module
-- arm BEFORE the shadow/key/completeness reads, so this module's own decls are visible
-- there — exactly as `fullUniverse` (which appended prog0) was.  keyEntryOf/bucketKeyEntries
-- and requiredMethodNames are the SAME builders buildKeyTable / ifaceRequiredMethods use,
-- so the accumulated tables are entry-for-entry the per-module rebuild.
appendUniverseAccums : List Decl -> Unit
appendUniverseAccums prog =
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeIfaceMethodsRef (namesToSet (allIfaceMethodNames prog) crossRun.value.universeIfaceMethodsRef.value)
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeFunNamesRef (namesToSet (map fst (funDefs prog)) crossRun.value.universeFunNamesRef.value)
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeKeyBucketsRef (bucketKeyEntries (flatMap keyEntryOf prog) crossRun.value.universeKeyBucketsRef.value)
  let _ = setRef
    crossRun.value.universeIfaceRequiredRef
    (insertIfaceRequired prog crossRun.value.universeIfaceRequiredRef.value)
  -- #154 PR2 (site #3): grow the methodIfaceParams / registeredIfaces accumulators by
  -- THIS module's interfaces, in lockstep (one decl walk).
  let acc = insertMethodIfaceParams prog (crossRun.value.universeMethodIfaceParamsRef.value, crossRun.value.universeRegisteredIfacesRef.value)
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeMethodIfaceParamsRef (fst acc)
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeRegisteredIfacesRef (snd acc)
  -- #154 PR-B: grow the dispatch-index accumulator by THIS module's interface indices.
  -- APPEND (universe first) so the accumulated list stays in the same core-first order the
  -- old `argDispatchIndices (accAll ++ prog)` flatMap produced; the sole reader is a
  -- first-wins lookupAssoc, so a prior-module entry this module never names is never queried.
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeMethodDispatchIdxRef (crossRun.value.universeMethodDispatchIdxRef.value ++ argDispatchIndices prog)
  -- #154 PR3 (sites #6/#7): grow the keyed obligation impl universe by THIS module's
  -- impls (append to keep forward declaration order within each bucket).  Uses the
  -- SAME `implDeclsWithReqs` (iface, tys, reqs) triples the old rebuild consumed, so
  -- the accumulated buckets are entry-for-entry that per-module `fullUniverse` rebuild.
  let obUni = growImplUniverse (implDeclsWithReqs prog) (ImplUniverse crossRun.value.obUnivConcreteRef.value crossRun.value.obUnivHeadlessRef.value crossRun.value.obUnivIfaceTagsRef.value)
  match obUni
    ImplUniverse c h t =>
      let _ = setRef crossRun.value.obUnivConcreteRef c
      let _ = setRef crossRun.value.obUnivHeadlessRef h
      setRef crossRun.value.obUnivIfaceTagsRef t

-- #154 PR-A: grow the persistent DATA-universe by [pubDecls] (THIS module's public data).
-- `registerData` / `registerVariants` hardcode the five working table refs, so grow by
-- pointing those refs at the persistent values, registering pubDecls over the accumulated
-- ctor env, and snapshotting all six results back.  Called at the END of checkBodyImpl's
-- Module arm, where the working table refs are DEAD (this module's inference is done and the
-- next module's resetState wipes them), so the clobber is invisible.  Registers each public
-- decl exactly ONCE across the whole run — the O(N) replacing the Module arm's old
-- per-module O(|accData|) re-registration.  Only publicDataDecls are folded in (private data
-- stays module-local), matching foldModules' `accData ++ publicDataDecls prog` threading.
-- #415 item 3: the cross-module DATA universe ⇄ per-run working tables copy, which was
-- open-coded as a 5-way lockstep mirror at THREE sites (the Module arm of the data-env
-- setup + appendDataUniverse's load and its store-back).  Adding a sixth universe table
-- meant remembering all three — the "silently absent from the other side" shape.  It now
-- has one body per direction, and the two are exact inverses of each other.
-- ⚠️ universeDataEnv is deliberately NOT copied here: it is threaded through
-- `registerAllData` by the CALLER (it is that call's env argument and its result), so it
-- has no meaning as a blind copy.
loadDataUniverse : Unit -> Unit
loadDataUniverse _ =
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.recordsRef crossRun.value.universeRecords.value
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.recordByNameRef crossRun.value.universeRecordByName.value
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.fieldOwnersRef crossRun.value.universeFieldOwners.value
  let _ = setRef perRun.value.dataParamKindsRef crossRun.value.universeDataParamKinds.value
  setRef perRun.value.aliasTableRef crossRun.value.universeAliasTable.value

-- the exact inverse of loadDataUniverse (same five tables, same order).
storeDataUniverse : Unit -> Unit
storeDataUniverse _ =
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeRecords perRun.value.recordsRef.value
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeRecordByName perRun.value.recordByNameRef.value
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeFieldOwners perRun.value.fieldOwnersRef.value
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeDataParamKinds perRun.value.dataParamKindsRef.value
  setRef crossRun.value.universeAliasTable perRun.value.aliasTableRef.value

appendDataUniverse : List Decl -> Unit
appendDataUniverse pubDecls =
  let _ = loadDataUniverse ()
  let env2 = registerAllData crossRun.value.universeDataEnv.value pubDecls
  let _ = storeDataUniverse ()
  let _ = setRef crossRun.value.universeDataEnv env2
  -- #674: keep the DECLS too (universeDataEnv is a bare-name ctor env — last-write-wins,
  -- LOSSY across a duplicate ctor name — so it cannot recover a SPECIFIC module's ctor
  -- for the import-scoped overlay).  checkBodyImpl re-registers the ctors of the types
  -- THIS module imports with `(..)` from these decls; available on BOTH the check path
  -- AND the elaborate/emit path (where the Module `accData` field is dead `[]`, #154 PR-C).
  setRef crossRun.value.universeDataDecls (crossRun.value.universeDataDecls.value ++ pubDecls)

-- #154 PR1: buildDefinerShadows with the `allIfaceMethodNames fullUniverse` scan
-- replaced by the persistent interface-method membership SET.  `direct` still filters
-- THIS module's localNames in declaration order, so `omHasKey n ifaceSet` is a
-- point-for-point substitute for `contains n methodNames` — same set, same order,
-- byte-identical output.
definerShadowsFromSet : OrdMap Unit -> List Decl -> List String
definerShadowsFromSet ifaceSet prog =
  let localNames = dedup (map fst (funDefs prog))
  let direct = filter (n => omHasKey n ifaceSet) localNames
  let bares = flatMap (n => match lookupAssoc n driverState.value.mangledShadowMapRef.value
    Some bare => [bare]
    None => []) localNames
  removeAllS (localBoundNames prog) (dedup (direct ++ bares))

-- #154 PR1: buildStandaloneShadows with BOTH O(N) universe scans
-- (`allIfaceMethodNames (implDecls ++ prog)` and `funDefs implDecls`) replaced by the
-- persistent interface-method and top-level-fn membership sets.  A name qualifies when
-- it is a universe fn, an interface method, and NOT defined in THIS module — the
-- `not (contains n localNames)` guard drops both this module's own funDefs (which the
-- set now includes) exactly as the original excluded them, so the result set is
-- identical.  It is consumed only by membership (standaloneValuesRef) and by unique-name
-- first-match lookup (shadowStandaloneSchemesRef), so the sorted `omKeys` iteration order
-- is immaterial to every downstream reader.  P0-18 (a shadowed interface declared
-- LOCALLY in the importing module) is preserved: appendUniverseAccums folds prog0's own
-- interface methods into the set, so a local `interface Sizeable … size` is visible here
-- exactly as the old `allIfaceMethodNames (implDecls ++ prog)` (which included prog) made it.
--
-- #411 (row 21, S4): the `mangled` arm is the IMPORTER peer of the recovery
-- definerShadowsFromSet has carried since P0-18 (its `bares`), and its absence WAS the
-- bug.  On the EMIT path every funDef is mangled (`prov__size`), so `direct` — which
-- intersects the MANGLED universe fn names against the BARE interface-method names —
-- matches nothing and this returned []; importerShadows was therefore empty on emit, so
-- standaloneValuesRef never listed the name, recordRLocalSite never recorded a site, and
-- resolveArgStamp's `RKey Int` CLOBBERED the mark pass's correct `RLocal prov__size`
-- seed → `emitDefaultRKey` → `no impl of method 'size' for type 'Int'` on a valid
-- program.  The definer twin (d4) never broke precisely because it had this arm.
-- Recovering the bare method name via the mangle map restores the site, and
-- resolveRLocalSite then applies the ORDINARY Fork-1 importer rule to it (live impl at
-- the receiver head ⇒ keep the RKey dispatch; no impl ⇒ RLocal standalone) — so i4's
-- `isEmpty [1, 2]` still reaches `Foldable.isEmpty`.  Empty on the un-mangled run/check
-- path (the map is empty there) → `direct` alone → byte-identical.
standaloneShadowsFromSet : OrdMap Unit -> OrdMap Unit -> List Decl -> List String
standaloneShadowsFromSet ifaceSet fnSet prog =
  let localNames = map fst (funDefs prog)
  let direct = filter (n => omHasKey n ifaceSet && not (contains n localNames)) (omKeys fnSet)
  let mangled = flatMap (n => match lookupAssoc n driverState.value.mangledShadowMapRef.value
    Some bare => if contains n localNames then [] else [bare]
    None => []) (omKeys fnSet)
  dedup (direct ++ mangled)

-- C5: graph-wide standalone-shadow names — every top-level funDef name in [userDecls]
-- (the user modules, NOT core) that is also an interface method name anywhere in
-- [allDecls].  Used by the MARK pass to rewrite such occurrences to EMethodAt (so the
-- route ref exists for resolveRLocalSites to stamp).  Excludes core's own decls as the
-- funDef source so core's elaboration (no standalone shadows there) is untouched.
-- P0-18: recover, per (module, iface-method) pair, the mangled standalone symbol the
-- mangler produced for a definer-shadow funDef.  Forward-constructs `mangledName mid
-- method` (using the KNOWN mid — exact, not lossy prefix-stripping) and checks it
-- against the module's actual funDef names; a hit is a `(mangledSymbol ↦ bareMethod)`
-- entry.  On the un-mangled path no funDef matches → [] → every consumer unchanged.
computeMangledShadowMap : List Decl -> List (String, List Decl) -> List (String, String)
computeMangledShadowMap allDecls units =
  let methodNames = allIfaceMethodNames allDecls
  flatMap (unitMangledShadows methodNames) units

unitMangledShadows : List String -> (String, List Decl) -> List (String, String)
unitMangledShadows methodNames (mid, decls) =
  let fns = dedup (map fst (funDefs decls))
  flatMap
    (m => if contains (mangledName mid m) fns then [(mangledName mid m, m)] else [])
    methodNames

buildStandaloneShadowsGraph : List Decl -> List Decl -> List String
buildStandaloneShadowsGraph allDecls userDecls =
  let methodNames = allIfaceMethodNames allDecls
  let fns = dedup (map fst (funDefs userDecls))
  let direct = filter (n => contains n methodNames) fns
  -- P0-18: on the EMIT path the funDef names are MANGLED (`<mid>__toList`), so the
  -- `direct` name-intersection misses them; recover them via the mangle map so the
  -- prePass marks their (mangled) occurrences `EMethodAt`.  Empty on the un-mangled
  -- path (the map is empty there) → `direct` alone → byte-identical.
  let mangled = filter (n => isSome (lookupAssoc n driverState.value.mangledShadowMapRef.value)) fns
  dedup (direct ++ mangled)

-- `public export data` / `public export record` decls — re-registered in every
-- importing module so their constructors + field layout are in scope.
-- ALSO carries public DInterface (so the multi-module path can register the
-- interface's method→iface-param map for #11 obligation checking — a numeric
-- literal's `fromInt`/`Num` obligation, recorded via recordImplObligation, needs
-- `Num`'s entry in methodIfaceParamsRef), AND public DImpl (so checkImplObligations'
-- implHeadsOf sees core's `impl Num Int` etc. — without it a legitimately Int-typed
-- literal's Num obligation finds no matching impl head and is spuriously rejected).
-- registerAllData and buildOracle read only DData, so the extra DInterface/
-- DImpl entries are inert for them.
publicDataDecls : List Decl -> List Decl
publicDataDecls [] = []
publicDataDecls ((DData VisPublic n ps vs ds)::rest) =
  DData VisPublic n ps vs ds :: publicDataDecls rest
publicDataDecls ((d@(DInterface { pub = True, ... }))::rest) =
  d :: publicDataDecls rest
publicDataDecls ((d@(DImpl { pub = True, ... }))::rest) =
  d :: publicDataDecls rest
publicDataDecls ((d@(DTypeAlias True _ _ _))::rest) = d :: publicDataDecls rest
publicDataDecls (_::rest) = publicDataDecls rest

-- WS-1a fix: every impl in scope, EXPORT OR NOT.  Instances are GLOBAL (coherence
-- + dispatch leak across module boundaries regardless of `export`), so the
-- superinterface existence query (checkSuperImpls/superImplExists) must see an
-- imported module's `impl Semigroup T` even when it isn't `export`-marked — else
-- `impl Monoid T` in an importer is falsely rejected for a "missing" super impl.
pickSchemes : List String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
pickSchemes [] _ = []
pickSchemes (n::ns) table = match lookupAssoc n table
  Some s => (n, s) :: pickSchemes ns table
  None => pickSchemes ns table

-- Bug C: for each importer-shadow name, pick from [table] the scheme that is the
-- imported STANDALONE — the one whose receiver (first arrow domain) heads in a
-- CONCRETE type constructor (`Map k v`), NOT the polymorphic interface-method scheme
-- (`t a`) that shares the same name in the seed.  A name with no concrete-domain
-- entry contributes nothing (its only binding is the method scheme; the ordinary
-- dispatch path handles it).
pickStandaloneSchemes : List String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
pickStandaloneSchemes [] _ = []
pickStandaloneSchemes (n::ns) table = match concreteDomainScheme n table
  Some s => (n, s) :: pickStandaloneSchemes ns table
  None => pickStandaloneSchemes ns table

-- the FIRST scheme bound to [n] in [table] whose first arrow domain has a concrete
-- (TCon-headed) receiver — i.e. the standalone rather than the `t a` method scheme.
concreteDomainScheme : String -> List (String, Scheme) -> Option Scheme
concreteDomainScheme _ [] = None
concreteDomainScheme n ((n2, s)::rest) =
  if n == n2 && schemeHasConcreteDomain s then
    Some s
  else
    concreteDomainScheme n rest

-- True iff the scheme's type is `dom -> _` and [dom]'s spine head is a TCon (a
-- concrete receiver), distinguishing the standalone `Map k v -> …` from the method
-- `t a -> …` whose domain head is a quantified tyvar.
schemeHasConcreteDomain : Scheme -> Bool
schemeHasConcreteDomain (Forall _ _ t) = match normalize t
  TFun dom _ _ => monoSpineHeadIsCon dom
  _ => False

monoSpineHeadIsCon : Mono -> Bool
monoSpineHeadIsCon m = match normalize m
  TApp a _ => monoSpineHeadIsCon a
  TCon _ => True
  _ => False

-- ── IMPORT-SCOPED per-module seeding (mirrors lib/typecheck.ml:4513-4543) ────
-- The multi-module drivers must NOT flat-seed the cumulative UNION of every
-- dependency's public schemes into each module's env: omInsert is last-write-wins,
-- so a later-loaded dependency's same-named binding (e.g. `string.reverse :
-- String -> String`) silently overwrites an earlier one (`list.reverse :
-- List a -> List a`) — mis-typing a module that explicitly `import list.{reverse}`.
-- Instead each module's seed is built from ONLY the names its own `DUse` decls
-- import, looked up in the SPECIFIC dependency they name.  The implicit prelude
-- (runtime + core) is always fully in scope and is threaded separately as baseSeed.

-- the bare member names a single `import` form names from its dependency —
-- mirrors lib/typecheck.ml's imported_names match (BEFORE mangle-key resolution):
--   UseGroup (_, ms) → the member names         (import dep.{a, b, c})
--   UseWild _        → ALL of the dep's schemes  (import dep.*)
--   UseName ns       → [last ns] if dotted (import a.b.name), else [] (bare name)
--   UseAlias _       → [] (alias only — no auto-imported value names)
-- `None` ⇒ wildcard (every scheme); `Some names` ⇒ exactly those member names.
-- (ORIGIN, LOCAL) per imported member.  They differ only under a member alias
-- (`import m.{a as b}`): the SCHEME is looked up under the origin `a` in the
-- dependency, and bound into this module's env under the local `b`.
importedBindings : UsePath -> Option (List (String, String))
importedBindings (UseGroup _ ms) = Some (map memberBinding ms)
importedBindings (UseWild _) = None
importedBindings (UseName ns)
  | listLen ns > 1 = Some [selfBinding (lastComponent ns)]
  | otherwise = Some []
-- A module alias enumerates the dependency's schemes, so it cannot be expressed as a
-- fixed member list — importFormSchemes handles it directly.
importedBindings (UseAlias _ _) = Some []

memberBinding : UseMember -> (String, String)
memberBinding m = (useMemberOrigin m, useMemberLocal m)

selfBinding : String -> (String, String)
selfBinding n = (n, n)

-- resolve one imported bare member name to its actual key in a dependency's public
-- schemes.  The golden path keys schemes by the bare name; the EMIT path runs after
-- universal mangling (backend.private_mangle), which rewrites a definition `member`
-- to `<dep>__member` but leaves the importer's `DUse` member name BARE — so the key
-- is the SUFFIX-matching scheme `…__member` (uniquely identified within THIS dep's
-- own schemes; distinct members never share an `__<name>` suffix).  Try the bare
-- key first (golden), then the `__<name>` suffix (mangled emit path).
memberSuffix : String -> String
memberSuffix n = "__" ++ n

-- local suffix test (string.endsWith is not imported here): `s` ends with `suf`.
endsWithStr : String -> String -> Bool
endsWithStr suf s =
  let ls = stringLength s
  let lsuf = stringLength suf
  lsuf <= ls && stringSlice (ls - lsuf) ls s == suf

-- Look the member up by its ORIGIN name, bind it under its LOCAL name.  Without an
-- alias the two coincide and this is the historical behaviour.
--
-- On the MANGLED emit path the bare origin is absent (the dep's keys are `<dep>__name`)
-- AND the reference in this module's body has itself been mangled to `<dep>__name` by
-- backend/private_mangle — so the suffix-matched entries are passed through under their
-- MANGLED keys, which is exactly the key the mangled reference will look up.  (A member
-- alias is erased by the same mangling, so it needs no rebinding there.)
resolveMemberSchemes : (String, String) -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
resolveMemberSchemes (origin, local) depSchemes = match lookupAssoc origin depSchemes
  Some s => [(local, s)]
  None => filter (kv => endsWithStr (memberSuffix origin) (fst kv)) depSchemes

-- `import m as A` binds EVERY value m exports under `A.name` — so it enumerates the
-- dependency's schemes rather than a fixed member list.
--
-- Same golden/mangled split as above, made exact by testing the dep's own mangle prefix
-- (`<sanitized mid>__`) instead of guessing: on the emit path the reference is already
-- `<mid>__name`, so the key must stay mangled; on the golden path the reference is the
-- flat `A.name` that desugar produced, so bind it under that.
aliasSchemes : String -> String -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
aliasSchemes mid alias depSchemes = map (aliasSchemeEntry mid alias) depSchemes

aliasSchemeEntry : String -> String -> (String, Scheme) -> (String, Scheme)
aliasSchemeEntry mid alias (k, s) =
  if isMangledFor mid k then
    (k, s)
  else
    (qualifiedLocal alias k, s)

-- does `k` carry the `<sanitized mid>__` prefix the mangler would have given it?
isMangledFor : String -> String -> Bool
isMangledFor mid k =
  let pre = mangledName mid ""
  let lp = stringLength pre
  lp <= stringLength k && stringSlice 0 lp k == pre

-- the (key, scheme) pairs a single `import` form brings into scope, resolving each
-- member to its (possibly mangled) key in the dependency's schemes.
importFormSchemes : String -> UsePath -> List (String, Scheme) -> List (String, Scheme)
importFormSchemes mid (UseAlias _ alias) depSchemes =
  aliasSchemes mid alias depSchemes
importFormSchemes _ path depSchemes = match importedBindings path
  None => depSchemes
  Some bindings => flatMap (b => resolveMemberSchemes b depSchemes) bindings

-- ── import aliasing: re-key the dict-arity / dict-iface tables ───────────────────
